sections.title_en: Opening

entries.farsi: بشنو از نى چون حكايت مى‏كند / از جدايى‏ها شكايت مى‏كند
entries.english: Listen to the reed how it tells a tale, complaining of separations—

entries.farsi: كز نيستان تا مرا ببريده‏اند / در نفيرم مرد و زن ناليده‏اند
entries.english: Saying, “Ever since I was parted from the reed-bed, my lament, has caused man and woman to moan.

entries.farsi: سينه خواهم شرحه شرحه از فراق / تا بگويم شرح درد اشتياق‏
entries.english: I want a bosom torn by severance, that I may unfold the pain of love-desire.

entries.farsi: هر كسى كاو دور ماند از اصل خويش / باز جويد روزگار وصل خويش‏
entries.english: Every one who is left far from his source wishes back the time when he was united with it.

entries.farsi: من به هر جمعيتى نالان شدم / جفت بد حالان و خوش حالان شدم‏
entries.english: In every company I uttered my grieving cry; I consorted with the unhappy and with them that rejoice.

entries.farsi: هر كسى از ظن خود شد يار من / از درون من نجست اسرار من‏
entries.english: Every one became my friend from his own opinion; none sought out my secrets from within me.

entries.farsi: سر من از ناله‏ى من دور نيست / ليك چشم و گوش را آن نور نيست‏
entries.english: My secret is not far from my complaint, but ear and eye lack the light.

entries.farsi: تن ز جان و جان ز تن مستور نيست / ليك كس را ديد جان دستور نيست‏
entries.english: Body is not veiled from soul, or soul from body, yet none is permitted to see the soul.

entries.farsi: آتش است اين بانگ ناى و نيست باد / هر كه اين آتش ندارد نيست باد
entries.english: This noise of the reed is fire, it is not wind: whoever has not this fire, may he be nothing!

entries.farsi: آتش عشق است كاندر نى فتاد / جوشش عشق است كاندر مى‏فتاد
entries.english: It is the fire of Love that is in the reed, it is the fervour of Love that is in the wine.

entries.farsi: نى حريف هر كه از يارى بريد / پرده‏هايش پرده‏هاى ما دريد
entries.english: The reed is the comrade of every one who has been parted from a friend: its strains pierced our hearts.

entries.farsi: همچو نى زهرى و ترياقى كه ديد / همچو نى دمساز و مشتاقى كه ديد
entries.english: Who ever saw a poison and antidote like the reed? Who ever saw a sympathiser and a longing lover like the reed?

entries.farsi: نى حديث راه پر خون مى‏كند / قصه‏هاى عشق مجنون مى‏كند
entries.english: The reed tells of the Way full of blood and recounts stories of the passion of Majnun.

entries.farsi: محرم اين هوش جز بى‏هوش نيست / مر زبان را مشترى جز گوش نيست‏
entries.english: Only to the senseless is this sense confided: the tongue has no customer save the ear.

entries.farsi: در غم ما روزها بى‏گاه شد / روزها با سوزها همراه شد
entries.english: In our woe the days have become untimely: our days travel hand in hand with burning griefs.

entries.farsi: روزها گر رفت گو رو باك نيست / تو بمان اى آن كه چون تو پاك نيست‏
entries.english: If our days are gone, let them go!—It is no matter. Do You remain, for none is holy as You are!

entries.farsi: هر كه جز ماهى ز آبش سير شد / هر كه بى‏روزى است روزش دير شد
entries.english: Whoever is not a fish becomes sated with His water; whoever is without daily bread finds the day long.

entries.farsi: درنيابد حال پخته هيچ خام / پس سخن كوتاه بايد و السلام‏
entries.english: None that is raw understands the state of the ripe: therefore my words must be brief. Farewell!

entries.farsi: بند بگسل، باش آزاد اى پسر / چند باشى بند سيم و بند زر
entries.english: O son, burst your chains and be free! How long will you be a bondsman toe silver and gold?

entries.farsi: گر بريزى بحر را در كوزه‏اى / چند گنجد قسمت يك روزه‏اى‏
entries.english: If you pour the sea into a pitcher, how much will it hold? One day’s store.

entries.farsi: كوزه‏ى چشم حريصان پر نشد / تا صدف قانع نشد پر در نشد
entries.english: The pitcher, the eye of the covetous, never becomes full: the oyster-shell is not filled with pearls until it is contented.

entries.farsi: هر كه را جامه ز عشقى چاك شد / او ز حرص و عيب كلى پاك شد
entries.english: He whose garment is rent by a love is purged of covetousness and all defect.

entries.farsi: شاد باش اى عشق خوش سوداى ما / اى طبيب جمله علتهاى ما
entries.english: Hail, O Love that bring us good gain — you that art the physician of all our ills

entries.farsi: اى دواى نخوت و ناموس ما / اى تو افلاطون و جالينوس ما
entries.english: The remedy of our pride and vainglory, our Plato and our Galen!

entries.farsi: جسم خاك از عشق بر افلاک شد / كوه در رقص آمد و چالاك شد
entries.english: Through Love the earthly body soared to the skies: the mountain began to dance and became nimble.

entries.farsi: عشق جان طور آمد عاشقا / طور مست و خر موسى صاعقا
entries.english: Love inspired Mount Sinai, O lover, Sinai drunk and Moses fell in a swoon.

entries.farsi: با لب دمساز خود گر جفتمى / همچو نى من گفتنيها گفتمى‏
entries.english: Were I joined to the lip of one in accord with me, I too, like the reed, would tell all that may be told;

entries.farsi: هر كه او از هم زبانى شد جدا / بى‏زبان شد گر چه دارد صد نوا
entries.english: Whoever is parted from one who speaks his language becomes dumb, though he have a hundred songs.

entries.farsi: چون كه گل رفت و گلستان در گذشت / نشنوى ز ان پس ز بلبل سر گذشت‏
entries.english: When the rose is gone and the garden faded, you will hear no more the nightingale’s story.

entries.farsi: جمله معشوق است و عاشق پرده‏اى / زنده معشوق است و عاشق مرده‏اى
entries.english: The Beloved is all and the lover a veil; the Beloved is living and the lover a dead thing.

entries.farsi: چون نباشد عشق را پرواى او / او چو مرغى ماند بى‏پر، واى او
entries.english: When Love has no care for him, he is left as a bird without wings. Alas for him then!

entries.farsi: من چگونه هوش دارم پيش و پس / چون نباشد نور يارم پيش و پس‏
entries.english: How should I have consciousness before or behind when the light of my Beloved is not before me and behind?

entries.farsi: عشق خواهد كاين سخن بيرون بود / آينه غماز نبود چون بود
entries.english: Love wills that this Word should be shown forth: if the mirror does not reflect, how is that?

entries.farsi: آينه‏ت دانى چرا غماز نيست / ز انكه زنگار از رخش ممتاز نيست‏
entries.english: Dost you know why the mirror reflects nothing? Because the rust is not cleared from its face.

entries.farsi: بشنويد اى دوستان اين داستان / خود حقيقت نقد حال ماست آن‏
entries.english: O my friends listen to this tale: in truth it is the very marrow of our inward state

sections.title_fa: حكايت عاشق شدن پادشاه بر كنيزك و بيمار شدن كنيزك و تدبير در صحت او
sections.title_en: The story of the king’s falling in love with a handmaiden and buying her.

entries.farsi: بود شاهى در زمانى پيش از اين / ملك دنيا بودش و هم ملك دين‏
entries.english: In olden time there was a king to whom belonged the power temporal and also the power spiritual.

entries.farsi: اتفاقا شاه روزى شد سوار / با خواص خويش از بهر شكار
entries.english: It chanced that one day he rode with his courtiers to the chase.

entries.farsi: يك كنيزك ديد شه بر شاه راه / شد غالم آن كنيزك جان شاه‏
entries.english: On the king’s highway the king espied a handmaiden: the soul of the king was enthralled by her.

entries.farsi: مرغ جانش در قفس چون مى‏طپيد / داد مال و آن كنيزك را خريد
entries.english: Forasmuch as the bird, his soul, was fluttering in its cage, he gave money and bought the handmaiden.

entries.farsi: چون خريد او را و برخوردار شد / آن كنيزك از قضا بيمار شد
entries.english: After he had bought her and won to his desire, by Divine destiny she sickened.

entries.farsi: آن يكى خر داشت، پالانش نبود / يافت پالان گرگ خر را در ربود
entries.english: A certain man had an ass but no pack-saddle: he got a saddle; the wolf carried away his ass.

entries.farsi: كوزه بودش آب مى‏نامد به دست / آب را چون يافت خود كوزه شكست
entries.english: He had a pitcher, but no water could be obtained: when he found water, the pitcher broke.

entries.farsi: شه طبيبان جمع كرد از چپ و راست / گفت جان هر دو در دست شماست‏
entries.english: The king gathered the physicians together from left and right and said to them, “The life of us both is in your hands.

entries.farsi: جان من سهل است جان جانم اوست / دردمند و خسته‏ام درمانم اوست‏
entries.english: My life is of no account, she is the life of my life. I am in pain and wounded: she is my remedy.

entries.farsi: هر كه درمان كرد مر جان مرا / برد گنج و در و مرجان مرا
entries.english: Whoever heals her that is my life will bear away with him my treasure and pearls, large and small?”

entries.farsi: جمله گفتندش كه جان‏بازى كنيم / فهم گرد آريم و انبازى كنيم‏
entries.english: They all answered him, saying, “We will hazard our lives and summon all our intelligence and put it into the common stock.

entries.farsi: هر يكى از ما مسيح عالمى است / هر الم را در كف ما مرهمى است‏
entries.english: Each one of us is the Messiah of a world: in our hands is a medicine for every pain.”

entries.farsi: گر خدا خواهد نگفتند از بطر / پس خدا بنمودشان عجز بشر
entries.english: In their arrogance they did not say, “If God will”; therefore God showed unto them the weakness of Man.

entries.farsi: ترك استثنا مرادم قسوتى است / نى همين گفتن كه عارض حالتى است‏
entries.english: I mean omission of the saving clause is a hardness of heart ; not the mere saying of these words, for that is a superficial circumstance.

entries.farsi: اى بسا ناورده استثنا به گفت / جان او با جان استثناست جفت‏
entries.english: How many a one has not pronounced the saving clause, and yet his soul is in harmony with the soul of it!

entries.farsi: هر چه كردند از عالج و از دوا / گشت رنج افزون و حاجت ناروا
entries.english: The more cures and remedies they applied, the more did the illness increase, and the need was not fulfilled.

entries.farsi: آن كنيزك از مرض چون موى شد / چشم شه از اشك خون چون جوى شد
entries.english: The sick gir1 became as a hair, the eyes of the king flowed with tears of blood, like a river.

entries.farsi: از قضا سركنگبين صفرا فزود / روغن بادام خشكى مى‏نمود
entries.english: By Divine destiny, oxymel produced bile, and oil of almonds was increasing the dryness.

entries.farsi: از هليله قبض شد اطالق رفت / آب آتش را مدد شد همچو نفت‏
entries.english: From myrobalm constipation resulted, relaxation ceased; and water fed the flames, like naphtha.

sections.title_fa: ظاهر شدن عجز حكيمان از معالجه‏ى كنيزك و روى آوردن، پادشاه به درگاه خدا و در خواب ديدن او ولى را
sections.title_en: How it became manifest to the king that the physicians were unable to cure the handmaiden and how he turned his face towards God and dreamed of a holy man.

entries.farsi: شه چو عجز آن حكيمان را بديد / پا برهنه جانب مسجد دويد
entries.english: When the king saw the powerlessness of those physicians, he ran bare-footed to the mosque.

entries.farsi: رفت در مسجد سوى محراب شد / سجده گاه از اشك شه پر آب شد
entries.english: He entered the mosque and advanced to the mihrab: the prayer-carpet was bathed in the king’s tears.

entries.farsi: چون به خويش آمد ز غرقاب فنا / خوش زبان بگشاد در مدح و ثنا
entries.english: On coming to himself out of the flood of ecstasy he opened his lips in goodly praise and laud,

entries.farsi: كاى كمينه بخششت ملك جهان / من چه گويم چون تو مى‏دانى نهان‏
entries.english: Saying, “O You whose least gift is the empire of the world, what shall I say, inasmuch as You know the hidden thing?

entries.farsi: اى هميشه حاجت ما را پناه / بار ديگر ما غلط كرديم راه‏
entries.english: O You with whom we always take refuge in our need, once again we have missed the way.

entries.farsi: ليك گفتى گر چه مى‏دانم سرت / زود هم پيدا كنش بر ظاهرت‏
entries.english: But You have said, ‘Although I know your secret, nevertheless declare it forthwith in your outward act.’”

entries.farsi: چون بر آورد از ميان جان خروش / اندر آمد بحر بخشايش به جوش‏
entries.english: When from the depths of his soul he raised a cry, the sea of Bounty began to surge.

entries.farsi: در ميان گريه خوابش در ربود / ديد در خواب او كه پيرى رو نمود
entries.english: Slumber overtook him in the midst of weeping: he dreamed that an old man appeared

entries.farsi: گفت اى شه مژده حاجاتت رواست / گر غريبى آيدت فردا ز ماست‏
entries.english: And said, “Good tidings, O king! Your prayers are granted. If tomorrow a stranger come for you, he is from me.

entries.farsi: چون كه آيد او حكيمى حاذق است / صادقش دان كه امين و صادق است‏
entries.english: When he comes, he is the skilled physician: deem him veracious, for he is trusty and true.

entries.farsi: در عالجش سحر مطلق را ببين / در مزاجش قدرت حق را ببين‏
entries.english: In his remedy behold absolute magic; in his temperament behold the power of Haqq.

entries.farsi: چون رسيد آن وعده‏گاه و روز شد / آفتاب از شرق، اختر سوز شد
entries.english: When the promised hour arrived and day broke and the sun, from the east, began to burn the stars,

entries.farsi: بود اندر منظره شه منتظر / تا ببيند آن چه بنمودند سر
entries.english: The king was in the belvedere, expecting to see that which had been shown mysteriously.

entries.farsi: ديد شخصى فاضلى پر مايه‏اى / آفتابى در ميان سايه‏اى‏
entries.english: He saw a person excellent and worshipful, a sun amidst a shadow,

entries.farsi: مى‏رسيد از دور مانند هالل / نيست بود و هست بر شكل خيال‏
entries.english: Coming from afar, like the new moon: he was non-existent, though existent in the form of imagination.

entries.farsi: نيست وش باشد خيال اندر روان / تو جهانى بر خيالى بين روان‏
entries.english: In the spirit imagination is as nothing; behold a world on a phantasy!

entries.farsi: بر خيالى صلح‏شان و جنگشان / وز خيالى فخرشان و ننگشان‏
entries.english: Their peace and their war on a thought, and their pride and their shame spring from idea;

entries.farsi: آن خيالاتى كه دام اولياست / عكس مه رويان بستان خداست‏
entries.english: Those imaginings which trap the saints are the reflection of the fair ones of the garden of God.

entries.farsi: آن خيالى كه شه اندر خواب ديد / در رخ مهمان همى‏آمد پديد
entries.english: In the countenance of the invisible guest was appearing that imagination which the king beheld in his dream.

entries.farsi: شه به جاى حاجيان واپيش رفت / پيش آن مهمان غيب خويش رفت‏
entries.english: The king himself, instead of the chamberlains, went forward to meet his guest from the Invisible.

entries.farsi: هر دو بحرى آشنا آموخته / هر دو جان بى‏دوختن بر دوخته‏
entries.english: Both were seamen who had learned to swim, the souls of both were knit together without sewing.

entries.farsi: گفت معشوقم تو بوده ستى نه آن / ليك كار از كار خيزد در جهان‏
entries.english: The king said, “You were my Beloved, not she; but in this world deed issues from deed.

entries.farsi: اى مرا تو مصطفى من چون عمر / از براى خدمتت بندم كمر
entries.english: O you who art to me Mustafa, while I am like unto `Umar— I will gird my loins to do you service.”

sections.title_fa: ‏از خداوند ولى التوفيق در خواستن توفيق رعايت ادب در همه حالها و بيان كردن وخامت ضررهاى بى‏ادبى
sections.title_en: Beseeching the Lord, who is our Helper, to help us to observe self-control in all circumstances, and explaining the harmful and pernicious consequences of indiscipline.

entries.farsi: از خدا جوييم توفيق ادب / بى‏ادب محروم گشت از لطف رب‏
entries.english: Let us implore God to help us to adab: one who lacks self-control is deprived of the grace of the Lord.

entries.farsi: بى‏ادب تنها نه خود را داشت بد / بلكه آتش در همه آفاق زد
entries.english: The undisciplined man does not mistreat himself alone, but he sets the whole world on fire.

entries.farsi: مايده از آسمان در مى‏رسيد / بى‏شرى و بيع و بى‏گفت و شنيد
entries.english: A table was coming down from heaven without headache and without selling and buying,

entries.farsi: در ميان قوم موسى چند كس / بى‏ادب گفتند كو سير و عدس‏
entries.english: Some of the people of Moses cried disrespectfully, “Where is garlic and lentils?”

entries.farsi: منقطع شد خوان و نان از آسمان / ماند رنج زرع و بيل و داسمان
entries.english: The heavenly bread and dishes were cut off: there remained the toil of sowing and mattock and scythe.

entries.farsi: باز عيسى چون شفاعت كرد، حق / خوان فرستاد و غنيمت بر طبق‏
entries.english: Again, when Jesus made intercession, God sent food and bounty on trays,

entries.farsi: باز گستاخان ادب بگذاشتند / چون گدايان زله‏ها برداشتند
entries.english: But once more the insolent fellows omitted to show respect and, like beggars, snatched away the food,

entries.farsi: البه كرده عيسى ايشان را كه اين / دايم است و كم نگردد از زمين
entries.english: Jesus entreated them, saying, “This is lasting and will not fail from off the earth.”

entries.farsi: بد گمانى كردن و حرص آورى / كفر باشد پيش خوان مهترى
entries.english: To show suspicion and greed at the table of Majesty is ingratitude.

entries.farsi: ز ان گدا رويان ناديده ز آز / آن در رحمت بر ايشان شد فراز
entries.english: Because of those impudent wretches who were blinded by greed, that gate of mercy was closed upon them.

entries.farsi: ابر برنايد پى منع زكات / وز زنا افتد وبا اندر جهات‏
entries.english: On account of withholding the poor-tax no rain-clouds arise, and in consequence of fornication the plague spreads in all directions.

entries.farsi: هر چه بر تو آيد از ظلمات و غم / آن ز بى‏باكى و گستاخى است هم‏
entries.english: Whatever befalls you of gloom and sorrow is the result of irreverence and lack of Adab nevertheless.

entries.farsi: هر كه بى‏باكى كند در راه دوست / ره زن مردان شد و نامرد اوست‏
entries.english: Any one behaving with irreverence in the path of the Friend is a brigand who robs men, and he is no man.

entries.farsi: از ادب پر نور گشته است اين فلك / وز ادب معصوم و پاك آمد ملك‏
entries.english: Through Adab this Heaven has been filled with light, and through discipline the angels became immaculate and holy

entries.farsi: بد ز گستاخى كسوف آفتاب / شد عزازيلى ز جرات رد باب‏
entries.english: By reason of Adab the sun was eclipsed, and insolence caused an ‘Azazil to be turned back from the door.

sections.title_fa: مالقات پادشاه با آن ولى كه در خوابش نمودند
sections.title_en: The meeting of the king with the divine physician whose coming had been announced to him in a dream

entries.farsi: دست بگشاد و كنارانش گرفت / همچو عشق اندر دل و جانش گرفت‏
entries.english: He opened his hands and clasped him to his breast and received him, like love, into his heart and soul,

entries.farsi: دست و پيشانيش بوسيدن گرفت / وز مقام و راه پرسيدن گرفت
entries.english: And began to kiss his hand and brow and inquire concerning his home and journey.

entries.farsi: پرس پرسان مى‏كشيدش تا به صدر / گفت گنجى يافتم آخر به صبر
entries.english: With many a question he led him to the dais. “At last,” said he, “I have found a treasure by being patient.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى نور حق و دفع حرج / معنى الصبر مفتاح الفرج‏
entries.english: He said, “ O gift from God and defence against trouble, the meaning of ‘Patience is the key of joy’!

entries.farsi: اى لقاى تو جواب هر سؤال / مشكل از تو حل شود بى‏قيل و قال‏
entries.english: O you whose countenance is the answer to every question, by you hard knots are loosed without discussion.

entries.farsi: ترجمانى هر چه ما را در دل است / دست گيرى هر كه پايش در گل است‏
entries.english: You interpret all that is in our hearts, you give a helping hand to every one whose foot is in the mud.

entries.farsi: مرحبا يا مجتبى يا مرتضى / إن تغب جاء القضاء ضاق الفضا
entries.english: Welcome, O chosen one, O approved one! If you vanish, Ghaza will come and the wide room will be straitened

entries.farsi: أنت مولى القوم من ال يشتهي / قد ردى كَلاَّ لَئِنْ لَمْ ينته‏
entries.english: You art the protector of the people. He that desires not has gone to perdition. Nay, verily, if he do not refrain. . .!”

sections.title_fa: بردن پادشاه آن طبيب را بر سر بيمار تا حال او را ببيند
sections.title_en: How the king led the physician to the bedside of the sick girl, that he might see her condition.

entries.farsi: قصه‏ى رنجور و رنجورى بخواند / بعد از آن در پيش رنجورش نشاند
entries.english: When that meeting and bounteous repast was over, he took his hand and conducted him to the harem.

entries.farsi: چون گذشت آن مجلس و خوان كرم / دست او بگرفت و برد اندر حرم‏
entries.english: He rehearsed the tale of the invalid and her illness, and then seated him beside the sick.

entries.farsi: رنگ رو و نبض و قاروره بديد / هم عالماتش هم اسبابش شنيد
entries.english: The physician observed the colour of her face, her pulse, and her urine; he heard both the symptoms and the causes of her malady.

entries.farsi: گفت هر دارو كه ايشان كرده‏اند / آن عمارت نيست ويران كرده‏اند
entries.english: He said, “None of the remedies which they have applied builds up: they have brought destruction.

entries.farsi: بى‏خبر بودند از حال درون / أستعيذ اللهَّ مما يفترون‏
entries.english: They were ignorant of the inward state. I seek refuge with God from that which they invent.”

entries.farsi: ديد رنج و كشف شد بر وى نهفت / ليك پنهان كرد و با سلطان نگفت
entries.english: He saw the pain, and the secret became open to him, but he concealed it and did not tell the king.

entries.farsi: ديد از زاريش كو زار دل است / تن خوش است و او گرفتار دل است‏
entries.english: Her pain was not from black or yellow bile: the smell of every type of wood appears from the smoke.

entries.farsi: رنجش از صفرا و از سودا نبود / بوى هر هيزم پديد آيد ز دود
entries.english: From her sore grief he perceived that she was heart-broken; well in body, but stricken in heart.

entries.farsi: عاشقى پيداست از زارى دل / نيست بيمارى چو بيمارى دل‏
entries.english: Being in love is made manifest by soreness of heart: there is no sickness like heart-sickness.

entries.farsi: علت عاشق ز علتها جداست / عشق اصطرالب اسرار خداست
entries.english: The lover’s ailment is separate from all other ailments: love is the astrolabe of the mysteries of God.

entries.farsi: عاشقى گر زين سر و گر ز ان سر است / عاقبت ما را بدان سر رهبر است
entries.english: Whether love be from this side or from that side, in the end it leads us beyond.

entries.farsi: هر چه گويم عشق را شرح و بيان / چون به عشق آيم خجل گردم از آن‏
entries.english: Whatever I say in exposition and explanation of Love, when I come to Love I am ashamed of that.

entries.farsi: گر چه تفسير زبان روشن‏گر است / ليك عشق بى‏زبان روشن‏تر است‏
entries.english: Although the commentary of the tongue makes clear, yet silent love is clearer.

entries.farsi: چون قلم اندر نوشتن مى‏شتافت / چون به عشق آمد قلم بر خود شكافت
entries.english: While the pen was making haste in writing, it split upon itself as soon as it came to Love.

entries.farsi: عقل در شرحش چو خر در گل بخفت / شرح عشق و عاشقى هم عشق گفت‏
entries.english: In expounding Love, the intellect lay down like an ass in the mire: it was Love that uttered the explanation of love and being in love.

entries.farsi: آفتاب آمد دليل آفتاب / گر دليلت بايد از وى رو متاب‏
entries.english: The proof of the sun is the sun: if you require the proof, do not avert your face from him!

entries.farsi: از وى ار سايه نشانى مى‏دهد / شمس هر دم نور جانى مى‏دهد
entries.english: If the shadow gives an indication of him, the sun gives spiritual light every moment.

entries.farsi: سايه خواب آرد ترا همچون سمر / چون بر آيد شمس انْشَقَّ القمر
entries.english: The shadow, like chat in the night-hours, brings sleep to you; when the sun rises the moon is cloven asunder.

entries.farsi: خود غريبى در جهان چون شمس نيست / شمس جان باقيى كش امس نيست‏
entries.english: There is nothing in the world so wondrous strange as the sun, the Sun of the spirit is everlasting: it has no yesterday.

entries.farsi: شمس جان كاو خارج آمد از اثير / نبودش در ذهن و در خارج نظير
entries.english: Although the external sun is unique, still it is possible to imagine one resembling it;

entries.farsi: شمس در خارج اگر چه هست فرد / مى‏توان هم مثل او تصوير كرد
entries.english: The spiritual Sun, which is beyond the ether, has no peer in the mind or externally.

entries.farsi: در تصور ذات او را گنج كو / تا در آيد در تصور مثل او
entries.english: Where is room in the imagination for His essence that the like of Him should come into the imagination?

entries.farsi: چون حديث روى شمس الدين رسيد / شمس چارم آسمان سر در كشيد
entries.english: When news arrived of the face of Shamsu’ddin, the sun of the fourth heaven drew in its head.

entries.farsi: واجب آيد چون كه آمد نام او / شرح كردن رمزى از انعام او
entries.english: Since his name has come (to my lips), I must set forth some hint of his bounty.

entries.farsi: اين نفس جان دامنم بر تافته ست / بوى پيراهان يوسف يافته ست‏
entries.english: At this moment my ‘Soul’ has plucked my skirt: he has caught the perfume of Joseph’s vest.

entries.farsi: از براى حق صحبت سالها / باز گو حالى از آن خوش حالها
entries.english: “For the sake of our years of companionship, recount one of those sweet ecstasies,

entries.farsi: تا زمين و آسمان خندان شود / عقل و روح و ديده صد چندان شود
entries.english: That earth and heaven may laugh, that intellect and spirit and eve may increase a hundredfold.”

entries.farsi: ال تكلفني فإنى في الفنا / كلت أفهامي فال أحصي ثنا
entries.english: “Do not lay tasks on me, for I have passed away from myself; my apprehensions are blunted and I know not how to praise.

entries.farsi: كل شى‏ء قاله غير المفيق / إن تكلف أو تصلف ال يليق‏
entries.english: Everything that is said by one, who has not returned to consciousness, if he constrains himself or boastfully exaggerates, is unseemly.

entries.farsi: من چه گويم يك رگم هشيار نيست / شرح آن يارى كه او را يار نيست‏
entries.english: How should I—not a vein of mine is sensible-- describe that Friend who has no peer?

entries.farsi: شرح اين هجران و اين خون جگر / اين زمان بگذار تا وقت دگر
entries.english: The description of this severance and this heart’s blood leave over till another time.”

entries.farsi: قال أطعمني فإني جائع / و اعتجل فالوقت سيف قاطع
entries.english: He said: “Feed me, for I am hungry, and hurry, for Time is a cutting sword.

entries.farsi: صوفى ابن الوقت باشد اى رفيق / نيست فردا گفتن از شرط طريق‏
entries.english: The Sufi is the son of the time, O comrade: it is not the rule of the Way to say ‘To-morrow.’

entries.farsi: تو مگر خود مرد صوفى نيستى / هست را از نسيه خيزد نيستى
entries.english: Are you not indeed a Sufi, then? That which is reduced to nothing by postponing the payment.”

entries.farsi: گفتمش پوشيده خوشتر سر يار / خود تو در ضمن حكايت گوش دار
entries.english: I said to him: “It is better that the secret of the Friend should be disguised: listen to the contents of the tale.

entries.farsi: خوشتر آن باشد كه سر دلبران / گفته آيد در حديث ديگران
entries.english: It is better that the lovers’ secret should be told in the talk of others.”

entries.farsi: گفت مكشوف و برهنه گوى اين / آشكارا به كه پنهان ذكر دين‏
entries.english: He said: “Tell it openly and nakedly and without unfaithfulness: do not put me off, O trifler!

entries.farsi: پرده بردار و برهنه گو كه من / مى‏نخسبم با صنم با پيرهن‏
entries.english: Lift the veil and speak openly, for I do not wear a shirt when I sleep with the Adored One.”

entries.farsi: گفتم ار عريان شود او در عيان / نى تو مانى نى كنارت نى ميان‏
entries.english: I said: “If He should become naked in vision, neither you, nor your bosom or your waist.

entries.farsi: آرزو مى‏خواه ليك اندازه خواه / بر نتابد كوه را يك برگ كاه‏
entries.english: Ask your wish, but ask with measure: a blade of straw will not support the mountain.

entries.farsi: آفتابى كز وى اين عالم فروخت / اندكى گر پيش آيد جمله سوخت‏
entries.english: If the Sun, by whom this world is illumined, should approach a little, all will be burned.

entries.farsi: فتنه و آشوب و خون‏ريزى مجوى / بيش از اين از شمس تبريزى مگوى‏
entries.english: Do not seek trouble and turmoil and bloodshed: say no more concerning the Sun of Tabriz!”

entries.farsi: اين ندارد آخر از آغاز گوى / رو تمام اين حكايت باز گوى
entries.english: This has no end: tell of the beginning. Go; relate the conclusion of this tale.

sections.title_fa: خلوت طلبيدن آن ولى از پادشاه جهت دريافتن رنج كنيزك
sections.title_en: How that saint demanded of the king to be alone with the handmaiden for the purpose of discovering her malady.

entries.farsi: گفت اى شه خلوتى كن خانه را / دور كن هم خويش و هم بيگانه را
entries.english: He said: “O king, empty the house; send away both kinsfolk and strangers.

entries.farsi: كس ندارد گوش در دهليزها / تا بپرسم زين كنيزك چيزها
entries.english: Let no one listen in the entrance-halls, that I may ask certain things of this slave girl.”

entries.farsi: خانه خالى ماند و يك ديار نى / جز طبيب و جز همان بيمار نى‏
entries.english: The house was left empty and not one inhabitant: nobody save the physician and that sick girl.

entries.farsi: نرم نرمك گفت شهر تو كجاست / كه عالج اهل هر شهرى جداست‏
entries.english: Very gently he said, “Where is your native town? For the treatment suitable to the people of each town is separate.

entries.farsi: و اندر آن شهر از قرابت كيستت / خويشى و پيوستگى با چيستت‏
entries.english: And in that town who is related to you? With whom do have you kinship and affinity? “

entries.farsi: دست بر نبضش نهاد و يك به يك / باز مى‏پرسيد از جور فلك‏
entries.english: He laid his hand on her pulse and put questions, one by one, about the injustice of Heaven.

entries.farsi: چون كسى را خار در پايش جهد / پاى خود را بر سر زانو نهد
entries.english: When a thorn darts into any one’s foot, he sets his foot upon his knee,

entries.farsi: وز سر سوزن همى‏جويد سرش / ور نيابد مى‏كند با لب ترش‏
entries.english: And keeps searching for its head with the point of a needle, and if he does not find it, he keeps moistening it with his lip.

entries.farsi: خار در پا شد چنين دشوار ياب / خار در دل چون بود واده جواب‏
entries.english: A thorn in the foot is so hard to find: how is it with a thorn in the heart? Answer!

entries.farsi: خار در دل گر بديدى هر خسى / دست كى بودى غمان را بر كسى‏
entries.english: If every low fellow had seen the thorn in the heart, when would sorrows gain the upper hand over any one?

entries.farsi: كس به زير دم خر خارى نهد / خر نداند دفع آن بر مى‏جهد
entries.english: Somebody sticks a thorn under a donkey’s tail: the donkey does not know how to get rid of it: he starts jumping.

entries.farsi: بر جهد و ان خار محكمتر زند / عاقلى بايد كه خارى بر كند
entries.english: He jumps, and the thorn sinks deeper: it needs an intelligent person to extract a thorn.

entries.farsi: خر ز بهر دفع خار از سوز و درد / جفته مى‏انداخت صد جا زخم كرد
entries.english: In order to get rid of the thorn, the donkey from irritation and pain went on kicking and dealing blows in a hundred places,

entries.farsi: آن حكيم خارچين استاد بود / دست مى‏زد جا به جا مى‏آزمود
entries.english: That thorn-removing physician was an expert: putting his hand on one spot after another, he tested.

entries.farsi: ز ان كنيزك بر طريق داستان / باز مى‏پرسيد حال دوستان‏
entries.english: He inquired of the girl concerning her friends, by way of narrative,

entries.farsi: با حكيم او قصه‏ها مى‏گفت فاش / از مقام و خاجگان و شهر تاش‏
entries.english: And she disclosed to the physician circumstances touching her home and masters and fellow-townsmen.

entries.farsi: سوى قصه گفتنش مى‏داشت گوش / سوى نبض و جستنش مى‏داشت هوش‏
entries.english: He listened to her story he continued to observe her pulse and its beating,

entries.farsi: تا كه نبض از نام كى گردد جهان / او بود مقصود جانش در جهان‏ا ن‏
entries.english: So that at whoever’s name her pulse should begin to throb, that person is the object of her soul’s desire in the world.

entries.farsi: دوستان شهر او را بر شمرد / بعد از آن شهرى دگر را نام برد
entries.english: He counted up the friends in her native town; then he mentioned another town by name.

entries.farsi: ت چون بيرون شدى از شهر خويش / در كدامين شهر بوده ستى تو بيش‏
entries.english: He said: “When you went forth from your own town, in which town did you live mostly?”

entries.farsi: نام شهرى گفت وز آن هم در گذشت / رنگ روى و نبض او ديگر نگشت‏
entries.english: She mentioned the name of a certain town and from that too she passed on there was no change in the colour of her face or in her pulse.

entries.farsi: خواجگان و شهرها را يك به يك / باز گفت از جاى و از نان و نمك‏
entries.english: Master’s and towns, one by one, she told of, and about dwelling-place and bread and salt.

entries.farsi: شهر شهر و خانه خانه قصه كرد / نى رگش جنبيد و نى رخ گشت زرد
entries.english: She told stories of many a town and many a house, no vein of her quivered nor did her cheek grow pale.

entries.farsi: نبض او بر حال خود بد بى‏گزند / تا بپرسيد از سمرقند چو قند
entries.english: Her pulse remained in its normal state, unimpaired, till he asked about Samarqand, the sweet as candy.

entries.farsi: نبض جست و روى سرخ و زرد شد / كز سمرقندى زرگر فرد شد
entries.english: Her pulse jumped and her face went red and pale, for she had been parted from a man of Samarqand, a goldsmith.

entries.farsi: چون ز رنجور آن حكيم اين راز يافت / اصل آن درد و بال را باز يافت‏
entries.english: When the physician found out this secret from the sick, he discerned the source of that grief and woe.

entries.farsi: گفت كوى او كدام است در گذر / او سر پل گفت و كوى غاتفر
entries.english: He said: “ In which quarter of the town does he live?” “Sar-i Pul,” she replied, “and Ghatafar street.”

entries.farsi: گفت دانستم كه رنجت چيست زود / در خالصت سحرها خواهم نمود
entries.english: Said he: “ I know why you are ill and I will at once display the arts of magic in delivering you’.

entries.farsi: شاد باش و فارغ و ايمن كه من / آن كنم با تو كه باران با چمن‏
entries.english: Be glad and care-free and have no fear, for I will do to you that which rain does to the meadow.

entries.farsi: من غم تو مى‏خورم تو غم مخور / بر تو من مشفق‏ترم از صد پدر
entries.english: I will be anxious for you, do not be anxious: I am kinder to you than a hundred fathers.

entries.farsi: هان و هان اين راز را با كس مگو / گر چه از تو شه كند بس جستجو
entries.english: Beware! Tell no one this secret, not even if the king should question you.

entries.farsi: چون كه اسرارت نهان در دل شود / آن مرادت زودتر حاصل شود
entries.english: When your heart becomes the grave of your secret, that desire of yours will be gained more quickly.

entries.farsi: فت پيغمبر كه هر كه سر نهفت / زود گردد با مراد خويش جفت‏
entries.english: The Prophet said that any one who hides his inmost thought will soon attain to the object of his desire.

entries.farsi: دانه چون اندر زمين پنهان شود / سر آن سر سبزى بستان شود
entries.english: When seeds are hidden in the earth, their inward secret becomes the verdure of the garden.

entries.farsi: زر و نقره گر نبودندى نهان / پرورش كى يافتندى زير كان‏
entries.english: If gold and silver were not hidden, how would they get nourishment in the mine?

entries.farsi: وعده‏ها و لطفهاى آن حكيم / كرد آن رنجور را ايمن ز بيم‏
entries.english: The promises and soothing words of the physician made the sick safe from fear.

entries.farsi: وعده‏ها باشد حقيقى دل پذير / وعده‏ها باشد مجازى تاسه‏گير
entries.english: There are true promises, grateful to the heart; there are false promises, fraught with disquietude.

entries.farsi: وعده‏ى اهل كرم گنج روان / وعده‏ى نااهل شد رنج روان‏
entries.english: The promise of the noble is current coin; the promise of the unworthy becomes anguish of soul.

sections.title_fa: دريافتن آن ولى رنج را و عرض كردن رنج او را پيش پادشاه‏
sections.title_en: How the saint, having discovered the illness, laid it before the king.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن برخاست و عزم شاه كرد / شاه را ز ان شمه‏اى آگاه كرد
entries.english: Then he arose and went to see the king and acquainted him with a portion of that matter.

entries.farsi: گفت تدبير آن بود كان مرد را / حاضر آريم از پى اين درد را
entries.english: “The plan,” said he, “is that we should bring the man here for the sake of this malady.

entries.farsi: مرد زرگر را بخوان ز ان شهر دور / با زر و خلعت بده او را غرور
entries.english: Summon the goldsmith from that far country; beguile him with gold and robes of honour.”

sections.title_fa: فرستادن پادشاه رسوالن به سمرقند به آوردن زرگر
sections.title_en: How the king sent messengers to Samarqand to fetch the goldsmith.

entries.farsi: تا سمرقند آمدند آن دو امير / پيش آن زرگر ز شاهنشه بشير
entries.english: The king sent thither one or two messengers, clever men and competent and very just.

entries.farsi: شه فرستاد آن طرف يك دو رسول / حاذقان و كافيان بس عدول‏
entries.english: To Samarqand came the two messengers for the goldsmith debonair and wanton,

entries.farsi: كاى لطيف استاد كامل معرفت / فاش اندر شهرها از تو صفت‏
entries.english: Saying, “O fine master, perfect in knowledge, you whose quality is famous in the lands,

entries.farsi: نك فالن شه از براى زرگرى / اختيارت كرد زيرا مهترى‏
entries.english: Lo, such-and-such a king has chosen you for the goldsmith’s craft, because you art eminent.

entries.farsi: اينك اين خلعت بگير و زر و سيم / چون بيايى خاص باشى و نديم‏
entries.english: Look now, receive this robe of honour and gold and silver; when you come, you will be a favourite and boon-companion.”

entries.farsi: مرد مال و خلعت بسيار ديد / غره شد از شهر و فرزندان بريد
entries.english: The man saw the much wealth and the many robes: he was beguiled; he parted from his town and children.

entries.farsi: اندر آمد شادمان در راه مرد / بى‏خبر كان شاه قصد جانش كرد
entries.english: Blithely the man came into the road, unaware that the king had formed a design against his life.

entries.farsi: اسب تازى بر نشست و شاد تاخت / خونبهاى خويش را خلعت شناخت‏
entries.english: He mounted an Arab horse and sped on joyously: the price of his blood he deemed a robe of honour.

entries.farsi: اى شده اندر سفر با صد رضا / خود به پاى خويش تا سوء القضا
entries.english: O, who with a hundred consents yourself with your own foot did enter on the journey to the fated ill!

entries.farsi: در خيالش ملك و عز و مهترى / گفت عزرائيل رو آرى برى‏
entries.english: He imagined riches, power, and lordship. Said `Azazil, “Go. Yes, you will get! “

entries.farsi: چون رسيد از راه آن مرد غريب / اندر آوردش به پيش شه طبيب‏
entries.english: When the stranger arrived from the road, the physician brought him into the presence of the king.

entries.farsi: سوى شاهنشاه بردندش به ناز / تا بسوزد بر سر شمع طراز
entries.english: Proudly and delicately they conducted him to the king of kings that he might burn on that candle of Tiraz.

entries.farsi: شاه ديد او را بسى تعظيم كرد / مخزن زر را بدو تسليم كرد
entries.english: The king beheld him, showed great regard, and entrusted to him the treasure-house of gold.

entries.farsi: پس حكيمش گفت كاى سلطان مه / آن كنيزك را بدين خواجه بده‏
entries.english: Then the physician said to him: “O mighty Sultan, give the handmaiden to this lord,

entries.farsi: تا كنيزك در وصالش خوش شود / آب وصلش دفع آن آتش شود
entries.english: In order that the slave girl may be happy in union with him, and that the water of union with him may put out the fire.”

entries.farsi: شه بدو بخشيد آن مه روى را / جفت كرد آن هر دو صحبت جوى را
entries.english: The king bestowed on him that moon-faced one and wedded those twain craving company.

entries.farsi: مدت شش ماه مى‏راندند كام / تا به صحت آمد آن دختر تمام‏
entries.english: During the space of six months they were satisfying their desire, till the girl was wholly restored to health.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن از بهر او شربت بساخت / تا بخورد و پيش دختر مى‏گداخت‏
entries.english: Thereafter he prepared for him a potion, so that when he drank it he began to dwindle away before her.

entries.farsi: چون ز رنجورى جمال او نماند / جان دختر در وبال او نماند
entries.english: When because of sickness his beauty remained not, the soul of the girl remained not in his pestilence.

entries.farsi: چون كه زشت و ناخوش و رخ زرد شد / اندك اندك در دل او سرد شد
entries.english: Since he became ugly and ill-favoured and sallow-cheeked, little by little he became cold in her heart.

entries.farsi: عشقهايى كز پى رنگى بود / عشق نبود عاقبت ننگى بود
entries.english: Those loves which are for the sake of a colour are not love: in the end they are a disgrace.

entries.farsi: كاش كان هم ننگ بودى يك سرى / تا نرفتى بر وى آن بد داورى
entries.english: Would that he too had been disgrace altogether, so that that evil judgment might not have come to pass upon him!

entries.farsi: خون دويد از چشم همچون جوى او / دشمن جان وى آمد روى او
entries.english: Blood ran from his eye like a river; his face became the enemy of his life.

entries.farsi: دشمن طاوس آمد پر او / اى بسى شه را بكشته فر او
entries.english: The peacock’s plumage is its enemy: O many the king who has been slain by his magnificence!

entries.farsi: گفت من آن آهوم كز ناف من / ريخت اين صياد خون صاف من‏
entries.english: He said, “I am the musk deer on account of whose gland that hunter shed my pure blood.

entries.farsi: اى من آن روباه صحرا كز كمين / سر بريدندش براى پوستين
entries.english: Oh, I am the fox of the field whose head they from the covert cut off for the sake of the fur.

entries.farsi: اى من آن پيلى كه زخم پيل بان / ريخت خونم از براى استخوان‏
entries.english: Oh, I am the elephant whose blood was shed by the blow of the mahout for the sake of the bone.

entries.farsi: آن كه كشتستم پى مادون من / مى‏نداند كه نخسبد خون من‏
entries.english: He who has slain me for that which is other than I does not know that my blood does not sleep.

entries.farsi: بر من است امروز و فردا بر وى است / خون چون من كس چنين ضايع كى است
entries.english: To-day it lays on me and to-morrow it lies on him: when does the blood of one such as I am, go to waste like this?

entries.farsi: گر چه ديوار افكند سايه‏ى دراز / باز گردد سوى او آن سايه باز
entries.english: Although the wall casts a long shadow, the shadow turns back again .towards it.

entries.farsi: اين جهان كوه است و فعل ما ندا / سوى ما آيد نداها را صدا
entries.english: This world is the mountain, and our action the shout: the echo of the shouts comes to us.”

entries.farsi: اين بگفت و رفت در دم زير خاك / آن كنيزك شد ز عشق و رنج پاك
entries.english: He said this and at the moment went under the earth. The handmaiden was purged of pain and love,

entries.farsi: ز انكه عشق مردگان پاينده نيست / ز انكه مرده سوى ما آينده نيست‏
entries.english: Because love of the dead is not enduring, because the dead one is never coming to us ;

entries.farsi: عشق زنده در روان و در بصر / هر دمى باشد ز غنچه تازه‏تر
entries.english: Love of the living is every moment fresher than a bud in the spirit and in the sight.

entries.farsi: عشق آن زنده گزين كاو باقى است / كز شراب جان فزايت ساقى است‏
entries.english: Choose the love of that Living One who is everlasting, who gives you to drink of the wine that increases life.

entries.farsi: عشق آن بگزين كه جمله انبيا / يافتند از عشق او كار و كيا
entries.english: Choose the love of Him from whose love all the prophets gained power and glory.

entries.farsi: تو مگو ما را بدان شه بار نيست / با كريمان كارها دشوار نيست‏
entries.english: Do not say, “We have no admission to that King.” Dealings with the generous are not difficult.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه كشتن و زهر دادن مرد زرگر به اشارت الهى بود نه به هواى نفس و تامل فاسد
sections.title_en: Setting forth how the slaying and poisoning of the goldsmith was by Divine suggestion, not by sensual desire and wicked meditation.

entries.farsi: كشتن آن مرد بر دست حكيم / نى پى اوميد بود و نى ز بيم‏
entries.english: The slaying of this man by the hand of the physician was not on account of hope or fear.

entries.farsi: او نكشتش از براى طبع شاه / تا نيامد امر و الهام اله‏
entries.english: He did not slay him to humour the king, until the Divine command and inspiration came.

entries.farsi: آن پسر را كش خضر ببريد حلق / سر آن را درنيابد عام خلق‏
entries.english: As for the boy whose throat was cut by Khadir, the vulgar do not comprehend the mystery thereof.

entries.farsi: آن كه از حق يابد او وحى و جواب / هر چه فرمايد بود عين صواب‏
entries.english: He that receives from God inspiration and answer, whatsoever he may command is the essence of right.

entries.farsi: آن كه جان بخشد اگر بكشد رواست / نايب است و دست او دست خداست‏
entries.english: If one who bestows life should slay, it is allowable: he is the vicegerent, and his hand is the hand of God.

entries.farsi: همچو اسماعيل پيشش سر بنه / شاد و خندان پيش تيغش جان بده‏
entries.english: Like Isma’il, lay your head before him; gladly and laughingly give up your soul before his dagger,

entries.farsi: تا بماند جانت خندان تا ابد / همچو جان پاك احمد با احد
entries.english: In order that your soul may remain laughing until eternity, like the pure soul of Ahmad with the One.

entries.farsi: عاشقان جام فرح آن گه كشند / كه به دست خويش خوبانشان كشند
entries.english: Lovers drain the cup of joy at the moment when the fair ones slay them with their own hand.

entries.farsi: شاه آن خون از پى شهوت نكرد / تو رها كن بد گمانى و نبرد
entries.english: The king did not commit that bloodshed because of lust: cease from thinking evil and disputing.

entries.farsi: تو گمان بردى كه كرد آلودگى / در صفا غش كى هلد پالودگى
entries.english: You thought that he committed a foul crime, in purity how should the sublimation leave alloy?

entries.farsi: بهر آن است اين رياضت وين جفا / تا بر آرد كوره از نقره جفا
entries.english: The purpose of this discipline and this rough treatment is that the furnace may extract the dross from the silver.

entries.farsi: بهر آن است امتحان نيك و بد / تا بجوشد بر سر آرد زر زبد
entries.english: The testing of good and bad is in order that the gold may boil and bring the scum to the top.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى كارش الهام اله / او سگى بودى دراننده نه شاه‏
entries.english: If his act were not the inspiration of God, he would have been a dog that rends, not a king.

entries.farsi: گر خضر در بحر كشتى را شكست / صد درستى در شكست خضر هست‏
entries.english: He was unstained by lust and covetousness and passion: he did well, but good that wore the aspect of evil.

entries.farsi: ‏ پاك بود از شهوت و حرص و هوا / نيك كرد او ليك نيك بد نما
entries.english: If Khadir stove the boat in the sea, in Khadir’s staving there are a hundred rightnesses.

entries.farsi: آن گل سرخ است تو خونش مخوان / مست عقل است او تو مجنونش مخوان
entries.english: The imagination of Moses, notwithstanding his illumination and excellence, was screened from that. Do not fly without wings!

entries.farsi: وهم موسى با همه نور و هنر / شد از آن محجوب، تو بى‏پر مپر
entries.english: That is a red rose; do not call it blood. He is intoxicated with Reason; do not call him a madman.

entries.farsi: گر بدى خون مسلمان كام او / كافرم گر بردمى من نام او‏
entries.english: Had it been his desire to shed the blood of a Moslem. I am an infidel if I would have mentioned his name.

entries.farsi: مى‏بلرزد عرش از مدح شقى / بد گمان گردد ز مدحش متقى‏
entries.english: The highest heaven trembles at praise of the wicked, and by praise of him the devout man is moved to think evil.

entries.farsi: شاه بود و شاه بس آگاه بود / خاص بود و خاصه‏ى اهلل بود
entries.english: He was a king and a very heedful king; he was elect and the elect, of God.

entries.farsi: آن كسى را كش چنين شاهى كشد / سوى بخت و بهترين جاهى كشد
entries.english: One who is slain by a king like this, he leads him to fortune and to the best estate.

entries.farsi: گر نديدى سود او در قهر او / كى شدى آن لطف مطلق قهر جو
entries.english: Unless he had seen advantage to him in doing violence to him, how should that absolute Mercy have sought to do violence?

entries.farsi: بچه مى‏لرزد از آن نيش حجام / مادر مشفق در آن غم شاد كام‏
entries.english: The child trembles at the barber’s scalpel the fond mother is happy in that pain

entries.farsi: نيم جان بستاند و صد جان دهد / آن چه در وهمت نيايد آن دهد‏
entries.english: He takes half a life and gives a hundred lives: he gives that which enters not into your imagination.

entries.farsi: تو قياس از خويش مى‏گيرى و ليك / بود بقالى و وى را طوطيى
entries.english: You are judging from yourself, but you have fallen far, far. Consider well!

sections.title_fa: حكايت بقال و طوطى و روغن ريختن طوطى در دكان
sections.title_en: The story of the greengrocer and the parrot and the parrot’s spilling the oil in the shop.

entries.farsi: خوش نوايى سبز و گويا طوطيى‏ / بر دكان بودى نگهبان دكان
entries.english: There was a greengrocer who had a parrot, a sweet-voiced green talking parrot.

entries.farsi: نكته گفتى با همه سوداگران‏ / در خطاب آدمى ناطق بدى
entries.english: On the bench, it would watch over the shop and talk finely to all the traders.

entries.farsi: در نواى طوطيان حاذق بدى‏ / جست از سوى دكان سويى گريخت
entries.english: In addressing human beings it would speak; it was skilled in the song of parrots.

entries.farsi: شيشه‏هاى روغن گل را بريخت / از سوى خانه بيامد خواجه‏اش
entries.english: It sprang from the bench and flew away; it spilled the bottles of rose-oil.

entries.farsi: بر دكان بنشست فارغ خواجه‏وش‏ / ديد پر روغن دكان و جامه چرب
entries.english: Its master came from the direction of his house and seated himself on the bench at his ease as a merchant does.

entries.farsi: بر سرش زد گشت طوطى كل ز ضرب / روزكى چندى سخن كوتاه كرد
entries.english: He saw the bench was full of oil and his clothes greasy; he smote the parrot on the head: it was made bald by the blow.

entries.farsi: مرد بقال از ندامت آه كرد / ريش بر مى‏كند و مى‏گفت اى دريغ
entries.english: For some few days it refrained from speech; the greengrocer, in repentance, heaved deep sighs,

entries.farsi: كافتاب نعمتم شد زير ميغ‏ / دست من بشكسته بودى آن زمان
entries.english: Tearing his beard and saying, “Alas! The sun of my prosperity has gone under the clouds.

entries.farsi: كه زدم من بر سر آن خوش زبان‏ / هديه‏ها مى‏داد هر درويش را
entries.english: Would that my hand had been broken at that moment! How did I strike on the head of that sweet-tongued one?”

entries.farsi: تا بيابد نطق مرغ خويش را / بعد سه روز و سه شب حيران و زار
entries.english: He was giving presents to every dervish, that he might get back the speech of his bird.

entries.farsi: بر دكان بنشسته بد نوميد وار / مى‏نمود آن مرغ را هر گون شگفت
entries.english: After three days and three nights, he was seated on the bench, distraught and sorrowful, like a man in despair,

entries.farsi: تا كه باشد كاندر آيد او بگفت‏ / جولقيى سر برهنه مى‏گذشت
entries.english: Showing the bird every sort of marvel that maybe it would begin to speak.

entries.farsi: با سر بى‏مو چو پشت طاس و طشت‏ / طوطى اندر گفت آمد در زمان
entries.english: Meanwhile a bald dervish, clad in a coarse woollen frock(jawlaq), passed by, with a head hairless as the outside of bowl and basin.

entries.farsi: بانگ بر درويش زد كه هى فالن / از چه اى كل با كالن آميختى
entries.english: Then the parrot began to talk, screeched at the dervish and said, “Hey, fellow!

entries.farsi: تو مگر از شيشه روغن ريختى / از قياسش خنده آمد خلق را
entries.english: How were you mixed up with the bald, O bald one? Did you, then, spill oil from the bottle?”

entries.farsi: كو چو خود پنداشت صاحب دلق را / كار پاكان را قياس از خود مگير
entries.english: The bystanders laughed at the parrot’s inference, because it deemed the wearer of the frock to be like itself.

entries.farsi: گر چه ماند در نبشتن شير و شير / جمله عالم زين سبب گمراه شد
entries.english: Do not measure the actions of holy men by yourself, though sher (lion) and shir (milk) are similar in writing.

entries.farsi: اوليا را همچو خود پنداشتند / گفته اينك ما بشر ايشان بشر
entries.english: On this account the whole world is gone astray: scarcely any one knows of God’s Abdal.

entries.farsi: كم كسى ز ابدال حق آگاه شد / همسرى با انبيا برداشتند
entries.english: They set up equality with the prophets; they supposed the saints to be like themselves.

entries.farsi: ما و ايشان بسته‏ى خوابيم و خور / اين ندانستند ايشان از عمى
entries.english: “Behold,” they said, “we are men, they are men; both we and they are in bondage to sleep and food.”

entries.farsi: هست فرقى در ميان بى‏منتها / هر دو گون زنبور خوردند از محل
entries.english: In blindness they did not perceive that there is an infinite difference between.

entries.farsi: ليك شد ز ان نيش و زين ديگر عسل‏ / هر دو گون آهو گيا خوردند و آب
entries.english: Both species of bee (zanbur)ate and drank from the place, but from that one came a sting, and from this other honey.

entries.farsi: زين يكى سرگين شد و ز ان مشك ناب‏ / هر دو نى خوردند از يك آب خور
entries.english: Both species of deer ate grass and drank water: from this one came dung, and from that one pure musk.

entries.farsi: اين يكى خالى و آن پر از شكر / صد هزاران اين چنين اشباه بين
entries.english: Both reeds drank from the same water-source, this one is empty and that one sugar.

entries.farsi: فرقشان هفتاد ساله راه بين‏ / اين خورد گردد پليدى زو جدا
entries.english: Consider hundreds of thousands of such likenesses and observe that the distance between the two is a seventy years’ journey.

entries.farsi: آن خورد گردد همه نور خدا / اين خورد زايد همه بخل و حسد
entries.english: This one eats, and filth is discharged from him; that one eats, and becomes entirely the light of God.

entries.farsi: و آن خورد زايد همه نور احد / اين زمين پاك و ان شوره ست و بد
entries.english: This one eats, is born nothing but avarice and envy; that one eats, is born nothing but love of the One.

entries.farsi: اين فرشته‏ى پاك و ان ديو است و دد / هر دو صورت گر بهم ماند رواست
entries.english: This one is good soil and that one brackish and bad; this one is a fair angel and that one a devil and wild beast.

entries.farsi: آب تلخ و آب شيرين را صفاست / جز كه صاحب ذوق كى شناسد بياب
entries.english: If both resemble each other in aspect, it may well be: bitter water and sweet water have clearness.

entries.farsi: او شناسد آب خوش از شوره آب‏ / سحر را با معجزه كرده قياس
entries.english: Who knows except a man possessed of taste? Find: he knows the sweet water from the brine.

entries.farsi: هر دو را بر مكر پندارد اساس / ساحران موسى از استيزه را
entries.english: Comparing magic with miracle, he fancies that both are founded on deceit.

entries.farsi: بر گرفته چون عصاى او عصا / زين عصا تا آن عصا فرقى است ژرف
entries.english: The magicians of Moses, for contention’s sake, lifted up a rod like his,

entries.farsi: زين عمل تا آن عمل راهى شگرف‏ / لعنة اللهَّ اين عمل را در قفا
entries.english: Between this rod and that rod there is a vast difference; from this action to that action is a great way.

entries.farsi: رحمه اللهَّ آن عمل را در وفا / كافران اندر مرى بوزينه طبع
entries.english: This action is followed by the curse of God that action receives in payment the mercy of God.

entries.farsi: آفتى آمد درون سينه طبع‏ / هر چه مردم مى‏كند بوزينه هم
entries.english: The infidels in contending have the nature of an ape: the nature is a canker within the breast.

entries.farsi: آن كند كز مرد بيند دم‏به‏دم‏ / او گمان برده كه من كژدم چو او
entries.english: Whatever a man does, the ape at every moment does the same thing that he sees done by the man.

entries.farsi: فرق را كى داند آن استيزه رو / اين كند از امر و او بهر ستيز
entries.english: He thinks, “I have acted like him”: how should that quarrelsome-looking one know the difference?

entries.farsi: بر سر استيزه رويان خاك ريز / آن منافق با موافق در نماز
entries.english: This one acts by the command, and he for the sake of quarrelling. Pour dust on the heads of those who have quarrelsome faces!

entries.farsi: از پى استيزه آيد نى نياز / در نماز و روزه و حج و زكات
entries.english: That hypocrite joins in ritual prayer with the conformist for quarrelling’s sake, not for supplication.

entries.farsi: با منافق مومنان در برد و مات‏ / مومنان را برد باشد عاقبت
entries.english: In prayer and fasting and pilgrimage and alms-giving the true believers are with the hypocrite in victory and defeat.

entries.farsi: بر منافق مات اندر آخرت‏ / گر چه هر دو بر سر يك بازى‏اند
entries.english: Victory in the end is to the true believers; upon the hypocrite defeat in the state hereafter.

entries.farsi: هر دو با هم مروزى و رازى‏اند / هر يكى سوى مقام خود رود
entries.english: Although both are intent on one game, in relation to each other they are the man of Merv and the man of Rayy.

entries.farsi: هر يكى بر وفق نام خود رود / مومنش خوانند جانش خوش شود
entries.english: Each one goes to his abiding-place; each one fares according to his name.

entries.farsi: نام اين مبغوض از آفات وى است‏ / ميم و واو و ميم و نون تشريف نيست
entries.english: If he be called a true believer, his soul rejoices; and if you say “hypocrite,” he becomes filled with fire.

entries.farsi: ور منافق تيز و پر آتش شود / نام او محبوب از ذات وى است
entries.english: His name is loved on account of its essence; this one’s name is loathed on account of its pestilent qualities.

entries.farsi: لفظ مومن جز پى تعريف نيست‏ / گر منافق خوانى‏اش اين نام دون
entries.english: Mim and waw and mim and nun do not confer honour: the word mumin is only for the sake of denotation.

entries.farsi: همچو كژدم مى‏خلد در اندرون / گرنه اين نام اشتقاق دوزخ است
entries.english: If you call him hypocrite, this vile name is stinging within like a scorpion.

entries.farsi: پس چرا در وى مذاق دوزخ است‏ / زشتى آن نام بد از حرف نيست
entries.english: If this name is not derived from Hell, then why is there the taste of Hell in it?

entries.farsi: تلخى آن آب بحر از ظرف نيست‏ / حرف ظرف آمد در او معنى چو آب
entries.english: The foulness of that ill name is not from the letters; the bitterness of that sea-water is not from the vessel.

entries.farsi: بحر معنى عِنْدَهُ أُمُّ الكتاب‏ / بحر تلخ و بحر شيرين در جهان
entries.english: The letters are the vessel: therein the meaning is like water; the sea of the meaning is—with Him is the Ummu ‘l-Kitab.

entries.farsi: در ميانشان بَرْزَخٌ ال يبغيان‏ / وانگه اين هر دو ز يك اصلى روان
entries.english: In this world the bitter sea and the sweet sea— between them is a barrier which they do not seek to cross.

entries.farsi: بر گذر زين هر دو رو تا اصل آن / زر قلب و زر نيكو در عيار
entries.english: Know that both these flow from one origin. Pass on from them both, go to their origin!

entries.farsi: بى‏محك هرگز ندانى ز اعتبار / هر كه را در جان خدا بنهد محك
entries.english: Without the touchstone you will never know in the assay adulterated gold and fine gold by judgment.

entries.farsi: آن گه آرامد كه بيرونش نهد / در هزاران لقمه يك خاشاك خرد
entries.english: Any one in whose soul God shall put the touchstone, he will distinguish certainty from doubt.

entries.farsi: هر يقين را باز داند او ز شك / در دهان زنده خاشاكى جهد
entries.english: A piece of rubbish jumps into the mouth of a living man, and only when he ejects it is he at ease.

entries.farsi: چون در آمد حس زنده پى ببرد / حس دنيا نردبان اين جهان
entries.english: When, amongst thousands of morsels, one little piece of rubbish entered, the living man’s sense tracked it down.

entries.farsi: حس دينى نردبان آسمان‏ / صحت اين حس بجوييد از طبيب
entries.english: The worldly sense is the ladder to this world; the religious sense is the ladder to Heaven.

entries.farsi: صحت آن حس بخواهيد از حبيب‏ / صحت اين حس ز معمورى تن
entries.english: Seek the well-being of the former sense from the physician; Beg the well-being of the latter sense from the Beloved.

entries.farsi: صحت آن حس ز تخريب بدن / راه جان مر جسم را ويران كند
entries.english: The health of the former arises from the flourishing state of the body; the health of the latter arises from the ruin of the body.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن ويرانى آبادان كند / كرد ويران خانه بهر گنج زر
entries.english: The spiritual way ruins the body and, after having ruined it, restores it to prosperity:

entries.farsi: وز همان گنجش كند معمورتر / آب را ببريد و جو را پاك كرد
entries.english: Ruined the house for the sake of the golden treasure, and with that same treasure builds it better;

entries.farsi: بعد از آن در جو روان كرد آب خورد / پوست را بشكافت و پيكان را كشيد
entries.english: Cut off the water and cleansed the river-bed, then caused drinking-water to flow in the river-bed;

entries.farsi: پوست تازه بعد از آتش بردميد / قلعه ويران كرد و از كافر ستد
entries.english: Cut the skin and drew out the iron point— then fresh skin grew over it;

entries.farsi: بعد از آن بر ساختش صد برج و سد / كار بى‏چون را كه كيفيت نهد
entries.english: Razed the fortress and took it from the infidel, then reared thereon a hundred towers and ramparts.

entries.farsi: اين كه گفتم هم ضرورت مى‏دهد / گه چنين بنمايد و گه ضد اين
entries.english: Who shall describe the action of Him who has no like? This that I have said necessity is affording.

entries.farsi: جز كه حيرانى نباشد كار دين‏ / نى چنان حيران كه پشتش سوى اوست
entries.english: Sometimes it appears like this and sometimes the contrary of this: the work of religion is nothing but bewilderment.

entries.farsi: بل چنين حيران و غرق و مست دوست‏ / آن يكى را روى او شد سوى دوست
entries.english: Not one bewildered in such wise that his back is towards Him; no, but one bewildered like this and drowned and intoxicated with the Beloved.

entries.farsi: و آن يكى را روى او خود روى دوست‏ / روى هر يك مى‏نگر مى‏دار پاس
entries.english: The face of the one is set towards the Beloved; the face of the other is just his own face.

entries.farsi: بو كه گردى تو ز خدمت رو شناس‏ / چون بسى ابليس آدم روى هست
entries.english: Look long on the face of every one, keep watch attentively: it may be that by doing service you will come to know the face.

entries.farsi: پس به هر دستى نشايد داد دست‏ / ز انكه صياد آورد بانگ صفير
entries.english: Since there is many a devil that has the face of Adam, it is not well to give your hand to every hand,

entries.farsi: تا فريبد مرغ را آن مرغ گير / بشنود آن مرغ بانگ جنس خويش
entries.english: Because the fowler produces a whistling sound in order to decoy the bird,

entries.farsi: از هوا آيد بيابد دام و نيش‏ / حرف درويشان بدزدد مرد دون
entries.english: The bird may hear the note of its congener and come down from the air and find trap and knife-point.

entries.farsi: تا بخواند بر سليمى ز ان فسون / كار مردان روشنى و گرمى است
entries.english: The vile man will steal the language of dervishes, that he may thereby chant a spell over one who is simple.

entries.farsi: كار دونان حيله و بى‏شرمى است‏ / شير پشمين از براى كد كنند
entries.english: The work of men is light and heat; the work of vile men is trickery and shamelessness.

entries.farsi: بو مسيلم را لقب احمد كنند / بو مسيلم را لقب كذاب ماند
entries.english: They make a woollen lion for the purpose of begging; they give the title of Ahmad to Bu Musaylim;

entries.farsi: مر محمد را اولو الالباب ماند / آن شراب حق ختامش مشك ناب
entries.english: To Bu Musaylim remained the title of Kadhdhab (Liar), to Mohammed remained Ulu ‘l-albab.

entries.farsi: باده را ختمش بود گند و عذاب‏
entries.english: The wine of God, its seal is pure musk, as for wine, its seal is stench and torment.

sections.title_fa: ‏داستان آن پادشاه جهود كه نصرانيان را مى‏كشت از بهر تعصب
sections.title_en: Story of the self-reliant king who, for bigotry’s sake,

entries.farsi: بود شاهى در جهودان ظلم ساز / دشمن عيسى و نصرانى گداز
entries.english: used to slay the Christians

entries.farsi: عهد عيسى بود و نوبت آن او / جان موسى او و موسى جان او
entries.english: Amongst the Self reliant ones there was a king who wrought oppression, an enemy of Jesus and a destroyer of Christians.

entries.farsi: شاه احول كرد در راه خدا / آن دو دمساز خدايى را جدا
entries.english: It was the epoch of Jesus and the turn was his: he was the soul of Moses, and Moses the soul of him;

entries.farsi: گفت استاد احولى را كاندر آ / رو برون آر از وثاق آن شيشه را
entries.english: The squint-eyed king separated in the way of God those two Divine who were (really) in accord.

entries.farsi: گفت احول ز ان دو شيشه من كدام / پيش تو آرم بكن شرح تمام‏
entries.english: The master said to a squint-eyed, “Come on; go, fetch that bottle out of the room.”

entries.farsi: گفت استاد آن دو شيشه نيست رو / احولى بگذار و افزون بين مشو
entries.english: Said the squint-eyed one: “Which of the two bottles shall I bring to you? Explain fully.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى استا مرا طعنه مزن / گفت استا ز ان دو يك را در شكن‏
entries.english: “There are not two bottles,” replied the master; “go, leave off squinting and do not be seeing more.”

entries.farsi: شيشه يك بود و به چشمش دو نمود / چون شكست او شيشه را ديگر نبود
entries.english: “O master,” said he, “don’t chide me.” Said the master, “Smash one of those two.”
The bottle was one, though in his eyes it seemed two; when he broke the bottle, there was no other.

entries.farsi: چون يكى بشكست هر دو شد ز چشم / مردم احول گردد از ميالن و خشم‏
entries.english: When one was broken, both vanished from sight: a man is made squint-eyed by propensity and anger.

entries.farsi: خشم و شهوت مرد را احول كند / ز استقامت روح را مبدل كند
entries.english: Anger and lust make a man squint-eyed; they change the spirit from rectitude.

entries.farsi: چون غرض آمد هنر پوشيده شد / صد حجاب از دل به سوى ديده شد
entries.english: When self-interest appears, virtue becomes hidden: a hundred veils rise from the heart to the eye.

entries.farsi: چون دهد قاضى به دل رشوت قرار / كى شناسد ظالم از مظلوم زار
entries.english: When the judge lets bribery gain hold of his heart, how should he know the guilty from the wretched victim?

entries.farsi: شاه از حقد جهودانه چنان / گشت احول كالامان يا رب امان‏
entries.english: The king, from Jewish rancour, became so squint-eyed that, “Mercy, O Lord, mercy.”

entries.farsi: صد هزاران مومن مظلوم كشت / كه پناهم دين موسى را و پشت‏
entries.english: He slew hundreds of thousands of wronged believers, saying, “I am the protection and support of the religion of Moses.”

sections.title_fa: آموختن وزير مكر پادشاه را
sections.title_en: How the vizier instructed the king to plot.

entries.farsi: او وزيرى داشت گبر و عشوه‏ده / كاو بر آب از مكر بر بستى گره‏
entries.english: He had a vizier, a miscreant and deceiver, who by reason of guile would tie knots on water.

entries.farsi: گفت ترسايان پناه جان كنند / دين خود را از ملك پنهان كنند
entries.english: “The Christians,” said he, “seek to save their lives; they hide their religion from the king.

entries.farsi: كم كش ايشان را كه كشتن سود نيست / دين ندارد بوى، مشك و عود نيست‏
entries.english: Slay them not, for slaying is useless: religion has no smell; it is not musk and aloes-wood.

entries.farsi: سر پنهان است اندر صد غالف / ظاهرش با تست و باطن بر خالف‏
entries.english: The secret is concealed in a hundred coverings: its outward form is with you, resembling you, the inward is disagreement.”

entries.farsi: شاه گفتش پس بگو تدبير چيست / چاره‏ى آن مكر و ان تزوير چيست‏
entries.english: The king said to him: “ Tell, then, what is the plan? What is the remedy against that deceit and imposture?—

entries.farsi: تا نماند در جهان نصرانيى / نى هويدا دين و نى پنهانيى
entries.english: So that there may not remain a single Christian in the world, neither one whose religion is manifest nor one who is concealed.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى شه گوش و دستم را ببر / بينى‏ام بشكاف و لب در حكم مر
entries.english: “O king,” said he, “cut off my ears and hands rip my nose by bitter decree;

entries.farsi: بعد از آن در زير دار آور مرا / تا بخواهد يك شفاعت‏گر مرا
entries.english: Then bring me under the gallows that an intercessor may plead for me.

entries.farsi: بر منادى گاه كن اين كار تو / بر سر راهى كه باشد چار سو
entries.english: Do this deed in the place for proclamation, on a highway where roads run in four directions.

entries.farsi: آن گهم از خود بران تا شهر دور / تا در اندازم در ايشان شر و شور
entries.english: Then banish me from your presence to a distant land, that I may cast mischief and confusion amongst them.

sections.title_fa: تلبيس وزير با نصارا
sections.title_en: How the vizier brought the Christians into doubt and perplexity.

entries.farsi: خواستم تا دين ز شه پنهان كنم / آن كه دين اوست ظاهر آن كنم‏
entries.english: Then I will say, ‘I am secretly a Christian. O God who knows things hidden, You know me.

entries.farsi: شاه واقف گشت از ايمان من / وز تعصب كرد قصد جان من‏
entries.english: The king was informed of my faith and from bigotry sought to take my life.

entries.farsi: پس بگويم من به سر نصرانى‏ام / اى خداى راز دان مى‏دانى‏ام‏
entries.english: I wished to hide my religion from the king and profess his religion,

entries.farsi: شاه بويى برد از اسرار من / متهم شد پيش شه گفتار من‏
entries.english: The king got a scent of my inmost beliefs, and my words were suspected before the king.

entries.farsi: گفت گفت تو چو در نان سوزن است / از دل من تا دل تو روزن است‏
entries.english: He said, “Your words are like a needle in bread; there is a window between my heart and yours.

entries.farsi: من از آن روزن بديدم حال تو / حال تو ديدم ننوشم قال تو
entries.english: Through that window I have seen your state : I see your state and will not heed your words.”

entries.farsi: بهر عيسى جان سپارم سر دهم / صد هزاران منتش بر خود نهم‏
entries.english: Had not the spirit of Jesus been my aid, he would in Jewish fashion have torn me to pieces.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى جان عيسى چاره‏ام / او جهودانه بكردى پاره‏ام‏
entries.english: For Jesus’ sake I would yield my life and give my head and lay on myself myriads of obligations to him.

entries.farsi: جان دريغم نيست از عيسى و ليك / واقفم بر علم دينش نيك نيك
entries.english: I do not grudge Jesus my life, but full well am I versed in the knowledge of his religion.

entries.farsi: حيف مى‏آمد مرا كان دين پاك / در ميان جاهالن گردد هالك‏
entries.english: Grief was coming over me that that holy religion should perish amongst those who are ignorant.

entries.farsi: شكر ايزد را و عيسى را كه ما / گشته‏ايم آن كيش حق را رهنما
entries.english: Thanks be to God and to Jesus that I have become a guide to the true faith.

entries.farsi: از جهود و از جهودى رسته‏ام / تا به زنارى ميان را بسته‏ام
entries.english: I have escaped from Self reliant ones and Judaism so that I have bound my waist with a girdle.

entries.farsi: دور دور عيسى است اى مردمان / بشنويد اسرار كيش او به جان‏
entries.english: The epoch is the epoch of Jesus. O men, listen with your souls to the mysteries of his religion!’”

entries.farsi: كرد با وى شاه آن كارى كه گفت / خلق حيران مانده ز ان مكر نهفت‏
entries.english: The king did to him that deed which he had proposed: the people remained in amazement at his deed.

entries.farsi: راند او را جانب نصرانيان / كرد در دعوت شروع او بعد از آن
entries.english: He drove him away to the Christians. After that, he began to proselytize.

sections.title_fa: قبول كردن نصارا مكر وزير را
sections.title_en: How the Christians let themselves be duped by the vizier.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران مرد ترسا سوى او / اندك اندك جمع شد در كوى او
entries.english: Myriads of Christian men gathered round him, little by little, in his abode,

entries.farsi: او به ظاهر واعظ احكام بود / ليك در باطن صفير و دام بود
entries.english: He secretly expounded to them the mysteries of Gospel and girdle and prayer.

entries.farsi: او بيان مى‏كرد با ايشان به راز / سر انگليون و زنار و نماز
entries.english: Outwardly he was a preacher of ordinances, but inwardly he was the whistle and snare.

entries.farsi: بهر اين بعضى صحابه از رسول / ملتمس بودند مكر نفس غول‏
entries.english: On this account some Companions begged of the Prophet the deceitfulness of the ghoul-like soul,

entries.farsi: كاو چه آميزد ز اغراض نهان / در عبادتها و در اخالص جان‏
entries.english: Saying, what of hidden selfish interests does it mingle in acts of worship and in pure spiritual devotion?”

entries.farsi: فضل طاعت را نجستندى از او / عيب ظاهر را بجستندى كه كو
entries.english: They were not seeking from him excellence of piety; they were not inquiring where lay the outward defect.

entries.farsi: مو به مو و ذره ذره مكر نفس / مى‏شناسيدند چون گل از كرفس‏
entries.english: Hair by hair, speck by speck, they were recognising the deceitfulness of the fleshly soul as the rose from parsley.

entries.farsi: موشكافان صحابه هم در آن / وعظ ايشان خيره گشتندى به جان‏
entries.english: Even the hair-splitters of the Companions used to become distraught in spirit at the admonition to them.

sections.title_fa: متابعت نصارا وزير را
sections.title_en: How the Christians followed the vizier.

entries.farsi: دل بدو دادند ترسايان تمام / خود چه باشد قوت تقليد عام‏
entries.english: The Christians all gave their hearts to him: what, indeed, is the strength of the conformity of the vulgar !

entries.farsi: در درون سينه مهرش كاشتند / نايب عيساش مى‏پنداشتند
entries.english: They planted love of him within their breasts; they were regarding him as the vicar of Jesus.

entries.farsi: او به سر دجال يك چشم لعين / اى خدا فريادرس نعم المعين
entries.english: He inwardly was the accursed one-eyed Antichrist. O God, answer the cry in—what a good helper art You!

entries.farsi: صد هزاران دام و دانه ست اى خدا / ما چو مرغان حريص بى‏نوا
entries.english: O God, there are myriads of snares and baits, and we are as greedy foodless birds.

entries.farsi: دم‏به‏دم ما بسته‏ى دام نويم / هر يكى گر باز و سيمرغى شويم‏
entries.english: From moment to moment we are caught in a fresh snare, though we become each one, a falcon or a Simurgh.

entries.farsi: مى‏رهانى هر دمى ما را و باز / سوى دامى مى‏رويم اى بى‏نياز
entries.english: Every moment You art delivering us, and again we are going to a snare, O You who art without want!

entries.farsi: ما در اين انبار گندم مى‏كنيم / گندم جمع آمده گم مى‏كنيم‏
entries.english: We are putting corn in this barn, we are losing the corn that has been garnered.

entries.farsi: مى‏نينديشيم آخر ما به هوش / كين خلل در گندم است از مكر موش‏
entries.english: After all, do not we consider with intelligent mind that this damage to the corn arises from the deceitfulness of the mouse?

entries.farsi: موش تا انبار ما حفره زده ست / وز فنش انبار ما ويران شده ست
entries.english: Since the mouse has made a hole in our barn, and our barn has been ravaged by its guile,

entries.farsi: اول اى جان دفع شر موش كن / وانگهان در جمع گندم جوش كن‏
entries.english: O soul, in the first place avert the mischief of the mouse, and then show fervour in garnering the corn.

entries.farsi: بشنو از اخبار آن صدر الصدور / ال صالة تم الا بالحضور
entries.english: Hear of the sayings related from the Chief of the Prophets: “No prayer is complete without ‘presence’.”

entries.farsi: گر نه موشى دزد در انبار ماست / گندم اعمال چل ساله كجاست
entries.english: If there is no thievish mouse in our barn, where is the corn of forty years works?

entries.farsi: بس ستاره‏ى آتش از آهن جهيد / و ان دل سوزيده پذرفت و كشيد
entries.english: Why is the daily sincerity not being stored, bit by bit, in this barn of ours

entries.farsi: ريزه ريزه صدق هر روزه چرا / جمع مى‏نايد در اين انبار ما
entries.english: Many a star of fire shot forth from the iron, and that burning heart received (it) and drew in;

entries.farsi: ليك در ظلمت يكى دزدى نهان / مى‏نهد انگشت بر استارگان‏
entries.english: But in the darkness a hidden thief is laying his finger upon the stars,

entries.farsi: مى‏كشد استارگان را يك به يك / تا كه نفروزد چراغى از فلك‏
entries.english: Extinguishing the stars one by one, that no lamp may shine from the sky.

entries.farsi: گر هزاران دام باشد در قدم / چون تو با مايى نباشد هيچ غم‏
entries.english: Though there are thousands of snares at our feet, when You are with us there is not any trouble.

entries.farsi: هر شبى از دام تن ارواح را / مى‏رهانى مى‏كنى الواح را
entries.english: Every night You free the spirits from the body’s snare, and erase the tablets .

entries.farsi: مى‏رهند ارواح هر شب زين قفس / فارغان، نه حاكم و محكوم كس‏
entries.english: The spirits are set free every night from this cage, done with ordinance and talk and tale.

entries.farsi: شب ز زندان بى‏خبر زندانيان / شب ز دولت بى‏خبر سلطانيان‏
entries.english: At night prisoners are unconscious of their prison, at night governors are unconscious of their power.

entries.farsi: نه غم و انديشه‏ى سود و زيان / نه خيال اين فالن و آن فالن‏
entries.english: There is no sorrow, no thought of gain or loss, no fancy of this person or that person.

entries.farsi: حال عارف اين بود بى‏خواب هم / گفت ايزد هُمْ رُقُودٌ زين مرم‏
entries.english: This is the state of the ‘arif, even without sleep: God said, deem while they slept. Shy not at this’.

entries.farsi: خفته از احوال دنيا روز و شب / چون قلم در پنجه‏ى تقليب رب‏
entries.english: He is asleep, day and night, to the affairs of the world, like a pen in the hand of the Lord’s control.

entries.farsi: آن كه او پنجه نبيند در رقم / فعل پندارد به جنبش از قلم‏
entries.english: One who sees not the hand in the writing thinks the act from the pen by means of movement.

entries.farsi: شمه‏اى زين حال عارف وانمود / خلق را هم خواب حسى در ربود
entries.english: He has shown forth some part of this state of the ‘arif, the vulgar too are carried off by sleep of the senses.

entries.farsi: رفته در صحراى بى‏چون جانشان / روحشان آسوده و ابدانشان‏
entries.english: Their souls are gone into the desert that is without description: their spirits and bodies are at rest;

entries.farsi: وز صفيرى باز دام اندر كشى / جمله را در داد و در داور كشى‏
entries.english: And with a whistle you lead them back to the snare, lead them all to justice and to the judge.

entries.farsi: فالِقُ الإِْصْ باحِ اسرافيل‏وار / جمله را در صورت آرد ز ان ديار
entries.english: Like Israfil, He who causes the dawn to break brings them all from those lands into form.

entries.farsi: روحهاى منبسط را تن كند / هر تنى را باز آبستن كند
entries.english: He embodies the spirits divested; He makes each body pregnant again.

entries.farsi: اسب جانها را كند عارى ز زين / سر النوم اخ الموت است اين‏
entries.english: He makes the steed of the souls bare of saddle: this is the inner meaning of “Sleep is the brother of Death”;

entries.farsi: ليك بهر آن كه روز آيند باز / بر نهد بر پايشان بند دراز
entries.english: But in order that they may return in the daytime, He puts a long tether on its leg,

entries.farsi: تا كه روزش واكشد ز ان مرغزار / وز چراگاه آردش در زير بار
entries.english: So that in the daytime He may lead it back from that meadow and bring it from the pasture under the load.

entries.farsi: تا از اين طوفان بيدارى و هوش / وارهيدى اين ضمير چشم و گوش
entries.english: Would that He had guarded this spirit as the Men of the Cave or as the Ark of Noah,

entries.farsi: كاش چون اصحاب كهف اين روح را / حفظ كردى يا چو كشتى نوح را
entries.english: That this mind and eye and ear might be delivered from the Flood of wakefulness and consciousness!

entries.farsi: اى بسى اصحاب كهف اندر جهان / پهلوى تو پيش تو هست اين زمان
entries.english: Oh, in the world there is many a Man of the Cave beside you, before you, at this time:

entries.farsi: غار با او يار با او در سرود / مهر بر چشم است و بر گوشت چه سود
entries.english: The Cave is with him, the Friend is in converse with him; but your eyes and ears are sealed, what does it avail?

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى ديدن خليفه ليلى را
sections.title_en: Story of the Caliph’s seeing Layla.

entries.farsi: گفت ليلى را خليفه كان توى / كز تو مجنون شد پريشان و غوى‏
entries.english: The Caliph said to Layla: “ Are you she by whom Majnun was distracted and led astray?

entries.farsi: از دگر خوبان تو افزون نيستى / گفت خامش چون تو مجنون نيستى‏
entries.english: You are not superior to other fair ones.” “Be silent,” she replied, “since you art not Majnun.”

entries.farsi: هر كه بيدار است او در خواب‏تر / هست بيداريش از خوابش بتر
entries.english: Whosoever is awake is the more asleep; his wakefulness is worse than his sleep.

entries.farsi: چون به حق بيدار نبود جان ما / هست بيدارى چو در بندان ما
entries.english: When our soul is not awake to God, wakefulness is like closing our doors.

entries.farsi: جان همه روز از لگدكوب خيال / وز زيان و سود وز خوف زوال‏
entries.english: All day long, from the buffets of imagination and from loss and gain and from fear of decline,

entries.farsi: نى صفا مى‏ماندش نى لطف و فر / نى به سوى آسمان راه سفر
entries.english: There remains to it neither joy nor grace and glory nor way of journeying to Heaven.

entries.farsi: خفته آن باشد كه او از هر خيال / دارد اوميد و كند با او مقال‏
entries.english: The one asleep is he who has hope of every vain fancy and holds parley with it.

entries.farsi: ديو را چون حور بيند او به خواب / پس ز شهوت ريزد او با ديو آب‏
entries.english: Diabolum per somnum videt tanquam virginem caelestem, deinde propter libidinem effundit cum diabolo aquam (seminis).

entries.farsi: چون كه تخم نسل را در شوره ريخت / او به خويش آمد خيال از وى گريخت‏
entries.english: Postquam semen generationis in terram salsuginosam infudit, ipse ad se rediit, fugit ab eo illa imago.
