entries.farsi: ضعف سر بيند از آن و تن پليد / آه از آن نقش پديد ناپديد
entries.english: Hine percipit languorem capitis et (videt) corpus polluturn. Proh dolor ob illud simulacrum visum (sed revera) non visum!

entries.farsi: مرغ بر بالا و زير آن سايه‏اش / مى‏دود بر خاك پران مرغ‏وش‏
entries.english: The bird is flying on high, and its shadow is speeding on the earth, flying like a bird:

entries.farsi: ابلهى صياد آن سايه شود / مى‏دود چندان كه بى‏مايه شود
entries.english: Some fool begins to chase the shadow, running so far that he becomes powerless,

entries.farsi: بى‏خبر كان عكس آن مرغ هواست / بى‏خبر كه اصل آن سايه كجاست‏
entries.english: Not knowing that it is the reflection of that bird in the air, not knowing where is the origin of the shadow.

entries.farsi: تير اندازد به سوى سايه او / تركشش خالى شود از جستجو
entries.english: He shoots arrows at the shadow; his quiver is emptied in seeking:

entries.farsi: تركش عمرش تهى شد عمر رفت / از دويدن در شكار سايه تفت‏
entries.english: The quiver of his life became empty: his life passed in running hotly in chase of the shadow.

entries.farsi: سايه‏ى يزدان بود بنده‏ى خدا / مرده او زين عالم و زنده‏ى خدا
entries.english: When the shadow of God is his nurse, it delivers him from phantom and shadow.

entries.farsi: سايه‏ى يزدان چو باشد دايه‏اش / وارهاند از خيال و سايه‏اش‏
entries.english: The shadow of God is that servant of God who is dead to this world and living through God.

entries.farsi: دامن او گير زودتر بى‏گمان / تا رهى در دامن آخر زمان‏
entries.english: Lay hold of his skirt most quickly without misgiving that you may be saved in the skirt of the last days.

entries.farsi: كَيْفَ مَدَّ الظِّ لَّ نقش اولياست / كاو دليل نور خورشيد خداست‏
entries.english: How He extended the shadow is the form of the saints, which guides to the light of the Divine Sun.

entries.farsi: اندر اين وادى مرو بى‏اين دليل / ال أُحِبُّ الآْ فِلِينَ گو چون خليل‏
entries.english: Do not go in this valley without this guide; say, like Khalil, “I love not them that set.”

entries.farsi: رو ز سايه آفتابى را بياب / دامن شه شمس تبريزى بتاب‏
entries.english: Go, from the shadow gain a sun: pluck the skirt of the king, Shams-i Tabrizi!

entries.farsi: ره ندانى جانب اين سور و عرس / از ضياء الحق حسام الدين بپرس
entries.english: If you do not know the way to this feast and bridal, ask of Ziya u’l Haqq Husamu’ddin.

entries.farsi: اى خنك آن كش حسد همراه نيست / عقبه‏اى زين صعب‏تر در راه نيست‏
entries.english: And if on the way envy seize you by the throat, it belongs to Iblis to go beyond bounds in envy;

entries.farsi: كاو ز آدم ننگ دارد از حسد / با سعادت جنگ دارد از حسد
entries.english: For he because of envy has disdain for Adam, and because of envy is at war with felicity.

entries.farsi: ور حسد گيرد ترا در ره گلو / در حسد ابليس را باشد غلو
entries.english: In the Way there is no harder pass than this. Oh, fortunate he who is not companioned by envy!

entries.farsi: اين جسد خانه‏ى حسد آمد بدان / از حسد آلوده باشد خاندان‏
entries.english: This body, you must know, is the house of envy, for the household are tainted with envy.

entries.farsi: گر جسد خانه‏ى حسد باشد و ليك / آن جسد را پاك كرد اللهَّ نيك‏
entries.english: If the body is the house of envy, yet God made that body very pure.

entries.farsi: طَ هِّرا بَيْتِيَ بيان پاكى است / گنج نور است ار طلسمش خاكى است‏
entries.english: Cleanse My house, ye two, is the explanation of purity: the purified heart is a treasure of light, though its talisman is of earth.

entries.farsi: چون كنى بر بى‏جسد مكر و حسد / ز آن حسد دل را سياهيها رسد
entries.english: When you practice deceit and envy against one who is without envy, from that envy black stains arise in your heart.

entries.farsi: خاك شو مردان حق را زير پا / خاك بر سر كن حسد را همچو ما
entries.english: Become dust under the feet of the men of God ; throw dust on the head of envy, even as we do.

sections.title_fa: بيان حسد وزير
sections.title_en: Explanation of the envy of the vizier

entries.farsi: آن وزيرك از حسد بودش نژاد / تا به باطل گوش و بينى باد داد
entries.english: That petty vizier had his origin from envy, so that for vanity he gave to the wind his ears and nose,

entries.farsi: بر اميد آن كه از نيش حسد / زهر او در جان مسكينان رسد
entries.english: In the hope that by the sting of envy his venom might enter the souls of the poor.

entries.farsi: هر كسى كاو از حسد بينى كند / خويشتن بى‏گوش و بى‏بينى كند
entries.english: Any one who from envy mutilates his nose makes himself without ear and without nose.

entries.farsi: بينى آن باشد كه او بويى برد / بوى او را جانب كويى برد
entries.english: The nose is that which catches a scent, and which the scent leads towards an abode.

entries.farsi: هر كه بويش نيست بى‏بينى بود / بوى آن بوى است كان دينى بود
entries.english: Whoever has no scent is without a nose; the scent is that scent which is religious.

entries.farsi: چون كه بويى برد و شكر آن نكرد / كفر نعمت آمد و بينيش خورد
entries.english: When he has caught a scent and given no thanks for it, ingratitude comes and devours his nose.

entries.farsi: شكر كن مر شاكران را بنده باش / پيش ايشان مرده شو پاينده باش‏
entries.english: Give thanks and be a slave to those who give thanks: be in their presence dead, be steadfast.

entries.farsi: ناصح دين گشته آن كافر وزير / كرده او از مكر در لوزينه سير
entries.english: Do not, like the vizier, make brigandage your stock-in-trade; do not turn the people away from the ritual prayer.

entries.farsi: چون وزير از ره زنى مايه مساز / خلق را تو بر مياور از نماز
entries.english: The miscreant vizier had become a true religious counsellor, he had craftily put garlic in the almond cake.

sections.title_fa: فهم كردن حاذقان نصارا مكر وزير را
sections.title_en: How the sagacious among the Christians perceived the guile of the vizier.

entries.farsi: هر كه صاحب ذوق بود از گفت او / لذتى مى‏ديد و تلخى جفت او
entries.english: Whoever was possessed of discernment was feeling a sweet savour in his words and, joined with, bitterness.

entries.farsi: نكته‏ها مى‏گفت او آميخته / در جالب قند زهرى ريخته
entries.english: He was saying fine things mixed with foul: he had poured some poison into the sugared julep.

entries.farsi: ظاهرش مى‏گفت در ره چيست شو / وز اثر مى‏گفت جان را سست شو
entries.english: The outward sense of it was saying, “Be diligent in the Way,” but in effect it was saying to the soul, “Be slack.”

entries.farsi: ظاهر نقره گر اسپيد است و نو / دست و جامه مى سيه گردد ازو
entries.english: If the surface of silver is white and new, the hands and dress are blackened by it.

entries.farsi: آتش ار چه سرخ روى است از شرر / تو ز فعل او سيه كارى نگر
entries.english: Although fire is red-faced with sparks, look at the black behaviour in its action.

entries.farsi: برق اگر نورى نمايد در نظر / ليك هست از خاصيت دزد بصر
entries.english: If the lightning appears luminous to the eye, from its distinctive property it is the robber of sight.

entries.farsi: هر كه جز آگاه و صاحب ذوق بود / گفت او در گردن او طوق بود
entries.english: Any who was not wary and possessed of discernment, the words of him were a collar on his neck.

entries.farsi: دت شش سال در هجران شاه / شد وزير اتباع عيسى را پناه‏
entries.english: During six years, in separation from the king, the vizier became a refuge for the followers of Jesus.

entries.farsi: دين و دل را كل بدو بسپرد خلق / پيش امر و حكم او مى‏مرد خلق‏
entries.english: To him the people wholly surrendered their religion and their hearts: at his command and decree they were ready to die.

sections.title_fa: پيغام شاه پنهان با وزير
sections.title_en: How the king sent messages in secret to the vizier.

entries.farsi: در ميان شاه و او پيغامها / شاه را پنهان بدو آرامها
entries.english: Messages between the king and him: the king had words of comfort from him in secret.

entries.farsi: پيش او بنوشت شه كاى مقبلم / وقت آمد زود فارغ كن دلم‏
entries.english: The king wrote to him, saying, “ O my fortunate one, the time is come: quickly set my mind at ease.”

entries.farsi: گفت اينك اندر آن كارم شها / كافكنم در دين عيسى فتنه‏ها
entries.english: He replied: “ Behold, O king, I am preparing to cast disorders into the religion of Jesus.”

sections.title_fa: بيان دوازده سبط از نصارا
sections.title_en: Explanation of the twelve tribes of the Christians

entries.farsi: قوم عيسى را بد اندر دار و گير / حاكمانشان ده امير و دو امير
entries.english: The people of Jesus had twelve amirs as rulers in authority over them.

entries.farsi: هر فريقى مر اميرى را تبع / بنده گشته مير خود را از طمع‏
entries.english: Each party followed one amir and had become devoted to its own amir from desire.

entries.farsi: اين ده و اين دو امير و قومشان / گشته بند آن وزير بدنشان
entries.english: These twelve amirs and their followers became the slaves of that vizier of evil sign.

entries.farsi: اعتماد جمله بر گفتار او / اقتداى جمله بر رفتار او
entries.english: They all put trust in his words; they all took his procedure as a pattern.

entries.farsi: پيش او در وقت و ساعت هر امير / جان بدادى گر بدو گفتى بمير
entries.english: Each amir would have given up his life in his presence at the time and hour, if he had bidden him die.

sections.title_fa: تخليط وزير در احكام انجيل
sections.title_en: How the vizier confused the ordinances of the Gospel.

entries.farsi: ساخت طومارى به نام هر يكى / نقش هر طومار ديگر مسلكى‏
entries.english: He prepared a scroll in the name of each one, the form of each scroll a different tenor,

entries.farsi: حكم‏هاى هر يكى نوعى دگر / اين خالف آن ز پايان تا به سر
entries.english: The ordinances of each a diverse kind, this contradicting that from the end to the beginning

entries.farsi: در يكى راه رياضت را و جوع / ركن توبه كرده و شرط رجوع‏
entries.english: In one he made the path of asceticism and hunger to be the basis of repentance and the condition for conversion.

entries.farsi: در يكى گفته رياضت سود نيست / اندر اين ره مخلصى جز جود نيست‏
entries.english: In one he said: “Asceticism profits nothing: in this Way there is no place of deliverance but generosity.”

entries.farsi: در يكى گفته كه جوع و جود تو / شرك باشد از تو با معبود تو
entries.english: In one he said: “Your hunger and generosity imply association on your part with the object of your worship.

entries.farsi: جز توكل جز كه تسليم تمام / در غم و راحت همه مكر است و دام‏
entries.english: Excepting trust and complete resignation in sorrow and joy, all is a deceit and snare.”

entries.farsi: در يكى گفته كه واجب خدمت است / ور نه انديشه‏ى توكل تهمت است
entries.english: In one he said: “It is incumbent to serve; else the thought of putting trust is suspicion.”

entries.farsi: در يكى گفته كه امر و نهيهاست / بهر كردن نيست شرح عجز ماست‏
entries.english: In one he said: “There are commands and prohibitions, are not for practice: they are to show our weakness,

entries.farsi: تا كه عجز خود ببينيم اندر آن / قدرت حق را بدانيم آن زمان‏
entries.english: So that we may behold our weakness therein and at that time recognise the power of God.”

entries.farsi: در يكى گفته كه عجز خود مبين / كفر نعمت كردن است آن عجز هين‏
entries.english: In one he said: “Do not regard your weakness: that weakness is an act of ingratitude. Beware!

entries.farsi: قدرت خود بين كه اين قدرت از اوست / قدرت تو نعمت او دان كه هوست‏
entries.english: Regard your power, for this power is from Him: know that your power is the gift of Him who is Hu.”

entries.farsi: از نظر چون بگذرى و از خيال / كشته باشى نيم شب شمع وصال‏
entries.english: In one he said: “Leave both these behind: whatsoever is contained in sight is an idol,”

entries.farsi: در يكى گفته كز اين دو بر گذر / بت بود هر چه بگنجد در نظر
entries.english: In one he said: “Do not put out this candle, for this sight is as a candle to concentration.

entries.farsi: در يكى گفته مكش اين شمع را / كين نظر چون شمع آمد جمع را
entries.english: When you relinquish sight and imagination, you will have put out the candle of union at midnight.”

entries.farsi: در يكى گفته بكش باكى مدار / تا عوض بينى نظر را صد هزار
entries.english: In one he said: “Put it out—have no fear— that you may see myriads of sights in exchange;

entries.farsi: كه ز كشتن شمع جان افزون شود / ليلى‏ات از صبر تو مجنون شود
entries.english: For by putting it out the candle of the spirit is increased: by your self-denial your Layla becomes your Majnun.

entries.farsi: ترك دنيا هر كه كرد از زهد خويش / بيش آيد پيش او دنيا و پيش‏
entries.english: If any one abandons the world by his own renunciation, the world comes to him more and more.”

entries.farsi: در يكى گفته كه آن چه‏ت داد حق / بر تو شيرين كرد در ايجاد حق‏
entries.english: In one he said: “That which God has given you He made sweet to you in bringing it into existence.

entries.farsi: بر تو آسان كرد و خوش آن را بگير / خويشتن را در ميفگن در زحير
entries.english: He made it easy to you, and do you take it gladly: do not throw yourself into anguish.”

entries.farsi: در يكى گفته كه بگذار آن خود / كان قبول طبع تو ردست و بد
entries.english: In one he said: “Let go all that belongs to self, for it is wrong and bad to comply with your nature.”

entries.farsi: راههاى مختلف آسان شده ست / هر يكى را ملتى چون جان شده ست‏
entries.english: Different roads have become easy: every one’s religion has become as as life.

entries.farsi: گر ميسر كردن حق ره بدى / هر جهود و گبر از او آگه بدى‏
entries.english: If God’s making easy were the road, every Jew and Zoroastrian would have knowledge of Him.

entries.farsi: در يكى گفته ميسر آن بود / كه حيات دل غذاى جان بود
entries.english: In one he said: “That is made easy that spiritual food should be the life of the heart.”

entries.farsi: هر چه ذوق طبع باشد چون گذشت / بر نيارد همچو شوره ريع و كشت‏
entries.english: When the enjoyments of the nature are past, like brackish soil they raise no produce and crop.

entries.farsi: جز پشيمانى نباشد ريع او / جز خسارت پيش نارد بيع او
entries.english: The produce thereof is nothing but penitence; their sale yields only loss, nothing more.

entries.farsi: آن ميسر نبود اندر عاقبت / نام او باشد معسر عاقبت‏
entries.english: That is not “easy” in the end; its name ultimately is “hard.”

entries.farsi: تو معسر از ميسر باز دان / عاقبت بنگر جمال اين و آن‏
entries.english: Distinguish the hard from the easy: consider the goodliness of this and that in the end.

entries.farsi: در يكى گفته كه استادى طلب / عاقبت بينى نيابى در حسب‏
entries.english: In one he said: “Seek a master: you will not find foresight as to the end among the qualities derived from ancestors.”

entries.farsi: عاقبت ديدند هر گون ملتى / الجرم گشتند اسير زلتى‏
entries.english: Every sort of religious sect foresaw the end: of necessity they fell captive to error.

entries.farsi: عاقبت ديدن نباشد دست‏باف / ور نه كى بودى ز دينها اختالف‏
entries.english: To foresee the end is not a hand-loom; otherwise, how would there have been difference in religions?

entries.farsi: در يكى گفته كه استا هم تويى / ز انكه استا را شناسا هم تويى‏
entries.english: In one he said: “You are the master, because you know the master.

entries.farsi: در يكى گفته كه اين جمله يكى است / هر كه او دو بيند احول مردكى است‏
entries.english: Be a man and be not subject to men. Go, take your own head, and be not one whose head is turning.”

entries.farsi: مرد باش و سخره‏ى مردان مشو / رو سر خود گير و سر گردان مشو
entries.english: In one he said: “ All this is one: whoever sees two is a squint-eyed manikin.”

entries.farsi: در يكى گفته كه صد يك چون بود / اين كى انديشد مگر مجنون بود
entries.english: In one he said: “How should a hundred be one? He who thinks this is surely mad.”

entries.farsi: هر يكى قولى است ضد همدگر / چون يكى باشد يكى زهر و شكر
entries.english: The doctrines, every one, are contrary to each other: how should they be one? Is poison and sugar one?

entries.farsi: تا ز زهر و از شكر در نگذرى / كى تو از گلزار وحدت بر برى‏
entries.english: Until you pass beyond poison and sugar, how will you catch a scent of unity and oneness?

entries.farsi: اين نمط وين نوع ده طومار و دو / بر نوشت آن دين عيسى را عدو‏
entries.english: Twelve books of this style and fashion were drawn up in writing by that enemy to the religion of Jesus.

sections.title_fa: ‏بيان آن كه اين اختالفات در صورت روش است نه در حقيقت راه
sections.title_en: Showing how this difference lies in the form of the doctrine, not in the real nature of the Way.

entries.farsi: او ز يك رنگى عيسى بو نداشت / جامه‏ى صد رنگ از آن خم صفا
entries.english: He had no scent of the unicolority of Jesus, nor had he a disposition from the tincture of the dyeing-vat of Jesus.

entries.farsi: ساده و يك رنگ گشتى چون صبا / نيست يك رنگى كز او خيزد مالل
entries.english: From that pure vat a garment of a hundred colours would become as simple and one-coloured as light.

entries.farsi: بل مثال ماهى و آب زالل‏ / گر چه در خشكى هزاران رنگهاست
entries.english: Is not the unicolority from which weariness ensues; no, it is like fish and clear water:

entries.farsi: ماهيان را با يبوست جنگهاست‏ / كيست ماهى چيست دريا در مثل
entries.english: Although there are thousands of colours on dry land, fish are at war with dryness.

entries.farsi: تا بدان ماند ملك عز و جل‏ / صد هزاران بحر و ماهى در وجود
entries.english: Who is the fish and what is the sea in simile, that the King Almighty and Glorious should resemble them?

entries.farsi: سجده آرد پيش آن اكرام و جود / چند باران عطا باران شده
entries.english: In existence myriads of seas and fishes prostrate themselves in adoration before that Munificence and Bounty.

entries.farsi: تا بدان آن بحر در افشان شده‏ / چند خورشيد كرم افروخته
entries.english: How many a rain of largesse has rained, so that the sea was made thereby to scatter pearls!

entries.farsi: تا كه ابر و بحر جود آموخته‏ / پرتو دانش زده بر آب و طين
entries.english: How many a sun of generosity has shone, so that cloud and sea learned to be bountiful!

entries.farsi: تا شده دانه پذيرنده‏ى زمين‏ / خاك امين و هر چه در وى كاشتى
entries.english: The sunbeams of Wisdom struck on soil and clay, so that the earth became receptive of the seed.

entries.farsi: بى‏خيانت جنس آن برداشتى‏ / اين امانت ز آن امانت يافته ست
entries.english: The soil is faithful to its trust, and whatever you have sown in it, you carry away the kind thereof without fraud.

entries.farsi: خاك سرها را نكرده آشكار / آن جوادى كه جمادى را بداد
entries.english: It has derived this faithfulness from that faithfulness, inasmuch as the sun of Justice has shone upon it.

entries.farsi: كافتاب عدل بر وى تافته ست‏ / تا نشان حق نيارد نو بهار
entries.english: Until springtime brings the token of God, the soil does not reveal its secrets.

entries.farsi: اين خبرها وين امانت وين سداد / مر جمادى را كند فضلش خبير
entries.english: The Bounteous One who gave to an inanimate thing this information, faithfulness and righteousness,

entries.farsi: با كه گويم در جهان يك گوش نيست‏ / هر كجا گوشى بد از وى چشم گشت
entries.english: His grace makes an inanimate thing informed; His wrath makes blind the men of understanding.

entries.farsi: عاقالن را كرده قهر او ضرير / جان و دل را طاقت آن جوش نيست
entries.english: Soul and heart cannot endure that ferment: to whom shall I speak? There is not in the world a single ear.

entries.farsi: هر كجا سنگى بد از وى يشم گشت‏ / كيميا ساز است چه بود كيميا
entries.english: Wherever there was an ear, through Him it became an eye; wherever there was a stone, through Him it became a jasper.

entries.farsi: معجزه بخش است چه بود سيميا / اين ثنا گفتن ز من ترك ثناست
entries.english: He is an alchemist-what is alchemy? He is a giver of miracles--what is magic?

entries.farsi: كين دليل هستى و هستى خطاست‏ / پيش هست او ببايد نيست بود
entries.english: This uttering of praise is the omission of praise on my part, for this is a proof of being, and being is a sin.

entries.farsi: چيست هستى پيش او كور و كبود / گر نبودى كور از او بگداختى
entries.english: It behoves to be not-being in the presence of His Being: in His presence what is being? Black and blue.

entries.farsi: كى فسردى همچو يخ اين ناحيت‏
entries.english: Were it not blind it would have been melted by Him: it would have known the heat of sun;

entries.farsi: گرمى خورشيد را بشناختى / ور نبودى او كبود از تعزيت
entries.english: And were it not blue from mourning, how would this region have frozen like ice?

sections.title_fa: بيان خسارت وزير در اين مكر
sections.title_en: Setting forth how the vizier incurred damnation from this plot.

entries.farsi: همچو شه نادان و غافل بد وزير / پنجه مى‏زد با قديم ناگزير
entries.english: The vizier was ignorant and heedless, like the king: he was wrestling with the eternal and inevitable,

entries.farsi: با چنان قادر خدايى كز عدم / صد چو عالم هست گرداند به دم
entries.english: With a God so mighty that in a moment He causes a hundred worlds like ours to come into existence from non-existence:

entries.farsi: صد چو عالم در نظر پيدا كند / چون كه چشمت را به خود بينا كند
entries.english: A hundred worlds like ours He displays to the sight, when He makes your eye seeing by Himself.

entries.farsi: اين جهان خود حبس جانهاى شماست / هين رويد آن سو كه صحراى شماست‏
entries.english: If the world appears to you vast and bottomless, know that to Omnipotence it is not an atom.

entries.farsi: گر جهان پيشت بزرگ و بى‏بنى است / پيش قدرت ذره اى مى‏دان كه نيست‏
entries.english: This world, indeed, is the prison of your souls: oh, go in yonder direction, for there lays your open country.

entries.farsi: اين جهان محدود و آن خود بى‏حد است / نقش و صورت پيش آن معنى سد است‏
entries.english: This world is finite, and truly that is infinite: image and form are a barrier to that Reality.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران نيزه‏ى فرعون را / در شكست از موسيى با يك عصا
entries.english: The myriads of Pharaoh’s lances were shattered by Moses with a single staff.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران طب جالينوس بود / پيش عيسى و دمش افسوس بود
entries.english: Myriads were the therapeutic arts of Galen: before Jesus and his breath they were a laughing-stock.

entries.farsi: با چنين غالب خداوندى كسى / چون نميرد گر نباشد او خسى‏
entries.english: Myriads were the books of poems: at the word of an illiterate they were shame.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران دفتر اشعار بود / پيش حرف اميى آن عار بود
entries.english: With such an all-conquering Lord, how should any one not die, unless he be a vile wretch?

entries.farsi: بس دل چون كوه را انگيخت او / مرغ زيرك با دو پا آويخت او
entries.english: Many a mind as a mountain did He uproot; the cunning bird He hung up by its two feet.

entries.farsi: فهم و خاطر تيز كردن نيست راه / جز شكسته مى‏نگيرد فضل شاه‏
entries.english: To sharpen the intelligence and wits is not the way: none but the broken wins the favour of the King. .

entries.farsi: اى بسا گنج آگنان كنج كاو / كان خيال انديش را شد ريش گاو
entries.english: Oh, many the treasure hunters, digging hole, who became an ox’s beard to that vain schemer!

entries.farsi: گاو كه بود تا تو ريش او شوى / خاك چه بود تا حشيش او شوى‏
entries.english: Who is the ox that you should become his beard? What is earth that you should become its stubble?

entries.farsi: چون زنى از كار بد شد روى زرد / مسخ كرد او را خدا و زهره كرد
entries.english: When a woman became pale-faced of wickedness, God metamorphosed her and made her Zuhra.

entries.farsi: عورتى را زهره كردن مسخ بود / خاك و گل گشتن نه مسخ است اى عنود
entries.english: To make a woman Zuhra was metamorphosis: what is it to become earth and clay, O contumacious one?

entries.farsi: خويشتن را مسخ كردى زين سفول / ز آن وجودى كه بد آن رشك عقول‏
entries.english: Your spirit was bearing you towards the highest sphere: you went towards the water and the clay amongst the lowest.

entries.farsi: روح مى‏بردت سوى چرخ برين / سوى آب و گل شدى در اسفلين‏
entries.english: By this fall you metamorphosed yourself from that existence which was the envy of the intelligences.

entries.farsi: پس ببين كين مسخ كردن چون بود / پيش آن مسخ اين به غايت دون بود
entries.english: Consider, then, how is this metamorphosis: compared with that metamorphosis this is exceedingly vile.

entries.farsi: اسب همت سوى اختر تاختى / آدم مسجود را نشناختى‏
entries.english: You urged the steed of ambition towards the stars: you did not acknowledge Adam who was worshipped.

entries.farsi: آخر آدم زاده‏اى اى ناخلف / چند پندارى تو پستى را شرف‏
entries.english: After all, you are a son of Adam. O degenerate! How long will you regard lowness as nobility?

entries.farsi: چند گويى من بگيرم عالمى / اين جهان را پر كنم از خود همى‏
entries.english: How long will you say, “I will conquer a whole world, I will make this world full of myself ”?

entries.farsi: گر جهان پر برف گردد سربه‏سر / تاب خور بگدازدش با يك نظر
entries.english: If the world should he filled with snow from end to end, the glow of the sun would melt it with a single look.

entries.farsi: وزر او و صد وزير و صد هزار / نيست گرداند خدا از يك شرار
entries.english: God by a single spark makes naught his burden and of a hundred Viziers and a hundred thousand.

entries.farsi: عين آن تخييل را حكمت كند / عين آن زهر آب را شربت كند
entries.english: He makes the essence of that imagination to be wisdom; He makes the essence of that poisoned water to be a drink.

entries.farsi: آن گمان انگيز را سازد يقين / مهرها روياند از اسباب كين‏
entries.english: ‘That which raises doubt He turns into certainty; He makes lovingkindnesses grow from the causes of hatred.

entries.farsi: پرورد در آتش ابراهيم را / ايمنى روح سازد بيم را
entries.english: He cherishes Abraham in the fire; He turns fear into security of spirit.

entries.farsi: از سبب سوزيش من سودايى‏ام / در خيالاتش چو سوفسطايى‏ام‏
entries.english: By His burning of secondary causes I am distraught; in fancies of Him I am like a sophist.

sections.title_fa: ‏مكر ديگر انگيختن وزير در اضالل قوم
sections.title_en: How the vizier started another plan to mislead the Christians

entries.farsi: مكر ديگر آن وزير از خود ببست / وعظ را بگذاشت و در خلوت نشست
entries.english: The vizier formed in his mind another plan: he abandoned preaching and sat alone in seclusion.

entries.farsi: در مريدان در فكند از شوق سوز / بود در خلوت چهل پنجاه روز
entries.english: He inspired ardour in his disciples from longing; he remained in seclusion forty or fifty days.

entries.farsi: خلق ديوانه شدند از شوق او / از فراق حال و قال و ذوق او
entries.english: The people became mad from longing for him and on account of being separated from his feeling and discourse and intuition.

entries.farsi: البه و زارى همى‏كردند و او / از رياضت گشته در خلوت دو تو
entries.english: They were making supplication and lament, while he in solitude was bent double by austerities.

entries.farsi: گفته ايشان نيست ما را بى‏تو نور / بى‏عصا كش چون بود احوال كور
entries.english: They said, “Without you we have no light: how is the state of a blind man without a leader?

entries.farsi: از سر اكرام و از بهر خدا / بيش از اين ما را مدار از خود جدا
entries.english: By way of showing favour and for God’s sake, do not keep us parted from you any longer.

entries.farsi: ما چو طفالنيم و ما را دايه تو / بر سر ما گستران آن سايه تو
entries.english: We are as children and you are our nurse: spread over us that shadow.”

entries.farsi: گفت جانم از محبان دور نيست / ليك بيرون آمدن دستور نيست‏
entries.english: He said, “My soul is not far from them that love, but there is no permission to come forth.”

entries.farsi: آن اميران در شفاعت آمدند / و آن مريدان در شناعت آمدند
entries.english: Those amirs came for intercession, and the disciples came in reproach,

entries.farsi: كاين چه بد بختى است ما را اى كريم / از دل و دين مانده ما بى‏تو يتيم‏
entries.english: Saying, “O noble sir, what a misfortune is this for us! Without you we are left orphaned of our hearts and our religion.

entries.farsi: تو بهانه مى‏كنى و ما ز درد / مى‏زنيم از سوز دل دمهاى سرد
entries.english: You are making pretence while we in grief are heaving cold sighs from the burning heat of our hearts.

entries.farsi: ما به گفتار خوشت خو كرده‏ايم / ما ز شير حكمت تو خورده‏ايم‏
entries.english: We have become accustomed to your sweet discourse; we have drunk of the milk of your wisdom.

entries.farsi: اللهَّ اهلل اين جفا با ما مكن / خير كن امروز را فردا مكن‏
entries.english: Allah! Allah! Do not you treat us with such cruelty: show kindness to-day, do not delay until tomorrow

entries.farsi: مى‏دهد دل مر ترا كاين بى‏دالن / بى‏تو گردند آخر از بى‏حاصالن‏
entries.english: Does your heart give to you that these who have lost their hearts should at last, being without you, become among them that have nothing left?

entries.farsi: جمله در خشكى چو ماهى مى‏تپند / آب را بگشا ز جو بر دار بند
entries.english: They all are writhing like fishes on dry land: let loose the water, remove the darn from the stream.

entries.farsi: اى كه چون تو در زمانه نيست كس / اللهَّ اللهَّ خلق را فرياد رس‏
entries.english: O you like whom there is none in the world, for God’s sake, for God’s sake, come to the aid of your people!”

sections.title_fa: دفع گفتن وزير مريدان را
sections.title_en: How the vizier refused the request of the disciples.

entries.farsi: گفت هان اى سخرگان گفت‏وگو / وعظ و گفتار زبان و گوش جو
entries.english: He said: “Beware, O you enslaved by words and talk, you who seek admonition of the speech of the tongue and of the ear.

entries.farsi: پنبه اندر گوش حس دون كنيد / بند حس از چشم خود بيرون كنيد
entries.english: Put cotton-wool in the ear of the low sense; take off the bandage of sense from your eyes!

entries.farsi: پنبه‏ى آن گوش سر گوش سر است / تا نگردد اين كر آن باطن كر است‏
entries.english: The ear of the head is the cotton-wool of the ear of the conscience: until the former becomes deaf, that inward is deaf.

entries.farsi: بى‏حس و بى‏گوش و بى‏فكرت شويد / تا خطاب ارْجِعِي را بشنويد
entries.english: Become without sense and without ear and without thought, that you may hear the call, ‘Return!’”

entries.farsi: تا به گفت‏وگوى بيدارى درى / تو ز گفت خواب بويى كى برى‏
entries.english: So long as you are in the conversation of wakefulness, how will you catch any scent of the conversation of sleep?

entries.farsi: سير بيرونى است قول و فعل ما / سير باطن هست بالاى سما
entries.english: Our speech and action is the exterior journey: the interior journey is above the sky.

entries.farsi: حس خشكى ديد كز خشكى بزاد / عيسى جان پاى بر دريا نهاد
entries.english: The sense saw dryness, because it was born of dryness: the Jesus of the spirit set foot on the sea.

entries.farsi: سير جسم خشك بر خشكى فتاد / سير جان پا در دل دريا نهاد
entries.english: The journey of the dry body happened on dry land, the journey of the spirit took place in the heart of the sea

entries.farsi: چون كه عمر اندر ره خشكى گذشت / گاه كوه و گاه صحرا گاه دشت‏
entries.english: Since your life has passed in travelling on land, now mountain, now river, now desert,

entries.farsi: آب حيوان از كجا خواهى تو يافت / موج دريا را كجا خواهى شكافت‏
entries.english: From where will you gain the Water of Life? Where will you part the waves of the Sea?

entries.farsi: موج خاكى وهم و فهم و فكر ماست / موج آبى محو و سكر است و فناست‏
entries.english: The waves of earth are our imagination and understanding and thought; the waves of water are self-effacement and intoxication and death (fana).

entries.farsi: تا در اين سكرى از آن سكرى تو دور / تا از اين مستى از آن جامى تو دور
entries.english: While you are in this intoxication, you are far from that intoxication; while you are drunk with this, you are blind to that cup.

entries.farsi: گفت‏وگوى ظاهر آمد چون غبار / مدتى خاموش خو كن هوش دار
entries.english: Outward speech and talk is as dust: for a time make a habit of silence. Take heed!

sections.title_fa: ‏مكرر كردن مريدان كه خلوت را بشكن
sections.title_en: How the disciples repeated their request that he should interrupt his seclusion.

entries.farsi: جمله گفتند اى حكيم رخنه جو / اين فريب و اين جفا با ما مگو
entries.english: They all said: “O sage who seeks a crevice, say not to us this guile and harshness.

entries.farsi: چار پا را قدر طاقت بار نه / بر ضعيفان قدر قوت كار نه‏
entries.english: Lay on the beast a burden in proportion to its endurance, lay on the weak a task in proportion to their strength.

entries.farsi: دانه‏ى هر مرغ اندازه‏ى وى است / طعمه‏ى هر مرغ انجيرى كى است‏
entries.english: The bait for every bird is according to its measure: how should a fig be the food for every bird?

entries.farsi: طفل را گر نان دهى بر جاى شير / طفل مسكين را از آن نان مرده گير
entries.english: If you give a babe bread instead of milk, take it that the poor babe will die of the bread;

entries.farsi: چون كه دندانها بر آرد بعد از آن / هم بخود گردد دلش جوياى نان‏
entries.english: Afterwards, when it grows teeth, that babe will of its own accord ask for bread.

entries.farsi: مرغ پر نارسته چون پران شود / لقمه‏ى هر گربه‏ى دران شود
entries.english: When an unfledged bird begins to fly, it becomes a mouthful for any rapacious cat;

entries.farsi: ديو را نطق تو خامش مى‏كند / گوش ما را گفت تو هش مى‏كند
entries.english: When it grows wings, it will fly of itself without trouble and without whistling, good or bad.

entries.farsi: چون بر آرد پر بپرد او به خود / بى‏تكلف بى‏صفير نيك و بد
entries.english: Your speech makes the Devil silent; your words make our ears intelligence.

entries.farsi: گوش ما هوش است چون گويا تويى / خشك ما بحر است چون دريا تويى‏
entries.english: Our ears are intelligence when you are speaking; our dry land is a river when you are the ocean.

entries.farsi: با تو ما را خاك بهتر از فلك / اى سماك از تو منور تا سمك‏
entries.english: With you, earth is better to us than heaven, O you by whom Arcturus to the Fish is illumined!

entries.farsi: بى‏تو ما را بر فلك تاريكى است / با تو اى ماه اين فلك بارى كى است‏
entries.english: Without you, darkness is over heaven for us, compared with you, O Moon, who is this heaven at all?

entries.farsi: صورت رفعت بود افلاک را / معنى رفعت روان پاك را
entries.english: The heavens have the form of sublimity; the essence of sublimity belongs to the pure spirit.

entries.farsi: صورت رفعت براى جسمهاست / جسمها در پيش معنى اسمهاست‏
entries.english: The form of sublimity is for bodies; beside the essence bodies are (mere) names.”

sections.title_fa: ‏جواب گفتن وزير كه خلوت را نمى‏شكنم
sections.title_en: The refusal of the vizier to interrupt his seclusion

entries.farsi: گفت حجتهاى خود كوته كنيد / پند را در جان و در دل ره كنيد
entries.english: He said: “Cut short your arguments, let my advice make its way into your souls and hearts.

entries.farsi: گر امينم متهم نبود امين / گر بگويم آسمان را من زمين‏
entries.english: If I am trustworthy, the trustworthy is not doubted, even though I should call heaven earth.

entries.farsi: گر كمالم با كمال انكار چيست / ور نيم اين زحمت و آزار چيست‏
entries.english: If I am perfection, why disbelief in my perfection? And if I am not, why this molestation and annoyance?

entries.farsi: من نخواهم شد از اين خلوت برون / ز آن كه مشغولم به احوال درون‏
entries.english: I will not go forth from this seclusion, because I am occupied with inward experiences.”

sections.title_fa: اعتراض مريدان در خلوت وزير
sections.title_en: How the disciples raised objections against the vizier’s secluding himself.

entries.farsi: جمله گفتند اى وزير انكار نيست / گفت ما چون گفتن اغيار نيست‏
entries.english: They all said; “ O vizier, it is not disbelief: our words are not as the words of strangers.

entries.farsi: اشك ديده‏ست از فراق تو دوان / آه آه است از ميان جان روان
entries.english: The tears of our eyes are running because of our separation from you; sigh after sigh is going from the midst of our souls.

entries.farsi: طفل با دايه نه استيزد و ليك / گريد او گر چه نه بد داند نه نيك‏
entries.english: A babe does not contend with its nurse, but it weeps, although it knows neither evil nor good.

entries.farsi: ما چون چنگيم و تو زخمه مى‏زنى / زارى از ما نى تو زارى مى‏كنى‏
entries.english: We are as the harp and you art striking the plectrum: the lamentation is not from us, it is you that art making lamentation.

entries.farsi: ما چو ناييم و نوا در ما ز تست / ما چو كوهيم و صدا در ما ز تست‏
entries.english: We are as the flute, and the music in us is from you; we are as the mountain, and the echo in us is from you.

entries.farsi: ما چو شطرنجيم اندر برد و مات / برد و مات ما ز تست اى خوش صفات‏
entries.english: We are as pieces of chess in victory and defeat: our victory and defeat is from you, O you whose qualities are comely!

entries.farsi: ما كه باشيم اى تو ما را جان جان / تا كه ما باشيم با تو در ميان‏
entries.english: Who are we, O you soul of our souls, that we should remain in being beside you?

entries.farsi: ما عدمهاييم و هستيهاى ما / تو وجود مطلقى فانى نما
entries.english: We and our existences are non-existences: you are the absolute Being which manifests the perishable

entries.farsi: حمله شان پيدا و ناپيداست باد / آن كه ناپيداست هرگز كم مباد
entries.english: We all are lions, but lions on a banner: because of the wind they are rushing onward from moment to moment.

entries.farsi: ما همه شيران ولى شير علم / حمله‏شان از باد باشد دم‏به‏دم‏
entries.english: Their onward rush is visible, and the wind is unseen: may that which is unseen not fail from us!

entries.farsi: باد ما و بود ما از داد تست / هستى ما جمله از ايجاد تست‏
entries.english: Our wind and our being are of your gift; our whole existence is from your bringing into being.

entries.farsi: لذت هستى نمودى نيست را / عاشق خود كرده بودى نيست را
entries.english: You did show the delightfulness of Being unto not-being, you had caused not-being to fall in love with you.

entries.farsi: لذت انعام خود را وامگير / نقل و باده و جام خود را وامگير
entries.english: Take not away the delightfulness of your bounty; take not away your dessert and wine and wine-cup!

entries.farsi: ور بگيرى كيت جستجو كند / نقش با نقاش چون نيرو كند
entries.english: And if you take it away; who is there that will make inquiry? How should the picture strive with the painter?

entries.farsi: منگر اندر ما، مكن در ما نظر / اندر اكرام و سخاى خود نگر
entries.english: Do not look on us; do not fix your gaze on us: look on your own kindness and generosity.

entries.farsi: ما نبوديم و تقاضامان نبود / لطف تو ناگفته‏ى ما مى‏شنود
entries.english: We were not, and there was no demand on our part, your grace was hearkening to our unspoken prayer”

entries.farsi: نقش باشد پيش نقاش و قلم / عاجز و بسته چو كودك در شكم‏
entries.english: Before the painter and the brush the picture is helpless and bound like a child in the womb.

entries.farsi: پيش قدرت خلق جمله بارگه / عاجزان چون پيش سوزن كارگه‏
entries.english: Before Omnipotence all the people of the court of audience are as helpless as the fabric before the needle.

entries.farsi: دست نه تا دست جنباند به دفع / نطق نه تا دم زند در ضر و نفع‏
entries.english: Now He makes the picture the Devil, now Adam; now He makes the picture joy, now grief.

entries.farsi: گاه نقشش ديو و گه آدم كند / گاه نقشش شادى و گه غم كند
entries.english: There is no power that he should move a hand in defence; no speech, that he should utter a word concerning injury or benefit.

entries.farsi: تو ز قرآن باز خوان تفسير بيت / گفت ايزد ما رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رميت‏
entries.english: Recite from the Qur’an the interpretation of the verse: God said, You did not throw when you threw.

entries.farsi: گر بپرانيم تير آن نه ز ماست / ما كمان و تير اندازش خداست
entries.english: If we let an arrow fly, that is not from us: we are the bow, and the shooter of the arrow is God.

entries.farsi: اين نه جبر اين معنى جبارى است / ذكر جبارى براى زارى است‏
entries.english: This is not jabr (compulsion); it is the meaning of jabbari (almightiness): the mention of almightiness is for the sake of humility.

entries.farsi: زارى ما شد دليل اضطرار / خجلت ما شد دليل اختيار
entries.english: Our humility is evidence of necessity; our sense of guilt is evidence of freewill.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى اختيار اين شرم چيست / وين دريغ و خجلت و آزرم چيست
entries.english: If there were not freewill, what is this shame? And what are sorrow, guilty confusion and abashment?

entries.farsi: زجر استادان و شاگردان چراست / خاطر از تدبيرها گردان چراست‏
entries.english: Why is there chiding between masters and pupils? Why is the mind changing from plans?

entries.farsi: ور تو گويى غافل است از جبر او / ماه حق پنهان كند در ابر رو
entries.english: And if you say that he takes no heed of His compulsion, God’s moon has become hidden in His cloud,

entries.farsi: هست اين را خوش جواب ار بشنوى / بگذرى از كفر و در دين بگروى‏
entries.english: There is a good answer to this; if you listen, you will relinquish unbelief and incline towards the religion.

entries.farsi: حسرت و زارى گه بيمارى است / وقت بيمارى همه بيدارى است‏
entries.english: Remorse and humility occur at the time of illness: the time of illness is wholly wakefulness.

entries.farsi: آن زمان كه مى‏شوى بيمار تو / مى‏كنى از جرم استغفار تو
entries.english: At the time when you are becoming ill, you pray God to forgive your trespass;

entries.farsi: مى‏نمايد بر تو زشتى گنه / مى‏كنى نيت كه باز آيم به ره‏
entries.english: The foulness of your sin is shown to you; you resolve to come back to the way;

entries.farsi: عهد و پيمان مى‏كنى كه بعد از اين / جز كه طاعت نبودم كار گزين‏
entries.english: You make promises and vows that henceforth your chosen course will be nothing but obedience:

entries.farsi: پس يقين گشت اين كه بيمارى ترا / مى‏ببخشد هوش و بيدارى ترا
entries.english: Therefore it has become certain that illness gives to you conscience and wakefulness.

entries.farsi: پس بدان اين اصل را اى اصل جو / هر كه را درد است او برده ست بو
entries.english: Note, then, this principle, O you that seek the principle; every one who suffers pain has caught the scent:

entries.farsi: هر كه او بيدارتر پر دردتر / هر كه او آگاه‏تر رخ زردتر
entries.english: The more wakeful any one is the fuller of suffering he is; the more aware he is, the paler he is in countenance

entries.farsi: گر ز جبرش آگهى زاريت كو / بينش زنجير جباريت كو
entries.english: If you are aware of His jabr (compulsion), where is your humility? Where is your feeling of the chain of His jabbari (almightiness)?

entries.farsi: بسته در زنجير چون شادى كند / كى اسير حبس آزادى كند
entries.english: How one should make merry who is bound in chains? When does the captive in prison behave like the man who is free?

entries.farsi: ور تو مى‏بينى كه پايت بسته‏اند / بر تو سرهنگان شه بنشسته‏اند
entries.english: And if you consider that your foot is shackled the king’s officers are sitting over you,

entries.farsi: پس تو سرهنگى مكن با عاجزان / ز آن كه نبود طبع و خوى عاجز آن‏
entries.english: Then do not act like an officer towards the helpless, inasmuch as that is not the nature and habit of a helpless man.

entries.farsi: چون تو جبر او نمى‏بينى مگو / ور همى‏بينى نشان ديد كو
entries.english: Since you do not feel His compulsion, do not say; and if you feel it, where is the sign of your feeling?

entries.farsi: در هر آن كارى كه ميل استت بدان / قدرت خود را همى‏بينى عيان‏
entries.english: In every act for which you have inclination, you are clearly conscious of your power,

entries.farsi: و اندر آن كارى كه ميلت نيست و خواست / خويش را جبرى كنى كاين از خداست‏
entries.english: In every act for which you have no inclination and desire, in regard to that you have become a necessitarian, saying, “This is from God.”

entries.farsi: انبيا در كار دنيا جبرى‏اند / كافران در كار عقبى جبرى‏اند
entries.english: The prophets are necessitarians in regard to the works of this world, the infidels are necessitarians in regard to the works of the next world.

entries.farsi: انبيا را كار عقبى اختيار / جاهالن را كار دنيا اختيار
entries.english: To the prophets the works of the next world are freewill; to the foolish the works of this world are freewill,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه هر مرغى به سوى جنس خويش / مى‏پرد او در پس و جان پيش پيش‏
entries.english: Because every bird flies to its own kind: it behind, and its spirit before.

entries.farsi: كافران چون جنس سجين آمدند / سجن دنيا را خوش آيين آمدند
entries.english: Inasmuch as the infidels were congeners of Sijjin (Hell), they were well-disposed to the prison (sijn) of this world.

entries.farsi: انبيا چون جنس عليين بدند / سوى عليين جان و دل شدند
entries.english: Inasmuch as the prophets were congeners of ‘Illiyyin (Heaven), they went towards the ‘Illiyyin of spirit and heart.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد ليك ما / باز گوييم آن تمامى قصه را
entries.english: This discourse has no end, but let us relate the story to its completion.

sections.title_fa: نوميد كردن وزير مريدان را از رفض خلوت
sections.title_en: How the vizier made the disciples lose hope of his abandoning seclusion

entries.farsi: آن وزير از اندرون آواز داد / كاى مريدان از من اين معلوم باد
entries.english: The vizier cried out from within, “O disciples, let this made known to you from me,

entries.farsi: كه مرا عيسى چنين پيغام كرد / كز همه ياران و خويشان باش فرد
entries.english: That Jesus has given me a such-like message: ‘Be separated from all friends and kinsfolk.

entries.farsi: روى در ديوار كن تنها نشين / وز وجود خويش هم خلوت گزين‏
entries.english: Set your face to the wall, sit alone, and choose to be secluded even from your own existence.’

entries.farsi: بعد از اين دستورى گفتار نيست / بعد از اين با گفت و گويم كار نيست‏
entries.english: After this there is no permission to speak; after this I have nothing to do with talk.

entries.farsi: الوداع اى دوستان من مرده‏ام / رخت بر چارم فلك بر برده‏ام‏
entries.english: Farewell, O friends! I am dead: I have carried my belongings up to the Fourth Heaven,

entries.farsi: تا به زير چرخ نارى چون حطب / من نسوزم در عنا و در عطب‏
entries.english: In order that beneath the fiery sphere I may not burn like firewood in woe and perdition,

entries.farsi: پهلوى عيسى نشينم بعد از اين / بر فراز آسمان چارمين‏
entries.english: Henceforth may sit beside Jesus at the top of the Fourth Heaven.”

sections.title_fa: ولى عهد ساختن وزير هر يك امير را جدا جدا
sections.title_en: How the vizier appointed each one of the amirs separately as his successor.

entries.farsi: و آن گهانى آن اميران را بخواند / يك به يك تنها به هر يك حرف راند
entries.english: And then he summoned those amirs one by one and conversed with each alone.

entries.farsi: گفت هر يك را به دين عيسوى / نايب حق و خليفه‏ى من توى‏
entries.english: He said to each one, “In the religion of Jesus you art the vicar of God and my Khalifa (vicegerent),

entries.farsi: هر اميرى كو كشيد گردن بگير / يا بكش يا خود همى‏دارش اسير
entries.english: And those other amirs are your followers: Jesus has made all of them your assistants.

entries.farsi: و آن اميران دگر اتباع تو / كرد عيسى جمله را اشياع تو
entries.english: Any amir who lifts his neck, seize him and either kill him or hold him captive;

entries.farsi: ليك تا من زنده‏ام اين وامگو / تا نميرم اين رياست را مجو
entries.english: But do not declare this while I am alive: do not seek this supreme authority until I am dead.

entries.farsi: اينك اين طومار و احكام مسيح / يك به يك بر خوان تو بر امت فصيح‏
entries.english: Until I am dead, do not reveal this: do not lay claim to sovereignty and dominion.

entries.farsi: تا نميرم من تو اين پيدا مكن / دعوى شاهى و استيال مكن
entries.english: Here is this scroll and the ordinances of the Messiah: recite them distinctly, one by one, to his people.”

entries.farsi: هر اميرى را چنين گفت او جدا / نيست نايب جز تو در دين خدا
entries.english: Thus he spoke to each amir separately, “There is no vicar in the religion of God except you.”

entries.farsi: هر يكى را كرد او يك يك عزيز / هر چه آن را گفت اين را گفت نيز
entries.english: He honoured each, one by one,: whatever he said to that he also said to this.

entries.farsi: هر يكى را او يكى طومار داد / هر يكى ضد دگر بود المراد
entries.english: To each he gave one scroll: every one was purposely the contrary of the other.

entries.farsi: جملگى طومارها بد مختلف / چون حروف آن جمله از يا تا الف‏
entries.english: All the scrolls were different, like the forms of the letters from ya to alif.

entries.farsi: حكم اين طومار ضد حكم آن / پيش از اين كرديم اين ضد را بيان‏
entries.english: The rule in this scroll was contrary to the rule in that: we have already explained this contradiction.

sections.title_fa: كشتن وزير خويشتن را در خلوت‏
sections.title_en: How the vizier killed himself in seclusion.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن چل روز ديگر در ببست / خويش كشت و از وجود خود برست‏
entries.english: After that, he shut the door for other forty days killed himself and escaped from his existence.

entries.farsi: ون كه خلق از مرگ او آگاه شد / بر سر گورش قيامت‏گاه شد
entries.english: When the people learned of his death, there came to pass at his grave the scene of the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: خلق چندان جمع شد بر گور او / موكنان جامه دران در شور او
entries.english: So great a multitude gathered at his grave, tearing their hair, rending their garments in wild grief for him,

entries.farsi: كان عدد را هم خدا داند شمرد / از عرب وز ترك و از رومى و كرد
entries.english: That only God can reckon the number of them— Arabs, Turks, Greeks and Kurds.

entries.farsi: خاك او كردند بر سرهاى خويش / درد او ديدند درمان جاى خويش‏
entries.english: They put his earth on their heads; they deemed anguish for him to be the remedy for themselves.

entries.farsi: آن خاليق بر سر گورش مهى / كرده خون را از دو چشم خود رهى‏
entries.english: During a month those multitudes over his grave made a way for blood from their eyes.

sections.title_fa: طلب كردن امت عيسى عليه السلام از امرا كه ولى عهد از شما كدام است
sections.title_en: How the people of Jesus—on him be peace!—asked the amirs, “Which one of you is the successor?”

entries.farsi: بعد ماهى خلق گفتند اى مهان / از اميران كيست بر جايش نشان‏
entries.english: After a month the people said, “O chiefs, which of amirs is designated in his place,

entries.farsi: تا به جاى او شناسيمش امام / دست و دامن را بدست او دهيم‏
entries.english: That we may acknowledge him as our religious leader instead of him, and give our hands and skirts into his hand?

entries.farsi: چون كه شد خورشيد و ما را كرد داغ / چاره نبود بر مقامش از چراغ‏
entries.english: Since the sun is gone and has branded us, is not a lamp the resource in his stead?

entries.farsi: چون كه شد از پيش ديده وصل يار / نايبى بايد از او مان يادگار
entries.english: Since union with the beloved has vanished from before our eves, we must have a vicar as a memorial of him.

entries.farsi: چون كه گل بگذشت و گلشن شد خراب / بوى گل را از كه يابيم از گالب‏
entries.english: Since the rose is past and the garden ravaged, from whom shall we get the perfume of the rose? From rosewater.”

entries.farsi: چون خدا اندر نيايد در عيان / نايب حق‏اند اين پيغمبران‏
entries.english: Inasmuch as God comes not into sight, these prophets are the vicars of God.

entries.farsi: نه غلط گفتم كه نايب با منوب / گر دو پندارى قبيح آيد نه خوب‏
entries.english: Nay, I have said wrongly; for if you suppose that the vicar and He who is represented by the vicar are two, it is bad, not good.

entries.farsi: نه دو باشد تا تويى صورت پرست / پيش او يك گشت كز صورت برست‏
entries.english: Nay; they are two so long as you are a worshipper of form, they have become one to him who has escaped from form.

entries.farsi: چون به صورت بنگرى چشم تو دست / تو به نورش درنگر كز چشم رست‏
entries.english: When you look at the form, your eye is two; look at its light, which grew from the eye.

entries.farsi: نور هر دو چشم نتوان فرق كرد / چون كه در نورش نظر انداخت مرد
entries.english: “It is impossible to distinguish the light of the two eyes, when a man has cast his look upon their light.

entries.farsi: ده چراغ ار حاضر آيد در مكان / هر يكى باشد به صورت غير آن‏
entries.english: If ten lamps are present in place, each differs in form from another :

entries.farsi: فرق نتوان كرد نور هر يكى / چون به نورش روى آرى بى‏شكى‏
entries.english: To distinguish without any doubt the light of each, when you turn your face towards their light, is impossible.

entries.farsi: گر تو صد سيب و صد آبى بشمرى / صد نماند يك شود چون بفشرى‏
entries.english: If you count a hundred apples or a hundred quinces, they do not remain a hundred become one, when you crush them.

entries.farsi: در معانى قسمت و اعداد نيست / در معانى تجزيه و افراد نيست‏
entries.english: In things spiritual there is no division and no numbers; in things spiritual -there is no partition and no individuals.

entries.farsi: اتحاد يار با ياران خوش است / پاى معنى گير صورت سركش است
entries.english: Sweet is the oneness of the Friend with His friends: catch the foot of spirit. Form is headstrong.

entries.farsi: صورت سركش گدازان كن به رنج / تا ببينى زير او وحدت چو گنج‏
entries.english: Make headstrong form waste away with tribulation, that beneath it you may descry unity, like a treasure;

entries.farsi: ور تو نگذارى عنايتهاى او / خود گدازد اى دلم موالى او
entries.english: And if you waste it not away, His favours will waste it— oh, my heart is His vassal.

entries.farsi: او نمايد هم به دلها خويش را / او بدوزد خرقه‏ى درويش را
entries.english: He even shows Himself to hearts, He sews the tattered frock of the dervish

entries.farsi: منبسط بوديم و يك جوهر همه / بى‏سر و بى‏پا بديم آن سر همه‏
entries.english: Simple were we and all one substance; we were all without head and without foot yonder.

entries.farsi: يك گهر بوديم همچون آفتاب / بى‏گره بوديم و صافى همچو آب‏
entries.english: We were one substance, like the Sun; we were knotless and pure, like water.

entries.farsi: چون به صورت آمد آن نور سره / شد عدد چون سايه‏هاى كنگره‏
entries.english: When that goodly Light took form, it became number like the shadows of a battlement.

entries.farsi: كنگره ويران كنيد از منجنيق / تا رود فرق از ميان اين فريق‏
entries.english: Raze the battlement with the catapult (manjaniq) that difference may vanish from amidst this company.

entries.farsi: شرح اين را گفتمى من از مرى / ليك ترسم تا نلغزد خاطرى‏
entries.english: I would have explained this with contention, but I fear lest some mind may stumble.

entries.farsi: نكته‏ها چون تيغ پوالد است تيز / گر ندارى تو سپر واپس گريز
entries.english: The points are sharp as a sword of steel; if you have not the shield, turn back and flee !

entries.farsi: پيش اين الماس بى‏اسپر ميا / كز بريدن تيغ را نبود حيا
entries.english: Do not come without shield against this adamant, for the sword is not ashamed of cutting.

entries.farsi: زين سبب من تيغ كردم در غالف / تا كه كج خوانى نخواند بر خالف‏
entries.english: For this cause I have put the sword in sheath, that none who misreads may read contrariwise.

entries.farsi: آمديم اندر تمامى داستان / وز وفادارى جمع راستان‏
entries.english: We come to complete the tale and of the loyalty of the multitude of the righteous,

entries.farsi: كز پس اين پيشوا برخاستند / بر مقامش نايبى مى‏خواستند
entries.english: Who rose up after this leader, demanding a vicar in his place

sections.title_fa: منازعت امرا در وليعهدى
sections.title_en: The quarrel of the amirs concerning’ the succession.

entries.farsi: يك اميرى ز آن اميران پيش رفت / پيش آن قوم وفا انديش رفت‏
entries.english: One of those amirs advanced and went before that loyal-minded people

entries.farsi: اينك اين طومار برهان من است / كاين نيابت بعد از او آن من است‏
entries.english: “Behold,” said he,” I am that man’s vicar: I am the vicar of Jesus at the present time.

entries.farsi: گفت اينك نايب آن مرد من / نايب عيسى منم اندر زمن‏
entries.english: Look, this scroll is my proof that after him the caliphate belongs to me.”

entries.farsi: آن امير ديگر آمد از كمين / دعوى او در خالفت بد همين‏
entries.english: Another amir came forth from ambush: his pretension regarding the caliphate was the same;

entries.farsi: از بغل او نيز طومارى نمود / تا بر آمد هر دو را خشم جهود
entries.english: He too produced a scroll from under his arm, so that in both there arose the Jewish anger.

entries.farsi: آن اميران دگر يك يك قطار / بر كشيده تيغهاى آب دار
entries.english: The rest of the amirs, one after another, drawing swords of keen mettle,

entries.farsi: هر يكى را تيغ و طومارى به دست / درهم‏افتادند چون پيالن مست
entries.english: Each with a sword and a scroll in his hand fell to combat like raging elephants.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران مرد ترسا كشته شد / تا ز سرهاى بريده پشته شد
entries.english: Hundreds of thousands of Christians were slain, so that there were mounds of severed heads;

entries.farsi: خون روان شد همچو سيل از چپ و راست / كوه كوه اندر هوا زين گرد خاست‏
entries.english: Blood flowed, on left and right, like a torrent; mountains of this dust rose in the air.

entries.farsi: تخمهاى فتنه‏ها كاو كشته بود / آفت سرهاى ايشان گشته بود
entries.english: The seeds of dissension which he had sown had become a calamity to their heads.

entries.farsi: جوزها بشكست و آن كان مغز داشت / بعد كشتن روح پاك نغز داشت‏
entries.english: The walnuts were broken, and those which had the kernel had, after being slain, a spirit pure and fair.

entries.farsi: كشتن و مردن كه بر نقش تن است / چون انار و سيب را بشكستن است‏
entries.english: Slaughter and death which befalls the bodily frame is like breaking pomegranates and apples:

entries.farsi: آن چه شيرين است او شد ناردانگ / و آن كه پوسيده ست نبود غير بانگ‏
entries.english: That which is sweet becomes pomegranate-syrup, and that which is rotten is naught but noise:

entries.farsi: آن چه با معنى است خود پيدا شود / و آن چه پوسيده ست او رسوا شود
entries.english: That which has reality is made manifest and that which is rotten is put to shame.

entries.farsi: رو به معنى كوش اى صورت پرست / ز آن كه معنى بر تن صورت پر است‏
entries.english: Go; strive after reality, O worshipper of form, inasmuch as reality is the wing on form’s body.

entries.farsi: همنشين اهل معنى باش تا / هم عطا يابى و هم باشى فتا
entries.english: Consort with the followers of reality, that you may both win the gift and be generous.

entries.farsi: جان بى‏معنى در اين تن بى‏خالف / هست همچون تيغ چوبين در غالف‏
entries.english: Beyond dispute, in this body the spirit devoid of reality is even as, a wooden sword in the sheath:

entries.farsi: تا غالف اندر بود با قيمت است / چون برون شد سوختن را آلت است‏
entries.english: While it remains in the sheath, it is valuable, when it has come forth it is an implement for burning.

entries.farsi: تيغ چوبين را مبر در كارزار / بنگر اول تا نگردد كار زار
entries.english: Do not take a wooden sword into the battle! First see, in order that your plight may not be wretched.

entries.farsi: گر بود چوبين برو ديگر طلب / ور بود الماس پيش آ با طرب‏
entries.english: If it is made of wood, go, seek another; and if it is a diamond, march forward joyously.

entries.farsi: تيغ در زرادخانه‏ى اولياست / ديدن ايشان شما را كيمياست‏
entries.english: The sword is in the armoury of the saints: to see them is for you the Elixir.

entries.farsi: جمله دانايان همين گفته همين / هست دانا رَحْ مَةً للعالمين‏
entries.english: All the wise have said this same thing: the wise man is a mercy to created beings.

entries.farsi: گر انارى مى‏خرى خندان بخر / تا دهد خنده ز دانه‏ى او خبر
entries.english: If you would buy a pomegranate, buy laughing, so that its laughter may give information as to its seeds.

entries.farsi: اى مبارك خنده‏اش كاو از دهان / مى‏نمايد دل چو در از درج جان‏
entries.english: Oh, blessed is its laughter, for through its mouth it shows the heart, like a pearl from the casket of the spirit.

entries.farsi: نار خندان باغ را خندان كند / صحبت مردانت از مردان كند
entries.english: Unblessed was the laughter of the red tulip, from whose mouth appeared the blackness of its heart.

entries.farsi: نامبارك خنده‏ى آن الله بود / كز دهان او سياهى دل نمود
entries.english: The laughing pomegranate makes the garden laughing: companionship with men makes you one of the men.

entries.farsi: گر تو سنگ صخره و مرمر شوى / چون به صاحب دل رسى گوهر شوى‏
entries.english: Though you are a rock or marble, you will become a jewel when you reach the awliya

entries.farsi: مهر پاكان در ميان جان نشان / دل مده الا به مهر دل خوشان‏
entries.english: Plant the love of the holy ones within your spirit; do not give your heart save to the love of them whose hearts are glad.

entries.farsi: كوى نوميدى مرو اميدهاست / سوى تاريكى مرو خورشيدهاست
entries.english: Do not go to the neighbourhood of despair: there are hopes. Go not in the direction of darkness: there are suns.

entries.farsi: دل ترا در كوى اهل دل كشد / تن ترا در حبس آب و گل كشد
entries.english: The heart leads you into the neighbourhood of the awliya; the body leads you into the prison of water and earth.

entries.farsi: هين غذاى دل بده از هم دلى / رو بجو اقبال را از مقبلى‏
entries.english: Oh, give your heart food from one who is in accord with it; go, seek advancement from one who is advanced.

sections.title_fa: تعظيم نعت مصطفى عليه السلام كه مذكور بود در انجيل
sections.title_en: How honour was paid to the description of Mustafa, on whom be peace, which was mentioned in the Gospel.

entries.farsi: بود در انجيل نام مصطفى / آن سر پيغمبران بحر صفا
entries.english: The name of Mustafa was in the Gospel, the chief of the prophets, the sea of purity.

entries.farsi: بود ذكر حليه‏ها و شكل او / بود ذكر غزو و صوم و اكل او
entries.english: There was mention of his characteristics and appearance; there was mention of his warring and fasting and eating.

entries.farsi: طايفه‏ى نصرانيان بهر ثواب / چون رسيدندى بدان نام و خطاب‏
entries.english: A party among the Christians, for the sake of the Divine reward, whenever they came to that name and discourse,

entries.farsi: بوسه دادندى بر آن نام شريف / رو نهادندى بر آن وصف لطيف‏
entries.english: Would bestow kisses on that noble name and stoop their faces towards that beauteous description.

entries.farsi: اندر اين فتنه كه گفتيم آن گروه / ايمن از فتنه بدند و از شكوه‏
entries.english: In this tribulation of which we have told, that party were secure from tribulation and dread,

entries.farsi: ايمن از شر اميران و وزير / در پناه نام احمد مستجير
entries.english: Secure from the mischief of the amirs and the vizier, seeking refuge in the protection of the Name of Ahmad.

entries.farsi: نسل ايشان نيز هم بسيار شد / نور احمد ناصر آمد يار شد
entries.english: Their offspring also multiplied: the Light of Ahmad aided and befriended them.

entries.farsi: و آن گروه ديگر از نصرانيان / نام احمد داشتندى مستهان‏
entries.english: And the other party among the Christians were holding the Name of Ahmad in contempt,

entries.farsi: مستهان و خوار گشتند از فتن / از وزير شوم راى شوم فن‏
entries.english: They became contemptible and despised through dissensions caused by the evil-counselling and evil-plotting vizier;

entries.farsi: هم مخبط دينشان و حكمشان / از پى طومارهاى كژ بيان‏
entries.english: Moreover, their religion and their law became corrupted in consequence of the scrolls which set forth all perversely.

entries.farsi: نام احمد اين چنين يارى كند / تا كه نورش چون نگهدارى كند
entries.english: The Name of Ahmad gives such help as this, so that how his Light keeps guard.

entries.farsi: نام احمد چون حصارى شد حصين / تا چه باشد ذات آن روح الامين‏
entries.english: Since the Name of Ahmad became an impregnable fortress, what then must be the Essence of that trusted Spirit?

sections.title_fa: حكايت پادشاه جهود ديگر كه در هالك دين عيسى سعى نمود
sections.title_en: The story of another Jewish king who endeavoured to destroy the religion of Jesus

entries.farsi: بعد از اين خون‏ريز درمان‏ناپذير / كاندر افتاد از بالى آن وزير
entries.english: After this irremediable bloodshed which befell through the affliction by the vizier,

entries.farsi: يك شه ديگر ز نسل آن جهود / در هالك قوم عيسى رو نمود
entries.english: Another king, of the progeny of that Jew, addressed himself to the destruction of the people of Jesus.

entries.farsi: گر خبر خواهى از اين ديگر خروج / سوره بر خوان و السما ذات البروج‏
entries.english: If you desire information about this second outbreak, read the chapter of the Qur’an: By Heaven which has the signs.

entries.farsi: سنت بد كز شه اول بزاد / اين شه ديگر قدم بر وى نهاد
entries.english: This second king set foot in the evil way that was originated by the former king.

entries.farsi: هر كه او بنهاد ناخوش سنتى / سوى او نفرين رود هر ساعتى‏
entries.english: Whoever establishes an evil traddtion, towards him goes malediction every hour.

entries.farsi: نيكوان رفتند و سنتها بماند / وز لئيمان ظلم و لعنتها بماند
entries.english: The righteous departed and their ways remained, and from the vile there remained injustice and execrations.

entries.farsi: تا قيامت هر كه جنس آن بدان / در وجود آيد بود رويش بدان‏
entries.english: Until the Resurrection, the face of every congener of those wicked men who comes into existence is turned towards that one.

entries.farsi: رگ رگ است اين آب شيرين و آب شور / رگ رگ است اين آب شيرين و آب شور
entries.english: Vein by vein is this sweet water and bitter water, flowing in creatures until the blast of the trumpet.

entries.farsi: نيكوان را هست ميراث از خوش‏آب / آن چه ميراث است أَوْرَثْنَا الكتاب‏
entries.english: To the righteous is the inheritance of the sweet water. What is that inheritance? We have caused to inherit the Book.

entries.farsi: شد نياز طالبان ار بنگرى / شعله‏ها از گوهر پيغمبرى‏
entries.english: If you will consider, the supplications of the seekers are rays from the substance of prophethood.

entries.farsi: شعله‏ها با گوهران گردان بود / شعله آن جانب رود هم كان بود
entries.english: The rays are circling with the substances: the ray goes in the direction where that is.

entries.farsi: نور روزن گرد خانه مى‏دود / ز آنكه خور برجى به برجى مى‏رود
entries.english: The window-gleam runs round the house, because the sun goes from sign to sign of the zodiac.

entries.farsi: هر كه را با اخترى پيوستگى است / مر و را با اختر خود هم تگى است‏
entries.english: Any one who has affinity with a star has a concurrence with his star.

entries.farsi: طالعش گر زهره باشد در طرب / ميل كلى دارد و عشق و طلب‏
entries.english: If his ascendant star be Venus, his whole inclination and love and desire is for joy;

entries.farsi: ور بود مريخى خون‏ريز خو / جنگ و بهتان و خصومت جويد او
entries.english: And if he be one born under Mars, one whose nature is to shed blood, he seeks war and malignity and enmity.

entries.farsi: اخترانند از وراى اختران / كه احتراق و نحس نبود اندر آن‏
entries.english: Beyond the stars are stars in which is no conflagration or sinister aspect,

entries.farsi: سايران در آسمانهاى دگر / غير اين هفت آسمان معتبر
entries.english: Moving in other heavens, not these seven heavens known to all,

entries.farsi: راسخان در تاب انوار خدا / نى بهم پيوسته نى از هم جدا
entries.english: Immanent in the radiance of the light of God, neither joined to each other nor separate from each other.

entries.farsi: هر كه باشد طالع او ز آن نجوم / نفس او كفار سوزد در رجوم‏
entries.english: When any one’s ascendant is those stars, his soul burns the infidels in driving off.

entries.farsi: خشم مريخى نباشد خشم او / منقلب رو غالب و مغلوب خو
entries.english: His anger is not the anger of the man born under Mars — perverse, and of such nature that it is dominant and dominated.

entries.farsi: نور غالب ايمن از نقص و غسق / در ميان اصبعين نور حق‏
entries.english: The dominant light is secure from defect and dimness between the two fingers of the Light of God.

entries.farsi: حق فشاند آن نور را بر جانها / مقبالن برداشته دامانها
entries.english: God has scattered that light over spirits, the fortunate have held up their skirts;

entries.farsi: و آن نثار نور را وايافته / روى از غير خدا بر تافته‏
entries.english: And he, having gained that scattered largesse of light, has turned his face away from all except God.

entries.farsi: جزوها را رويها سوى كل است / بلبالن را عشق با روى گل است‏
entries.english: Whosoever has lacked a skirt of love is left without share in that scattered largesse of light.

entries.farsi: هر كه را دامان عشقى نابده / ز آن نثار نور بى‏بهره شده‏
entries.english: The faces of particulars are set towards the universal: nightingales play the game of love with the rose.

entries.farsi: گاو را رنگ از برون و مرد را / از درون جو رنگ سرخ و زرد را
entries.english: The ox has his colour outside, but in man’s case seek the red and yellow hues within.

entries.farsi: رنگهاى نيك از خم صفاست / رنگ زشتان از سياه‏آبه‏ى جفاست‏
entries.english: The good colours are from the vat of purity; the colour of the wicked is from the black water of iniquity.

entries.farsi: صِ بْغَةَ اللهَِّ نام آن رنگ لطيف / لَعْنَةُ اللهَِّ بوى اين رنگ كثيف‏
entries.english: The baptism of God is the name of that subtle colour; the curse of God is the smell of that gross colour.

entries.farsi: آن چه از دريا به دريا مى‏رود / از همانجا كامد آن جا مى‏رود
entries.english: That which is of the sea is going to the sea: it is going to the same place where it came

entries.farsi: از سر كه سيلهاى تيز رو / وز تن ما جان عشق آميز رو
entries.english: From the mountain-top the swift-rushing torrents, and from our body the soul whose motion is mingled with love.

sections.title_fa: ‏آتش كردن پادشاه جهود و بت نهادن پهلوى آتش كه هر كه اين بت را سجود كند از آتش برست
sections.title_en: How the self reliant king made a fire and placed an idol beside it, saying, “Whoever bows down to this idol shall escape the fire.”

entries.farsi: آن جهود سگ ببين چه راى كرد / پهلوى آتش بتى بر پاى كرد
entries.english: Now see what a plan this currish Jew contrived! He set up an idol beside the fire,

entries.farsi: كان كه اين بت را سجود آرد برست / ور نيارد در دل آتش نشست‏
entries.english: Saying, “He that bows down to this idol is saved, and if he bows not, he shall sit in the heart of the fire.”

entries.farsi: چون سزاى اين بت نفس او نداد / از بت نفسش بتى ديگر بزاد
entries.english: Inasmuch as he did not give due punishment to this idol of nafs, from the idol of his self the other idol was born.

entries.farsi: مادر بتها بت نفس شماست / ز آن كه آن بت مار و اين بت اژدهاست‏
entries.english: The idol of your nafs is the mother of idols, because that idol is a snake, while this idol is a dragon.

entries.farsi: آهن و سنگ است نفس و بت شرار / آن شرار از آب مى‏گيرد قرار
entries.english: The nafs is iron and stone, while the idol is the sparks: those sparks are quieted by water.

entries.farsi: بت سياه‏آبه‏ست در كوزه نهان / نفس مر آب سيه را چشمه دان‏
entries.english: How should the stone and iron be allayed by water? How should a man, having these two, be secure?

entries.farsi: سنگ و آهن ز آب كى ساكن‏شود / آدمى با اين دو كى ايمن شود
entries.english: The idol is the black water in a jug; the nafs is a fountain for the black water.

entries.farsi: آن بت منحوت چون سيل سياه / نفس بتگر چشمه‏اى بر آب راه‏
entries.english: That sculptured idol is like the black torrent; the idol-making self is a fountain full of water for it.

entries.farsi: صد سبو را بشكند يك پاره سنگ / و آب چشمه مى‏زهاند بى‏درنگ‏
entries.english: A single piece of stone will break a hundred pitchers, but the fountain is jetting forth water incessantly.

entries.farsi: بت شكستن سهل باشد نيك سهل / سهل ديدن نفس را جهل است جهل‏
entries.english: It is easy to break an idol, very easy; to regard the nafs as easily broken is folly, folly.

entries.farsi: صورت نفس ار بجويى اى پسر / قصه‏ى دوزخ بخوان با هفت در
entries.english: O son, if you seek the form of the self, read the story of Hell with its seven gates.

entries.farsi: هر نفس مكرى و در هر مكر ز آن / غرقه صد فرعون با فرعونيان‏
entries.english: Every moment an act of deceit, and in every one of those deceits a hundred Pharaohs are drowned together with their followers.

entries.farsi: در خداى موسى و موسى گريز / آب ايمان را ز فرعونى مريز
entries.english: Flee to the God of Moses and to Moses, do not from Pharaoh’s quality spill the water of the Faith.

entries.farsi: دست را اندر احد و احمد بزن / اى برادر واره از بو جهل تن‏
entries.english: Lay your hand on the One and Ahmad! O brother, escape from the Bu Jahl of the body!

sections.title_fa: به سخن آمدن طفل در ميان آتش و تحريض كردن خلق را در افتادن به آتش
sections.title_en: How a child began to speak amidst the fire and urged the people to throw themselves into the fire.

entries.farsi: يك زنى با طفل آورد آن جهود / پيش آن بت و آتش اندر شعله بود
entries.english: That Jew brought to that idol a woman with her child and the fire was blazing.

entries.farsi: طفل از او بستد در آتش در فكند / زن بترسيد و دل از ايمان بكند
entries.english: He took the child from her and cast it into the fire: the woman was affrighted and withdrew her heart from her faith.

entries.farsi: خواست تا او سجده آرد پيش بت / بانگ زد آن طفل إني لم أمت‏
entries.english: She was about to bow down before the idol the child cried, “Truly, I am not dead.

entries.farsi: اندر آ اى مادر اينجا من خوشم / گر چه در صورت ميان آتشم‏
entries.english: Come in, O mother: I am happy here, although in appearance I am amidst the fire.

entries.farsi: چشم بند است آتش از بهر حجاب / رحمت است اين سر بر آورده ز جيب‏
entries.english: The fire is a spell that binds the eye for the sake of screening; this is a Divine mercy which has raised its head from the collar.

entries.farsi: اندر آ مادر ببين برهان حق / تا ببينى عشرت خاصان حق
entries.english: Come in, mother, and see the evidence of God, that you may behold the delight of God’s elect.

entries.farsi: اندر آ و آب بين آتش مثال / از جهانى كاتش است آبش مثال‏
entries.english: Come in, and see water that has the semblance of fire; from a world which is fire and has the semblance of water.

entries.farsi: اندر آ اسرار ابراهيم بين / كاو در آتش يافت سرو و ياسمين‏
entries.english: Come in, and see the mysteries of Abraham, who in the fire found roses and jasmine.

entries.farsi: مرگ مى‏ديدم گه زادن ز تو / سخت خوفم بود افتادن ز تو
entries.english: I was seeing death at the time of birth from you: sore was my dread of falling from you;

entries.farsi: چون بزادم رستم از زندان تنگ / در جهان خوش هواى خوب رنگ
entries.english: When I was born, I escaped from the narrow prison into a world of pleasant air and beautiful colour.

entries.farsi: من جهان را چون رحم ديدم كنون / چون در اين آتش بديدم اين سكون‏
entries.english: Now I deem the world to be like the womb, since in this fire I have seen such rest:

entries.farsi: اندر اين آتش بديدم عالمى / چون در اين آتش بديدم اين سكون‏
entries.english: In this fire I have seen a world wherein every atom possesses the breath of Jesus.

entries.farsi: نك جهان نيست شكل هست ذات / و آن جهان هست شكل بى‏ثبات‏
entries.english: Lo, a world apparently non-existent essentially existent, while that world is apparently existent has no permanence.

entries.farsi: اندر آ مادر به حق مادرى / بين كه اين آذر ندارد آذرى‏
entries.english: Come in, mother, by the right of motherhood: see this fire, how it has no fieriness.

entries.farsi: اندر آ مادر كه اقبال آمده ست / اندر آ مادر مده دولت ز دست‏
entries.english: Come in, mother, for felicity is come; come in, mother, do not let fortune slip from your hand.

entries.farsi: قدرت آن سگ بديدى اندر / تا ببينى قدرت و لطف خدا
entries.english: You have seen the power of that cur: come in, that you may see the power of God’s grace.

entries.farsi: من ز رحمت مى‏كشانم پاى تو / كز طرب خود نيستم پرواى تو
entries.english: It is out of pity that I am drawing your feet, for indeed such is my rapture that I have no care for you.

entries.farsi: اندر آ و ديگران را هم بخوان / كاندر آتش شاه بنهاده ست خوان‏
entries.english: Come in and call the others also, for the King has spread a table within the fire.

entries.farsi: اندر آييد اى مسلمانان همه / غير عذب دين عذاب است آن همه‏
entries.english: O true believers, come in, all of you: except this sweetness (‘adhbi) all is torment (‘adhab).

entries.farsi: اندر آييد اى همه پروانه‏وار / اندر اين بهره كه دارد صد بهار
entries.english: Oh, come in, all of you, like moths; into this fortune which has a hundred spring times.”

entries.farsi: بانگ مى‏زد در ميان آن گروه / بانگ مى‏زد در ميان آن گروه
entries.english: He was crying amidst that multitude: the souls of the people were filled with awe.

entries.farsi: خلق خود را بعد از آن بى‏خويشتن / مى‏فگندند اندر آتش مرد و زن‏
entries.english: After that, the folk, men and women, cast themselves unwittingly into the fire

entries.farsi: بى‏موكل بى‏كشش از عشق دوست / ز آن كه شيرين كردن هر تلخ از اوست‏
entries.english: Without custodian, without being dragged, for love of the Friend, because from Him is the sweetening of every bitter-ness—

entries.farsi: آن يهودى شد سيه رو و خجل / شد پشيمان زين سبب بيمار دل‏
entries.english: Until it came to pass that the myrmidons were holding back the people, saying, “Do not enter the fire!”

entries.farsi: تا چنان شد كان عوانان خلق را / منع مى‏كردند كاتش در ميا
entries.english: The Jew became black-faced and dismayed; he became sorry and sick at heart,

entries.farsi: كاندر ايمان خلق عاشق‏تر شدند / در فناى جسم صادق‏تر شدند
entries.english: Because the people grew more loving in their Faith and more firm in mortification of the body

entries.farsi: مكر شيطان هم در او پيچيد شكر / ديو هم خود را سيه رو ديد شكر
entries.english: Thanks be to God, the Devil’s plot caught him in its toils; Thanks be to God, the Devil saw himself disgraced

entries.farsi: آن چه مى‏ماليد در روى كسان / جمع شد در چهره‏ى آن ناكس آن‏
entries.english: That which he was rubbing on the faces of those persons was all accumulated on the visage of that vile wretch.

entries.farsi: آن كه مى‏دريد جامه‏ى خلق چست / شد دريده آن او ايشان درست‏
entries.english: He who was busy rending the garment of the people -- his own was rent, they were unhurt.

sections.title_fa: كج ماندن دهان آن مرد كه نام محمد را عليه السلام به تسخر خواند
sections.title_en: How the mouth remained awry of a man who pronounced the name of Mohammed, on whom be peace, derisively.

entries.farsi: آن دهان كژ كرد و از تسخر بخواند / مر محمد را دهانش كژ بماند
entries.english: He made his mouth wry and called the name of Ahmad in derision: his mouth remained awry.

entries.farsi: باز آمد كاى محمد عفو كن / اى ترا الطاف و علم من لدن‏
entries.english: He came back, saying, “Pardon me, O Mohammed, O you to whom belong the gifts of esoteric knowledge.

entries.farsi: من ترا افسوس مى‏كردم ز جهل / من بدم افسوس را منسوب و اهل‏
entries.english: In my folly I was ridiculing you, I myself was related to ridicule and deserving it.’’

entries.farsi: چون خدا خواهد كه پرده‏ى كس درد / ميلش اندر طعنه‏ى پاكان برد
entries.english: When God wishes to rend the veil of any ore, He turns his inclination towards reviling holy men.

entries.farsi: چون خدا خواهد كه پوشد عيب كس / كم زند در عيب معيوبان نفس‏
entries.english: When God wishes to hide the blame of any one, he does not breathe a word of blame against the blameworthy.

entries.farsi: چون خدا خواهد كه‏مان يارى كند / ميل ما را جانب زارى كند
entries.english: When God wishes to help us, He turns our inclination towards humble lament.

entries.farsi: اى خنك چشمى كه آن گريان اوست / وى همايون دل كه آن بريان اوست‏
entries.english: Oh, happy the eye that is weeping for His sake! Oh, fortunate the heart that is seared for His sake!

entries.farsi: آخر هر گريه آخر خنده‏اى است / مرد آخر بين مبارك بنده‏اى است‏
entries.english: The end of every weeping is laughter at last; the man who foresees the end is a blessed servant.

entries.farsi: هر كجا آب روان سبزه بود / هر كجا اشك روان رحمت شود
entries.english: Wherever is flowing water, there is greenery: wherever are running tears, mercy is shown.

entries.farsi: باش چون دوالب نالان چشم تر / تا ز صحن جانت بر رويد خضر
entries.english: Be moaning and moist-eyed like the water-wheel that green herbs may spring up from the courtyard of your soul.

entries.farsi: اشك خواهى رحم كن بر اشك بار / رحم خواهى بر ضعيفان رحم آر
entries.english: If you desire tears, have mercy on one who sheds tears; if you desire mercy, show mercy to the weak.

sections.title_fa: عتاب كردن آتش را آن پادشاه جهود
sections.title_en: How the fire reproached the Self Reliant king.

entries.farsi: رو به آتش كرد شه كاى تند خو / آن جهان سوز طبيعى خوت كو
entries.english: The king turned his face to the fire, saying, “O fierce-tempered one, where is your world-consuming natural disposition?

entries.farsi: مى‏نبخشايى تو بر آتش پرست / آن كه نپرستد ترا او چون برست‏
entries.english: How art you not burning? What has become of your specific property? Or has your intention changed because of our fortune?

entries.farsi: چون نمى‏سوزى چه شد خاصيتت / يا ز بخت ما دگر شد نيتت‏
entries.english: You have no pity on the fire-worshipper: how has he been saved who does not worship you?

entries.farsi: هرگز اى آتش تو صابر نيستى / چون نسوزى چيست قادر نيستى‏
