entries.english: Never, O fire, are you patient: How do you not burn? What is it? Have you not the power?

entries.farsi: چشم بند است اين عجب يا هوش بند / چون نسوزاند چنين شعله‏ى بلند
entries.english: Is this a spell, I wonder, that binds the eye or the mind? How does the lofty pyre not burn?

entries.farsi: جادويى كردت كسى يا سيمياست / يا خالف طبع تو از بخت ماست‏
entries.english: Has some one bewitched you? Or is it magic, or is your unnatural behaviour’ from our fortune?”

entries.farsi: گفت آتش من همانم اى شمن / اندر آ تو تا ببينى تاب من‏
entries.english: The fire said: “ I am the same, I am fire: come in, that you may feel my heat.

entries.farsi: طبع من ديگر نگشت و عنصرم / تيغ حقم هم به دستورى برم‏
entries.english: My nature and element have not changed: I am the sword of God and by His Destur I cut.

entries.farsi: بر در خرگه سگان تركمان / چاپلوسى كرده پيش ميهمان‏
entries.english: The Turcoman dogs fawn at the tent-door before the guest,

entries.farsi: ور به خرگه بگذرد بيگانه رو / حمله بيند از سگان شيرانه او
entries.english: but if any one having the face of a stranger pass by the tent; he will see the dogs rushing at him like lions.

entries.farsi: من ز سگ كم نيستم در بندگى / كم ز تركى نيست حق در زندگى‏
entries.english: I am not less than a dog in devotion, nor is God less than a Turcoman in life.”

entries.farsi: آتش طبعت اگر غمگين كند / سوزش از امر مليك دين كند
entries.english: If the fire of your nature makes you suffer pain, it burns by command of the Lord of Judgment;

entries.farsi: آتش طبعت اگر شادى دهد / اندر او شادى مليك دين نهد
entries.english: If the fire of your nature give you joy, the Lord of the Way puts joy within.

entries.farsi: چون كه غم بينى تو استغفار كن / غم به امر خالق آمد كار كن‏
entries.english: When you feel pain, ask pardon of God: pain, by command of the Creator, is efficacious.

entries.farsi: باد و خاك و آب و آتش بنده‏اند / با من و تو مرده با حق زنده‏اند
entries.english: When He pleases, pain itself becomes joy; bondage itself becomes freedom

entries.farsi: چون بخواهد عين غم شادى شود / عين بند پاى، آزادى شود
entries.english: Air and earth and water and fire are slaves: with you and me they are dead, but with God they are alive.

entries.farsi: پيش حق آتش هميشه در قيام / همچو عاشق روز و شب پيچان مدام‏
entries.english: Before God, fire is always standing, writhing continually day and night, like a lover.

entries.farsi: سنگ بر آهن زنى بيرون جهد / هم به امر حق قدم بيرون نهد
entries.english: If you strike stone on iron, it leaps out: it is by God’s command that it puts forth its foot.

entries.farsi: آهن و سنگ ستم بر هم مزن / كاين دو مى‏زايند همچون مرد و زن‏
entries.english: Do not strike together the iron and stone of injustice, for these two generate like man and woman.

entries.farsi: سنگ و آهن خود سبب آمد و ليك / تو به بالاتر نگر اى مرد نيك‏
entries.english: The stone and the iron are indeed causes, but look higher, O good man!

entries.farsi: كاين سبب را آن سبب آورد پيش / بى‏سبب كى شد سبب هرگز ز خويش
entries.english: For this cause was produced by that cause: when did a cause ever proceed from itself without a cause?

entries.farsi: و آن سببها كانبيا را رهبر است / آن سببها زين سببها برتر است‏
entries.english: And those causes which guide the prophets on their way are higher than these causes.

entries.farsi: اين سبب را آن سبب عامل كند / باز گاهى بى‏بر و عاطل كند
entries.english: That cause makes this cause operative; sometimes, again, it makes it fruitless and ineffectual.

entries.farsi: اين سبب را محرم آمد عقلها / و آن سببها راست محرم انبيا
entries.english: Minds are familiar with this cause, but the prophets are familiar with those causes.

entries.farsi: اين سبب چه بود به تازى گو رسن / اندر اين چه اين رسن آمد به فن‏
entries.english: What is this “cause” (sabab) in Arabic? Say: “cord” (rasan). This cord came into this well by artifice.

entries.farsi: گردش چرخه رسن را علت است / چرخه گردان را نديدن زلت است‏
entries.english: The revolution of the water-wheel causes the cord, not to see the mover of the water-wheel is an error.

entries.farsi: اين رسنهاى سببها در جهان / هان و هان زين چرخ سر گردان مدان‏
entries.english: Beware, beware! Do not regard these cords of causation in the world as from the giddy wheel,

entries.farsi: تا نمانى صفر و سر گردان چو چرخ / تا نسوزى تو ز بى‏مغزى چو مرخ‏
entries.english: Lest you remain empty and giddy like the wheel, lest through brainlessness you burn like markh wood.

entries.farsi: باد آتش مى‏خورد از امر حق / هر دو سر مست آمدند از خمر حق
entries.english: By the command of God air becomes fire: both are drunk with the wine of God.

entries.farsi: آب حلم و آتش خشم اى پسر / هم ز حق بينى چو بگشايى بصر
entries.english: O son, when you open your eyes you will see that from God too are the water of clemency and the fire of anger.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى واقف از حق جان باد / هود گرد مومنان خطى كشيد
entries.english: Had not the soul of the wind been informed by God, how would it have distinguished amongst the people of ‘Ad?

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى باد كه در عهد هود عليه السلام قوم عاد را هالك كرد
sections.title_en: The story of the wind which destroyed the people of ‘Ad in the time of (the prophet) Hud, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: نرم مى‏شد باد كانجا مى‏رسيد / هر كه بيرون بود ز آن خط جمله را
entries.english: Hud drew a line round the believers: the wind would become soft when it reached that place,

entries.farsi: پاره پاره مى‏گسست اندر هوا / همچنين شيبان راعى مى‏كشيد
entries.english: It was dashing to pieces in the, air all who were outside of the line.

entries.farsi: گرد بر گرد رمه خطى پديد / چون به جمعه مى‏شد او وقت نماز
entries.english: Likewise Shayban the shepherd used to draw a visible line round his flock

entries.farsi: تا نيارد گرگ آن جا ترك تاز / هيچ گرگى در نرفتى اندر آن
entries.english: Whenever he went to the Friday service at prayer-time, in order that the wolf might not raid and ravage there:

entries.farsi: گوسفندى هم نگشتى ز آن نشان‏ / باد حرص گرگ و حرص گوسفند
entries.english: No wolf would go into that, nor would any sheep stray beyond that mark ;

entries.farsi: دايره‏ى مرد خدا را بود بند / همچنين باد اجل با عارفان
entries.english: The wind of the wolf ’s and sheep’s strong desire was barred because of the circle of the man of God.

entries.farsi: نرم و خوش همچون نسيم يوسفان‏ / آتش ابراهيم را دندان نزد
entries.english: Even so, to those who know God (‘arifan) the wind of Death is soft and pleasant as the breeze of ones like Joseph.

entries.farsi: چون گزيده‏ى حق بود چونش گزد / ز آتش شهوت نسوزد اهل دين
entries.english: The fire did not set its teeth in Abraham: how should it bite him, since he is the chosen of God?

entries.farsi: باقيان را برده تا قعر زمين‏ / موج دريا چون به امر حق بتاخت
entries.english: The religious were not afflicted by the fire of lust which bore all the rest down to the bottom of the earth.

entries.farsi: اهل موسى را ز قبطى واشناخت‏ / خاك قارون را چو فرمان در رسيد
entries.english: The waves of the sea, when they charged on by God’s command, discriminated the people of Moses from the Egyptians.

entries.farsi: با زر و تختش به قعر خود كشيد / آب و گل چون از دم عيسى چريد
entries.english: The earth, when the command came, drew Qarun with his gold and throne into its lowest depth.

entries.farsi: بال و پر بگشاد مرغى شد پريد / هست تسبيحت بخار آب و گل
entries.english: The water and clay, when it fed on the breath of Jesus, spread wings and pinions, became a bird, and flew.

entries.farsi: مرغ جنت شد ز نفخ صدق دل‏ / كوه طور از نور موسى شد به رقص
entries.english: Your glorification is an exhalation from the water and clay: it became a bird of Paradise through the breathing of your heart’s sincerity.

entries.farsi: صوفى كامل شد و رست او ز نقص‏ / چه عجب گر كوه صوفى شد عزيز
entries.english: Mount Sinai, from the radiance of Moses, began to dance, became a perfect Sufi, and was freed from blemish.

entries.farsi: جسم موسى از كلوخى بود نيز / اين عجايب ديد آن شاه جهود
entries.english: What wonder if the mountain became a venerable Sufi? The body of Moses also was from a piece of clay.

sections.title_fa: ‏طنز و انكار كردن پادشاه جهود و قبول نكردن نصيحت خاصان خويش
sections.title_en: How Self Reliant king scoffed and denied and would not accept

entries.farsi: جز كه طنز و جز كه انكارش نبود / ناصحان گفتند از حد مگذران
entries.english: the counsel of his intimates

entries.farsi: مركب استيزه را چندين مران‏ / ناصحان را دست بست و بند كرد
entries.english: The king of the Jews beheld these marvellous things; he had nothing except mockery and denial.

entries.farsi: ظلم را پيوند در پيوند كرد / بانگ آمد كار چون اينجا رسيد
entries.english: His counsellors said, “Do not let go beyond bounds, do not drive the steed of obstinacy so far.”

entries.farsi: پاى دار اى سگ كه قهر ما رسيد / بعد از آن آتش چهل گز بر فروخت
entries.english: He handcuffed the counsellors and confined them; he committed one injustice after another.

entries.farsi: حلقه گشت و آن جهودان را بسوخت‏ / اصل ايشان بود آتش ابتدا
entries.english: When the matter reached this pass, a shout came— “Hold your foot, O cur! For Our vengeance is come.”

entries.farsi: سوى اصل خويش رفتند انتها / هم ز آتش زاده بودند آن فريق
entries.english: After that, the fire blazed up forty feet high, became a ring, and consumed those Jews.

entries.farsi: جزوها را سوى كل باشد طريق / آتشى بودند مومن سوز و بس
entries.english: From fire was their origin in the beginning: they went to their origin in the end.

entries.farsi: سوخت خود را آتش ايشان چو خس / آن كه بوده ست امه الهاويه
entries.english: That company was born of fire: the way of particulars is towards the universal.

entries.farsi: هاويه آمد مر او را زاويه‏ / مادر فرزند جويان وى است
entries.english: They were only a fire to consume the true believers: their fire consumed itself like rubbish.

entries.farsi: اصلها مر فرعها را در پى است‏ / آب اندر حوض اگر زندانى است
entries.english: He whose mother is Hawiya (Hell-fire)— Hawiya shall become his zavieh.

entries.farsi: باد نشفش مى‏كند كار كانى است‏ / مى‏رهاند مى‏برد تا معدنش
entries.english: The mother of the child is seeking it: the fundamentals pursue the derivatives.

entries.farsi: اندك اندك تا نبينى بردنش‏ / وين نفس جانهاى ما را همچنان
entries.english: If water is imprisoned in a tank, the wind sucks it up, for it belongs to the original:

entries.farsi: اندك اندك دزدد از حبس جهان‏ / تا إليه يصعد أطياب الكلم
entries.english: It sets it free; it wafts it away to its source, little by little, so that you do not see its wafting;

entries.farsi: صاعدا منا إلى حيث علم‏ / ترتقي أنفاسنا بالمنتقى
entries.english: And our souls likewise this breath steals away, little by little, from the prison of the world.

entries.farsi: متحفا منا إلى دار البقا / ثم تاتينا مكافات المقال
entries.english: The perfumes of our words ascend even unto Him, ascending from us whither God knows.
Our breaths soar up with the choice, as a gift from us, to the abode of everlastingness;

entries.farsi: ضعف ذاك رحمة من ذى الجلال‏ / ثم يلجينا الى امثالها
entries.english: Then comes to us the recompense of our speech, a double thereof, as a mercy from the Glorious;

entries.farsi: كى ينال العبد مما نالها / هكذا تعرج و تنزل دايما
entries.english: Then He causes us to repair to good words like those, that His servant may obtain of what he has obtained.

entries.farsi: ذا فال زلت عليه قائما / پارسى گوييم يعنى اين كشش
entries.english: Thus do they ascend while it descends continually: may you never cease to keep up that!

entries.farsi: ز آن طرف آيد كه آمد آن چشش‏ / چشم هر قومى به سويى مانده است
entries.english: Let us speak Persian: the meaning is that this attraction comes from the same quarter as that savour.

entries.farsi: كان طرف يك روز ذوقى رانده است‏ / ذوق جنس از جنس خود باشد يقين
entries.english: The eves of every set of people remain in the direction where one day they satisfied a delight.

entries.farsi: ذوق جزو از كل خود باشد ببين‏ / يا مگر آن قابل جنسى بود
entries.english: Every kind of delight is certainly in its own kind: the delight of the part, observe, is in its whole;

entries.farsi: چون بدو پيوست جنس او شود / همچو آب و نان كه جنس ما نبود
entries.english: Or else, that part s surely capable of a attachment to another kind and, when it has attached itself, becomes homogeneous with it,

entries.farsi: گشت جنس ما و اندر ما فزود / نقش جنسيت ندارد آب و نان
entries.english: As water and bread, which were not our congeners, became homogeneous with us and increased within us.

entries.farsi: ز اعتبار آخر آن را جنس دان‏ / ور ز غير جنس باشد ذوق ما
entries.english: Water and bread have not the appearance of being our congeners, from consideration of the end deem them to be homogeneous.

entries.farsi: آن مگر مانند باشد جنس را / آن كه مانند است باشد عاريت
entries.english: And if our delight is from something not homogeneous, that will surely resemble the congener.

entries.farsi: عاريت باقى نماند عاقبت‏ / مرغ را گر ذوق آيد از صفير
entries.english: That which bears a resemblance is a loan: a loan is impermanent in the end.

entries.farsi: چون كه جنس خود نيابد شد نفير / تشنه را گر ذوق آيد از سراب
entries.english: Although the bird is delighted by a whistle, it takes fright when it does not find its own congener.

entries.farsi: چون رسد در وى گريزد جويد آب‏ / مفلسان هم خوش شوند از زر قلب
entries.english: Although the thirsty man is delighted by the mirage, he runs away when he comes up to it, seeking water.

entries.farsi: ليك آن رسوا شود در دار ضرب‏ / تا زر اندوديت از ره نفگند
entries.english: Although the insolvent are pleased with base gold, yet that is put to shame in the mint.

entries.farsi: تا خيال كژ ترا چه نفگند / از كليله باز جو آن قصه را
entries.english: Lest imposture cast you out of the way, lest false imagination cast you into the well.

entries.farsi: و اندر آن قصه طلب كن حصه را / طايفه‏ى نخجير در وادى خوش
entries.english: Seek the story from Kalila and Dimna, and search out the moral in the story.

sections.title_fa: بيان توكل و ترك جهد گفتن نخجيران به شير
sections.title_en: Setting forth how the beasts of chase told the lion to trust in God and cease from exerting himself.

entries.farsi: بودشان از شير دايم كش مكش‏ / بس كه آن شير از كمين درمى‏ربود
entries.english: A number of beasts of chase in a pleasant valley were harassed by a lion.

entries.farsi: آن چرا بر جمله ناخوش گشته بود / حيله كردند آمدند ايشان بشير
entries.english: Inasmuch as the lion was from ambush and carrying them away, that pasturage had become unpleasant to them all.

entries.farsi: كز وظيفه ما ترا داريم سير / بعد از اين اندر پى صيدى ميا
entries.english: They made a plot: they came to the lion, saving, “We will keep you full-fed by means of an allowance.

entries.farsi: تا نگردد تلخ بر ما اين گيا
entries.english: Do not go after any prey beyond your allowance, in order that this grass may not become bitter to us.”

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن شير نخجيران را و فايده‏ى جهد گفتن
sections.title_en: How the lion answered the beasts and explained

entries.farsi: گفت آرى گر وفا بينم نه مكر / مكرها بس ديده‏ام از زيد و بكر
entries.english: the advantage of exertion.

entries.farsi: من هالك فعل و مكر مردمم / من گزيده‏ى زخم مار و كژدمم‏
entries.english: “Yes,” said he, “if I see good faith, not fraud, for often have I seen frauds from Zayd and Bakr.

entries.farsi: مردم نفس از درونم در كمين / از همه مردم بتر در مكر و كين‏
entries.english: I am done to death by the cunning and fraud of men; I am bitten by the sting of snake and scorpion;

entries.farsi: گوش من ال يلدغ المؤمن شنيد / قول پيغمبر به جان و دل گزيد
entries.english: Worse than all men in fraud and spite is the man of the nafs lying in wait within me.

entries.farsi: جمله گفتند اى حكيم با خبر / الحذر دع ليس يغنى عن قدر
entries.english: My ear heard ‘The believer is not bitten,’ and adopted saying of the Prophet with heart and soul.”

sections.title_fa: ترجيح نهادن نخجيران توكل را بر جهد و اكتساب
sections.title_en: How the beasts asserted the superiority of trust in God to exertion and acquisition.

entries.farsi: در حذر شوريدن شور و شر است / رو توكل كن توكل بهتر است‏
entries.english: They all said: “O knowing sage, let precaution alone: it is of no avail against the Divine decree.
In precaution is the embroilment of broil and woe: go, put your trust in God: trust in God is better.

entries.farsi: با قضا پنجه مزن اى تند و تيز / تا نگيرد هم قضا با تو ستيز
entries.english: Do not grapple with Destiny, O fierce and furious one, lest Destiny also pick a quarrel with you.

entries.farsi: مرده بايد بود پيش حكم حق / تا نيايد زخم از رب الفلق‏
entries.english: One must be dead in presence of the decree of God, so that no blow may come from the Lord of the daybreak.”

sections.title_fa: ‏ترجيح نهادن شير جهد و اكتساب را بر توكل و تسليم
sections.title_en: How the lion upheld the superiority of exertion and acquisition to trust in God and resignation.

entries.farsi: گفت آرى گر توكل رهبر است / اين سبب هم سنت پيغمبر است‏
entries.english: “Yes,” he said; if trust in God is the guide, the means too is the Prophet’s tradition (sunna).

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر به آواز بلند / با توكل زانوى اشتر ببند
entries.english: The Prophet said with a loud voice, ‘While trusting in God, bind the knee of your camel.’

entries.farsi: رمز الكاسب حبيب اللهَّ شنو / از توكل در سبب كاهل مشو
entries.english: Hearken to the signification of ‘The earner is beloved of God’: through trusting in God do not become neglectful as to the means.”

sections.title_fa: ترجيح نهادن نخجيران توكل را بر اجتهاد
sections.title_en: How the beasts preferred trust in God to exertion.

entries.farsi: قوم گفتندش كه كسب از ضعف خلق / لقمه‏ى تزوير دان بر قدر حلق‏
entries.english: The party answered him, saying, “Regard acquisition, arising from the infirmity of creatures, as a mouthful of deceit proportionate to the size of the gullet.

entries.farsi: نيست كسبى از توكل خوبتر / چيست از تسليم خود محبوبتر
entries.english: There is no work better than trust in God: what, indeed, is dearer than resignation?

entries.farsi: بس گريزند از بال سوى بال / بس جهند از مار سوى اژدها
entries.english: Often do they flee from affliction to affliction; often do they recoil from the snake to the dragon.

entries.farsi: حيله كرد انسان و حيله‏ش دام بود / آن كه جان پنداشت خون آشام بود
entries.english: Man devised, and his device was a snare: that which he thought to be life was the drainer of his blood.

entries.farsi: در ببست و دشمن اندر خانه بود / حيله‏ى فرعون زين افسانه بود
entries.english: He locked the door while the foe was in the house: the plot of Pharaoh was a story of this sort.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران طفل كشت آن كينه كش / و آن كه او مى‏جست اندر خانه‏اش‏
entries.english: That vengeful man slew hundreds of thousands of babes, while the one he was searching after was in his house.

entries.farsi: ديده‏ى ما چون بسى علت در اوست / ديد ما را ديد او نعم العوض
entries.english: Since in our eyesight there is much defect, go, let your own sight pass away (fana) in the sight of the Friend.

entries.farsi: يابى اندر ديد او كل غرض‏ / طفل تا گيرا و تا پويا نبود
entries.english: His sight for ours—what a goodly recompense! In His sight you will find the whole object of your desire.

entries.farsi: مركبش جز گردن بابا نبود / چون فضولى گشت و دست و پا نمود
entries.english: So long as the child could neither grasp nor run, he had nothing to ride on but his father’s neck;

entries.farsi: در عنا افتاد و در كور و كبود / جانهاى خلق پيش از دست و پا
entries.english: When he became a busybody and plied hand and foot, he fell into trouble and wretchedness.

entries.farsi: مى‏پريدند از وفا اندر صفا / چون به امر اهْبِطُ وا بندى شدند
entries.english: The spirits of created beings, before hand and foot, by reason of their faithfulness were flying in purity;

entries.farsi: حبس خشم و حرص و خرسندى شدند / ما عيال حضرتيم و شير خواه
entries.english: When they were constrained by the command, Get ye down, they became ensnared in anger, covetousness and contentment.

entries.farsi: هم تواند كاو ز رحمت نان دهد
entries.english: We are the family of the Lord and craving after milk: he said, ‘The people are God’s family.’

entries.farsi: گفت الخلق عيال لإلله‏ / آن كه او از آسمان باران دهد
entries.english: He who gives rain from heaven is also able, from His mercy, to give us bread.”

sections.title_fa: باز ترجيح‏نهادن شير جهد را بر توكل
sections.title_en: How the lion again pronounced exertion to be superior to trust in God.

entries.farsi: پاى دارى چون كنى خود را تو لنگ / دست دارى چون كنى پنهان تو چنگ
entries.english: “Yes,” said the lion; “but the Lord of His servants set a ladder before our feet.

entries.farsi: پايه پايه رفت بايد سوى بام / هست جبرى بودن اينجا طمع خام‏
entries.english: Step by step must we climb towards the roof: to be a necessitarian here is foolish hopes.

entries.farsi: گفت شير آرى ولى رب العباد / نردبانى پيش پاى ما نهاد
entries.english: You have feet: why do you make yourself out to be lame? You have hands why do you conceal the fingers?

entries.farsi: خواجه چون بيلى به دست بنده داد / بى‏زبان معلوم شد او را مراد
entries.english: When the master put a spade in the slave’s hand, his object was made known to him without tongue.

entries.farsi: دست همچون بيل اشارتهاى اوست / آخر انديشى عبارتهاى اوست‏
entries.english: Hand and spade alike are His implicit signs; thinking upon the end are His explicit declarations.

entries.farsi: چون اشارتهاش را بر جان نهى / در وفاى آن اشارت جان دهى
entries.english: When you take His signs to heart, you will devote your life to fulfilling that indication.

entries.farsi: پس اشارتهاى اسرارت دهد / بار بر دارد ز تو كارت دهد
entries.english: He will give you many hints of mysteries; He will remove the burden from you and give you authority.

entries.farsi: حاملى محمول گرداند ترا / قابلى مقبول گرداند ترا
entries.english: Do you bear? He will cause you to be borne. Do you receive? He will cause you to be received.

entries.farsi: قابل امر ويى قايل شوى / وصل جويى بعد از آن واصل شوى‏
entries.english: If you accept His command, you will become the spokesman; if you seek union, thereafter you will become united.

entries.farsi: سعى شكر نعمتش قدرت بود / جبر تو انكار آن نعمت بود
entries.english: Freewill is the endeavour to thank for His beneficence: your Jabr is the denial of that beneficence.

entries.farsi: شكر قدرت قدرتت افزون كند / جبر نعمت از كفت بيرون كند
entries.english: Thanksgiving for the power increases your power ; Jabr takes the gift out of your hand.

entries.farsi: جبر تو خفتن بود در ره مخسب / تا نبينى آن در و درگه مخسب‏
entries.english: Your Jabr is sleeping on the road: do not sleep! Sleep not, until you see the gate and the dergagh!

entries.farsi: هان مخسب اى جبرى بى‏اعتبار / جز به زير آن درخت ميوه‏دار
entries.english: Beware! Do not sleep, O inconsiderate necessitarian, save underneath that fruit-laden tree,

entries.farsi: تا كه شاخ افشان كند هر لحظه باد / بر سر خفته بريزد نقل و زاد
entries.english: So that every moment the wind may shake the boughs and shower upon the sleeper dessert and provision for the journey

entries.farsi: جبر و خفتن در ميان ره زنان / مرغ بى‏هنگام كى يابد امان
entries.english: Jabr is to sleep amidst highwaymen: how should the untimely bird receive quarter?

entries.farsi: ور اشارتهاش را بينى زنى / مرد پندارى و چون بينى زنى‏
entries.english: And if you turn up your nose at His signs, you deem a man, but when you consider, you are a woman.

entries.farsi: اين قدر عقلى كه دارى گم شود / سر كه عقل از وى بپرد دم شود
entries.english: This measure of understanding which you possess is lost: a head from which the logical brain is severed becomes a tail,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه بى‏شكرى بود شوم و شنار / مى‏برد بى‏شكر را در قعر نار
entries.english: Because ingratitude is wickedness and disgrace and brings the ingrate to the bottom of Hell-fire

entries.farsi: گر توكل مى‏كنى در كار كن / كشت كن پس تكيه بر جبار كن‏
entries.english: If you are putting trust in God, put trust as regards work: sow, then rely upon the Almighty.”

sections.title_fa: باز ترجيح نهادن نخجيران توكل را بر جهد
sections.title_en: How the beasts once more asserted the superiority of trust

entries.farsi: جمله با وى بانگها برداشتند / كان حريصان كه سببها كاشتند
entries.english: in God to exertion.

entries.farsi: صد هزار اندر هزار از مرد و زن / پس چرا محروم ماندند از زمن‏
entries.english: They all lifted up their voices with him, saying, “Those covetous ones who sowed means,

entries.farsi: صد هزاران قرن ز آغاز جهان / همچو اژدرها گشاده صد دهان‏
entries.english: Myriads on myriads of men and women— why, then, did they remain deprived of fortune?

entries.farsi: مكرها كردند آن دانا گروه / كه ز بن بر كنده شد ز آن مكر كوه‏
entries.english: From the beginning of the world myriads of generations have opened a hundred mouths, like dragons:

entries.farsi: كرد وصف مكرهاشان ذو الجلال / لتزول منه اقالل الجبال‏
entries.english: Those clever people devised plots that the mountain thereby was torn up from its foundation.
The Glorious described their plots: that the tops of the mountains might be moved thereby.

entries.farsi: جز كه آن قسمت كه رفت اندر ازل / روى ننمود از شكار و از عمل‏
entries.english: Except the portion which came to pass in eternity, nothing showed its face from their scheming’ and doing.

entries.farsi: جمله افتادند از تدبير و كار / ماند كار و حكم‏هاى كردگار
entries.english: They all fell from plan and act: the acts and decrees of the Maker remained.

entries.farsi: كسب جز نامى مدان اى نامدار / جهد جز وهمى مپندار اى عيار
entries.english: O illustrious one; do not regard work as anything but a name! O cunning one, do not think that exertion is anything but a vain fancy!”

sections.title_fa: نگريستن عزراييل بر مردى و گريختن آن مرد در سراى سليمان و تقرير ترجيح توكل بر جهد و قلت فايده‏ى جهد
sections.title_en: How Azrael looked at a certain man, and how that man fled to the palace if Solomon; and setting forth the superiority of trust in God to exertion and the uselessness of the latter.

entries.farsi: زاد مردى چاشتگاهى در رسيد / در سرا عدل سليمان در دويد
entries.english: One forenoon a freeborn man arrived and ran into Solomon’s hall of justice,

entries.farsi: رويش از غم زرد و هر دو لب كبود / پس سليمان گفت اى خواجه چه بود
entries.english: His countenance pale with anguish and both lips blue. Then Solomon said, “Good sir, what is the matter?”

entries.farsi: گفت عزراييل در من اين چنين / يك نظر انداخت پر از خشم و كين‏
entries.english: He replied, “Azrael cast on me such a look, so full of wrath and hate.”

entries.farsi: تا مرا ز ينجا به هندستان برد / بو كه بنده كان طرف شد جان برد
entries.english: “Come,” said the king, “what do you desire now? Ask!” “O protector of my life,” said he, “command the wind

entries.farsi: گفت هين اكنون چه مى‏خواهى بخواه / گفت فرما باد را اى جان پناه‏
entries.english: To bear me from here to India. Maybe, when your slave is there he his life will be saved.”

entries.farsi: نك ز درويشى گريزانند خلق / لقمه‏ى حرص و امل ز آنند خلق‏
entries.english: Lo, the people are fleeing from poverty: hence are they a mouthful for covetousness and expectation.

entries.farsi: ترس درويشى مثال آن هراس / حرص و كوشش را تو هندستان شناس‏
entries.english: The fear of poverty is like that terror: know you that covetousness and striving are India.

entries.farsi: باد را فرمود تا او را شتاب / برد سوى قعر هندستان بر آب‏
entries.english: He commanded the wind to bear him quickly over the water to the uttermost part of India

entries.farsi: روز ديگر وقت ديوان و لقا / پس سليمان گفت عزراييل را
entries.english: Next day, at the time of conference and meeting, Solomon said to Azrael:

entries.farsi: كان مسلمان را بخشم از چه چنان / بنگريدى تا شد آواره ز خان‏
entries.english: “Did you look with anger on that Moslem in order that he might wander far from his home?”

entries.farsi: گفت من از خشم كى كردم نظر / از تعجب ديدمش در رهگذر
entries.english: Azrael said, “When did I look angrily? I saw him as I passed by, in astonishment,

entries.farsi: كه مرا فرمود حق كه امروز هان / جان او را تو به هندستان ستان‏
entries.english: For God had commanded me, saying, ‘Listen, to-day, take his spirit in India.’

entries.farsi: از عجب گفتم گر او را صد پر است / او به هندستان شدن دور اندر است‏
entries.english: From wonder I said, if he has a hundred wings, it is a far journey for him to be in India.’”

entries.farsi: تو همه كار جهان را همچنين / كن قياس و چشم بگشا و ببين‏
entries.english: In like manner judge of all the affairs of this world and open your eye and see!

entries.farsi: از كه بگريزيم از خود اى محال / از كه برباييم از حق اى وبال‏
entries.english: From whom shall we flee? From ourselves? Oh, absurdity! From whom shall we take away? From God? Oh, crime!

sections.title_fa: باز ترجيح‏نهادن شير جهد را بر توكل و فوايد جهد را بيان كردن
sections.title_en: How the lion again declared exertion to be superior to trust in God and expounded the advantages of exertion.

entries.farsi: شير گفت آرى و ليكن هم ببين / جهدهاى انبيا و مومنين‏
entries.english: “Yes,” said the lion; “but at the same time consider the exertions of the prophets and the true believers.

entries.farsi: حق تعالى جهدشان را راست كرد / آن چه ديدند از جفا و گرم و سرد
entries.english: God, exalted is He, prospered their exertion and what they suffered of oppression and heat and cold.

entries.farsi: حيله‏هاشان جمله حال آمد لطيف / كل شي‏ء من ظريف هو ظريف‏
entries.english: Their plans were excellent in all circumstances: everything done by a good man is good.

entries.farsi: دامهاشان مرغ گردونى گرفت / نقصهاشان جمله افزونى گرفت‏
entries.english: Their snares caught the Heavenly bird, all their deficiencies turned to increment.”

entries.farsi: با قضا پنجه زدن نبود جهاد / ز آن كه اين را هم قضا بر ما نهاد
entries.english: O master, exert yourself so long as you can in the way of the prophets and saints!

entries.farsi: جهد مى‏كن تا توانى اى كيا / در طريق انبيا و اوليا
entries.english: Endeavour is not a struggle with Destiny, because Destiny itself has laid this upon us.

entries.farsi: كافرم من گر زيان كرده ست كس / در ره ايمان و طاعت يك نفس‏
entries.english: I am an infidel if any one has suffered loss a single moment in the way of faith and obedience.

entries.farsi: سر شكسته نيست اين سر را مبند / يك دو روزك جهد كن باقى بخند
entries.english: Your head is not broken: do not bandage this head. Exert yourself for a day or two, and laugh unto everlasting!

entries.farsi: بد محالى جست كاو دنيا بجست / نيك حالى جست كاو عقبى بجست‏
entries.english: An evil resort sought he that sought this world; a good state sought he that sought the world to come.

entries.farsi: مكرها در كسب دنيا بارد است / مكرها در ترك دنيا وارد است‏
entries.english: Plots for gaining this world are worthless; plots for renouncing this world are inspired.

entries.farsi: مكر آن باشد كه زندان حفره كرد / آن كه حفره بست آن مكرى ست سرد
entries.english: The plot is that he digs a hole in his prison; if he blocks up the hole, that is a foolish plot.

entries.farsi: اين جهان زندان و ما زندانيان / حفره كن زندان و خود را وارهان‏
entries.english: This world is the prison, and we are the prisoners: dig a hole in the prison and let yourself out!

entries.farsi: چيست دنيا از خدا غافل بدن / نى قماش و نقره و ميزان و زن‏
entries.english: What is this world? To be forgetful of God; it is not merchandise, silver, weigh-scales and women.

entries.farsi: مال را كز بهر دين باشى حمول / نعم مال صالح خواندش رسول‏
entries.english: As regards the wealth that you carry for religion’s sake, “How good is righteous wealth!” as the Prophet recited.

entries.farsi: آب در كشتى هالك كشتى است / آب اندر زير كشتى پشتى است‏
entries.english: Water in the boat is the ruin of the boat, water underneath the boat is a support.

entries.farsi: چون كه مال و ملك را از دل براند / ز آن سليمان خويش جز مسكين نخواند
entries.english: Since he cast out from his heart wealth and possessions, on that account Solomon did not call himself but “poor.”

entries.farsi: كوزه‏ى سر بسته اندر آب زفت / از دل پر باد فوق آب رفت‏
entries.english: The sealed jar, in rough water, floated on the water because of its air-filled heart.

entries.farsi: باد درويشى چو در باطن بود / بر سر آب جهان ساكن بود
entries.english: When the wind of poverty is within, he rests at peace on the surface of the water of the world;

entries.farsi: گر چه جمله‏ى اين جهان ملك وى است / ملك در چشم دل او ال شى است‏
entries.english: Although the whole of this world is his kingdom, in the eye of his heart the kingdom is nothing.

entries.farsi: پس دهان دل ببند و مهر كن / پر كنش از باد كبر من لدن‏
entries.english: Therefore stopper and seal the mouth of your heart, and fill it from the inward ventilator.

entries.farsi: جهد حق است و دوا حق است و درد / منكر اندر نفى جهدش جهد كرد
entries.english: Exertion is a reality, and medicine and disease are realities: the skeptic in his denial of exertion practised exertion.

sections.title_fa: مقرر شدن ترجيح جهد بر توكل
sections.title_en: How the superiority of exertion to trust in God was established.

entries.farsi: زين نمط بسيار برهان گفت شير / كز جواب آن جبريان گشتند سير
entries.english: The lion gave many proofs in this style, so that those necessitarians became tired of answering.

entries.farsi: روبه و آهو و خرگوش و شغال / جبر را بگذاشتند و قيل و قال‏
entries.english: Fox and deer and hare and jackal abandoned necessity and disputation.

entries.farsi: عهدها كردند با شير ژيان / كاندر اين بيعت نيفتد در زيان
entries.english: They made covenants with the furious lion, that he should incur no loss in this bargain,

entries.farsi: قسم هر روزش بيايد بى‏جگر / حاجتش نبود تقاضاى دگر
entries.english: The daily ration should come to him without trouble, and that he should not need to make a further demand.

entries.farsi: قرعه بر هر كه فتادى روز روز / سوى آن شير او دويدى همچو يوز
entries.english: Day by day the one on whom the lot fell would run to the lion as a cheetah.

entries.farsi: چون به خرگوش آمد اين ساغر به دور / بانگ زد خرگوش كاخر چند جور
entries.english: When this cup came round to the hare, the hare cried out, “Why, how long are we to endure injustice?”

sections.title_fa: انكار كردن نخجيران بر خرگوش در تاخير رفتن بر شير
sections.title_en: How the beasts of chase blamed the hare for his delay in going to the lion.

entries.farsi: قوم گفتندش كه چندين گاه ما / جان فدا كرديم در عهد و وفا
entries.english: The company said to him: “All this time we have sacrificed our lives in faith and loyalty.

entries.farsi: تو مجو بد نامى ما اى عنود / تا نرنجد شير رو رو زود زود
entries.english: Do not seek to give us a bad name, O rebellious one! Lest the lion be aggrieved, go, go! Quick! Quick!”

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن خرگوش نخجيران را
sections.title_en: How the hare answered the beasts.

entries.farsi: گفت اى ياران مرا مهلت دهيد / تا به مكرم از بال بيرون جهيد
entries.english: “O friends,” said he, “grant me a respite, that by my cunning you may escape from trickery,

entries.farsi: تا امان يابد به مكرم جانتان / ماند اين ميراث فرزندانتان‏
entries.english: That by my cunning your lives may be saved and remain as a heritage to your children.”

entries.farsi: هر پيمبر امتان را در جهان / همچنين تا مخلصى مى‏خواندشان‏
entries.english: Every prophet amidst the peoples used to call them after this manner to a place of deliverance,

entries.farsi: كز فلك راه برون شو ديده بود / در نظر چون مردمك پيچيده بود
entries.english: For he had seen from Heaven the way of escape, in sight he was contracted like the pupil of the eye.

entries.farsi: مردمش چون مردمك ديدند خرد / در بزرگى مردمك كس ره نبرد
entries.english: Men regarded him as small like the pupil: none attained to the greatness of the pupil.

sections.title_fa: ‏اعتراض نخجيران بر سخن خرگوش
sections.title_en: How the beasts objected to the proposal of the hare.

entries.farsi: قوم گفتندش كه اى خر گوش دار / خويش را اندازه‏ى خرگوش دار
entries.english: The company said to him: “O donkey, listen! Keep yourself within the measure of a hare!

entries.farsi: هين چه الف است اين كه از تو بهتران / در نياوردند اندر خاطر آن‏
entries.english: Eh, what brag is this--- which your betters never brought into their minds?

entries.farsi: معجبى يا خود قضامان در پى است / ور نه اين دم اليق چون تو كى است‏
entries.english: You are strange, or Destiny is pursuing us; else, how is this speech suitable to one like you?”

sections.title_fa: جواب خرگوش نخجيران را
sections.title_en: How the hare again answered the beasts.

entries.farsi: گفت اى ياران حقم الهام داد / مر ضعيفى را قوى رايى فتاد
entries.english: He said: “ O friends, God gave me inspiration: to a weakling there came a strong judgment.”

entries.farsi: آن چه حق آموخت مر زنبور را / آن نباشد شير را و گور را
entries.english: That which God taught to the bees is not to the lion and the wild ass.

entries.farsi: خانه‏ها سازد پر از حلواى تر / حق بر او آن علم را بگشاد در
entries.english: It makes houses of juicy halwa: God opened to it the door of that knowledge;

entries.farsi: آن چه حق آموخت كرم پيله را / هيچ پيلى داند آن گون حيله را
entries.english: That which God taught to the silkworm— does any elephant know such a device?

entries.farsi: آدم خاكى ز حق آموخت علم / تا به هفتم آسمان افروخت علم‏
entries.english: Adam created of earth, learned knowledge from God: knowledge shot beams up to the Seventh Heaven.

entries.farsi: نام و ناموس ملك را در شكست / كورى آن كس كه در حق درشك است‏
entries.english: He broke the name and fame of the angels, to the confusion of that one who is in doubt concerning God.

entries.farsi: زاهد چندين هزاران ساله را / پوز بندى ساخت آن گوساله را
entries.english: He made the ascetic of so many thousand years a muzzle for that young calf,

entries.farsi: تا نتاند شير علم دين كشيد / تا نگردد گرد آن قصر مشيد
entries.english: That he might not be able to drink the milk of knowledge of religion, and that he might not roam around that lofty castle.

entries.farsi: علمهاى اهل حس شد پوز بند / تا نگيرد شير ز آن علم بلند
entries.english: The sciences of the followers of sense became a muzzle, so that he might not receive milk from that sublime knowledge.

entries.farsi: قطره‏ى دل را يكى گوهر فتاد / كان به درياها و گردونها نداد
entries.english: Into the blood-drop of the heart there fell an essence not given to the seas and skies.

entries.farsi: چند صورت آخر اى صورت پرست / جان بى‏معنيت از صورت نرست‏
entries.english: How long form? After all, O form-worshipper, has your reality-lacking soul not escaped from form?

entries.farsi: گر به صورت آدمى انسان بدى / احمد و بو جهل خود يكسان بدى‏
entries.english: If a human being were a man in virtue of form, Ahmad and Bu Jahl would be just the same.

entries.farsi: نقش بر ديوار مثل آدم است / بنگر از صورت چه چيز او كم است‏
entries.english: The painting on the wall is like Adam: see from the form what thing in it wants.

entries.farsi: جان كم است آن صورت با تاب را / رو بجو آن گوهر كمياب را
entries.english: The spirit is wanting in that resplendent form: go, seek that essence rarely found!

entries.farsi: شد سر شيران عالم جمله پست / چون سگ اصحاب را دادند دست‏
entries.english: The heads of all the lions in the world were laid low when they gave a hand to the dog of the Companions.

entries.farsi: چه زيان استش از آن نقش نفور / چون كه جانش غرق شد در بحر نور
entries.english: What loss does it suffer from that abhorred shape, inasmuch as its spirit was plunged in the ocean of light?

entries.farsi: وصف صورت نيست اندر خامه‏ها / عالم و عادل بود در نامه‏ها
entries.english: It is not in. pens to describe form: in letters is “learned” and “just”;

entries.farsi: عالم و عادل همه معنى است بس / كش نيابى در مكان و پيش و پس‏
entries.english: “ Learned” and just” are only the spiritual essence which you will not find in place or in front or behind.

entries.farsi: مى‏زند بر تن ز سوى المكان / مى‏نگنجد در فلك خورشيد جان‏
entries.english: The sun of the spirit strikes on the body from the quarter where place does not exist: it is not contained in the sky.

sections.title_fa: ذكر دانش خرگوش و بيان فضيلت و منافع دانستن‏
sections.title_en: An account of the knowledge of the hare and an explanation of the excellence and advantages of knowledge

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد هوش دار / گوش سوى قصه‏ى خرگوش دار
entries.english: This topic has no end. Pay attention! Listen to the story of the hare.

entries.farsi: گوش خر بفروش و ديگر گوش خر / كاين سخن را در نيابد گوش خر
entries.english: Sell your asinine ear and buy another ear, for the asinine ear will not apprehend this discourse.

entries.farsi: رو تو روبه بازى خرگوش بين / مكر و شير اندازى خرگوش بين
entries.english: Go, behold the foxy tricks played by the hare; behold how the hare made a plot to catch the lion.

entries.farsi: خاتم ملك سليمان است علم / جمله عالم صورت و جان است علم‏
entries.english: Knowledge is the seal of the kingdom of Solomon: the whole world is form, and knowledge is the spirit.

entries.farsi: آدمى را زين هنر بى‏چاره گشت / خلق درياها و خلق كوه و دشت‏
entries.english: Because of this virtue, the creatures of the seas and those of mountain and plain are helpless before man.

entries.farsi: زو پلنگ و شير ترسان همچو موش / زو نهنگ و بحر در صفرا و جوش
entries.english: Of him the leopard and lion are afraid, like the mouse; from him the crocodile of the great river is in pallor and agitation.

entries.farsi: زو پرى و ديو ساحلها گرفت / هر يكى در جاى پنهان جا گرفت‏
entries.english: From him peri and demon took to the shores: each took abode in some hiding-place.

entries.farsi: آدمى را دشمن پنهان بسى است / آدمى با حذر عاقل كسى است‏
entries.english: Man has many a secret enemy: the cautious man is a wise one.

entries.farsi: خلق پنهان زشتشان و خوبشان / مى‏زند در دل بهر دم كوبشان‏
entries.english: Hidden creatures, evil and good: at every instant their blows are striking on the heart.

entries.farsi: بهر غسل ار در روى در جويبار / بر تو آسيبى زند در آب خار
entries.english: If you go into the river to wash yourself, a thorn in the water pierces your skin

entries.farsi: گر چه پنهان خار در آب است پست / چون كه در تو مى‏خلد دانى كه هست‏
entries.english: Although the thorn is hidden low in the water, you know it is there, since it is pricking you.

entries.farsi: خار خار وحيها و وسوسه / از هزاران كس بود نى يك كسه
entries.english: The pricks of inspirations and temptations are from thousands of beings, not from one.

entries.farsi: باش تا حسهاى تو مبدل شود / تا ببينيشان و مشكل حل شود
entries.english: Wait for your senses to be transformed, so that you may see them, and the difficulty may be solved,

entries.farsi: تا سخنهاى كيان رد كرده‏اى / تا كيان را سرور خود كرده‏اى‏
entries.english: So that whose words you have rejected and whom you have made your captain

sections.title_fa: باز طلبيدن نخجيران از خرگوش سر انديشه‏ى او را
sections.title_en: How the beasts requested the hare to tell the

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفتند كاى خرگوش چست / در ميان آر آن چه در ادراك تست‏
entries.english: secret of his thought.

entries.farsi: اى كه با شيرى تو در پيچيده‏اى / باز گو رايى كه انديشيده‏اى‏
entries.english: Afterwards they said, “O nimble hare, communicate what is in your apprehension.

entries.farsi: مشورت ادراك و هشيارى دهد / عقلها مر عقل را يارى دهد
entries.english: O you, who has grappled with a lion, declare the plan which you hast thought of.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر بكن اى رايزن / مشورت كالمستشار موتمن‏
entries.english: Counsel gives perception and understanding: the mind is helped by minds.

entries.farsi: گفت هر رازى نشايد باز گفت / جفت طاق آيد گهى گه طاق جفت‏
entries.english: The Prophet said, ‘ O adviser, take counsel, for he whose counsel is sought is trusted.’”

sections.title_fa: منع كردن خرگوش راز را از ايشان‏
sections.title_en: How the hare withheld the secret from them.

entries.farsi: از صفا گر دم زنى با آينه / تيره گردد زود با ما آينه‏
entries.english: He said, “One ought not to say forth every secret: sometimes the even number turns out to be odd, and sometimes the odd number to be even.”

entries.farsi: در بيان اين سه كم جنبان لبت / از ذهاب و از ذهب وز مذهبت‏
entries.english: If from guilelessness you breathe words to a mirror, the mirror at once becomes dim to us.

entries.farsi: كين سه را خصم است بسيار و عدو / در كمينت ايستد چون داند او
entries.english: Do not move your lip in explanation of these three things, (namely) concerning your path and your gold and your belief;

entries.farsi: ور بگويى با يكى دو الوداع / كل سر جاوز الاثنين شاع‏
entries.english: For to these three there is many an adversary and foes standing in wait for you when he knows.

entries.farsi: گر دو سه پرنده را بندى به هم / بر زمين مانند محبوس از الم‏
entries.english: And if you tell one or two, farewell: every secret that goes beyond the two is published abroad.

entries.farsi: مشورت دارند سرپوشيده خوب / در كنايت با غلط افكن مشوب‏
entries.english: If you tie two or three birds together, they will remain on the ground, imprisoned by grief;
They hold a consultation well-disguised and mingled, in its signifi­ cance, with that which casts error.

entries.farsi: مشورت كردى پيمبر بسته سر / گفته ايشانش جواب و بى‏خبر
entries.english: The Prophet used to take counsel, cryptically, and they would answer him and without knowledge.

entries.farsi: در مثالى بسته گفتى راى را / تا نداند خصم از سر پاى را
entries.english: He would speak his opinion in a covert parable, in order that the adversary might not know foot from head.

entries.farsi: او جواب خويش بگرفتى از او / وز سؤالش مى‏نبردى غير بو
entries.english: He would receive his answer from him, while the other would not catch the smell of his question.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى مكر خرگوش‏
sections.title_en: The story of the hare’s stratagem

entries.farsi: ساعتى تاخير كرد اندر شدن / بعد از آن شد پيش شير پنجه زن‏
entries.english: He delayed awhile in going, and then he went before the lion who rends with claws.

entries.farsi: ز آن سبب كاندر شدن او ماند دير / خاك را مى‏كند و مى‏غريد شير
entries.english: Because he tarried late in going, the lion was tearing up the earth and roaring.

entries.farsi: گفت من گفتم كه عهد آن خسان / خام باشد خام و سست و نارسان‏
entries.english: “I said,” cried the lion, “that the promise of those vile ones would be vain, vain, frail and unfulfilled

entries.farsi: دمدمه‏ى ايشان مرا از خر فگند / چند بفريبد مرا اين دهر چند
entries.english: Their idle chatter has duped me: how long will this Time deceive me, how long?”

entries.farsi: سخت درماند امير سست ريش / چون نه پس بيند نه پيش از احمقيش‏
entries.english: The prince who has no strength in his beard is left sorely in the lurch when by reason of his folly he looks neither backwards nor forwards.

entries.farsi: راه هموار است و زيرش دامها / قحط معنى در ميان نامها
entries.english: The road is smooth, and under it are pitfalls: amidst the names there is a dearth of meaning.

entries.farsi: لفظها و نامها چون دامهاست / لفظ شيرين ريگ آب عمر ماست‏
entries.english: Words and names are like pitfalls: the sweet word is the sand for the water of our life.

entries.farsi: آن يكى ريگى كه جوشد آب ازو / سخت كمياب است رو آن را بجو
entries.english: The one sand whence water gushes is seldom to be found: go, seek it.

entries.farsi: منبع حكمت شود حكمت طلب / فارغ آيد او ز تحصيل و سبب‏
entries.english: He that searches after wisdom becomes a fountain of wisdom; he becomes independent of acquisition and means.

entries.farsi: لوح حافظ لوح محفوظى شود / عقل او از روح محظوظى شود
entries.english: The guarding tablet becomes a Guarded Tablet ; his understanding becomes enriched by the Spirit.

entries.farsi: چون معلم بود عقلش ز ابتدا / بعد از اين شد عقل شاگردى و را
entries.english: When a man’s understanding has been his teacher, after this the understanding becomes his pupil.

entries.farsi: عقل چون جبريل گويد احمدا / گر يكى گامى نهم سوزد مرا
entries.english: The understanding says, like Gabriel, “O Ahmad, if I take one step, it will burn me;

entries.farsi: تو مرا بگذار زين پس پيش ران / حد من اين بود اى سلطان جان‏
entries.english: Leave me, go on: this is my limit, O sultan of the soul!”

entries.farsi: هر كه ماند از كاهلى بى‏شكر و صبر / او همين داند كه گيرد پاى جبر
entries.english: Whoever, through heedlessness, remains without thanksgiving and patience, knows but this, that he should follow in the heels of necessity (jabr).

entries.farsi: هر كه جبر آورد خود رنجور كرد / تا همان رنجورى‏اش در گور كرد
entries.english: Any one who pleads necessity feigns himself to be ill, with the result that the illness brings him to the grave.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه رنجورى به الغ / رنج آرد تا بميرد چون چراغ‏
entries.english: The Prophet said, “ Illness in jest brings disease, so that he dies like a lamp.”

entries.farsi: جبر چه بود بستن اشكسته را / يا بپيوستن رگى بگسسته را
entries.english: What is jabr? To bind up a broken or tie a severed vein.

entries.farsi: چون در اين ره پاى خود نشكسته‏اى / بر كه مى‏خندى چه پا را بسته‏اى‏
entries.english: Inasmuch as you have not broken your foot in this path, whom are you mocking? Why have you bandaged your foot?

entries.farsi: و آن كه پايش در ره كوشش شكست / در رسيد او را براق و بر نشست‏
entries.english: But as for him who broke his foot in the path of exertion, Buraq came up to him, and he mounted.

entries.farsi: حامل دين بود او محمول شد / قابل فرمان بد او مقبول شد
entries.english: He was a bearer of the religion, and he became one who is borne; he was an accepter of the command, and he became accepted.

entries.farsi: تا كنون فرمان پذيرفتى ز شاه / بعد از اين فرمان رساند بر سپاه‏
entries.english: Until now, he was receiving commands from the King; henceforth he delivers the commands to the people.

entries.farsi: تا كنون اختر اثر كردى در او / بعد از اين باشد امير اختر او
entries.english: Until now, the stars were influencing him; henceforth he is the ruler of the stars.

entries.farsi: گر ترا اشكال آيد در نظر / پس تو شك دارى در انْشَقَّ القمر
entries.english: If perplexity arise in your sight, then you will have doubts concerning The moon was cloven asunder.

entries.farsi: تا هوا تازه ست ايمان تازه نيست / كاين هوا جز قفل آن دروازه نيست‏
entries.english: Refresh your faith, not with talk of the tongue, O you who hassecretly refreshed your desire.

entries.farsi: تازه كن ايمان نه از گفت زبان / اى هوا را تازه كرده در نهان‏
entries.english: So long as desire is fresh, faith is not fresh, for it is this desire that locks that gate.

entries.farsi: كرده‏اى تاويل حرف بكر را / خويش را تاويل كن نى ذكر را
entries.english: You hast interpreted the virgin Word: interpret yourself, not the Book.

entries.farsi: بر هوا تاويل قرآن مى‏كنى / پست و كژ شد از تو معنى سنى
entries.english: You interpret the Qur’an according to your desire: by you the sublime meaning is degraded and perverted.

sections.title_fa: زيافت تاويل ركيك مگس
sections.title_en: The baseness of the foul interpretation given by the fly

entries.farsi: آن مگس بر برگ كاه و بول خر / همچو كشتى‏بان همى‏افراشت سر
entries.english: The fly was lifting up his head, like a pilot, on a blade of straw a pool of ass’s urine.

entries.farsi: گفت من دريا و كشتى خوانده‏ام / مدتى در فكر آن مى‏مانده‏ام‏
entries.english: “I have called sea and ship,” said he; “I have been pondering over that for a long while.

entries.farsi: اينك اين دريا و اين كشتى و من / مرد كشتيبان و اهل و رايزن‏
entries.english: Look! Here is this sea and this ship, and I am the pilot and skilled and judicious.”

entries.farsi: بر سر دريا همى‏راند او عمد / مى‏نمودش آن قدر بيرون ز حد
entries.english: He was propelling the raft on the “sea”: that quantity toss appeared to him illimitable.

entries.farsi: بود بى‏حد آن چمين نسبت بدو / آن نظر كه بيند آن را راست كو
entries.english: That urine was boundless in relation to him: where was the vision that should see it truly?

entries.farsi: عالمش چندان بود كش بينش است / چشم چندين بحر هم چندينش است‏
entries.english: His world extends as far as his sight reaches; his eye is so big, his “sea” is big in the same proportion.

entries.farsi: صاحب تاويل باطل چون مگس / وهم او بول خر و تصوير خس‏
entries.english: So with the false interpreter: like the fly, his imagination is ass’s urine and his conception a straw.

entries.farsi: گر مگس تاويل بگذارد به راى / آن مگس را بخت گرداند هماى‏
entries.english: If the fly leaves off interpreting by opinion, Fortune will turn that fly into a pheonix (humay).

entries.farsi: آن مگس نبود كش اين عبرت بود / روح او نى در خور صورت بود
entries.english: One who possesses this indication is not a fly: his spirit is not analogous to his form.

sections.title_fa: توليدن شير از دير آمدن خرگوش‏
sections.title_en: How the lion roared wrathfully because the hare was late in coming.

entries.farsi: همچو آن خرگوش كاو بر شير زد / روح او كى بود اندر خورد قد
entries.english: As the hare who struck against the lion: how was his spirit analogous to his stature?

entries.farsi: شير مى‏گفت از سر تيزى و خشم / كز ره گوشم عدو بر بست چشم‏
entries.english: The lion from fury and rage was saying, “By means of my ear the enemy has bound up my eye.

entries.farsi: مكرهاى جبريانم بسته كرد / تيغ چوبينشان تنم را خسته كرد
entries.english: The tricks of the necessitarians have bound me; their wooden sword has wounded my body.

entries.farsi: بردران اى دل تو ايشان را مه‏ايست / پوستشان بر كن كشان جز پوست نيست‏
entries.english: After this I will not hearken to their idle chatter: all that is the cry of demons and ghouls.

entries.farsi: زين سپس من نشنوم آن دمدمه / بانگ ديوان است و غوالن آن همه
entries.english: O my heart; tear them to pieces, do not lag; rend their skins, for they have naught but skin.”

entries.farsi: پوست چه بود گفتهاى رنگ رنگ / چون زره بر آب كش نبود درنگ‏
entries.english: What is skin? Specious words, like ripples on water which have no continuance.

entries.farsi: اين سخن چون پوست و معنى مغز دان / اين سخن چون نقش و معنى همچو جان‏
entries.english: Know that these words are as the skin, and the meaning is the kernel; these words are as the form, and the meaning is like the spirit.

entries.farsi: پوست باشد مغز بد را عيب پوش / مغز نيكو را ز غيرت غيب پوش‏
entries.english: The skin hides the defect of the bad kernel; it hides jealously the secrets of the good kernel.

entries.farsi: چون قلم از باد بد دفتر ز آب / هر چه بنويسى فنا گردد شتاب‏
entries.english: When the pen is of wind and the scroll of water, whatever you write perishes speedily;

entries.farsi: نقش آب است ار وفا جويى از آن / باز گردى دستهاى خود گزان‏
entries.english: It is written on water: if you seek constancy from it, you will return biting your hands.

entries.farsi: باد در مردم هوا و آرزوست / چون هوا بگذاشتى پيغام هوست‏
entries.english: The wind in men is vanity and desire; when you have abandoned vanity, is the message from Him.

entries.farsi: خوش بود پيغامهاى كردگار / كاو ز سر تا پاى باشد پايدار
entries.english: Sweet are the messages of the Maker, for it from head to foot is enduring.

entries.farsi: خطبه‏ى شاهان بگردد و آن كيا / جز كيا و خطبه‏هاى انبيا
entries.english: The khutbas for kings changes, and their empire; except the empire and khutbas (insignia) of the prophets,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه بوش پادشاهان از هواست / بار نامه‏ى انبيا از كبرياست‏
entries.english: Because the pomp of kings is from vanity, the glorious privilege of the prophets is from Majesty.

entries.farsi: از درمها نام شاهان بر كنند / نام احمد تا ابد بر مى‏زنند
entries.english: The names of kings are removed from the dirhems, the name of Ahmad is stamped on them for ever.

entries.farsi: نام احمد نام جمله انبياست / چون كه صد آمد نود هم پيش ماست‏
entries.english: The name of Ahmad is the name of all the prophets: when the hundred comes, ninety is with us as well.

sections.title_fa: هم در بيان مكر خرگوش
sections.title_en: Further setting forth the stratagem of the hare

entries.farsi: در شدن خرگوش بس تاخير كرد / مكر را با خويشتن تقرير كرد
entries.english: The hare made much delay in going; he rehearsed to himself the tricks.

entries.farsi: در ره آمد بعد تاخير دراز / تا به گوش شير گويد يك دو راز
entries.english: After long delay he came on the road, that he might say one or two secrets into the ear of the lion.

entries.farsi: تا چه عالمهاست در سوداى عقل / تا چه با پهناست اين درياى عقل‏
entries.english: Think what worlds are in commerce with Reason! How wide is this ocean of Reason!

entries.farsi: صورت ما اندر اين بحر عذاب / مى‏دود چون كاسه‏ها بر روى آب‏
entries.english: In this sweet ocean our forms are moving fast, like cups on the surface of water:

entries.farsi: تا نشد پر بر سر دريا چو طشت / چون كه پر شد طشت در وى غرق گشت‏
entries.english: Until they become full, like bowls on the top of the sea, when the bowl is filled it sinks.

entries.farsi: عقل پنهان است و ظاهر عالمى / صورت ما موج يا از وى نمى‏
entries.english: Reason is hidden, and a world is visible: our forms are the waves or a spray of it.

entries.farsi: هر چه صورت مى وسيلت سازدش / ز آن وسيلت بحر دور اندازدش
entries.english: Whatever the form makes a means of approach to Reason, by that means the ocean casts it far away.

entries.farsi: تا نبيند دل دهنده‏ى راز را / تا نبيند تير دور انداز را
entries.english: So long as the heart does not see the Giver of conscience, so long as the arrow does not see the far-shooting Archer,

entries.farsi: اسب خود را ياوه داند وز ستيز / مى‏دواند اسب خود در راه تيز
entries.english: He thinks his horse is lost, though he is obstinately speeding his horse on the road.

entries.farsi: اسب خود را ياوه داند آن جواد / و اسب خود او را كشان كرده چو باد
entries.english: That fine fellow thinks his horse is lost, while his horse is sweeping him onward like the wind.

entries.farsi: در فغان و جستجو آن خيره‏سر / هر طرف پرسان و جويان دربدر
entries.english: In lamentation and inquiry that scatterbrain from door to door in every direction, asking and searching:

entries.farsi: كان كه دزديد اسب ما را كو و كيست / اين كه زير ران تست اى خواجه چيست‏
entries.english: “Where and who is he that stole my horse?” What is this under your thigh, O master?

entries.farsi: آرى اين اسب است ليك اين اسب كو / با خود آ اى شهسوار اسب جو
entries.english: “Yes, this is the horse, but where is the horse?” O dexterous rider in search of your horse, come to yourself!

entries.farsi: جان ز پيدايى و نزديكى است گم / چون شكم پر آب و لب خشكى چو خم‏
entries.english: The Jaan is lost because of its being so manifest and near: how, having your belly full of water, are you dry-lipped like a jar?

entries.farsi: كى ببينى سرخ و سبز و فور را / تا نبينى پيش از اين سه نور را
entries.english: How will you see red and green and grey, unless before these three you see the light?

entries.farsi: ليك چون در رنگ گم شد هوش تو / شد ز نور آن رنگها رو پوش تو
entries.english: But since your mind was lost in the colour, those colours became to you a veil from the light.

entries.farsi: چون كه شب آن رنگها مستور بود / پس بديدى ديد رنگ از نور بود
entries.english: Inasmuch as at night those colours were hidden, you saw that your vision of the colour was from the light.

entries.farsi: اين برون از آفتاب و از سها / و اندرون از عكس انوار على‏
entries.english: There is no vision of colour without the external light: even so it is with the colour of inward imagining.

entries.farsi: نور نور چشم خود نور دل است / نور چشم از نور دلها حاصل است‏
entries.english: This outward is from the sun and from Suha, while the inward is from the reflection of the beams of Glory.

entries.farsi: نيست ديد رنگ بى‏نور برون / همچنين رنگ خيال اندرون‏
entries.english: The light which gives light to the eye is in truth the light of the heart: the light of the eye is produced by the light of hearts.

entries.farsi: باز نور نور دل نور خداست / كاو ز نور عقل و حس پاك و جداست‏
entries.english: Again, the light which gives light to the heart is the Light of God, which is pure and separate from the light of intellect and sense.

entries.farsi: ديدن نور است آن گه ديد رنگ / وين به ضد نور دانى بى‏درنگ‏
entries.english: At night there was no light: you did not see the colour; then it was made manifest by the opposite of light.

entries.farsi: شب نبد نورى نديدى رنگها / پس به ضد نور پيدا شد ترا
entries.english: Comes the seeing of light, then the seeing of colour; and this you know immediately by the opposite of light.

entries.farsi: رنج و غم را حق پى آن آفريد / تا بدين ضد خوش دلى آيد پديد
entries.english: God created pain and sorrow for the purpose that happiness might be made manifest by means of this opposite.

entries.farsi: پس نهانيها به ضد پيدا شود / چون كه حق را نيست ضد پنهان بود
entries.english: Hidden things, then, are manifested by means of their opposite; since God has no opposite, He is hidden;

entries.farsi: كه نظر بر نور بود آن گه به رنگ / ضد به ضد پيدا بود چون روم و زنگ‏
entries.english: For the sight fell on the light, then on the colour: opposite is made manifest by opposite, like Greeks and Ethiopians

entries.farsi: پس به ضد نور دانستى تو نور / ضد ضد را مى‏نمايد در صدور
entries.english: Therefore you knew light by its opposite: opposite reveals opposite in coming forth.

entries.farsi: نور حق را نيست ضدى در وجود / تا به ضد او را توان پيدا نمود
entries.english: The Light of God has no opposite in existence, that by means of that opposite it should be possible to make Him manifest:

entries.farsi: الجرم أبصارنا ال تدركه / و هو يدرك بين تو از موسى و كه‏
entries.english: Necessarily our eyes do not perceive Him, though He perceives: see this from Moses and the mountain.

entries.farsi: صورت از معنى چو شير از بيشه دان / يا چو آواز و سخن ز انديشه دان‏
entries.english: Know that form springs from spirit as the lion from the jungle, or as voice and speech from thought.

entries.farsi: اين سخن و آواز از انديشه خاست / تو ندانى بحر انديشه كجاست‏
entries.english: This speech and voice arose from thought; you know not where the sea of thought is,

entries.farsi: ليك چون موج سخن ديدى لطيف / بحر آن دانى كه باشد هم شريف‏
entries.english: But since you have seen that the waves of speech are fair, you know that their sea also is noble.

entries.farsi: چون ز دانش موج انديشه بتاخت / از سخن و آواز او صورت بساخت‏
entries.english: When the waves of thought sped on from Wisdom, it made the form of speech and voice.

entries.farsi: از سخن صورت بزاد و باز مرد / موج خود را باز اندر بحر برد
entries.english: The form was born of the Word and died again, the wave drew itself back into the sea.

entries.farsi: صورت از بى‏صورتى آمد برون / باز شد كه إِنَّا إِلَيْهِ راجعون‏
entries.english: The form came forth from Formlessness and went back, for Verily unto Him are we returning.

entries.farsi: پس ترا هر لحظه مرگ و رجعتى است / مصطفى فرمود دنيا ساعتى است‏
entries.english: Every instant, then, you are dying and returning: ‘Mustafa declared that this world is a moment.

entries.farsi: فكر ما تيرى است از هو در هوا / در هوا كى پايد آيد تا خدا
entries.english: Our thought is an arrow from Him (Hu) into the air (hawa): how should it stay in the air? It comes to God.

entries.farsi: هر نفس نو مى‏شود دنيا و ما / بى‏خبر از نو شدن اندر بقا
entries.english: Every moment the world is renewed, and we are unaware of its being renewed whilst it remains.

entries.farsi: عمر همچون جوى نو نو مى‏رسد / مستمرى مى‏نمايد در جسد
entries.english: Life is ever arriving anew, like the stream, though in the body it has the semblance of continuity.

entries.farsi: آن ز تيرى مستمر شكل آمده ست / چون شرر كش تيز جنبانى به دست‏
entries.english: From its swiftness it appears continuous, like the spark which you whirl rapidly with your hand.

entries.farsi: شاخ آتش را بجنبانى به ساز / در نظر آتش نمايد بس دراز
entries.english: If you whirl a firebrand with dexterity, it appears to the sight as a very long fire.

entries.farsi: اين درازى مدت از تيزى صنع / مى‏نمايد سرعت انگيزى صنع‏
entries.english: The swift motion produced by the action of God presents this length of duration as from the rapidity of Divine action.

entries.farsi: طالب اين سر اگر عالمه‏اى است / نك حسام الدين كه سامى نامه‏اى است
entries.english: Even if the seeker of this mystery is an exceedingly learned man, “Lo, Husamu’ddin, who is a sublime book.”

sections.title_fa: رسيدن خرگوش به شير و خشم شير بر وى
sections.title_en: The hare’s coming to the lion and the lion’s anger with him.

entries.farsi: شير اندر آتش و در خشم و شور / ديد كان خرگوش مى‏آيد ز دور
entries.english: The lion, incensed and wrathful and frantic, saw the hare coming from afar,

entries.farsi: مى‏دود بى‏دهشت و گستاخ او / خشمگين و تند و تيز و ترش رو
entries.english: Running undismayed and confidently, looking angry and fierce and fell and sour,

entries.farsi: كز شكسته آمدن تهمت بود / وز دليرى دفع هر ريبت بود
entries.english: For by coming humbly suspicion would be, while by boldness every cause of doubt would be removed.

entries.farsi: چون رسيد او پيشتر نزديك صف / بانگ بر زد شيرهاى اى ناخلف‏
entries.english: When he came further on, near to the “shoe-row’,” the lion shouted— “Ha, villain!

entries.farsi: من كه گاوان را ز هم بدريده‏ام / من كه گوش پيل نر ماليده‏ام‏
entries.english: I who have torn oxen limb from limb, I who have rubbed the ear of the ferocious elephant—

entries.farsi: ترك خواب غفلت خرگوش كن / غره‏ى اين شير اى خر گوش كن‏
entries.english: Who is a half-witted hare, that he should throw on the ground my command? “

entries.farsi: نيم خرگوشى كه باشد كه چنين / امر ما را افكند او بر زمين‏
entries.english: Abandon the hare’s slumber and heedlessness! Give ear, O donkey, to the roaring of this lion!

sections.title_fa: عذر گفتن خرگوش‏
sections.title_en: The hare’s apology

entries.farsi: گفت خرگوش الامان عذريم هست / گر دهد عفو خداونديت دست‏
entries.english: “Mercy!” cried the hare,” I have an excuse, if your Lordship’s pardon come to my aid’.”

entries.farsi: مرغ بى‏وقتى سرت بايد بريد / عذر احمق را نمى شايد شنيد
entries.english: “What excuse? “Said he. “Oh, the cleverness of fools!” Is this the time for them to come into the presence of kings?

entries.farsi: گفت چه عذر اى قصور ابلهان / اين زمان آيند در پيش شهان
entries.english: You are an untimely bird: your head must be cut off. One ought not to hear the excuse of a fool.

entries.farsi: عذر احمق بدتر از جرمش بود / عذر نادان زهر هر دانش بود
entries.english: The fool’s excuse is worse than his crime; the excuse of the ignorant is the poison that kills wisdom.

entries.farsi: عذرت اى خرگوش از دانش تهى / من چه خرگوشم كه در گوشم نهى‏
entries.english: Your excuse, O hare, is devoid of wisdom: what hare am I that you should put it in my ear? “

entries.farsi: گفت اى شه ناكسى را كس شمار / عذر استم ديده‏اى را گوش دار
entries.english: “O king,” he replied, “account a worthless one to be worthy: listen to the excuse of one who has suffered oppression.

entries.farsi: خاص از بهر زكات جاه خود / گمرهى را تو مران از راه خود
entries.english: In particular, as an alms for your high estate, do not drive out of your way one whose way is lost.

entries.farsi: بحر كاو آبى به هر جو مى‏دهد / هر خسى را بر سر و رو مى‏نهد
entries.english: The ocean, which gives some water to every stream, carries on its head and face every piece of rubbish.

entries.farsi: كم نخواهد گشت دريا زين كرم / از كرم دريا نگردد بيش و كم‏
entries.english: By this bounty the sea will not become less: the sea is neither increased nor diminished by its bounty.”

entries.farsi: گفت دارم من كرم بر جاى او / جامه‏ى هر كس برم بالاى او
entries.english: The lion said, “I will bestow bounty in its place, I will cut every one’s clothes according to his stature.”

entries.farsi: گفت بشنو گر نباشم جاى لطف / سر نهادم پيش اژدرهاى عنف‏
entries.english: “Listen,” cried the hare, “if I am not a fit object for grace, I lay my head before the dragon of violence.

entries.farsi: من به وقت چاشت در راه آمدم / با رفيق خود سوى شاه آمدم‏
entries.english: At breakfast-time I set out on the way, I came towards the king with my comrade.

entries.farsi: با من از بهر تو خرگوشى دگر / جفت و همره كرده بودند آن نفر
entries.english: That party had appointed, for your sake, another hare to go along with me as consort and companion.

entries.farsi: شيرى اندر راه قصد بنده كرد / قصد هر دو همره آينده كرد
entries.english: On the road a lion attacked your humble slave, attacked both the companions in travel who were coming.

entries.farsi: گفتمش ما بنده‏ى شاهنشه‏ايم / خواجه‏تاشان كه آن درگه‏ايم‏
entries.english: I said to him, we are the slaves of the King of kings, the lowly fellow-servants of that court.’

entries.farsi: گفت شاهنشه كه باشد شرم دار / پيش من تو ياد هر ناكس ميار
entries.english: He said, ‘ The King of kings! Who is he? Be ashamed! Do not make mention of every base loon in my presence.

entries.farsi: هم ترا و هم شهت را بر درم / گر تو با يارت بگرديد از درم‏
entries.english: Both you and your king I will tear to pieces, if you and your friend turn back from my door.’

entries.farsi: گفت همره را گرو نه پيش من / ور نه قربانى تو اندر كيش من‏
entries.english: I said to him, ‘ Let me behold once more the face of the king and bear the news of you.’

entries.farsi: گفتمش بگذار تا بار دگر / روى شه بينم برم از تو خبر
entries.english: He said, ‘Place your comrade with me as a pledge; otherwise, you are a sacrifice according to my law.’

entries.farsi: البه كرديمش بسى سودى نكرد / يار من بستد مرا بگذاشت فرد
entries.english: We entreated him much: it was no use. He seized my friend and left me to go alone.

entries.farsi: يارم از زفتى دو چندان بد كه من / هم به لطف و هم به خوبى هم به تن‏
entries.english: My friend, from his plumpness, made three of me both in comeliness and beauty and body.

entries.farsi: بعد از اين ز آن شير اين ره بسته شد / رشته‏ى ايمان ما بگسسته شد
entries.english: Henceforth this road is barred by that lion: the thread of our covenants is broken.

entries.farsi: از وظيفه بعد از اين اوميد بر / حق همى‏گويم ترا و الحق مر
entries.english: Cut off hope of the allowance henceforth; I am telling you the truth, and truth is bitter.

entries.farsi: گر وظيفه بايدت ره پاك كن / هين بيا و دفع آن بى‏باك كن‏
entries.english: If you want the allowance, clear the way! Hey, come on and repel that irreverent one!”

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن شير خرگوش را و روان شدن با او
sections.title_en: How the lion answered the hare and set off with him.

entries.farsi: گفت بسم اللهَّ بيا تا او كجاست / پيش در شو گر همى‏گويى تو راست‏
entries.english: “Come on in God’s name,” said he, “let me see where he is! Go in front, if you are speaking truth,

entries.farsi: تا سزاى او و صد چون او دهم / ور دروغ است اين سزاى تو دهم‏
entries.english: That I may give him and a hundred like him the punishment they deserve or if this is a lie, that I may give your deserts to you.”

entries.farsi: اندر آمد چون قالووزى به پيش / تا برد او را به سوى دام خويش‏
entries.english: The hare set out on the way, in front like a guide, that he might lead him towards his snare,

entries.farsi: سوى چاهى كاو نشانش كرده بود / چاه مغ را دام جانش كرده بود
entries.english: Towards the well which he had designated: he had made the deep well a snare for his life

entries.farsi: مى‏شدند اين هر دو تا نزديك چاه / اينت خرگوشى چو آبى زير كاه‏
entries.english: These two were going until they neared the well. Look, a hare as water under straw.

entries.farsi: آب كاهى را به هامون مى‏برد / آب كوهى را عجب چون مى‏برد
entries.english: The water bears a blade of straw to the plain: how, I wonder, will the straw bear away a mountain?

entries.farsi: دام مكر او كمند شير بود / طرفه خرگوشى كه شيرى مى‏ربود
entries.english: The snare of his guile was a noose for the lion: a marvellous hare, who was carrying off a lion!

entries.farsi: موسيى فرعون را با رود نيل / مى‏كشد با لشكر و جمع ثقيل‏
entries.english: A Moses draws Pharaoh, with his army and mighty host, into the river Nile;

entries.farsi: پشه‏اى نمرود را با نيم پر / مى‏شكافد بى‏محابا درز سر
entries.english: A single gnat with half a wing cleaves intrepidly the suture of Nimrod’s skull.

entries.farsi: حال آن كاو قول دشمن را شنود / بين جزاى آن كه شد يار حسود
entries.english: Behold the state of him who listened to the words of his enemy, and the retribution of him who became the friend of the envious one

entries.farsi: حال فرعونى كه هامان را شنود / حال نمرودى كه شيطان را شنود
entries.english: The state of a Pharaoh who hearkened to Haman, and the state of a Nimrod who hearkened to Satan

entries.farsi: دشمن ار چه دوستانه گويدت / دام دان گر چه ز دانه گويدت‏
entries.english: Even if the enemy speaks to you in a friendly tone, know the snare, though he speaks to you of the grain.

entries.farsi: گر ترا قندى دهد آن زهر دان / گر به تن لطفى كند آن قهر دان‏
entries.english: If he gives you some candy, regard it as poison; if he does a kindness to your body, regard it as cruelty.

entries.farsi: چون قضا آيد نبينى غير پوست / دشمنان را باز نشناسى ز دوست‏
entries.english: When the result comes to pass, you see nothing but the skin: you do not distinguish enemies from friends.

entries.farsi: چون چنين شد ابتهال آغاز كن / ناله و تسبيح و روزه ساز كن‏
entries.english: Since the case is thus, begin humble supplication; set about lamenting and glorifying and fasting.

entries.farsi: ناله مى‏كن كاى تو عالم الغيوب / زير سنگ مكر بد ما را مكوب
entries.english: Lament continually, crying, “O You who well know the hidden things, do not crush us beneath the stone of evil contrivance.

entries.farsi: گر سگى كرديم اى شير آفرين / شير را مگمار بر ما زين كمين‏
entries.english: O Creator of the lion, if we have wrought currishness, do not set the lion on us from this covert.

entries.farsi: آب خوش را صورت آتش مده / اندر آتش صورت آبى منه‏
entries.english: Do not give to sweet water the form of fire; do not put upon fire the form of water.

entries.farsi: از شراب قهر چون مستى دهى / نيستها را صورت هستى دهى‏
entries.english: When You make drunk with the wine of Your wrath, You give to things non-existent the form of existence.”

entries.farsi: چيست مستى بند چشم از ديد چشم / تا نمايد سنگ گوهر پشم يشم‏
entries.english: What is drunkenness? That which binds the eye from eyesight, so that a stone appears a jewel, and wool (pashm) a jasper (yashm).

entries.farsi: چيست مستى حسها مبدل شدن / چوب گز اندر نظر صندل شدن‏
entries.english: What is drunkenness? The perversion of the senses, the change of tamarisk-wood into sandal-wood in the sight

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى هدهد و سليمان در بيان آن كه چون قضا آيد چشمهاى روشن بسته شود
sections.title_en: Story of the hoopoe and Solomon, showing that when the Divine destiny comes to pass,

entries.farsi: چون سليمان را سراپرده زدند / جمله مرغانش به خدمت آمدند
entries.english: clear eyes are sealed.

entries.farsi: هم زبان و محرم خود يافتند / پيش او يك يك به جان بشتافتند
entries.english: When the tent-pavilion was pitched for Solomon, the birds came before him to pay obeisance.

entries.farsi: جمله مرغان ترك كرده جيك جيك / با سليمان گشته افصح من اخيك‏
entries.english: They found speaking the same tongue and familiar with them: one by one they sped with soul into his presence.

entries.farsi: هم زبانى خويشى و پيوندى است / مرد با نامحرمان چون بندى است‏
entries.english: All the birds, having ceased from twittering, with Solomon became more distinct than your own brother.

entries.farsi: اى بسا هندو و ترك هم زبان / اى بسا دو ترك چون بيگانگان‏
entries.english: To speak the same tongue is a kinship and affinity: a man, with those in whom he cannot confide, is like a prisoner in chains.

entries.farsi: پس زبان محرمى خود ديگر است / هم دلى از هم زبانى بهتر است‏
entries.english: Oh, many are the Indians and Turks that speak the same tongue; oh, many the pair of Turks that are as strangers.

entries.farsi: غير نطق و غير ايما و سجل / صد هزاران ترجمان خيزد ز دل‏
entries.english: Therefore the tongue of mutual understanding is different indeed: to be one in heart is better than to be one in tongue.

entries.farsi: جمله مرغان هر يكى اسرار خود / از هنر وز دانش و از كار خود
entries.english: Without speech and without sign or scroll, hundreds of thousands of interpreters arise from the heart.

entries.farsi: با سليمان يك به يك وامى‏نمود / از براى عرضه خود را مى‏ستود
entries.english: The birds, all and each, their secrets of skill and knowledge and practice

entries.farsi: از تكبر نى و از هستى خويش / بهر آن تا ره دهد او را به پيش‏
entries.english: Were revealing, one by one, to Solomon, and were praising themselves by way of submitting a request,
Not from pride and self-conceit, in order that he might give them access to him.

entries.farsi: چون ببايد برده‏اى را خواجه‏اى / عرضه دارد از هنر ديباجه‏اى‏
entries.english: When a captive wants a lord, he offers a preface of his talent;

entries.farsi: چون كه دارد از خريداريش ننگ / خود كند بيمار و كر و شل و لنگ‏
entries.english: When he is ashamed (disgusted) at his buying him, he makes himself out to be sick and palsied and deaf and lame.

entries.farsi: نوبت هدهد رسيد و پيشه‏اش / و آن بيان صنعت و انديشه‏اش‏
entries.english: The turn came for the hoopoe and his craft and the explanation of his skill and thoughtfulness.

entries.farsi: گفت اى شه يك هنر كان كهتر است / باز گويم گفت كوته بهتر است‏
entries.english: “O king,” said he, “I will declare one talent, which is an inferior one ; it is better to speak briefly.”

entries.farsi: گفت بر گو تا كدام است آن هنر / گفت من آن گه كه باشم اوج بر
entries.english: “Tell on,” said Solomon; “let me hear what talent that is.” The hoopoe said, “At the time when I am at the zenith,

entries.farsi: بنگرم از اوج با چشم يقين / من ببينم آب در قعر زمين‏
entries.english: I gaze from the zenith with the eye of certainty and I see the water at the bottom of the earth,

entries.farsi: تا كجايست و چه عمق استش چه رنگ / از چه مى‏جوشد ز خاكى يا ز سنگ‏
entries.english: So that where it is and what is its depth; what its colour is, whence it gushes forth—from clay or from rock.

entries.farsi: اى سليمان بهر لشكرگاه را / در سفر مى‏دار اين آگاه را
entries.english: O Solomon, for the sake of your army’s camping place keep this wise one on your expeditions.”

entries.farsi: پس سليمان گفت اى نيكو رفيق / در بيابانهاى بى‏آب عميق‏
entries.english: Then said Solomon, “O good companion in waterless far-stretching wastes!”

sections.title_fa: طعنه‏ى زاغ در دعوى هدهد
sections.title_en: How the crow impugned the claim of the hoopoe.

entries.farsi: زاغ چون بشنود آمد از حسد / با سليمان گفت كاو كژ گفت و بد
entries.english: When the crow heard, from jealousy he came and said to Solomon, “He has spoken false and ill.

entries.farsi: از ادب نبود به پيش شه مقال / خاصه خود الف دروغين و محال‏
entries.english: It is not respectful to speak in the king’s presence, in particular lying and absurd self-praise.

entries.farsi: گر مر او را اين نظر بودى مدام / چون نديدى زير مشتى خاك دام‏
entries.english: If he had always had this sight, how would not he have seen the snare beneath a handful of earth?

entries.farsi: چون گرفتار آمدى در دام او / چون قفس اندر شدى ناكام او
entries.english: How would he have been caught in the snare? How would he have gone into the cage willy-nilly? “

entries.farsi: پس سليمان گفت اى هدهد رواست / كز تو در اول قدح اين درد خاست‏
entries.english: Then Solomon said: “O hoopoe is it right that these dregs have risen from you at the first cup?

entries.farsi: چون نمايى مستى اى خورده تو دوغ / پيش من الفى زنى آن گه دروغ‏
entries.english: O you who have drunk buttermilk, how do you pretend intoxication and brag in my presence and tell lies besides?”

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن هدهد طعنه‏ى زاغ را
sections.title_en: The hoopoe’s answer to the attack of the crow

entries.farsi: گفت اى شه بر من عور گداى / قول دشمن مشنو از بهر خداى‏
entries.english: He said, “O king, for God’s sake do not listen to the enemy’s words against me, bare beggar as I am.

entries.farsi: گر به بطالن است دعوى كردنم / من نهادم سر ببر اين گردنم‏
entries.english: If this which I claim is not true, I lay my head: sever this neck of mine.

entries.farsi: زاغ كاو حكم قضا را منكر است / گر هزاران عقل دارد كافر است‏
entries.english: The crow, who disbelieves in the authority of the Divine destiny, is an infidel, though he have thousands of wits.

entries.farsi: در تو تا كافى بود از كافران / جاى گند و شهوتى چون كاف ران‏
entries.english: While there is in you a single k from the kafiran (infidels), you are the seat of stench and lust, the crack between the legs.

entries.farsi: من ببينم دام را اندر هوا / گر نپوشد چشم عقلم را قضا
entries.english: I see the snare in the air, if the Divine destiny does not muffle the eye of my intelligence.

entries.farsi: چون قضا آيد شود دانش به خواب / مه سيه گردد بگيرد آفتاب‏
