entries.english: When the Divine destiny comes, wisdom goes to sleep, the moon becomes black, the sun is stopped.

entries.farsi: از قضا اين تعبيه كى نادر است / از قضا دان كاو قضا را منكر است‏
entries.english: How is this disposal by the Divine destiny singular?’ Know that it is by the Divine destiny that he disbelieves in the Divine destiny.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى آدم عليه السلام و بستن قضا نظر او را از مراعات صريح نهى و ترك تاويل
sections.title_en: The story of Adam, on whom be peace, and how the Divine destiny sealed up his sight so that he failed to observe the plain meaning of the prohibition and to refrain from interpreting it.

entries.farsi: بو البشر كاو علم الاسما بگ است / صد هزاران علمش اندر هر رگ است‏
entries.english: The father of mankind, who is the lord of He taught the Names, has hundreds of thousands of sciences in every vein.

entries.farsi: اسم هر چيزى چنان كان چيز هست / تا به پايان جان او را داد دست‏
entries.english: To his soul accrued the name of every thing, even as that thing exists unto the end.

entries.farsi: هر لقب كاو داد آن مبدل نشد / آن كه چستش خواند او كاهل نشد
entries.english: No title that he gave became changed: that one whom he called ‘brisk’ did not become ‘lazy.’

entries.farsi: هر كه آخر مومن است اول بديد / هر كه آخر كافر او را شد پديد
entries.english: Whoso is a believer at the last, he saw at the first; whoso is an infidel at the last, to him it became manifest.

entries.farsi: اسم هر چيزى تو از دانا شنو / سر رمز علم الاسما شنو
entries.english: Do you hear the name of every thing from the knower: hear the inmost meaning of the mystery of He taught the Names.

entries.farsi: اسم هر چيزى بر ما ظاهرش / اسم هر چيزى بر خالق سرش
entries.english: With us, the name of every thing is its outward; with the Creator, the name of every thing is its inward.

entries.farsi: نزد موسى نام چوبش بد عصا / نزد خالق بود نامش اژدها
entries.english: In the eyes of Moses the name of his rod was ‘staff ’; in the eyes of the Creator its name was ‘dragon’.

entries.farsi: بد عمر را نام اينجا بت پرست / ليك مومن بود نامش در الست‏
entries.english: Here the name of Umar was ‘idolater,’ but in Alast his name was ‘believer.’

entries.farsi: آن كه بد نزديك ما نامش منى / پيش حق اين نقش بد كه با منى‏
entries.english: That of which the name, with us, was ‘seed’ was, in the sight of God, you who are at this moment beside me.

entries.farsi: صورتى بود اين منى اندر عدم / پيش حق موجود نه بيش و نه كم‏
entries.english: This ‘seed’ was a form in non-existence, existent with God, neither more nor less.

entries.farsi: حاصل آن آمد حقيقت نام ما / پيش حضرت كان بود انجام ما
entries.english: In brief, that which is our end is really our name with God.

entries.farsi: مرد را بر عاقبت نامى نهد / نه بر آن كاو عاريت نامى نهد
entries.english: He bestows on a man a name according to his final state, not according to that to which He gives the name of ‘a loan.’

entries.farsi: چشم آدم چون به نور پاك ديد / جان و سر نامها گشتش پديد
entries.english: Inasmuch as the eye of Adam saw by means of the Pure Light, the soul and inmost sense of the names became evident to him.

entries.farsi: چون ملك انوار حق در وى بيافت / در سجود افتاد و در خدمت شتافت
entries.english: Since the angels perceived in him the rays of God, they fell in worship and hastened to do homage.

entries.farsi: مدح اين آدم كه نامش مى‏برم / قاصرم گر تا قيامت بشمرم‏
entries.english: The Adam like this whose name I am celebrating, if I praise till the Resurrection, I fall short.

entries.farsi: اين همه دانست و چون آمد قضا / دانش يك نهى شد بر وى خطا
entries.english: All this he knew; when the Divine destiny came, he was at fault in the knowledge of a single prohibition,

entries.farsi: در دلش تاويل چون ترجيح يافت / طبع در حيرت سوى گندم شتافت‏
entries.english: Wondering whether the prohibition was for the purpose of making unlawful, or whether it admitted of an interpretation and was a cause of perplexity.

entries.farsi: كاى عجب نهى از پى تحريم بود / يا به تاويلى بد و توهيم بود
entries.english: When interpretation prevailed in his heart, his nature hastened in bewilderment towards the wheat

entries.farsi: باغبان را خار چون در پاى رفت / دزد فرصت يافت، كالا برد تفت‏
entries.english: When the thorn went into the foot of the gardener, the thief found an opportunity and quickly carried off the goods.

entries.farsi: چون ز حيرت رست باز آمد به راه / ديد برده دزد رخت از كارگاه‏
entries.english: As soon as he escaped from bewilderment, he returned into the road; he saw that the thief had carried off the dress from the bazaar stall.

entries.farsi: ربنا إنا ظلمنا گفت و آه / يعنى آمد ظلمت و گم گشت راه‏
entries.english: He cried, ‘O Lord, we have done wrong,’ and ‘Alas,’ that is to say, ‘darkness came and the way was lost.’

entries.farsi: پس قضا ابرى بود خورشيد پوش / شير و اژدرها شود زو همچو موش‏
entries.english: This Divine destiny is a cloud that covers the sun: thereby lions and dragons become as mice.

entries.farsi: من اگر دامى نبينم گاه حكم / من نه تنها جاهلم در راه حكم‏
entries.english: If I do not see a snare in the hour of Divine ordainment, it is not I alone who am ignorant in the course of Divine ordainment.”

entries.farsi: اى خنك آن كاو نكو كارى گرفت / زور را بگذاشت او زارى گرفت‏
entries.english: Oh, happy he that clung to righteousness, he let strength go and took to supplication!

entries.farsi: گر قضا پوشد سيه همچون شبت / هم قضا دستت بگيرد عاقبت‏
entries.english: If the Divine destiny shrouds you in black like night, yet the Divine destiny will take your hand at the last.

entries.farsi: گر قضا صد بار قصد جان كند / هم قضا جانت دهد درمان كند
entries.english: If the Divine destiny a hundred times attempts your life, yet the Divine destiny gives you life and heals you.

entries.farsi: اين قضا صد بار اگر راهت زند / بر فراز چرخ خرگاهت زند
entries.english: This Divine destiny, if a hundred times it waylays you, pitches your tent on the top of Heaven.

entries.farsi: از كرم دان اين كه مى‏ترساندت / تا به ملك ايمنى بنشاندت‏
entries.english: Know that this is from the loving-kindness, that He terrifies you in order that He may establish you in the kingdom of Iman.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد گشت دير / گوش كن تو قصه‏ى خرگوش و شير
entries.english: This subject has no end. It is late. Listen to the story of the hare and the lion.

sections.title_fa: پاى واپس كشيدن خرگوش از شير چون نزديك چاه رسيد
sections.title_en: How the hare drew back from the lion when he approached the well.

entries.farsi: چون كه نزد چاه آمد شير ديد / كز ره آن خرگوش ماند و پا كشيد
entries.english: When the lion came near the well, he saw that the hare lagged on the way and stepped back.

entries.farsi: گفت كو پايم كه دست و پاى رفت / جان من لرزيد و دل از جاى رفت‏
entries.english: He said, “Why have you stepped back? Do not step back, come on!”

entries.farsi: گفت پا واپس كشيدى تو چرا / پاى را واپس مكش پيش اندر آ
entries.english: The hare said, “Where is my foot? For hand and foot are gone. My soul trembles and my heart has fled.

entries.farsi: رنگ رويم را نمى‏بينى چو زر / ز اندرون خود مى‏دهد رنگم خبر
entries.english: Do you not the colour of my face as gold? My colour indeed is giving knowledge of my inward state.

entries.farsi: حق چو سيما را معرف خوانده است / چشم عارف سوى سيما مانده است‏
entries.english: Since God has called the sign informative, the eye of the gnostic has remained turned towards the sign.

entries.farsi: رنگ و بو غماز آمد چون جرس / از فرس آگه كند بانگ فرس‏
entries.english: Colour and scent are significant like a bell: the neigh of a horse makes acquainted with the horse.

entries.farsi: بانگ هر چيزى رساند زو خبر / تا بدانى بانگ خر از بانگ در
entries.english: The sound made by any thing conveys knowledge of it, so that you may distinguish the bray of an ass from the creak of a door.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر به تمييز كسان / مرء مخفى لدى طى اللسان‏
entries.english: Touching the discrimination of persons, the Prophet said, ‘A man is hidden when his tongue is folded up.’

entries.farsi: رنگ رو از حال دل دارد نشان / رحمتم كن مهر من در دل نشان‏
entries.english: The colour of the face indicates the state of the heart: have pity on me, implant love of me in your heart.

entries.farsi: رنگ روى سرخ دارد بانگ شكر / بانگ روى زرد باشد صبر و نكر
entries.english: A red complexion has the sound of thankfulness; the sound of a pale complexion is patience and deceit.

entries.farsi: در من آمد آن كه دست و پا برد / رنگ رو و قوت و سيما برد
entries.english: There has come upon me that which took away hand and foot, took away colour of face and strength and mark ;

entries.farsi: آن كه در هر چه در آيد بشكند / هر درخت از بيخ و بن او بر كند
entries.english: That which shatters every thing it comes upon, tears up every tree from root and bottom;

entries.farsi: اين خود اجزايند كليات از او / زرد كرده رنگ و فاسد كرده بو
entries.english: There has come upon me that by which man, animal, mineral and plants have been checkmated.

entries.farsi: در من آمد آن كه از وى گشت مات / آدمى و جانور جامد نبات‏
entries.english: These indeed are parts; wholes are by him made yellow in hue and corrupt in odour,

entries.farsi: تا جهان گه صابر است و گه شكور / بوستان گه حله پوشد گاه عور
entries.english: So that the world is now patient, now thankful; the garden now puts on a robe and again is bare.

entries.farsi: آفتابى كاو بر آيد نارگون / ساعتى ديگر شود او سر نگون‏
entries.english: The sun, which rises fire-coloured, at another hour, sinks headlong.

entries.farsi: اختران تافته بر چار طاق / لحظه لحظه مبتالى احتراق‏
entries.english: Stars shining in the four quarters are, from time to time, afflicted with burning.

entries.farsi: ماه كاو افزود ز اختر در جمال / شد ز رنج دق او همچون خيال‏
entries.english: The moon, which excels the stars in beauty, becomes like a phantom from the malady of a hectic fever.

entries.farsi: اى بسا كه زين بالى مرده‏ريگ / گشته است اندر جهان او خرد و ريگ‏
entries.english: This earth, quiet and controlled, is thrown by earthquakes into feverish tremors.

entries.farsi: اين زمين با سكون با ادب / اندر آرد زلزله‏ش در لرز تب‏
entries.english: Oh, from this inherited woe many a mountain in the world has become tiny fragments and sand.

entries.farsi: اين هوا با روح آمد مقترن / چون قضا آيد وبا گشت و عفن‏
entries.english: This air is conjoined with the spirit, when the Divine destiny comes, it turns foul and stinking.

entries.farsi: آب خوش كاو روح را همشيره شد / در غديرى زرد و تلخ و تيره شد
entries.english: The sweet water that was a sister to the spirit, in a pool, became yellow and bitter and turbid.

entries.farsi: آتشى كاو باد دارد در بروت / هم يكى بادى بر او خواند يموت‏
entries.english: The fire that has wind in its moustache— a single puff of wind calls death upon it.

entries.farsi: حال دريا ز اضطراب و جوش او / فهم كن تبديلهاى هوش او
entries.english: The state of the sea from its agitation and commotion perceive the changes of its mind.

entries.farsi: چرخ سر گردان كه اندر جستجوست / حال او چون حال فرزندان اوست‏
entries.english: The whirling heaven, which is in seeking and searching— its state is like the state of its children;

entries.farsi: گه حضيض و گه ميانه گاه اوج / اندر او از سعد و نحسى فوج فوج‏
entries.english: Now nadir, now middle, now zenith: therein are host on host of stars fortunate and unlucky.

entries.farsi: از خود اى جزوى ز كلها مختلط / فهم مى‏كن حالت هر منبسط
entries.english: From yourself, O part made up of wholes; apprehend the state of every simple (uncompounded) thing.

entries.farsi: چون كه كليات را رنج است و درد / جزو ايشان چون نباشد روى زرد
entries.english: Inasmuch as wholes suffer grief and pain, how should their part not be pale-faced?

entries.farsi: خاصه جزوى كاو ز اضداد است جمع / ز آب و خاك و آتش و باد است جمع‏
entries.english: Especially a part which is composed of contraries— of water and earth and fire and air

entries.farsi: اين عجب نبود كه ميش از گرگ جست / اين عجب كاين ميش دل در گرگ بست‏
entries.english: It is no wonder that the sheep recoiled from the wolf; the wonder is that this sheep set its heart on the wolf.

entries.farsi: زندگانى آشتى ضدهاست / مرگ آن كاندر ميانشان جنگ خاست‏
entries.english: Life is the peace of contraries; death is the fact that war arose between them.

entries.farsi: لطف حق اين شير را و گور را / الف داده ست اين دو ضد دور را
entries.english: The grace of God has given amity to this lion and wild-ass these two far distant contraries.

entries.farsi: چون جهان رنجور و زندانى بود / چه عجب رنجور اگر فانى بود
entries.english: Since the world is sick and a prisoner, what wonder if the sick one is passing away?”

entries.farsi: خواند بر شير او از اين رو پندها / گفت من پس مانده‏ام زين بندها
entries.english: From this point of view he recited counsels to the lion. “I have lagged behind,” said he, “because of these bonds.”

sections.title_fa: پرسيدن شير از سبب پاى واپس كشيدن خرگوش‏
sections.title_en: How the lion asked the reason of the hare’s drawing back.

entries.farsi: شير گفتش تو ز اسباب مرض / اين سبب گو خاص كاين استم غرض‏
entries.english: The lion said to him, “Amongst the causes of your malady tell the special cause, for this is my object.”

entries.farsi: قعر چه بگزيد هر كى عاقل است / ز آن كه در خلوت صفاهاى دل است‏
entries.english: “That lion,” he said, “lives in this well: within this fortress he is safe from harms.”

entries.farsi: گفت آن شير اندر اين چه ساكن است / اندر اين قلعه ز آفات ايمن است‏
entries.english: Every one who is wise chose the bottom of the well, because spiritual joys are in solitude.

entries.farsi: ظلمت چه به كه ظلمتهاى خلق / سر نبرد آن كس كه گيرد پاى خلق‏
entries.english: The darkness of the well is better than the dark shades of the world: he that followed at the heels of the world never saved his head.

entries.farsi: گفت پيش آ زخمم او را قاهر است / تو ببين كان شير در چه حاضر است‏
entries.english: “Come on,” said the lion; “my blow subdues him: see you whether that lion is in the well at present.”

entries.farsi: گفت من سوزيده‏ام ز آن آتشى / تو مگر اندر بر خويشم كشى‏
entries.english: The hare answered, “ I am consumed with that fieriness: perhaps you will take me beside you,

entries.farsi: تا بپشت تو من اى كان كرم / چشم بگشايم به چه در بنگرم‏
entries.english: That with your support, O mine of generosity, I may open my eyes and look into the well.”

sections.title_fa: نظر كردن شير در چاه و ديدن عكس خود را و آن خرگوش را
sections.title_en: How the lion looked into the well and saw the reflection of himself and the hare in the water.

entries.farsi: چون كه شير اندر بر خويشش كشيد / در پناه شير تا چه مى‏دويد
entries.english: When the lion took him to his side, under the lion’s protection he began to run towards the well.

entries.farsi: چون كه در چه بنگريدند اندر آب / اندر آب از شير و او در تافت تاب‏
entries.english: As soon as they looked at the water in the well, there shone forth in the water the light from the lion and him.

entries.farsi: شير عكس خويش ديد از آب تفت / شكل شيرى در برش خرگوش زفت‏
entries.english: The lion saw his own reflection: from the water shone the image of a lion with a plump hare at his side.

entries.farsi: چون كه خصم خويش را در آب ديد / مر و را بگذاشت و اندر چه جهيد
entries.english: When he beheld his adversary in the water, he left him and sprang into the well.

entries.farsi: در فتاد اندر چهى كاو كنده بود / ز آن كه ظلمش در سرش آينده بود
entries.english: He fell into the well which he had dug, because his iniquity was coming on his own head.

entries.farsi: چاه مظلم گشت ظلم ظالمان / اين چنين گفتند جمله عالمان‏
entries.english: The iniquity of evil-doers became a dark well: so have said all the wise.

entries.farsi: هر كه ظالمتر چهش با هول‏تر / عدل فرموده ست بدتر را بتر
entries.english: The more iniquitous one is, the more frightful is his well: Justice has ordained worse for worse.

entries.farsi: اى كه تو از ظلم چاهى مى‏كنى / دان كه بهر خويش دامى مى‏كنى‏
entries.english: O you who from iniquity are digging a well, you are making a snare for yourself.

entries.farsi: گرد خود چون كرم پيله بر متن / بهر خود چه مى‏كنى اندازه كن‏
entries.english: Do not weave round yourself, like the silkworm. You are digging a well for yourself: dig with moderation.

entries.farsi: مر ضعيفان را تو بى‏خصمى مدان / از نبى ذا جاء نصر اللهَّ خوان‏
entries.english: Deem not the weak to be without a champion: recite from the Qur’an, When the help of God shall come.

entries.farsi: گر تو پيلى خصم تو از تو رميد / نك جزا طيرا ابابيلت رسيد
entries.english: If you are an elephant and your foe fled from you, lo, the retribution came upon you, birds in flocks.

entries.farsi: گر ضعيفى در زمين خواهد امان / غلغل افتد در سپاه آسمان‏
entries.english: If any poor man on the earth begs for mercy, a loud tumult falls on the Host of Heaven.

entries.farsi: گر بدندانش گزى پر خون كنى / درد دندانت بگيرد چون كنى
entries.english: If you bite him with your teeth and make him bleed, toothache will attack you—how will you do?

entries.farsi: شير خود را ديد در چه وز غلو / خويش را نشناخت آن دم از عدو
entries.english: The lion saw himself in the well, and in his fury he did not know himself at that moment from the enemy.

entries.farsi: عكس خود را او عدوى خويش ديد / ال جرم بر خويش شمشيرى كشيد
entries.english: He regarded his own reflection as his enemy: necessarily he drew a sword against himself.

entries.farsi: اى بسا ظلمى كه بينى از كسان / خوى تو باشد در ايشان اى فالن‏
entries.english: Oh, many an iniquity that you see in others is your own nature in them, O reader!

entries.farsi: اندر ايشان تافته هستى تو / از نفاق و ظلم و بد مستى تو
entries.english: In them shone forth all that you are in your hypocrisy and iniquity and insolence.

entries.farsi: آن تويى و آن زخم بر خود مى‏زنى / بر خود آن دم تار لعنت مى‏تنى‏
entries.english: You are that, and you are striking those blows at yourself: it is yourself you are cursing at that moment.

entries.farsi: در خود آن بد را نمى‏بينى عيان / ور نه دشمن بوديى خود را به جان
entries.english: You do not see clearly the evil in yourself, else you would hate yourself with your soul.

entries.farsi: حمله بر خود مى‏كنى اى ساده مرد / همچو آن شيرى كه بر خود حمله كرد
entries.english: You are assaulting yourself, O simpleton, like the lion who made a rush at himself.

entries.farsi: چون به قعر خوى خود اندر رسى / پس بدانى كز تو بود آن ناكسى‏
entries.english: When you reach the bottom of your own nature, then you will know that that vileness was from yourself.

entries.farsi: شير را در قعر پيدا شد كه بود / نقش او آن كش دگر كس مى‏نمود
entries.english: At the bottom it became manifest to the lion that he who seemed to him to be another was his own image.

entries.farsi: اى بديده عكس بد بر روى عم / بد نه عم است آن تويى از خود مرم‏
entries.english: Whoever tears out the teeth of a poor wretch is doing what the falsely-seeing lion did.

entries.farsi: هر كه دندان ضعيفى مى‏كند / كار آن شير غلط بين مى‏كند
entries.english: O you who see the bad reflection on the face of your uncle, it is not your uncle that is bad, it is you: do not run away from yourself!

entries.farsi: مومنان آيينه‏ى همديگرند / اين خبر مى‏از پيمبر آورند
entries.english: The Faithful are mirrors to one another: this saying is related from the Prophet.

entries.farsi: پيش چشمت داشتى شيشه‏ى كبود / ز آن سبب عالم كبودت مى‏نمود
entries.english: You held a blue glass before your eye: for that reason the world seemed to you to be blue.

entries.farsi: گر نه كورى اين كبودى دان ز خويش / خويش را بد گو، مگو كس را تو بيش‏
entries.english: Unless you are blind, know that this blueness comes from yourself: speak ill of yourself, speak no more ill of any one.

entries.farsi: مومن ار ينظر بنور اللهَّ نبود / غيب مومن را برهنه چون نمود
entries.english: If the true believer was not seeing by the Light of God, how did things unseen appear naked to the true believer?

entries.farsi: چون كه تو ينظر بنار اللهَّ بدى / در بدى از نيكويى غافل شدى‏
entries.english: Inasmuch as you were seeing by the Fire of God, you did not discern the difference between good and evil.

entries.farsi: اندك اندك آب بر آتش بزن / تا شود نار تو نور اى بو الحزن‏
entries.english: Little by little throw water on the fire, that your fire may become light, O man of sorrow!

entries.farsi: تو بزن يا ربنا آب طهور / تا شود اين نار عالم جمله نور
entries.english: Throw, O Lord, the purifying water, that this world-fire may become wholly light.

entries.farsi: آب دريا جمله در فرمان تست / آب و آتش اى خداوند آن تست‏
entries.english: All the water of the sea is under Your command; water and fire, O Lord, are Yours.

entries.farsi: گر تو خواهى آتش آب خوش شود / ور نخواهى آب هم آتش شود
entries.english: If You will, fire becomes sweet water; and if You will not, even water becomes fire.

entries.farsi: اين طلب در ما هم از ايجاد تست / رستن از بى‏داد يا رب داد تست‏
entries.english: This search in us is also brought into existence by You; deliverance from iniquity is Your gift, O Lord.

entries.farsi: بى‏طلب تو اين طلب‏مان داده‏اى / گنج احسان بر همه بگشاده‏اى‏
entries.english: Without seeking You have given us this search, You have given gifts without number and end.

sections.title_fa: مژده بردن خرگوش سوى نخجيران كه شير در چاه افتاد
sections.title_en: How the hare brought to the beasts of chase the news that the lion had fallen into the well.

entries.farsi: چون كه خرگوش از رهايى شاد گشت / سوى نخجيران دوان شد تا به دشت‏
entries.english: When the hare was gladdened by deliverance, he began to run towards the beasts until the desert.

entries.farsi: دست مى‏زد چون رهيد از دست مرگ / سبز و رقصان در هوا چون شاخ و برگ‏
entries.english: Having seen the lion miserably slain in the well, he was skipping joyously all the way to the meadow,

entries.farsi: شير را چون ديد در چه كشته زار / چرخ مى‏زد شادمان تا مرغزار
entries.english: Clapping his hands because he had escaped from the hand of Death; fresh and dancing in the air, like bough and leaf.

entries.farsi: شاخ و برگ از حبس خاك آزاد شد / سر بر آورد و حريف باد شد
entries.english: Bough and leaf were set free from the prison of earth, lifted their heads, and became comrades of the wind;

entries.farsi: برگها چون شاخ را بشكافتند / تا به بالاى درخت اشتافتند
entries.english: The leaves, when they had burst the bough, made haste to reach the top of the tree;

entries.farsi: با زبان شطاه شكر خدا / مى‏سرايد هر بر و برگى جدا
entries.english: With the tongue of (seed that put forth) its sprouts each fruit and tree severally is singing thanks to God,

entries.farsi: كه بپرورد اصل ما را ذو العطا / تا درخت استغلظ آمد و استوى
entries.english: Saying, “The Bounteous Giver nourished our root until the tree grew big and stood upright.”

entries.farsi: جانهاى بسته اندر آب و گل / چون رهند از آب و گلها شاد دل‏
entries.english: The spirits bound in clay, when they escape glad at heart from their clay,

entries.farsi: در هواى عشق حق رقصان شوند / همچو قرص بدر بى‏نقصان شوند
entries.english: Begin to dance in the air of Divine Love and become flawless like the full moon’s orb,

entries.farsi: جسمشان در رقص و جانها خود مپرس / و آن كه گرد جان از آنها خود مپرس‏
entries.english: Their bodies dancing, and their souls—nay, do not ask; and those things from which comes the soul’s delight—nay, do not ask!

entries.farsi: شير را خرگوش در زندان نشاند / ننگ شيرى كاو ز خرگوشى بماند
entries.english: The hare lodged the lion in prison. Shame on a lion who was discomfited by a hare!

entries.farsi: در چنان ننگى و آن گه اين عجب / فخر دين خواهد كه گويندش لقب‏
entries.english: He is in such a disgrace, and still—this is a wonder— he would fain be addressed by the title of Fakhr-i Din.

entries.farsi: اى تو شيرى در تك اين چاه فرد / نفس چون خرگوش خونت ريخت و خورد
entries.english: O you lion that lies at the bottom of this lonely well, your hare-like soul (nafs) has shed and drunk your blood;

entries.farsi: نفس خرگوشت به صحرا در چرا / تو به قعر اين چه چون و چرا
entries.english: Your hare-soul is feeding in the desert; you art at the bottom of this well of “How?” and “Why?”

entries.farsi: سوى نخجيران دويد آن شير گير / كابشروا يا قوم إذ جاء البشير
entries.english: That lion-catcher ran towards the beasts, crying, “Rejoice, O people, since the announcer of joy is come.

entries.farsi: مژده مژده اى گروه عيش‏ساز / كان سگ دوزخ به دوزخ رفت باز
entries.english: Glad news! Glad news, O company of merry-makers! That hell-hound has gone back to Hell.

entries.farsi: مژده مژده كان عدوى جانها / كند قهر خالقش دندانها
entries.english: Glad news! Glad news! The enemy of your lives— his teeth have been torn out by the vengeance of his Creator.

entries.farsi: آن كه از پنجه بسى سرها بكوفت / همچو خس جاروب مرگش هم بروفت‏
entries.english: He who smote many heads with his claws— him too the broom of Death has swept away like rubbish.”

sections.title_fa: جمع شدن نخجيران گرد خرگوش و ثنا گفتن او را
sections.title_en: How the beasts gathered round the hare and spoke in praise of him.

entries.farsi: جمع گشتند آن زمان جمله وحوش / شاد و خندان از طرب در ذوق و جوش
entries.english: Then all the wild beasts assembled, joyous and laughing gleefully in rapture and excitement.

entries.farsi: حلقه كردند او چو شمعى در ميان / سجده آوردند و گفتندش كه هان‏
entries.english: They formed a ring, he in the midst like a candle: all the animals of the desert bowed to him.

entries.farsi: تو فرشته‏ى آسمانى يا پرى / نى تو عزراييل شيران نرى‏
entries.english: “Are you a heavenly angel or a peri? No, you art the Azrael of fierce lions.

entries.farsi: هر چه هستى جان ما قربان تست / دست بردى دست و بازويت درست‏
entries.english: Whatever you art, our souls are offered in sacrifice to you. You have prevailed. Health to your hand and arm!

entries.farsi: باز گو تا چون سگاليدى به مكر / آن عوان را چون بماليدى به مكر
entries.english: God turned this water into your stream. Blessing on your hand and arm!

entries.farsi: راند حق اين آب را در جوى تو / آفرين بر دست و بر بازوى تو
entries.english: Explain how you did meditate with guile, and how you did guilefully wipe out that ruffian.

entries.farsi: باز گو تا قصه درمانها شود / باز گو تا مرهم جانها شود
entries.english: Explain, in order that the tale may be the means of curing explain that it may be a salve for our souls.

entries.farsi: باز گو كز ظلم آن استم نما / صد هزاران زخم دارد جان ما
entries.english: Explain! For in consequence of the iniquity of that tyrant our souls have myriads of wounds.”

entries.farsi: گفت تاييد خدا بود اى مهان / ور نه خرگوشى كه باشد در جهان‏
entries.english: “O Sirs,” said he, “it was God’s aid; else, who in the world is a hare?

entries.farsi: قوتم بخشيد و دل را نور داد / نور دل مر دست و پا را زور داد
entries.english: He bestowed power on me and gave light to my heart: the light in my heart gave strength to hand and foot.”

entries.farsi: از بر حق مى‏رسد تفضيلها / باز هم از حق رسد تبديلها
entries.english: From God come preferments, from God also come changes.

entries.farsi: حق به دور و نوبت اين تاييد را / مى‏نمايد اهل ظن و ديد را
entries.english: God in course and turn is ever displaying this aid to doubters and seers.

entries.farsi: هين به ملك نوبتى شادى مكن / اى تو بسته‏ى نوبت آزادى مكن‏
entries.english: Take heed! Do not exult in a kingdom bestowed in turns. O you who are the bondsman of Vicissitude, do not act as though you were free!

entries.farsi: آن كه ملكش برتر از نوبت تنند / برتر از هفت انجمش نوبت زنند
entries.english: Those for whom is prepared a kingdom beyond change, for them the drums are beaten beyond the Seven Planets.

entries.farsi: برتر از نوبت ملوك باقى‏اند / دور دايم روحها با ساقى‏اند
entries.english: Beyond change are the kings everlasting: their spirits are circling with the Cupbearer perpetually.

entries.farsi: ترك اين شرب ار بگويى يك دو روز / در كنى اندر شراب خلد پوز
entries.english: If you will renounce this drinking for a day or two, you will dip your mouth in the drink of Paradise.

sections.title_fa: تفسير رجعنا من الجهاد الاصغر الى الجهاد الاكبر
sections.title_en: Commentary on “We have returned from the lesser jihad to the greater jihad.”

entries.farsi: اى شهان كشتيم ما خصم برون / ماند خصمى زو بتر در اندرون‏
entries.english: O kings, we have slain the outward enemy, there remains within a worse enemy than he.

entries.farsi: كشتن اين كار عقل و هوش نيست / شير باطن سخره‏ى خرگوش نيست‏
entries.english: To slay this is not the work of reason and intelligence: the inward lion is not subdued by the hare.

entries.farsi: دوزخ است اين نفس و دوزخ اژدهاست / كاو به درياها نگردد كم و كاست‏
entries.english: This carnal self (nafs) is Hell, and Hell is a dragon which is not diminished by oceans.

entries.farsi: هفت دريا را در آشامد هنوز / كم نگردد سوزش آن خلق سوز
entries.english: It would drink up the Seven Seas, and still the blazing of that consumer of all creatures would not become less.

entries.farsi: سنگها و كافران سنگ دل / اندر آيند اندر او زار و خجل‏
entries.english: Stones and stony-hearted infidels enter it, miserable and shamefaced,

entries.farsi: هم نگردد ساكن از چندين غذا / تا ز حق آيد مر او را اين ندا
entries.english: Still it is not appeased by all this food, until there comes to it from God this call

entries.farsi: سير گشتى سير گويد نى هنوز / اينت آتش اينت تابش اينت سوز
entries.english: “Are you filled, are you filled?” It says, “Not yet; lo, here is the fire, here is the glow, here is the burning!”

entries.farsi: عالمى را لقمه كرد و در كشيد / معده‏اش نعره زنان هَلْ مِنْ مزيد
entries.english: It made a mouthful of and swallowed a whole world, its belly crying aloud, “Is there any more?”

entries.farsi: حق قدم بر وى نهد از ال مكان / آن گه او ساكن شود از كن فكان‏
entries.english: God, from where place is not, sets His foot on it: then it subsides at Be, and it was.

entries.farsi: چون كه جزو دوزخ است اين نفس ما / طبع كل دارد هميشه جزوها
entries.english: Inasmuch as this self of ours is a part of Hell, and all parts have the nature of the whole,

entries.farsi: اين قدم حق را بود كاو را كشد / غير حق خود كى كمان او كشد
entries.english: To God belongs this foot to kill it: who, indeed, but God should draw its bow?

entries.farsi: در كمان ننهند الا تير راست / اين كمان را باژگون كژ تيرهاست‏
entries.english: Only the straight arrow is put on the bow, this bow has arrows bent back and crooked.

entries.farsi: راست شو چون تير و واره از كمان / كز كمان هر راست بجهد بى‏گمان‏
entries.english: Be straight, like an arrow, and escape from the bow, for without doubt every straight will fly from the bow.

entries.farsi: چون كه واگشتم ز پيكار برون / روى آوردم به پيكار درون‏
entries.english: When I turned back from the outer warfare, I set my face towards the inner warfare.

entries.farsi: قد رجعنا من جهاد الاصغريم / با نبى اندر جهاد اكبريم‏
entries.english: We have returned from the lesser Jihad, we are engaged along with the Prophet in the greater Jihad.

entries.farsi: سهل شيرى دان كه صفها بشكند / شير آن است آن كه خود را بشكند
entries.english: I pray God to grant me strength and aid and boasting, that I may root up with a needle this mountain of Qaf.

entries.farsi: قوت از حق خواهم و توفيق و الف / تا به سوزن بر كنم اين كوه قاف‏
entries.english: Deem of small account the lion that breaks the ranks: the lion is he that breaks himself.

sections.title_fa: آمدن رسول روم تا نزد عمر و ديدن او كرامات عمر را
sections.title_en: How the ambassador of Rum came to the Commander of the Faithful, `Umar, may God be well-pleased with him, and witnessed the gifts of grace
with which ‘Umar, may God be well-pleased with him, was endowed.

entries.farsi: تا عمر آمد ز قيصر يك رسول / در مدينه از بيابان نغول‏
entries.english: To `Umar in Medina there came through the wide desert an ambassador from the Emperor of Rum

entries.farsi: گفت كو قصر خليفه اى حشم / تا من اسب و رخت را آن جا كشم‏
entries.english: He said, “O you attendants, where is the palace of the Caliph, that I may take my horse and baggage there?”

entries.farsi: قوم گفتندش كه او را قصر نيست / مر عمر را قصر، جان روشنى است
entries.english: The folk said to him, “He has no (jaan i roshan) palace: `Umar’s palace is an illumined spirit.

entries.farsi: گر چه از ميرى و را آوازه‏اى است / همچو درويشان مر او را كازه‏اى است‏
entries.english: Though he has renown from being Commander, he has a hut, like the poor.

entries.farsi: اى برادر چون ببينى قصر او / چون كه در چشم دلت رسته ست مو
entries.english: O brother, how will you behold his palace, when hair has grown in the eye of your heart?

entries.farsi: چشم دل از مو و علت پاك آر / و آن گهان ديدار قصرش چشم دار
entries.english: Purge your heart’s eye of hair and defect, and then hope to behold his palace.

entries.farsi: هر كه را هست از هوسها جان پاك / زود بيند حضرت و ايوان پاك‏
entries.english: Whoever has a spirit purged of desires will at once behold the Presence and the Holy Porch.

entries.farsi: چون محمد پاك شد زين نار و دود / هر كجا رو كرد وجه اللهَّ بود
entries.english: When Mohammed was purged of this fire and smoke, wherever he turned his face, was the Face of Allah.

entries.farsi: چون رفيقى وسوسه‏ى بد خواه را / كى بدانى ثم وجه اهلل را
entries.english: Inasmuch as you are a friend to the evil suggestions of the malign one, how will you know: There is the Face of Allah?

entries.farsi: هر كه را باشد ز سينه فتح باب / او ز هر شهرى ببيند آفتاب‏
entries.english: Every one in whose breast the gate is opened will behold from every city the sun.

entries.farsi: حق پديد است از ميان ديگران / همچو ماه اندر ميان اختران‏
entries.english: God is manifest amongst others as the moon amidst the stars.

entries.farsi: دو سر انگشت بر دو چشم نه / هيچ بينى از جهان انصاف ده‏
entries.english: Lay two finger-ends on your two eyes, and will you see any part of the world? Deal justly.

entries.farsi: گر نبينى اين جهان معدوم نيست / عيب جز ز انگشت نفس شوم نيست‏
entries.english: If you do not see this world, it is not non-existent: The fault lies not save in the finger of your evil self.

entries.farsi: تو ز چشم انگشت را بردار هين / و آن گهانى هر چه مى‏خواهى ببين‏
entries.english: Come, lift the finger from your eye, and then behold whatever you wish.

entries.farsi: نوح را گفتند امت كو ثواب / گفت او ز آن سوى و استغشوا ثياب‏
entries.english: To Noah his people said, ‘Where is the Divine recompense?’ He said, ‘On the other side of they cover themselves with their garments.

entries.farsi: رو و سر در جامه‏ها پيچيده‏ايد / ال جرم با ديده و ناديده‏ايد
entries.english: You have wrapped your faces and heads in your clothes: of necessity you have eyes and see not.

entries.farsi: آدمى ديد است و باقى پوست است / ديد آن است آن كه ديد دوست است‏
entries.english: Man is eye, and the rest is skin: the sight of that is seeing the Beloved (Doost).

entries.farsi: چون كه ديد دوست نبود كور به / دوست كاو باقى نباشد دور به‏
entries.english: When there is not sight of the Beloved, it is better blind; the beloved who is not everlasting is better afar.”

entries.farsi: ديده را بر جستن عمر گماشت / رخت را و اسب را ضايع گذاشت‏
entries.english: When the ambassador of Rum admitted these fresh words into his hearing, he became more full of longing.

entries.farsi: چون رسول روم اين الفاظ تر / در سماع آورد شد مشتاق‏تر
entries.english: He fixed his eye on seeking `Umar, he let his baggage and horse be lost

entries.farsi: هر طرف اندر پى آن مرد كار / مى‏شدى پرسان او ديوانه‏وار
entries.english: He was going in every direction after that man of accomplishment, inquiring madly for him,

entries.farsi: كاين چنين مردى بود اندر جهان / وز جهان مانند جان باشد نهان
entries.english: Saying, “Can there be in the world such a man, and he be hid, like the spirit, from the world?”

entries.farsi: جست او را تاش چون بنده بود / ال جرم جوينده يابنده بود
entries.english: He sought him that he might be as a slave to him: inevitably the seeker is a finder.

entries.farsi: ديد اعرابى زنى او را دخيل / گفت عمر نك به زير آن نخيل‏
entries.english: An Arab woman of the desert saw that he was a stranger-guest. “Look,” said she, “there is `Umar under that palm.

entries.farsi: زير خرما بن ز خلقان او جدا / زير سايه خفته بين سايه‏ى خدا
entries.english: There he is under the palm-tree, apart from the people: behold the Shadow of God asleep in the shade!”

sections.title_fa: يافتن رسول روم عمر را خفته در زير درخت
sections.title_en: How the ambassador of Rum found the Commander of the Faithful, Umar, may God be well-pleased with him, sleeping under the palm-tree.

entries.farsi: آمد او آن جا و از دور ايستاد / مر عمر را ديد و در لرز اوفتاد
entries.english: He came thither and stood afar off; he saw `Umar and fell a-trembling.

entries.farsi: هيبتى ز آن خفته آمد بر رسول / حالتى خوش كرد بر جانش نزول‏
entries.english: Awe came upon the ambassador from that slumbering man, a sweet ecstasy lodged in his soul.

entries.farsi: مهر و هيبت هست ضد همدگر / اين دو ضد را ديد جمع اندر جگر
entries.english: Love and awe are contrary to each other: he saw these two contraries united in his heart.

entries.farsi: از شهانم هيبت و ترسى نبود / هيبت اين مرد هوشم را ربود
entries.english: He said to himself: “I have seen kings, I have been great and chosen in the presence of sultans:

entries.farsi: رفته‏ام در بيشه‏ى شير و پلنگ / روى من ز يشان نگردانيد رنگ‏
entries.english: I had no awe or dread of kings, awe of this man has robbed me of my wits.

entries.farsi: گفت با خود من شهان را ديده‏ام / پيش سلطانان مه و بگزيده‏ام‏
entries.english: I have gene into a jungle of lions and leopards, and my face did not change colour because of them;

entries.farsi: بس شده‏ستم در مصاف و كارزار / همچو شير آن دم كه باشد كار زار
entries.english: Often where the ranks are arrayed on the field of battle have I become as a lion at the time when the affair is grievous;

entries.farsi: بس كه خوردم بس زدم زخم گران / دل قوى تر بوده‏ام از ديگران‏
entries.english: Many a heavy blow have I suffered and inflicted, I have been stouter in heart than the others.

entries.farsi: بى‏سالح اين مرد خفته بر زمين / من به هفت اندام لرزان چيست اين‏
entries.english: This man is asleep on the earth, unarmed; I am trembling in my seven limbs: what is this?

entries.farsi: هر كه ترسيد از حق و تقوى گزيد / ترسد از وى جن و انس و هر كه ديد
entries.english: This is awe of God, it is not from created beings, and it is not awe of this man who wears the frock of a dervish.

entries.farsi: هيبت حق است اين از خلق نيست / هيبت اين مرد صاحب دلق نيست‏
entries.english: Whoever is afraid of God and has chosen fear of God, the Jinn and mankind and every one who sees are afraid of him.”

entries.farsi: اندر اين فكرت به حرمت دست بست / بعد يك ساعت عمر از خواب جست‏
entries.english: Thus meditating, he folded his hands reverently. After a while `Umar sprang up from sleep.

sections.title_fa: سالم كردن رسول روم بر عمر
sections.title_en: How the ambassador of Rum saluted the Commander of the Faithful, may God be well-pleased with him.

entries.farsi: كرد خدمت مر عمر را و سالم / گفت پيغمبر سالم آن گه كالم‏
entries.english: He did homage to `Umar and salaamed: the Prophet said,) the salaam, then the talk (kallam).”

entries.farsi: پس عليكش گفت و او را پيش خواند / ايمنش كرد و به پيش خود نشاند
entries.english: Then he said, “Greetings,” Umar called him forward, reassured him, and bade him sit down by his side.

entries.farsi: ال تخافوا هست نزل خايفان / هست در خور از براى خايف آن‏
entries.english: Fear ye not is the hospitality offered to those who fear: that is proper for one who is afraid.

entries.farsi: هر كه ترسد مر و را ايمن كنند / مر دل ترسنده را ساكن كنند
entries.english: When any one is afraid, they make him secure; they soothe fearful heart.

entries.farsi: آن كه خوفش نيست چون گويى مترس / درس چه دهى نيست او محتاج درس‏
entries.english: How should you say “Fear not “to one who has no fear? Why give lessons? He needs no lessons.

entries.farsi: آن دل از جا رفته را دل شاد كرد / خاطر ويرانش را آباد كرد
entries.english: He made that disturbed mind of good cheer and made his desolate heart flourishing.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفتش سخنهاى دقيق / وز صفات پاك حق نعم الرفيق‏
entries.english: Afterwards he addressed him with subtle discourses and the holy attributes of God—how good a Friend is He!

entries.farsi: وز نوازشهاى حق ابدال را / تا بداند او مقام و حال را
entries.english: And of the lovingkindnesses of God to the Abdal, in order that he might know maqam and hal

entries.farsi: حال چون جلوه ست ز آن زيبا عروس / وين مقام آن خلوت آمد با عروس‏
entries.english: The hal is like the unveiling of that beauteous bride, while the maqam is the being alone with the bride.

entries.farsi: جلوه بيند شاه و غير شاه نيز / وقت خلوت نيست جز شاه عزيز
entries.english: The unveiling is witnessed by the king and by others as well, at the time of being alone there is no one except the mighty king.

entries.farsi: جلوه كرده خاص و عامان را عروس / خلوت اندر شاه باشد با عروس‏
entries.english: The bride unveils before commons and nobles; in the bridal chamber the king is with the bride.

entries.farsi: هست بسيار اهل حال از صوفيان / نادر است اهل مقام اندر ميان‏
entries.english: There is many a one of the Sufis, who enjoys hal; he that has attained to maqam is rare amongst them.

entries.farsi: از منازلهاى جانش ياد داد / وز سفرهاى روانش ياد داد
entries.english: He reminded him of the stages traversed by the soul, and he reminded him of the journeys of the spirit,

entries.farsi: وز زمانى كز زمان خالى بده ست / وز مقام قدس كه اجلالی بده ست‏
entries.english: And of the Time which has been void of time, and of the Station of Holiness which has been majestic,

entries.farsi: وز هوايى كاندر او سيمرغ روح / پيش از اين ديده ست پرواز و فتوح‏
entries.english: And of the atmosphere wherein the Simurgh of the spirit, before this, has flown and experienced grace,

entries.farsi: هر يكى پروازش از آفاق بيش / وز اميد و نهمت مشتاق بيش‏
entries.english: Every single flight thereof greater than the horizons and greater than the hope and greed of the longing lover

entries.farsi: شيخ كامل بود و طالب مشتهى / مرد چابك بود و مركب درگهى‏
entries.english: When `Umar found the stranger in appearance a friend, he found his soul seeking the mysteries.

entries.farsi: چون عمر اغيار رو را يار يافت / جان او را طالب اسرار يافت‏
entries.english: The Shaykh was adept and the disciple eager: the man was quick and the beast belonged to the royal court.

entries.farsi: ديد آن مرشد كه او ارشاد داشت / تخم پاك اندر زمين پاك كاشت‏
entries.english: That spiritual guide perceived that he possessed guidance: he sowed the good seed in the good soil.

sections.title_fa: سؤال كردن رسول روم از عمر
sections.title_en: How the ambassador of Rum questioned the Commander of the Faithful, may God be well-pleased with him

entries.farsi: مرد گفتش كاى امير المؤمنين / جان ز بالا چون در آمد در زمين‏
entries.english: The man said to him, “O Commander of the Faithful, how did the spirit come to the earth from above?

entries.farsi: مرغ بى‏اندازه چون شد در قفص / گفت حق بر جان فسون خواند و قصص
entries.english: How did the infinite bird go into the cage? “He replied, “God recited spells and incantations over the spirit.

entries.farsi: بر عدمها كان ندارد چشم و گوش / چون فسون خواند همى‏آيد به جوش‏
entries.english: When He recites spells over the non-existences which have no eye or ear, they begin to stir.

entries.farsi: از فسون او عدمها زود زود / خوش معلق مى‏زند سوى وجود
entries.english: Because of His spells the non-existences at that very moment are dancing joyously into existence.

entries.farsi: باز بر موجود افسونى چو خواند / زو دو اسبه در عدم موجود راند
entries.english: When, again, He recited a spell over the existent, at His word the existent marched immediately into non-existence.

entries.farsi: گفت در گوش گل و خندانش كرد / گفت با سنگ و عقيق كانش كرد
entries.english: He spoke into the ear of the rose and made it laugh; He spoke to the stone and made it a cornelian of the mine.

entries.farsi: گفت با جسم آيتى تا جان شد او / گفت با خورشيد تا رخشان شد او
entries.english: He spoke to the body a sign, so that it became spirit; He spoke to the sun, so that it became radiant.

entries.farsi: باز در گوشش دمد نكته‏ى مخوف / در رخ خورشيد افتد صد كسوف
entries.english: Again He puts into its ear a fearful saying and upon the face of the sun fall a hundred eclipses.

entries.farsi: تا به گوش ابر آن گويا چه خواند / كاو چو مشك از ديده‏ى خود اشك راند
entries.english: Consider what that Speaker chanted into the ear of the cloud, so that it poured tears from its eye, like a water skin.

entries.farsi: تا به گوش خاك حق چه خوانده است / كاو مراقب گشت و خامش مانده است
entries.english: Consider what God has chanted into the ear of the earth, so that it became regardful and has remained silent.”

entries.farsi: در تردد هر كه او آشفته است / حق به گوش او معما گفته است‏
entries.english: Whoever in perplexity is sorely troubled, God has spoken the riddle into his ear,

entries.farsi: تا كند محبوسش اندر دو گمان / آن كنم كاو گفت يا خود ضد آن‏
entries.english: That He may imprison him in two thoughts, “ Shall I do what He told or the contrary? “

entries.farsi: هم ز حق ترجيح يابد يك طرف / ز آن دو يك را بر گزيند ز آن كنف
entries.english: From God also, one side obtains the preponderance, and from that quarter he chooses one of the two.

entries.farsi: گر نخواهى در تردد هوش جان / كم فشار اين پنبه اندر گوش جان‏
entries.english: If you wouldst not have the mind of your spirit in perplexity, do not stuff this cotton-wool into your spiritual ear,

entries.farsi: تا كنى فهم آن معماهاش را / تا كنى ادراك رمز و فاش را
entries.english: So that you may understand those riddle of His, so that you may apprehend the secret sign and the open.

entries.farsi: پس محل وحى گردد گوش جان / وحى چه بود گفتنى از حس نهان
entries.english: Then the spiritual ear becomes the place where wahy descends. What is wahy (inspiration)? A speech hidden from sense-perception.

entries.farsi: گوش جان و چشم جان جز اين حس است / گوش عقل و گوش ظن زين مفلس است
entries.english: The spiritual ear and eye are other than this sense-perception, the ear of reason and the ear of opinion are destitute of this.

entries.farsi: لفظ جبرم عشق را بى‏صبر كرد / و آن كه عاشق نيست حبس جبر كرد
entries.english: The word “compulsion” (jabr) made me impatient for love’s sake, while it confined in compulsion him who is not a lover.

entries.farsi: اين معيت با حق است و جبر نيست / اين تجلى مه است اين ابر نيست‏
entries.english: This is union with God, and it is not compulsion: this is the shining forth of the moon, this is not a cloud.

entries.farsi: ور بود اين جبر جبر عامه نيست / جبر آن اماره‏ى خودكامه نيست
entries.english: And if this be compulsion, it is not the compulsion of the ordinary: it is not the compulsion of the evil-commanding self-willed.

entries.farsi: جبر را ايشان شناسند اى پسر / كه خدا بگشادشان در دل بصر
entries.english: O son, they know compulsion in whose hearts God has opened the sight.

entries.farsi: غيب و آينده بر ايشان گشت فاش / ذكر ماضى پيش ايشان گشت الش‏
entries.english: To them the unseen things of the future became manifest; to them recollection of the past became naught.

entries.farsi: اختيار و جبر ايشان ديگر است / قطره‏ها اندر صدفها گوهر است‏
entries.english: Their freewill and compulsion is different: in oyster-shells drops are pearls.

entries.farsi: هست بيرون قطره‏ى خرد و بزرگ / در صدف آن در خرد است و سترگ‏
entries.english: Outside it is a drop of water, small or great, within the shell it is a small or big pearl.

entries.farsi: طبع ناف آهو است آن قوم را / از برون خون و درونشان مشكها
entries.english: Those persons have the nature of the musk deer’s gland: externally they are blood, while within them is the fragrance of musk.

entries.farsi: تو مگو كاين مايه بيرون خون بود / چون رود در ناف مشكى چون شود
entries.english: Do not say, “This substance externally is blood: how should it become a musky perfume when it goes into the gland?”

entries.farsi: تو مگو كاين مس برون بد محتقر / در دل اكسير چون گيرد گهر
entries.english: Do not say, “This copper externally was despicable: how should it assume nobility in the heart of the elixir?”

entries.farsi: اختيار و جبر در تو بد خيال / چون در ايشان رفت شد نور جلال‏
entries.english: In you freewill and compulsion was a fancy, when it went into them it became the light of Majesty.

entries.farsi: نان چو در سفره ست باشد آن جماد / در تن مردم شود او روح شاد
entries.english: When bread is in the tablecloth it is the inanimate thing, in the human body it becomes the glad spirit.

entries.farsi: در دل سفره نگردد مستحيل / مستحيلش جان كند از سلسبيل‏
entries.english: It does not become transmuted in the heart of the tablecloth: the soul transmutes it with Salsabil.

entries.farsi: قوت جان است اين اى راست خوان / تا چه باشد قوت آن جان جان‏
entries.english: O you who read correctly, such is the power of the soul: what, then, must be the power of that Soul of soul?

entries.farsi: گوشت پاره‏ى آدمى با عقل و جان / مى‏شكافد كوه را با بحر و كان‏
entries.english: The piece of flesh which is Man, endowed with intelligence and soul, cleaves mountain and sea and mine

entries.farsi: زور جان كوه كن شق حجر / زور جان جان در انْشَقَّ القمر
entries.english: The strength of the mountain-riving soul is the splitting of rocks; the strength of the Soul of soul in the moon was split asunder.

entries.farsi: گر گشايد دل سر انبان راز / جان به سوى عرش سازد ترك تاز
entries.english: If the heart should open the lid of the wallet of mystery, the soul would rush towards the highest heaven.

sections.title_fa: اضافت كردن آدم آن زلت را به خويشتن كه رَبَّنا ظَلَمْناو اضافت كردن ابليس گناه خود را به خدا كه بِما أَغْ وَيْتَنِي
sections.title_en: How Adam imputed that fault to himself, saying, “O Lord, we have done wrong,” and how Iblis imputed his own sin to God, saying, “Because You have seduced me.”

entries.farsi: كرد حق و كرد ما هر دو ببين / كرد ما را هست دان پيداست اين‏
entries.english: Consider both our action and the action of God. Regard our action as existent. This is manifest.

entries.farsi: گر نباشد فعل خلق اندر ميان / پس مگو كس را چرا كردى چنان‏
entries.english: If the action of created beings be not in the midst, then say not to any one, “Why have you acted thus?”

entries.farsi: خلق حق افعال ما را موجد است / فعل ما آثار خلق ايزد است‏
entries.english: The creative act of God brings our actions into existence: our actions are the effects of the creative act of God.

entries.farsi: ناطقى يا حرف بيند يا غرض / كى شود يك دم محيط دو عرض‏
entries.english: A rational being perceives either the letter or the purpose: how should he comprehend two aspects at once?

entries.farsi: گر به معنى رفت شد غافل ز حرف / پيش و پس يك دم نبيند هيچ طرف‏
entries.english: If he goes to the spirit, he becomes unmindful of the letter: no eye sees forward and backward at the same moment.

entries.farsi: آن زمان كه پيش بينى آن زمان / تو پس خود كى ببينى اين بدان‏
entries.english: At the time when you look in front, how at the same time can you look behind you? Recognise this.

entries.farsi: چون محيط حرف و معنى نيست جان / چون بود جان خالق اين هر دوان‏
entries.english: Inasmuch as the soul does not comprehend the letter and the spirit, how should the soul be the creator of them both?

entries.farsi: حق محيط جمله آمد اى پسر / وا ندارد كارش از كار دگر
entries.english: O son, God comprehends both: the action does not hinder Him from the other action.

entries.farsi: گفت شيطان كه بِما أغويتني / كرد فعل خود نهان ديو دنى‏
entries.english: Satan said Because You have seduced me: the vile Devil concealed his own act.

entries.farsi: گفت آدم كه ظلمنا نفسنا / او ز فعل حق نبد غافل چو ما
entries.english: Adam said We have done wrong unto ourselves: he was not, like us, ignorant of the action of God.

entries.farsi: در گنه او از ادب پنهانش كرد / ز آن گنه بر خود زدن او بر بخورد
entries.english: From respect he concealed it in the sin: by casting the sin upon himself he ate fruit.

entries.farsi: بعد توبه گفتش اى آدم نه من / آفريدم در تو آن جرم و محن‏
entries.english: After his repentance, He said to him, “O Adam, did not I create in you that sin and tribulations?

entries.farsi: نه كه تقدير و قضاى من بد آن / چون به وقت عذر كردى آن نهان‏
entries.english: Was it not My foreordainment and destiny? How did you conceal that at the time of excusing yourself?”

entries.farsi: گفت ترسيدم ادب نگذاشتم / گفت هم من پاس آنت داشتم‏
entries.english: He said, “I was afraid I did not let respect go.” He said, “I too have observed it towards you.”

entries.farsi: هر كه آرد حرمت او حرمت برد / هر كه آرد قند لوزينه خورد
entries.english: Whoever brings reverence gets reverence: whoever brings sugar eats almond-cake.

entries.farsi: طيبات از بهر كه للطيبين / يار را خوش كن برنجان و ببين‏
entries.english: For whom are the good women? For the good men. Treat your friend with honour; offend and see.

entries.farsi: يك مثال اى دل پى فرقى بيار / تا بدانى جبر را از اختيار
entries.english: O heart, bring a parable for the sake of a difference, that you may know compulsion from freewill.

entries.farsi: دست كان لرزان بود از ارتعاش / و آن كه دستى را تو لرزانى ز جاش‏
entries.english: A hand that is shaking from tremor and a person whose hand you cause to shake from its place.

entries.farsi: ز آن پشيمانى كه لرزانيدى‏اش / مرتعش را كى پشيمان ديدى‏اش‏
entries.english: Know that both movements are created by God, but it is impossible to compare the latter with the former.

entries.farsi: هر دو جنبش آفريده‏ى حق شناس / ليك نتوان كرد اين با آن قياس‏
entries.english: You are sorry for having caused it to shake: how is the man afflicted with tremor not sorry?

entries.farsi: بحث عقل است اين چه عقل آن حيله‏گر / تا ضعيفى ره برد آن جا مگر
entries.english: This is the intellectual quest. What is quest, O ingenious one? That perchance a man of weak understanding may find his way to that place.

entries.farsi: بحث عقلى گر در و مرجان بود / آن دگر باشد كه بحث جان بود
entries.english: The intellectual quest, though it be pearls and coral, is other than the spiritual quest.

entries.farsi: بحث جان اندر مقامى ديگر است / باده‏ى جان را قوامى ديگر است‏
entries.english: The spiritual quest is on another plane: the spiritual wine has another consistency.

entries.farsi: آن زمان كه بحث عقلى ساز بود / اين عمر با بو الحكم هم راز بود
entries.english: At the time when the intellectual quest was in keeping, this `Umar was intimate with Bu ‘l-Hakam,

entries.farsi: سوى حس و سوى عقل او كامل است / گر چه خود نسبت به جان او جاهل است‏
entries.english: When `Umar went away from intellect towards spirit, Bu ‘1-Hakam became Bu Jahl in searching into that.

entries.farsi: چون عمر از عقل آمد سوى جان / بو الحكم بو جهل شد در حكم آن‏
entries.english: He is perfect on the side of sense-perception and understanding, though indeed he is ignorant in regard to the spirit.

entries.farsi: بحث عقل و حس اثر دان يا سبب / بحث جانى يا عجب يا بو العجب‏
entries.english: Know that the quest of the intellect and the senses is effects or secondary causes. The spiritual quest is either wonder or the father of wonder.

entries.farsi: ضوء جان آمد نماند اى مستضى / الزم و ملزوم و نافى مقتضى‏
entries.english: The illumination of the spirit comes: there remains not, O you who seek illumination, conclusion and premise or that which contradicts that which renders necessary,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه بينايى كه نورش بازغ است / از دليل چون عصا بس فارغ است
entries.english: Because the seer on whom His Light is dawning is quite independent of the proof which resembles a staff.

sections.title_fa: ْتفسير وَ هُوَ مَعَكُمْ أَيْنَ ما كُنْتُم
sections.title_en: Commentary on “And He is with you wherever you be.”

entries.farsi: بار ديگر ما به قصه آمديم / ما از آن قصه برون خود كى شديم‏
entries.english: Once more we come back to the tale: when, indeed, did we go forth from the tale?

entries.farsi: گر به جهل آييم آن زندان اوست / ور به علم آييم آن ايوان اوست‏
entries.english: If we come to ignorance, that is His prison, and if we come to knowledge, that is His palace;

entries.farsi: ور به خواب آييم مستان وى‏ايم / ور بگرييم ابر پر زرق وى‏ايم
entries.english: And if we come to sleep, we are His intoxicated ones; and if to wakefulness, we are in His hands;

entries.farsi: ور بخنديم آن زمان برق وى‏ايم‏ / ور به خشم و جنگ عكس قهر اوست
entries.english: And if we weep, we are a cloud laden with the bounty dispensed by Him; and if we laugh, at that time we are His lightning;

entries.farsi: ور به صلح و عذر عكس مهر اوست‏ / ما كه‏ايم اندر جهان پيچ پيچ
entries.english: And if to wrath and war, it is the reflection of His Might; and if to peace and forgiveness, it is the reflection of His Love.

entries.farsi: چون الف او خود چه دارد هيچ هيچ‏
entries.english: Who are we? In this tangled world what indeed has He like alif? Nothing, nothing

sections.title_fa: سؤال كردن رسول روم از عمر از سبب ابتالى ارواح با اين آب و گل اجساد
sections.title_en: How the ambassador asked Umar, may God be well-pleased with him, concerning the cause of the tribulation suffered by spirits in these bodies of clay.

entries.farsi: گفت يا عمر چه حكمت بود و سر / حبس آن صافى در اين جاى كدر
entries.english: He said, “O `Umar, what was the wisdom and mystery of imprisoning that pure one in this dirty place?

entries.farsi: آب صافى در گلى پنهان شده / جان صافى بسته‏ى ابدان شده‏
entries.english: The pure water has become hidden in mud: the pure spirit has become bound in bodies.”

entries.farsi: گفت تو بحثى شگرفى مى‏كنى / معنيى را بند حرفى مى‏كنى‏
entries.english: He said, “You are making a profound inquiry, you are confining a meaning in a word.

entries.farsi: حبس كردى معنى آزاد را / بند حرفى كرده اى تو ياد را
entries.english: You have imprisoned the free meaning; you have bound the wind in a word.

entries.farsi: از براى فايده اين كرده‏اى / تو كه خود از فايده در پرده‏اى‏
entries.english: This you have done for a benefit, O you who yourself are blind to the benefit of God.

entries.farsi: آن كه از وى فايده زاييده شد / چون نبيند آن چه ما را ديده شد
entries.english: He from whom benefit was born, how should He not see that which was seen by us?

entries.farsi: صد هزاران فايده ست و هر يكى / صد هزاران پيش آن يك اندكى‏
entries.english: There are myriads of benefits, and every myriad is a few beside that one.

entries.farsi: آن دم نطقت كه جزو جزوهاست / فايده شد كل كل خالى چراست‏
entries.english: The breath of your speech, which is a part of the parts, became beneficial: why is the whole of the whole devoid?

entries.farsi: تو كه جزوى كار تو با فايده ست / پس چرا در طعن كل آرى تو دست‏
entries.english: You who are a part—your act is beneficial: why do you lift your hand to assail the whole?

entries.farsi: گفت را گر فايده نبود مگو / ور بود هل اعتراض و شكر جو
entries.english: If there is no benefit in speech, do not speak; and if there is, leave off making objections, and endeavour to give thanks.”

entries.farsi: شكر يزدان طوق هر گردن بود / نه جدال و رو ترش كردن بود
entries.english: Thanksgiving to God is a collar on every neck; it is not to dispute and make one’s face look sour.

entries.farsi: گر ترش رو بودن آمد شكر و بس / پس چو سركه شكر گويى نيست كس‏
entries.english: If thanksgiving is only to look sour, then there is no thanks-giver like vinegar.

entries.farsi: سركه را گر راه بايد در جگر / گو بشو سركنگبين او از شكر
entries.english: If vinegar wants the way to the liver, let it become oxymel by sugar.

entries.farsi: معنى اندر شعر جز با خبط نيست / چون قالسنگ است اندر ضبط نيست‏
entries.english: The meaning in poetry has no sureness of direction: it is like the sling, it is not under control.

sections.title_fa: در معنى آن كه من أراد أن يجلس مع اللهَّ فليجلس مع أهل التصوف
sections.title_en: On the inner sense of “Let him who desires to sit with God sit with the Sufis.”

entries.farsi: آن رسول از خود بشد زين يك دو جام / نه رسالت ياد ماندش نه پيام‏
entries.english: The ambassador became beside himself from these one or two cups: neither his title nor message remained in his memory.

entries.farsi: واله اندر قدرت اهلل شد / آن رسول اينجا رسيد و شاه شد
entries.english: He became distraught at the power of God. The ambassador arrived at this place and became a king.

entries.farsi: سيل چون آمد به دريا بحر گشت / دانه چون آمد به مزرع گشت كشت‏
entries.english: When the torrent reached the sea, it became the sea; when the seed reached the wheat field, it became the crop of wheat.

entries.farsi: چون تعلق يافت نان با بو البشر / نان مرده زنده گشت و با خبر
entries.english: When the bread attained to connection with the animal the dead bread became living and endowed with knowledge.

entries.farsi: موم و هيزم چون فداى نار شد / ذات ظلمانى او انوار شد
entries.english: When the wax and firewood were devoted to the fire, their dark essence became light.

entries.farsi: سنگ سرمه چون كه شد در ديده‏گان / گشت بينايى شد آن جا ديدبان‏
entries.english: When the stone of antimony went into the eyes, it turned to sight and there became a scout.

entries.farsi: اى خنك آن مرد كز خود رسته شد / در وجود زنده‏اى پيوسته شد
entries.english: Oh, happy is the man who was freed from himself and united with the existence of a living one!

entries.farsi: واى آن زنده كه با مرده نشست / مرده گشت و زندگى از وى بجست‏
entries.english: Alas for the living one who consorted with the dead! He became dead, and life sped away from him.

entries.farsi: چون تو در قرآن حق بگريختى / با روان انبيا آميختى‏
entries.english: When you have fled to the Qur’an of God, you have mingled with the spirit of the prophets.

entries.farsi: هست قرآن حالهاى انبيا / ماهيان بحر پاك كبريا
entries.english: The Qur’an is the states of the prophets, the fishes of the holy sea of Majesty.

entries.farsi: ور بخوانى و نه‏اى قرآن پذير / انبيا و اوليا را ديده گير
entries.english: And if you read and do not accept he Qur’an, suppose you have seen the prophets and saints?

entries.farsi: ور پذيرايى چو بر خوانى قصص / مرغ جانت تنگ آيد در قفص‏
entries.english: But if you are accepting, when you read the stories, the bird, your soul, will be distressed in its cage.

entries.farsi: مرغ كاو اندر قفس زندانى است / مى‏نجويد رستن از نادانى است‏
entries.english: The bird that is a prisoner in a cage, is not seeking to escape, it is from ignorance.

entries.farsi: روحهايى كز قفسها رسته‏اند / انبياى رهبر شايسته‏اند
entries.english: The spirits which have escaped from their cages are the prophets, worthy guides.

entries.farsi: از برون آوازشان آيد ز دين / كه ره رستن ترا اين است اين‏
entries.english: From without comes their voice, of religion, “This, this is the way of escape for you.

entries.farsi: ما به دين رستيم زين ننگين قفس / جز كه اين ره نيست چاره‏ى اين قفس‏
entries.english: By this we escaped from this narrow cage: there is no means of escape from this cage but this way,

entries.farsi: خويش را رنجور سازى زار زار / تا ترا بيرون كنند از اشتهار
entries.english: You should make yourself ill, exceedingly wretched, in order that you may be let out from reputation.”

entries.farsi: كه اشتهار خلق بند محكم است / در ره اين از بند آهن كى كم است‏
entries.english: Worldly reputation is a strong chain: in the Way how is this less than a chain of iron?

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى بازرگان كه طوطى محبوس او او را پيغام داد به طوطيان هندوستان هنگام رفتن به تجارت
sections.title_en: The story of the merchant to whom the parrot gave a message for the parrots of India on the occasion of his going to trade

entries.farsi: بود بازرگانی او را طوطيى / در قفس محبوس زيبا طوطيى‏
entries.english: There was a merchant, and he had a parrot imprisoned in a cage, a beautiful parrot.

entries.farsi: چون كه بازرگان سفر را ساز كرد / سوى هندستان شدن آغاز كرد
entries.english: When the merchant made ready for travel and was about to depart to India,

entries.farsi: هر غالم و هر كنيزك را ز جود / گفت بهر تو چه آرم گوى زود
entries.english: Because of his generosity he said to each male slave and each handmaid, “What shall I bring for you? Tell quickly.”

entries.farsi: هر يكى از وى مرادى خواست كرد / جمله را وعده بداد آن نيك مرد
entries.english: Each one asked him for some object of desire: that good man gave his promise to them all.

entries.farsi: گفت طوطى را چه خواهى ارمغان / كارمت از خطه‏ى هندوستان‏
entries.english: He said to the parrot, “What present would you like me to bring for you from the land of India?”

entries.farsi: گفتش آن طوطى كه آن جا طوطيان / چون ببينى كن ز حال من بيان‏
entries.english: The parrot said, “When you see the parrots there, explain my state,

entries.farsi: كان فالن طوطى كه مشتاق شماست / از قضاى آسمان در حبس ماست‏
entries.english: ‘Such and such a parrot, who is longing for you, is in my prison by the destiny of Heaven.

entries.farsi: بر شما كرد او سالم و داد خواست / وز شما چاره و ره ارشاد خواست‏
entries.english: She salutes you and asks for justice and desires from you the means and way of being rightly guided.

entries.farsi: گفت مى‏شايد كه من در اشتياق / جان دهم اينجا بميرم در فراق‏
entries.english: She says, “Is it proper that I in yearning should give up the ghost and die here in separation?

entries.farsi: اين روا باشد كه من در بند سخت / گه شما بر سبزه گاهى بر درخت‏
entries.english: Is this right—I in grievous bondage, while ye are now on green plants, now on trees?

entries.farsi: اين چنين باشد وفاى دوستان / من در اين حبس و شما در بوستان‏
entries.english: The faith kept by friends, is it like this?— I in this prison and you in the rose-garden.

entries.farsi: ياد آريد اى مهان زين مرغ زار / يك صبوحى در ميان مرغزار
entries.english: O you noble ones, call to mind this piteous bird, a morning-draught amongst the meadows!

entries.farsi: ياد ياران يار را ميمون بود / خاصه كان ليلى و اين مجنون بود
entries.english: Happy it is for a friend to be remembered by friends, in particular when that is Layla and this Majnun.

entries.farsi: اى حريفان بت موزون خود / من قدحها مى‏خورم پر خون خود
entries.english: O you who consort with your charming and adored one, am I to drink cups filled with my own blood?

entries.farsi: يك قدح مى نوش كن بر ياد من / گر همى‏خواهى كه بدهى داد من‏
entries.english: Quaff one cup of wine in memory of me, if you desire to do me justice,

entries.farsi: يا به ياد اين فتاده‏ى خاك بيز / چون كه خوردى جرعه اى بر خاك ريز
entries.english: Or, when you have drunk, spill one draught on the earth in memory of this fallen one who sifts dust.

entries.farsi: اى عجب آن عهد و آن سوگند كو / وعده‏هاى آن لب چون قند كو
entries.english: Oh, where, I wonder, is that covenant and oath? Where are the promises of that lip like candy?

entries.farsi: گر فراق بنده از بد بندگى است / چون تو با بد بد كنى پس فرق چيست‏
entries.english: If your having forsaken your slave is because of ill service— when you do ill to the ill-doer, then what is the difference

entries.farsi: اى بدى كه تو كنى در خشم و جنگ / با طرب تر از سماع و بانگ چنگ‏
entries.english: Oh, the ill you do in wrath and quarrel is more delightful than music and the sound of the harp.

entries.farsi: اى جفاى تو ز دولت خوبتر / و انتقام تو ز جان محبوبتر
entries.english: Oh, your cruelty is better than felicity, and your vengeance dearer than life.

entries.farsi: نار تو اين است نورت چون بود / ماتم اين تا خود كه سورت چون بود
entries.english: This is your fire: how must be your light! This is mourning, so how indeed must be your festival!

entries.farsi: از حالوتها كه دارد جور تو / وز لطافت كس نيابد غور تو
entries.english: In respect of the sweetness which your cruelty has, and in respect of your beauty, no one gets to the bottom of you.

entries.farsi: نالم و ترسم كه او باور كند / وز كرم آن جور را كمتر كند
entries.english: I complain, and I fear lest he believe me and from kindness make that cruelty less

entries.farsi: عاشقم بر قهر و بر لطفش به جد / بو العجب من عاشق اين هر دو ضد
entries.english: I am exceedingly enamoured of his violence and his gentleness: it is marvellous I in love with both these contraries.

entries.farsi: و اهلل ار زين خار در بستان شوم / همچو بلبل زين سبب نالان شوم‏
entries.english: By God, if from this thorn and enter the garden, because of this I shall begin to moan like the nightingale.

entries.farsi: اين عجب بلبل كه بگشايد دهان / تا خورد او خار را با گلستان‏
entries.english: This is a wondrous nightingale that opens his mouth to eat thorns and roses together

entries.farsi: اين چه بلبل اين نهنگ آتشى است / جمله ناخوشها ز عشق او را خوشى است‏
entries.english: What nightingale is this? it is a fiery monster : because of love all sour things are sweet to him.

entries.farsi: عاشق كل است و خود كل است او / عاشق خويش است و عشق خويش جو
entries.english: He is a lover of the Universal, and he himself is the Universal: he is in love with himself and seeking his own love.”

sections.title_fa: صفت اجنحه‏ى طيور عقول الهى
sections.title_en: Description of the wings of the birds that are Divine Intelligences

entries.farsi: قصه‏ى طوطى جان زين سان بود / كو كسى كو محرم مرغان بود
entries.english: Such-like is the tale of the parrot which is the soul: where is that one who is the confidant of birds?

entries.farsi: كو يكى مرغى ضعيفى بى‏گناه / و اندرون او سليمان با سپاه‏
entries.english: Where is a bird, weak and innocent, and within him Solomon with his host?

entries.farsi: چون بنالد زار بى‏شكر و گله / افتد اندر هفت گردون غلغله‏
entries.english: When he moans bitterly, without thanksgiving or complaint, a noise of tumult falls on the Seven Spheres

entries.farsi: هر دمش صد نامه صد پيك از خدا / يا ربى زو شصت لبيك از خدا
entries.english: At every moment to him from God a hundred missives, a hundred couriers: from him one “O my Lord!” and from God sixty “Labbayka.”

entries.farsi: زلت او به ز طاعت نزد حق / پيش كفرش جمله ايمانها خلق‏
entries.english: In the sight of God his backsliding is better than obedience; beside his infidelity all faiths are tattered.

entries.farsi: هر دمى او را يكى معراج خاص / بر سر تاجش نهد صد تاج خاص‏
entries.english: Every moment he has ascends peculiar to him self: He lays upon his crown a hundred peculiar crowns.

entries.farsi: المكانى نه كه در فهم آيدت / هر دمى در وى خيالى زايدت
entries.english: His form is on earth and his spirit in “no-place,” a “la-makam” beyond the imagination of travellers:

entries.farsi: صورتش بر خاك و جان بر المكان / المكانى فوق وهم سالكان‏
entries.english: Not such a “no-place” that it should come into your understanding (or that) a fancy about it should be born in you every moment;

entries.farsi: بل مكان و المكان در حكم او / همچو در حكم بهشتى چارجو
entries.english: No, place and “no-place “are in his control, just as the four rivers are in the control of one who dwells in Paradise

entries.farsi: شرح اين كوته كن و رخ زين بتاب / دم مزن و اهلل اعلم بالصواب‏
entries.english: Cut short the explanation of this and avert your face from it: do not breathe a word—and God knows best what is right.

entries.farsi: باز مى‏گرديم ما اى دوستان / سوى مرغ و تاجر و هندوستان‏
entries.english: We return from this, O friends, to the bird and the merchant and India.

entries.farsi: مرد بازرگان پذيرفت اين پيام / كاو رساند سوى جنس از وى سالم‏
entries.english: The merchant accepted this message that he would convey the greeting from her to her congeners.

sections.title_fa: ديدن خواجه طوطيان هندوستان را در دشت و پيغام رسانيدن از آن طوطى
sections.title_en: How the merchant saw the parrots of India in the plain and delivered the parrot’s message.

entries.farsi: چون كه تا اقصاى هندوستان رسيد / در بيابان طوطى چندى بديد
entries.english: When he reached the farthest bounds of India, he saw a number of parrots in the plain.

entries.farsi: مركب استانيد پس آواز داد / آن سالم و آن امانت باز داد
entries.english: He halted his beast; then he gave voice, delivered the greeting and the trust.

entries.farsi: طوطيى ز آن طوطيان لرزيد بس / اوفتاد و مرد و بگسستش نفس‏
entries.english: One of those parrots trembled exceedingly, fell, and died, and its breath stopped.

entries.farsi: شد پشيمان خواجه از گفت خبر / گفت رفتم در هالك جانور
entries.english: The merchant repented of having told the news, and said, “ I have gone about to destroy the creature.

entries.farsi: اين مگر خويش است با آن طوطيك / اين مگر دو جسم بود و روح يك‏
entries.english: This one, surely, is kin to that little parrot: they must have been two bodies and one spirit.

entries.farsi: اين چرا كردم چرا دادم پيام / سوختم بى‏چاره را زين گفت خام‏
entries.english: Why did I do this? Why did I give the message? I have consumed the poor creature by this raw speech.”

entries.farsi: اين زبان چون سنگ و هم آهن‏وش است / و آن چه بجهد از زبان چون آتش است‏
entries.english: This tongue is like stone and is also fire-like, and that which springs from the tongue is like fire.

entries.farsi: سنگ و آهن را مزن بر هم گزاف / گه ز روى نقل و گاه از روى الف‏
entries.english: Do not vainly strike stone and iron against each other, now for the sake of relating, now for the sake of boasting,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه تاريك است و هر سو پنبه زار / در ميان پنبه چون باشد شرار
entries.english: Because it is dark, and on every side are fields of cotton: how should sparks be amongst cotton?

entries.farsi: ظالم آن قومى كه چشمان دوختند / ز آن سخنها عالمى را سوختند
entries.english: Iniquitous are those persons who shut their eyes and by such words set a whole world ablaze.

entries.farsi: جانها در اصل خود عيسى دمند / يك زمان زخمند و گاهى مرهمند
entries.english: A single word lays waste a world, turns dead foxes into lions.

entries.farsi: عالمى را يك سخن ويران كند / روبهان مرده را شيران كند
entries.english: Spirits in their original nature have the breath of Jesus, one breath of it is a wound, and the other a plaster.

entries.farsi: گر حجاب از جانها برخاستى / گفت هر جانى مسيح آساستى‏
entries.english: If the screen were removed from the spirits, the speech of every spirit would be like the Messiah.

entries.farsi: گر سخن خواهى كه گويى چون شكر / صبر كن از حرص و اين حلوا مخور
entries.english: If you wish to utter words like sugar, refrain from concupiscence and do not eat this sweetmeat.

entries.farsi: صبر باشد مشتهاى زيركان / هست حلوا آرزوى كودكان‏
entries.english: Self-control is the thing desired by the intelligent; sweetmeat is what children long for.

entries.farsi: هر كه صبر آورد گردون بر رود / هر كه حلوا خورد واپس‏تر رود
entries.english: Whoever practices self-control ascends to Heaven, whoever eats sweetmeat falls farther behind.

sections.title_fa: تفسير قول فريد الدين عطار قدس اللهَّ روحه: تو صاحب نفسى اى غافل ميان خاك خون مى‏خور كه صاحب دل اگر زهرى خورد آن انگبين باشد
sections.title_en: Commentary on the saying of Faridu’ddin Attar, may God sanctify his spirit—
“You are a sensualist: O heedless one, drink blood amidst the dust, For if the spiritualist drinks a poison, it will be an antidote”

entries.farsi: صاحب دل را ندارد آن زيان / گر خورد او زهر قاتل را عيان‏
entries.english: It does not harm the spiritualist though he drinks deadly poison for all to see,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه صحت يافت و از پرهيز رست / گفت پيغمبر كه اى مرد جرى
entries.english: Because he has attained to health and has been set free from abstinence, the poor seeker is in the fever.

entries.farsi: هان مكن با هيچ مطلوبى مرى‏ / در تو نمرودى است آتش در مرو
entries.english: The Prophet said, “ O bold seeker, beware! Do not contend with any one who is sought.”

entries.farsi: رفت خواهى اول ابراهيم شو / چون نه‏اى سباح و نه درياييى
entries.english: In you is a Nimrod: do not go into the fire. If you wish to go in, first become Abraham!

entries.farsi: از زيانها سود بر سر آورد / كاملى گر خاك گيرد زر شود
entries.english: When you art neither a swimmer nor a seaman, do not cast yourself from a self-conceit.

entries.farsi: در ميفكن خويش از خود راييى‏ / او ز آتش ورد احمر آورد
entries.english: He fetches pearls from the bottom of the sea; from losses he brings gain to the surface.

entries.farsi: ناقص ار زر برد خاكستر شود / چون قبول حق بود آن مرد راست
entries.english: If a perfect man takes earth, it becomes gold; if an imperfect one has carried away gold, it becomes ashes.

entries.farsi: دست او در كارها دست خداست‏ / دست ناقص دست شيطان است و ديو
entries.english: Since that righteous man is accepted of God, his hand in things is the hand of God.

entries.farsi: ز آن كه اندر دام تكليف است و ريو / جهل آيد پيش او دانش شود
entries.english: The hand of the imperfect man is the hand of Devil and demon, because he is in the trap of imposition and guile.

entries.farsi: جهل شد علمى كه در ناقص رود / هر چه گيرد علتى علت شود
entries.english: If ignorance comes to him, it becomes knowledge, the knowledge that goes into the imperfect man becomes ignorance.

entries.farsi: سر نخواهى برد اكنون پاى دار
entries.english: Whatever an ill man takes becomes illness, if a perfect man takes infidelity, it becomes religion.

entries.farsi: كفر گيرد كاملى ملت شود / اى مرى كرده پياده با سوار
entries.english: O you who, being on foot has contended with a horseman, you will not save your head. Now hold your foot!

sections.title_fa: تعظيم ساحران مر موسى را عليه السلام كه چه فرمايى اول تو اندازى عصا يا ما
sections.title_en: How the magicians paid respect to Moses, on whom be peace, saying, “What do you command? Will you cast down your rod first, or shall we?”

entries.farsi: ساحران در عهد فرعون لعين / چون مرى كردند با موسى به كين‏
entries.english: The magicians in the time of the accursed Pharaoh, when they contended with Moses in enmity,

entries.farsi: ليك موسى را مقدم داشتند / ساحران او را مكرم داشتند
entries.english: Yet gave Moses the precedence the magicians held him in honour

entries.farsi: ز آن كه گفتندش كه فرمان آن تست / گر تو مى‏خواهى عصا بفكن نخست
entries.english: Because they said to him, “ it is for you to command: you wish to be the first, cast down your rod first.”

entries.farsi: گفت نى اول شما اى ساحران / افكنيد آن مكرها را در ميان‏
entries.english: Nay,” said he, “ first, O magicians, cast down those tricks into the middle.”

entries.farsi: اين قدر تعظيم دينشان را خريد / كز مرى آن دست و پاهاشان بريد
entries.english: This amount of respect purchased their religion, so that it cut off the hands and feet of their contention.

entries.farsi: ساحران چون حق او بشناختند / دست و پا در جرم آن درباختند
entries.english: When the magicians acknowledged his right, they sacrificed their hands and feet for the sin of that.

entries.farsi: لقمه و نكته ست كامل را حالل / تو نه‏اى كامل مخور مى‏باش الل‏
entries.english: To the perfect man mouthful and saying is lawful. ‘You are not perfect: do not eat, be mute,

entries.farsi: چون تو گوشى او زبان نى جنس تو / گوشها را حق بفرمود أَنْصِ تُوا
entries.english: Inasmuch as you are an ear and he a tongue, not your congener: God said to the ears, “Be silent.”

entries.farsi: كودك اول چون بزايد شير نوش / مدتى خامش بود او جمله گوش‏
entries.english: When the sucking babe is born, at first it keeps silence for a while, it is all ear.

entries.farsi: مدتى مى‏بايدش لب دوختن / از سخن تا او سخن آموختن‏
entries.english: For a while it must close its lips from speech, until it learns to speak;

entries.farsi: ور نباشد گوش و تى‏تى مى‏كند / خويشتن را گنگ گيتى مى‏كند
entries.english: And if it is not an ear but makes babbling sounds, it makes itself the dumbest creature in the world.

entries.farsi: كر اصلى كش نبود آغاز گوش / الل باشد كى كند در نطق جوش‏
entries.english: He that is deaf by nature, he that had no ear at the beginning, is mute: how should he burst into speech?

entries.farsi: ز آن كه اول سمع بايد نطق را / سوى منطق از ره سمع اندر آ
entries.english: Since, in order to speak, one must first hear, come to speech by the way of hearing.

entries.farsi: ادخلوا األبيات من أبوابها / و اطلبوا األغراض في أسبابها
entries.english: Enter the houses by their doors, and seek you the ends in their causes.

entries.farsi: نطق كان موقوف راه سمع نيست / جز كه نطق خالق بى‏طمع نيست
entries.english: There is no speech independent of the way of hearing except the speech of the Creator who is without want.

entries.farsi: مبدع است او تابع استاد نى / مسند جمله و را اسناد نى‏
entries.english: He is the Originator, He follows no master; He is the support of all things, He has no support,

entries.farsi: باقيان هم در حرف هم در مقال / تابع استاد و محتاج مثال‏
entries.english: The rest, in handicrafts and talk, follow a master and have need of a pattern.

entries.farsi: زين سخن گر نيستى بيگانه‏اى / دلق و اشكى گير در ويرانه‏اى
entries.english: If you art not alien to this discourse, assume the frock of a dervish and tears in some deserted place,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه آدم ز آن عتاب از اشك رست / اشك تر باشد دم توبه پرست‏
entries.english: Because Adam by means of tears escaped from that reproof: moist tears are the breath of the penitent.

entries.farsi: بهر گريه آمد آدم بر زمين / تا بود گريان و نالان و حزين‏
