entries.english: For weeping’s sake Adam came to the earth, that he might be weeping and moaning and sorrowful.

entries.farsi: آدم از فردوس و از بالاى هفت / پاى ماچان از براى عذر رفت‏
entries.english: Adam, from Paradise and from above the Seven, went to the “shoe-row”” for the purpose of excusing himself

entries.farsi: گر ز پشت آدمى وز صلب او / در طلب مى‏باش هم در طلب او
entries.english: If you are from the back of Adam and from his loins, be constant in seeking amongst his company.

entries.farsi: ز آتش دل و آب ديده نقل ساز / بوستان از ابر و خورشيد است باز
entries.english: Prepare a dessert of heart-fire and eye-water: the garden is made open by cloud and sun.

entries.farsi: تو چه دانى قدر آب ديده‏گان / عاشق نانى تو چون ناديدگان‏
entries.english: What do you know of the taste of the water of the eyes? You are a lover of bread, like the blind.

entries.farsi: گر تو اين انبان ز نان خالى كنى / پر ز گوهرهاى اجلالی كنى‏
entries.english: If you make this wallet empty of bread, you will make it full of glorious jewels.

entries.farsi: تا تو تاريك و ملول و تيره‏اى / دان كه با ديو لعين همشيره‏اى‏
entries.english: Wean the babe, your soul, from the Devil’s milk, and after that make it consort with the Angel.

entries.farsi: طفل جان از شير شيطان باز كن / بعد از آنش با ملك انباز كن‏
entries.english: While you are dark and vexed and gloomy, know that you are sucking from the same breast as the accursed Devil.

entries.farsi: لقمه‏اى كان نور افزود و كمال / آن بود آورده از كسب حالل‏
entries.english: The mouthful that gave increase of light and perfection is obtained from lawful earnings.

entries.farsi: روغنى كايد چراغ ما كشد / آب خوانش چون چراغى را كشد
entries.english: The oil that comes and quenches our lamp— when it quenches a lamp, call it water.

entries.farsi: علم و حكمت زايد از لقمه‏ى حالل / عشق و رقت آيد از لقمه‏ى حالل‏
entries.english: From the lawful morsel are born knowledge and wisdom; from the lawful morsel come love and tenderness.

entries.farsi: چون ز لقمه تو حسد بينى و دام / جهل و غفلت زايد آن را دان حرام‏
entries.english: When from a morsel you see envy and guile, ignorance and heedlessness are born, know that it is unlawful.

entries.farsi: هيچ گندم كارى و جو بر دهد / ديده‏اى اسبى كه كره‏ى خر دهد
entries.english: Will you sow wheat and will it produce barley? Have you seen a mare bring forth an ass’s colt?

entries.farsi: زايد از لقمه‏ى حالل اندر دهان / ميل خدمت عزم رفتن آن جهان‏
entries.english: The morsel is seed, and thoughts are its fruit; the morsel is the sea, and thoughts are its pearls.

entries.farsi: لقمه تخم است و برش انديشه‏ها / لقمه بحر و گوهرش انديشه‏ها
entries.english: From the lawful morsel in the mouth is born the inclination to serve and the resolve to go to yonder world.

sections.title_fa: ‏باز گفتن بازرگان با طوطى آن چه ديد از طوطيان هندوستان
sections.title_en: How the merchant related to the parrot what he had witnessed on the part of the parrots of India.

entries.farsi: كرد بازرگان تجارت را تمام / باز آمد سوى منزل دوست كام‏
entries.english: The merchant finished his trading and returned home glad at heart.

entries.farsi: هر غالمى را بياورد ارمغان / هر كنيزك را ببخشيد او نشان‏
entries.english: He brought a present for every male slave; he gave a token to every slave-girl

entries.farsi: گفت طوطى ارمغان بنده كو / آن چه ديدى و آن چه گفتى باز گو
entries.english: “Where is my present?” asked the parrot. “Relate what you have said and seen.”

entries.farsi: گفت نى من خود پشيمانم از آن / دست خود خايان و انگشتان گزان‏
entries.english: “No,” said he, “indeed I am repenting of that, gnawing my hand and biting my fingers.

entries.farsi: من چرا پيغام خامى از گزاف / بردم از بى‏دانشى و از نشاف‏
entries.english: Why, from ignorance and folly, did I idly bear an inconsiderate message?”

entries.farsi: گفت اى خواجه پشيمانى ز چيست / چيست آن كاين خشم و غم را مقتضى است‏
entries.english: “O master,” said the parrot, “what is your repentance for? What is it that causes this anger and grief?”

entries.farsi: گفت گفتم آن شكايتهاى تو / با گروهى طوطيان همتاى تو
entries.english: “I told your complaints,” said he, “to a company of parrots resembling you.

entries.farsi: آن يكى طوطى ز دردت بوى برد / زهره‏اش بدريد و لرزيد و بمرد
entries.english: One parrot got scent of your pain: her heart broke”, and she trembled and died.

entries.farsi: من پشيمان گشتم اين گفتن چه بود / ليك چون گفتم پشيمانى چه سود
entries.english: I became sorry, ‘why did I say this?’ but what was the use of repenting after I had said it?”

entries.farsi: نكته اى كان جست ناگه از زبان / همچو تيرى دان كه جست آن از كمان‏
entries.english: Know that a word which suddenly shot from the tongue is like an arrow shot from the bow.

entries.farsi: وانگردد از ره آن تير اى پسر / بند بايد كرد سيلى را ز سر
entries.english: O son, that arrow does not turn back on its way: you must dam a torrent at the source.

entries.farsi: چون گذشت از سر جهانى را گرفت / گر جهان ويران كند نبود شگفت‏
entries.english: When it left the source behind, it swept over a world: if it lays waste the world, it is no wonder.

entries.farsi: فعل را در غيب اثرها زادنى است / و آن مواليدش به حكم خلق نيست‏
entries.english: There is an unseen bringing forth of effects to action, and the results born of it are not in the control of creatures:

entries.farsi: بى‏شريكى جمله مخلوق خداست / آن مواليد ار چه نسبتشان به ماست‏
entries.english: Those results are all created by God without any partner, though they are imputed to us.

entries.farsi: زيد پرانيد تيرى سوى عمر / عمر را بگرفت تيرش همچو نمر
entries.english: Zayd let fly an arrow in the direction of ‘Amr: his arrow gripped Amr like a leopard.

entries.farsi: مدت سالى همى‏زاييد درد / دردها را آفريند حق نه مرد
entries.english: During a long time, a (whole) year, it was producing pain: pains are created by God, not by man.

entries.farsi: زيد رامى آن دم ار مرد از وجل / دردها مى‏زايد آن جا تا اجل‏
entries.english: If Zayd who shot died of fright at the moment, pains are continually being produced there until death.

entries.farsi: آن وجعها را بدو منسوب دار / گر چه هست آن جمله صنع كردگار
entries.english: Inasmuch as he died from the results of the wound, for this cause call Zayd, who shot, the murderer

entries.farsi: ز آن مواليد وجع چون مرد او / زيد را ز اول سبب قتال گو
entries.english: Impute those pains to him, though all of them are the work of the Creator.

entries.farsi: همچنين كشت و دم و دام و جماع / آن مواليد است حق را مستطاع
entries.english: So with sowing, breathing, snares and sexual intercourse: the results of those are amenable to God.

entries.farsi: اوليا را هست قدرت از اله / تير جسته باز آرندش ز راه‏
entries.english: The saints possess power from God: they turn back from its course the arrow that has sped.

entries.farsi: بسته درهاى مواليد از سبب / چون پشيمان شد ولى ز آن دست رب‏
entries.english: When the saint repents, he closes the doors of the results from the cause by that hand of the Lord.

entries.farsi: گفته ناگفته كند از فتح باب / تا از آن نه سيخ سوزد نه كباب‏
entries.english: Through the opening of the door, he makes unsaid what has been said, so that neither spit nor roast-meat is burnt thereby.

entries.farsi: از همه دلها كه آن نكته شنيد / آن سخن را كرد محو و ناپديد
entries.english: He wipes out the saying from all the minds that heard it, and makes it imperceptible.

entries.farsi: گرت برهان بايد و حجت مها / باز خوان مِنْ آيَةٍ أَوْ ننسها
entries.english: O sire, if you need to have demonstration and proof, recite “verse or cause to be forgotten.”

entries.farsi: آيت أَنْسَوْكُمْ ذِكْرِي بخوان / قدرت نسيان نهادنشان بدان
entries.english: Read the verse “They made you forget My warning”: acknowledge their power to put forgetfulness.

entries.farsi: چون به تذكير و به نسيان قادراند / بر همه دلهاى خلقان قاهراند
entries.english: Since they are able to make remember and forget, they are mighty over all the hearts of creatures.

entries.farsi: چون به نسيان بست او راه نظر / كار نتوان كرد ور باشد هنر
entries.english: When he has blocked the road of mental perception by means of forgetfulness, it is impossible to act, even if there be virtue.

entries.farsi: خلتم سخريه اهل السمو / از نبى خوانيد تا أنسوكم‏
entries.english: Think you those exalted ones are a laughing-stock? Recite from the Qur’an as far as “They made you forget.”

entries.farsi: صاحب ده پادشاه جسمهاست / صاحب دل شاه دلهاى شماست‏
entries.english: He that owns a village is king over bodies; he that owns a heart is king over your hearts.

entries.farsi: فرع ديد آمد عمل بى‏هيچ شك / پس نباشد مردم الا مردمك‏
entries.english: Without any doubt, action is a branch of seeing: therefore Man is nothing but “the little man”.

entries.farsi: من تمام اين نيارم گفت از آن / منع مى‏آيد ز صاحب مركزان‏
entries.english: I dare not expound the whole of this: hindrance is coming from those who are at the centre.

entries.farsi: چون فراموشى خلق و يادشان / با وى است و او رسد فريادشان‏
entries.english: Inasmuch as the forgetfulness and recollection of creatures are with him, and he comes at their call for help,

entries.farsi: صد هزاران نيك و بد را آن بهى / مى‏كند هر شب ز دلهاشان تهى‏
entries.english: Every night that glorious one is emptying from their hearts hundreds of thousands of good and evil,

entries.farsi: روز دلها را از آن پر مى‏كند / آن صدفها را پر از در مى‏كند
entries.english: In the daytime he is filling their hearts— he is filling those oyster-shells with pearls.

entries.farsi: آن همه انديشه‏ى پيشانها / مى‏شناسند از هدايت جانها
entries.english: By guidance all those thoughts of former things recognise the spirits.

entries.farsi: پيشه و فرهنگ تو آيد به تو / تا در اسباب بگشايد به تو
entries.english: Your handicraft and skill come to you, that they may open to you the door of means.

entries.farsi: پيشه‏ى زرگر به آهنگر نشد / خوى اين خوش خوبه آن منكر نشد
entries.english: The goldsmith’s craft did not go to the ironsmith; the disposition of the good-natured man did not go to the disagreeable one.

entries.farsi: پيشه‏ها و خلقها همچون جهيز / سوى خصم آيند روز رستخيز
entries.english: On the day of Resurrection the handicrafts and dispositions will come, like articles of property, to the claimant.

entries.farsi: پيشه‏ها و خلقها از بعد خواب / واپس آيد هم به خصم خود شتاب‏
entries.english: After sleep also, the handicrafts and dispositions come back in haste to him that claims them as his.

entries.farsi: پيشه‏ها و انديشه‏ها در وقت صبح / هم بدانجا شد كه بود آن حسن و قبح‏
entries.english: At the hour of dawn the handicrafts and thoughts went to the same place where that good and evil were.

entries.farsi: چون كبوترهاى پيك از شهرها / سوى شهر خويش آرد بهرها
entries.english: Like carrier pigeons, they bring things useful from cities to their own city.

sections.title_fa: شنيدن آن طوطى حركت آن طوطيان و مردن آن طوطى در قفس و نوحه‏ى خواجه بر وى
sections.title_en: How the parrot heard what those parrots had done, and died inthe cage, and how the merchant made lament for her.

entries.farsi: چون شنيد آن مرغ كان طوطى چه كرد / پس بلرزيد اوفتاد و گشت سرد
entries.english: When the bird heard what that parrot had done, she trembled exceedingly, fell, and became cold.

entries.farsi: خواجه چون ديدش فتاده همچنين / بر جهيد و زد كله را بر زمين‏
entries.english: The merchant, seeing her thus fallen, sprang up and dashed his cap on the ground.

entries.farsi: چون بدين رنگ و بدين حالش بديد / خواجه بر جست و گريبان را دريد
entries.english: When he saw her in this guise and in this state, the merchant sprang forward and tore the breast of his garment.

entries.farsi: گفت اى طوطى خوب خوش حنين / اين چه بودت اين چرا گشتى چنين‏
entries.english: He said, “O beautiful parrot with your sweet cry, what is this that has happened to you? Why have you become like this?

entries.farsi: اى دريغا مرغ خوش آواز من / اى دريغا هم دم و هم راز من‏
entries.english: Oh, alas for my sweet-voiced bird! Oh, alas for my bosom-friend and confidant!

entries.farsi: اى دريغا مرغ خوش الحان من / راح روح و روضه و ريحان من‏
entries.english: Oh, alas for my melodious bird, the wine of my spirit and my garden and my sweet basil!

entries.farsi: گر سليمان را چنين مرغى بدى / كى خود او مشغول آن مرغان شدى
entries.english: Had Solomon possessed a bird like this, how indeed should he have become occupied with those birds?

entries.farsi: اى دريغا مرغ كارزان يافتم / زود روى از روى او بر تافتم‏
entries.english: Oh, alas for the bird which I gained cheaply and soon turned my face away from her countenance!

entries.farsi: اى زبان تو بس زيانى بر ورى / چون تويى گويا چه گويم من ترا
entries.english: O tongue, you art a great damage to me, since you art speaking, what should I say to you?

entries.farsi: اى زبان هم آتش و هم خرمنى / چند اين آتش در اين خرمن زنى‏
entries.english: O tongue, you art both the fire and the stack: how long will you dart this fire upon this stack?

entries.farsi: در نهان جان از تو افغان مى‏كند / گر چه هر چه گويى‏اش آن مى‏كند
entries.english: Secretly my soul is groaning because of you, although it is doing whatever you bid it.

entries.farsi: اى زبان هم گنج بى‏پايان تويى / اى زبان هم رنج بى‏درمان تويى‏
entries.english: O tongue, you are a treasure without end. O tongue, you are also a disease without remedy.

entries.farsi: هم صفير و خدعه‏ى مرغان تويى / هم انيس وحشت هجران تويى‏
entries.english: You are at once a whistle and decoy for birds, and a comforter in the desolation of absence.

entries.farsi: چند امانم مى‏دهى اى بى‏امان / اى تو زه كرده به كين من كمان‏
entries.english: How long will you grant me mercy, O merciless one, O you who have drawn the bow to take vengeance on me?

entries.farsi: نك بپرانيده اى مرغ مرا / در چراگاه ستم كم كن چرا
entries.english: Lo, you have made my bird fly away. Do not browse l in the pasture of injustice!

entries.farsi: يا جواب من بگو يا داد ده / يا مرا ز اسباب شادى ياد ده‏
entries.english: Either answer me or give redress or mention to me the means of joy.

entries.farsi: اى دريغا نور ظلمت سوز من / اى دريغا صبح روز افروز من‏
entries.english: Oh, alas for my darkness-consuming dawn! Oh, alas for my day-enkindling light!

entries.farsi: اى دريغا مرغ خوش پرواز من / ز انتها پريده تا آغاز من‏
entries.english: Oh, alas for my bird of goodly flight, that has flown from my end to my beginning.

entries.farsi: عاشق رنج است نادان تا ابد / خيز ال أُقْسِمُ بخوان تا فِي كبد
entries.english: The ignorant man is in love with pain unto everlasting. Arise and read I swear as far as in trouble.

entries.farsi: از كبد فارغ بدم با روى تو / وز زبد صافى بدم در جوى تو
entries.english: With your face I was free from trouble, and in your river I was unsoiled by froth.

entries.farsi: اين دريغاها خيال ديدن است / وز وجود نقد خود ببريدن است‏
entries.english: These cries of ‘Alas’ are the phantasy of seeing and separation from my present existence.

entries.farsi: غيرت حق بود و با حق چاره نيست / كو دلى كز حكم حق صد پاره نيست‏
entries.english: It was the jealousy of God, and there is no device against God: where is a heart that is not in a hundred pieces by God’s love?

entries.farsi: غيرت آن باشد كه او غير همه ست / آن كه افزون از بيان و دمدمه ست‏
entries.english: The otherness is this, that He is other than all things that He is beyond explanation and the noise of words.

entries.farsi: اى دريغا اشك من دريا بدى / تا نثار دل بر زيبا بدى‏
entries.english: Oh, alas! Would that my tears were an ocean, that they might be strewn as an offering to the fair charmer!

entries.farsi: طوطى من مرغ زيركسار من / ترجمان فكرت و اسرار من‏
entries.english: My parrot, my clever-headed bird, the interpreter of my thought and inmost consciousness,

entries.farsi: هر چه روزى داد و ناداد آيدم / او ز اول گفته تا ياد آيدم‏
entries.english: She has told me from the first that I might remember it, whatever should come to me as my allotted portion of right and wrong.”

entries.farsi: اندرون تست آن طوطى نهان / عكس او را ديده تو بر اين و آن
entries.english: The parrot whose voice comes from inspiration and whose beginning was before the beginning of existence

entries.farsi: طوطيى كايد ز وحى آواز او / پيش از آغاز وجود آغاز او
entries.english: That parrot is hidden within you: you have seen the refection of her upon this and that.

entries.farsi: مى‏برد شاديت را تو شاد از او / مى‏پذيرى ظلم را چون داد از او
entries.english: She takes away your joy, and because of her you art rejoicing: you receive injury from her as though it were justice.

entries.farsi: اى كه جان را بهر تن مى‏سوختى / سوختى جان را و تن افروختى
entries.english: O you who were burning the soul for the body’s sake, you have burned the soul and illumined the body.

entries.farsi: سوختم من سوخته خواهد كسى / تا ز من آتش زند اندر خسى‏
entries.english: I am burning: does any one want tinder, let him set his rubbish ablaze with fire from me.

entries.farsi: سوخته چون قابل آتش بود / سوخته بستان كه آتش كش بود
entries.english: Inasmuch as tinder is combustible, take tinder that catches fire.

entries.farsi: اى دريغا اى دريغا اى دريغ / كانچنان ماهى نهان شد زير ميغ‏
entries.english: O alas, O alas, O alas that such a moon became hidden under the clouds!

entries.farsi: چون زنم دم كاتش دل تيز شد / شير هجر آشفته و خون ريز شد
entries.english: How should I utter a word? For the fire in my heart is grown fierce, the lion of separation has become raging and blood-shedding.

entries.farsi: آن كه او هوشيار خود تند است و مست / چون بود چون او قدح گيرد به دست‏
entries.english: One that even when sober is violent and furious, how will it be when he takes the wine-cup in his hand?

entries.farsi: شير مستى كز صفت بيرون بود / از بسيط مرغزار افزون بود
entries.english: The furious Lion who is beyond description is too great for the wide expanse of the meadow.

entries.farsi: قافيه انديشم و دل دار من / گويدم منديش جز ديدار من‏
entries.english: I am thinking of rhymes, and my Sweetheart says to me, “Do not think of aught except vision of Me.

entries.farsi: خوش نشين اى قافيه انديش من / قافيه‏ى دولت تويى در پيش من‏
entries.english: Sit at your ease, My rhyme-meditating: in My presence you art rhymed with felicity.

entries.farsi: حرف چه بود تا تو انديشى از آن / حرف چه بود خار ديوار رزان‏
entries.english: What are words that you should think of them? What are words? Thorns in the hedge of the vineyard

entries.farsi: حرف و صوت و گفت را بر هم زنم / تا كه بى‏اين هر سه با تو دم زنم‏
entries.english: I will throw word, sound and speech into confusion, that without these three I may converse with you.

entries.farsi: آن دمى كز آدمش كردم نهان / با تو گويم اى تو اسرار جهان‏
entries.english: That word which I kept hidden from Adam I will speak to you, O consciousness of the world.

entries.farsi: آن دمى را كه نگفتم با خليل / و آن غمى را كه نداند جبرئيل‏
entries.english: That word which I did not communicate to Abraham, and that pain which Gabriel does not know.”

entries.farsi: آن دمى كز وى مسيحا دم نزد / حق ز غيرت نيز بى‏ما هم نزد
entries.english: That word, of which the Messiah breathed not a word God, from jealousy, did not utter even without we.

entries.farsi: ما چه باشد در لغت اثبات و نفى / من نه اثباتم منم بى‏ذات و نفى‏
entries.english: What is ma in language? Positive and negative. I am not positive, I am selfless and negated.

entries.farsi: جمله شاهان بنده‏ى بنده‏ى خودند / جمله خلقان مرده‏ى مرده‏ى خودند
entries.english: I found individuality in non-individuality: therefore I wove individuality into non-individuality.

entries.farsi: جمله شاهان پست، پست خويش را / جمله خلقان مست، مست خويش را
entries.english: All kings are enslaved to their slaves, all people are dead for one who dies for them.

entries.farsi: من كسى در ناكسى دريافتم / پس كسى در ناكسى دربافتم‏
entries.english: All kings are prostrate before one who is prostrate before them; all people are intoxicated with one who is intoxicated with them

entries.farsi: مى‏شود صياد، مرغان را شكار / تا كند ناگاه ايشان را شكار
entries.english: The fowler becomes a prey to the birds in order that of a sudden he may make them his prey.

entries.farsi: بى‏دالن را دلبران جسته به جان / جمله معشوقان شكار عاشقان
entries.english: The hearts of heart-ravishers are captivated by those who have lost their hearts: all loved ones are the prey of lovers.

entries.farsi: هر كه عاشق ديدى‏اش معشوق دان / كو به نسبت هست هم اين و هم آن‏
entries.english: Whomever you deemed to be a lover, regard as the loved one, for relatively he is both this and that.

entries.farsi: تشنگان گر آب جويند از جهان / آب جويد هم به عالم تشنگان‏
entries.english: If they that are thirsty seek water from the world, water too seeks in the world them that are thirsty.

entries.farsi: چون كه عاشق اوست تو خاموش باش / او چو گوشت مى‏كشد تو گوش باش‏
entries.english: Inasmuch as He is lover, be silent: as He is pulling your ear, be you ear.

entries.farsi: بند كن چون سيل سيالنى كند / ور نه رسوايى و ويرانى كند
entries.english: Dam the torrent when it runs in flood; else it will work shame and ruin.

entries.farsi: من چه غم دارم كه ويرانى بود / زير ويران گنج سلطانى بود
entries.english: What care I though ruin be? Under the ruin there is a royal treasure.

entries.farsi: غرق حق خواهد كه باشد غرق‏تر / همچو موج بحر جان زير و زبر
entries.english: He that is drowned in God wishes to be more drowned, his spirit up and down like the waves of the sea,

entries.farsi: زير دريا خوشتر آيد يا زبر / تير او دل كش تر آيد يا سپر
entries.english: “Is the bottom of the sea more delightful, or the top? Is His arrow more fascinating, or the shield?”

entries.farsi: پاره كرده‏ى وسوسه باشى دال / گر طرب را باز دانى از بال
entries.english: O heart, you are torn asunder by evil suggestion if you recognise any difference between joy and woe.

entries.farsi: گر مرادت را مذاق شكر است / بى‏مرادى نه مراد دل بر است‏
entries.english: Although the object of your desire has the taste of sugar, is not absence of any object of desire the object of the Beloved’s desire?

entries.farsi: هر ستاره‏ش خونبهاى صد هالل / خون عالم ريختن او را حالل‏
entries.english: Every star of His is the blood-price of a hundred new moons: it is lawful for Him to shed the blood of the (whole) world.

entries.farsi: اى حيات عاشقان در مردگى / دل نيابى جز كه در دل بردگى
entries.english: We gained the price and the blood-price: we hastened to gamble our soul away.

entries.farsi: ما بها و خونبها را يافتيم / جانب جان باختن بشتافتيم‏
entries.english: Oh, the life of lovers consists in death: you will not win the heart except in losing your own.

entries.farsi: من دلش جسته به صد ناز و دالل / او بهانه كرده با من از مالل‏
entries.english: I sought His heart with a hundred airs and graces, He made excuses to me in disdain.

entries.farsi: گفتم آخر غرق تست اين عقل و جان / گفت رو رو بر من اين افسون مخوان
entries.english: I said, “After all, this mind and soul are drowned in You.” “Begone,” said He, “begone ! Do not chant these spells over Me

entries.farsi: اى گران جان خوار ديده ستى و را / ز آن كه بس ارزان خريده ستى و را
entries.english: Do I not know what thought you have conceived? O you who have seen double, how have you regarded the Beloved?

entries.farsi: من ندانم آن چه انديشيده‏اى / اى دو ديده دوست را چون ديده‏اى‏
entries.english: O gross-spirited one, you have held Me in light esteem, because you have bought Me very cheaply.

entries.farsi: هر كه او ارزان خرد ارزان دهد / گوهرى طفلى به قرصى نان دهد
entries.english: He that buys cheaply gives cheaply: a child will give a pearl for a loaf of bread.”

entries.farsi: غرق عشقى‏ام كه غرق است اندر اين / عشقهاى اولين و آخرين‏
entries.english: I am drowned in a love that therein are drowned the first loves and the last.

entries.farsi: مجملش گفتم نكردم ز آن بيان / ور نه هم افهام سوزد هم زبان‏
entries.english: I have told it summarily, I have not explained it, otherwise both perceptions and tongue would be consumed.

entries.farsi: من چو لب گويم لب دريا بود / من چو ال گويم مراد الا بود
entries.english: When I speak of “lip,” it is the lip of the Sea ; when I say “not,” the intended meaning is “ except.”

entries.farsi: من ز شيرينى نشستم رو ترش / من ز بسيارى گفتارم خمش‏
entries.english: By reason of sweetness I sit with sour face: from fullness of speech I am silent,

entries.farsi: تا كه شيرينى ما از دو جهان / در حجاب رو ترش باشد نهان‏
entries.english: That in the sour-faced mask of my sweetness may be kept hidden from the two worlds.

entries.farsi: تا كه در هر گوش نايد اين سخن / يك همى‏گويم ز صد سر لدن
entries.english: In order that this subject may not come to every ear, I am telling one out of a hundred esoteric mysteries.

sections.title_fa: تفسير قول حكيم: به هرچ از راه وامانى چه كفر آن حرف و چه ايمان به هرچ از دوست دور افتى چه زشت آن نقش و چه زيبا در معنى قوله عليه السلام إن سعدا لغيور و أنا أغير من سعد و اللهَّ أغير مني و من غيرته حرم َالْفَواحِشَ ما ظَهَرَ مِنْها وَ ما بَطَن

sections.title_en: Commentary on the saying of the Hakim: “Any thing that causes you to be left behind on the Way, what matter whether it be infidelity or faith? Any form that causes you to fall far from the Beloved,

what matter whether it be ugly or beautiful?”— and on the meaning of the words of the Prophet, on whom be peace: “Verily, Sa‘ d is jealous (Ghayat), and I am more jealous (Ghayat) than Sa‘ d, and Allah is more jealous (Ghayat) than I; and because of His jealousy (Ghayat)
He hath forbidden foul actions both outward and inward.”

entries.farsi: جمله عالم ز آن غيور آمد كه حق / برد در غيرت بر اين عالم سبق‏
entries.english: The whole world became jealous (Ghayat) because God is superior to all the world in jealousy (Ghayat).

entries.farsi: او چو جان است و جهان چون كالبد / كالبد از جان پذيرد نيك و بد
entries.english: He is like the spirit, and the world is like the body: the body receives from the spirit good and evil.

entries.farsi: هر كه محراب نمازش گشت عين / سوى ايمان رفتنش مى‏دان تو شين‏
entries.english: Any one whose prayer-niche is turned to the revelation, do you regard his going to faith as shameful.

entries.farsi: هر كه شد مر شاه را او جامه‏دار / هست خسران بهر شاهش اتجار
entries.english: Any one who has become Master of the robes to the King; it is loss for him to traffic on the King’s behalf.

entries.farsi: هر كه با سلطان شود او همنشين / بر درش بودن بود حيف و غبين‏
entries.english: Any one who becomes the intimate friend of the Sultan; it is an injury and swindle to wait at his door.

entries.farsi: دست‏بوسش چون رسيد از پادشاه / گر گزيند بوس پا باشد گناه‏
entries.english: When kissing the hand has been bestowed on him by the King, it is a sin if he prefers to kiss the foot.

entries.farsi: گر چه سر بر پا نهادن خدمت است / پيش آن خدمت خطا و زلت است‏
entries.english: Although to lay the head on the foot is an act of obeisance, compared with the former act of obeisance it is a fault and backsliding.

entries.farsi: شاه را غيرت بود بر هر كه او / بو گزيند بعد از آن كه ديد رو
entries.english: The King is jealous (Ghayat) of any one who, after having seen the face, prefers the scent.

entries.farsi: غيرت حق بر مثل گندم بود / كاه خرمن غيرت مردم بود
entries.english: To speak in parables, God’s jealousy (Ghayat) is the wheat, men’s jealousy (Ghayat) is the straw in the stack.

entries.farsi: اصل غيرتها بدانيد از اله / آن خلقان فرع حق بى‏اشتباه‏
entries.english: Know that the root of jealousy (Ghayat) is in God: those of mankind are an offshoot from God, without resemblance.

entries.farsi: شرح اين بگذارم و گيرم گله / از جفاى آن نگار ده دله‏
entries.english: I will leave the explanation of this and will begin to complain of the cruelty of that fickle Beauty.

entries.farsi: نالم ايرا ناله‏ها خوش آيدش / از دو عالم ناله و غم بايدش‏
entries.english: I wail because wailings are pleasant to Him: He wants from the two worlds wailing and grief.

entries.farsi: چون ننالم تلخ از دستان او / چون نيم در حلقه‏ى مستان او
entries.english: How should I not wail bitterly on account of His deceit, since I am not in the circle of those intoxicated with Him?

entries.farsi: چون نباشم همچو شب بى‏روز او / بى‏وصال روى روز افروز او
entries.english: How should I not mourn, like night, without His day and without the favour of His day-illuming countenance?

entries.farsi: ناخوش او خوش بود در جان من / جان فداى يار دل رنجان من‏
entries.english: His sourness is sweet in my soul: may my soul be sacrificed to the Beloved who grieves my heart!

entries.farsi: عاشقم بر رنج خويش و درد خويش / بهر خشنودى شاه فرد خويش‏
entries.english: I am in love with my grief and pain for the sake of pleasing my peerless King.

entries.farsi: خاك غم را سرمه سازم بهر چشم / تا ز گوهر پر شود دو بحر چشم‏
entries.english: I make the dust of sorrow a salve for mine eye, that the two seas of mine eyes may be filled with jewels.

entries.farsi: اشك كان از بهر او بارند خلق / گوهر است و اشك پندارند خلق‏
entries.english: The tears which people shed for His sake are pearls and people think they are tears.

entries.farsi: من ز جان جان شكايت مى‏كنم / من نيم شاكى روايت مى‏كنم‏
entries.english: I am complaining of the Soul of the soul, I am not complaining: I am relating.

entries.farsi: دل همى‏گويد كز او رنجيده‏ام / وز نفاق سست مى‏خنديده‏ام
entries.english: My heart is saying, “I am tormented by Him,” and I have been laughing at its poor pretence.

entries.farsi: راستى كن اى تو فخر راستان / اى تو صدر و من درت را آستان‏
entries.english: Do right, O glory of the righteous, O You who are the dais, and I the threshold of Your door!

entries.farsi: آستان و صدر در معنى كجاست / ما و من كو آن طرف كان يار ماست‏
entries.english: Where are threshold and dais in meaning? In the quarter where our Beloved is, where are “we” and “I”?

entries.farsi: اى رهيده جان تو از ما و من / اى لطيفه‏ى روح اندر مرد و زن‏
entries.english: O You whose soul is free from “we” and “I,” O You who are the subtle essence of the spirit in man and woman,

entries.farsi: مرد و زن چون يك شود آن يك تويى / چون كه يك جا محو شد آنك تويى‏
entries.english: When man and woman become one, You are that One; when the units are wiped out, lo, You are that.

entries.farsi: اين من و ما بهر آن بر ساختى / تا تو با خود نرد خدمت باختى‏
entries.english: You did contrive this “I” and “we” in order that You might play the game of worship with Yourself,

entries.farsi: تا من و توها همه يك جان شوند / عاقبت مستغرق جانان شوند
entries.english: That all “I’s” and “you’s” should become one soul and at last should be submerged in the Beloved.

entries.farsi: اين همه هست و بيا اى امر كن / اى منزه از بيان و از سخن‏
entries.english: All this is, and do You come, O Creative Word, O You who transcends “Come” and speech!

entries.farsi: جسم جسمانه تواند ديدنت / در خيال آرد غم و خنديدنت
entries.english: The body can see You in bodily fashion: it fancies Your sadness or laughter.

entries.farsi: دل كه او بسته‏ى غم و خنديدن است / تو مگو كاو اليق آن ديدن است‏
entries.english: Do not say that the heart that is bound by sadness and laughter is worthy of seeing You.

entries.farsi: آن كه او بسته‏ى غم و خنده بود / او بدين دو عاريت زنده بود
entries.english: He who is bound by sadness and laughter is living by means of these two borrowed things.

entries.farsi: باغ سبز عشق كاو بى‏منتهاست / جز غم و شادى در او بس ميوه‏هاست
entries.english: In the verdant garden of Love, which is without end, there are many fruits besides sorrow and joy.

entries.farsi: عاشقى زين هر دو حالت برتر است / بى‏بهار و بى‏خزان سبز و تر است‏
entries.english: Love is higher than these two states of feeling: without spring and without autumn it is green and fresh.

entries.farsi: ده زكات روى خوب اى خوب رو / شرح جان شرحه شرحه باز گو
entries.english: Pay the taxes on Your fair face, O Beauteous One: relate the story of the soul that is rent in pieces,

entries.farsi: كز كرشم غمزه‏ى غمازه‏اى / بر دلم بنهاد داغى تازه‏اى‏
entries.english: For by the coquetry of a glance One who is given to glancing amorously has branded my heart anew.

entries.farsi: من حاللش كردم از خونم بريخت / من همى‏گفتم حالل او مى‏گريخت‏
entries.english: I absolved Him if He shed my blood: I was saying, “It is lawful,” and He was fleeing.

entries.farsi: اى كه هر صبحى كه از مشرق بتافت / همچو چشمه‏ى مشرقت در جوش يافت‏
entries.english: Since You are fleeing from the lament of those who are dust, why do you pour You sorrow on the hearts of the sorrowful?

entries.farsi: چون گريزانى ز ناله‏ى خاكيان / غم چه ريزى بر دل غمناكيان‏
entries.english: O You, whom every dawn that shone from the East found overflowing like the bright fountain,

entries.farsi: اى جهان كهنه را تو جان نو / از تن بى‏جان و دل افغان شنو
entries.english: How didst You give evasion to Your frenzied lover, O You the sugar of whose lips hath no price?

entries.farsi: چون بهانه دادى اين شيدات را / اى بهانه شكر لبهات را
entries.english: O You who art a new soul to the old world, hear the cry from my body without soul and heart.

entries.farsi: شرح گل بگذار از بهر خدا / شرح بلبل گو كه شد از گل جدا
entries.english: Leave the tale of the Ruse! For God’s sake set forth the tale of the Nightingale that is parted from the Rose!

entries.farsi: از غم و شادى نباشد جوش ما / با خيال و وهم نبود هوش ما
entries.english: Our emotion is not caused by grief and joy, our consciousness is not related to fancy and imagination.

entries.farsi: حالتى ديگر بود كان نادر است / تو مشو منكر كه حق بس قادر است‏
entries.english: There is another state, which is rare: do not disbelieve, for God is very mighty.

entries.farsi: تو قياس از حالت انسان مكن / منزل اندر جور و در احسان مكن‏
entries.english: Do not judge from the state of man; do not abide in wrong-doing and in well-doing.

entries.farsi: جور و احسان رنج و شادى حادث است / حادثان ميرند و حقشان وارث است‏
entries.english: Wrong-doing and well-doing, grief and joy are things that come into existence; those who come into existence die: God is their heir.

entries.farsi: صبح شد اى صبح را پشت و پناه / عذر مخدومى حسام الدين بخواه‏
entries.english: It is morning. O You who are the support and refuge of the dawn, ask pardon of my Lord Husamu’ddin!

entries.farsi: عذر خواه عقل كل و جان تويى / جان جان و تابش مرجان تويى‏
entries.english: You are He who asks pardon of the Universal Mind and Soul, You are the Soul of the soul and the Splendour of the coral.

entries.farsi: تافت نور صبح و ما از نور تو / در صبوحى با مى منصور تو
entries.english: The light of dawn has shone forth, and from Your light we are engaged in drinking the morning-drink with the wine of Your Mansur.

entries.farsi: داده‏ى تو چون چنين دارد مرا / باده كه بود كاو طرب آرد مرا
entries.english: Inasmuch as Your gift keeps me thus, who is wine that it should bring me rapture?

entries.farsi: باده در جوشش گداى جوش ماست / چرخ در گردش گداى هوش ماست‏
entries.english: Wine in ferment is a beggar suing for our ferment; Heaven in revolution is a beggar suing for our consciousness.

entries.farsi: باده از ما مست شد نى ما از او / قالب از ما هست شد نى ما از او
entries.english: Wine became intoxicated by us, not we with it; the body came into being from we, not we from it.

entries.farsi: ما چو زنبوريم و قالبها چو موم / خانه خانه كرده قالب را چو موم‏
entries.english: We are as bees, and bodies are as wax (honeycomb): we have made the body, cell by cell, like wax.

sections.title_fa: رجوع به حكايت خواجه‏ى تاجر
sections.title_en: Reverting to the tale of the merchant who went to trade

entries.farsi: بس دراز است اين حديث خواجه گو / تا چه شد احوال آن مرد نكو
entries.english: This is very long. Tell the story of the merchant that we may see what happened to that good man.

entries.farsi: خواجه اندر آتش و درد و حنين / صد پراكنده همى‏گفت اين چنين‏
entries.english: The merchant in fire and anguish and yearning was uttering a hundred distracted phrases like this,

entries.farsi: مرد غرقه گشته جانى مى‏كند / دست را در هر گياهى مى‏زند
entries.english: Now self-contradiction, now disdain, now supplication, now passion for reality, now metaphor.

entries.farsi: گه تناقض گاه ناز و گه نياز / گاه سوداى حقيقت گه مجاز
entries.english: The drowning man suffers an agony of soul and clutches at every straw.

entries.farsi: تا كدامش دست گيرد در خطر / دست و پايى مى‏زند از بيم سر
entries.english: For fear of his head, he flings about hand and foot to see whether any one will take his hand in peril.

entries.farsi: دوست دارد يار اين آشفتگى / كوشش بى‏هوده به از خفتگى
entries.english: The Friend loves this agitation: it is better to struggle vainly than to lie still.

entries.farsi: آن كه او شاه است او بى‏كار نيست / ناله از وى طرفه كاو بيمار نيست‏
entries.english: He who is the King is not idle; complaint from Him would be a marvel, for He is not ill.

entries.farsi: بهر اين فرمود رحمان اى پسر / كُلَّ يَوْمٍ هُوَ فِي شَأْنٍ اى پسر
entries.english: For this reason said the Merciful, O son, “Every day He is in an affair,” O son.

entries.farsi: اندر اين ره مى‏تراش و مى‏خراش / تا دم آخر دمى فارغ مباش‏
entries.english: In this Way be you ever scraping and scratching: until your last breath do not be unoccupied for a moment,

entries.farsi: تا دم آخر دمى آخر بود / كه عنايت با تو صاحب سر بود
entries.english: So that your last breath may be a last breath in which the favour is your bosom-friend.

entries.farsi: هر چه مى‏كوشند اگر مرد و زن است / گوش و چشم شاه جان بر روزن است‏
entries.english: Whatsoever the soul which is in man and woman strives to do, the ear and eye of the soul’s King are at the window.

sections.title_fa: ‏برون انداختن مرد تاجر طوطى را از قفس و پريدن طوطى مرده
sections.title_en: How the merchant cast the parrot out of the cage and how the dead parrot flew away.

entries.farsi: بعد از آنش از قفس بيرون فگند / طوطيك پريد تا شاخ بلند
entries.english: After that, he cast her out of the cage. The little parrot flew to a lofty bough

entries.farsi: طوطى مرده چنان پرواز كرد / كافتاب از چرخ تركى تاز كرد
entries.english: The dead parrot made such a flight as when the orient sun rushed onward.

entries.farsi: خواجه حيران گشت اندر كار مرغ / بى‏خبر ناگه بديد اسرار مرغ‏
entries.english: The merchant was bewildered at the action of the bird: without understanding he suddenly beheld the mysteries of the bird.

entries.farsi: روى بالا كرد و گفت اى عندليب / از بيان حال خودمان ده نصيب‏
entries.english: He lifted up his face and said, “O nightingale, give us a portion by explaining your case.

entries.farsi: او چه كرد آن جا كه تو آموختى / ساختى مكرى و ما را سوختى‏
entries.english: What did she do there, that you did learn, devise a trick, and burn us”?

entries.farsi: گفت طوطى كاو به فعلم پند داد / كه رها كن لطف آواز و وداد
entries.english: The parrot said, “ She by her act counselled me— ‘Abandon your charm of voice and your affection,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه آوازت ترا در بند كرد / خويشتن مرده پى اين پند كرد
entries.english: Because your voice has brought you into bondage’: she feigned herself dead for the sake of this counsel,

entries.farsi: يعنى اى مطرب شده با عام و خاص / مرده شو چون من كه تا يابى خالص‏
entries.english: Meaning, ‘O you who have become a singer to high and low, become dead like me, that you may gain release.’”

entries.farsi: دانه باشى مرغكانت بر چنند / غنچه باشى كودكانت بر كنند
entries.english: If you are a grain, the little birds will peck you up; if you are a bud, the children will pluck you off.

entries.farsi: دانه پنهان كن بكلى دام شو / غنچه پنهان كن گياه بام شو
entries.english: Hide the grain, become wholly a snare; hide the bud, become the grass on the roof.

entries.farsi: هر كه داد او حسن خود را در مزاد / صد قضاى بد سوى او رو نهاد
entries.english: Any one who offers his beauty to auction; a hundred evil fates set out towards him.

entries.farsi: چشمها و خشمها و رشكها / بر سرش ريزد چو آب از مشكها
entries.english: Plots and angers and envies pour upon his head, like water from water skins.

entries.farsi: دشمنان او را ز غيرت مى‏درند / دوستان هم روزگارش مى‏برند
entries.english: Foes tear him to pieces from jealousy; even friends take his lifetime away.

entries.farsi: آن كه غافل بود از كشت بهار / او چه داند قيمت اين روزگار
entries.english: He that was heedless of the sowing and the springtide, how should he know the value of this lifetime?

entries.farsi: در پناه لطف حق بايد گريخت / كاو هزاران لطف بر ارواح ريخت‏
entries.english: You must flee to the shelter of God’s grace, who shed thousand fold grace upon spirits,

entries.farsi: تا پناهى يابى آن گه چون پناه / آب و آتش مر ترا گردد سپاه‏
entries.english: That you may find a refuge. Then how shelter? Water and fire will become our army.

entries.farsi: نوح و موسى را نه دريا يار شد / نه بر اعداشان به كين قهار شد
entries.english: Did not the sea become a friend to Noah and Moses? Did it not become overbearing in vengeance against their enemies?

entries.farsi: آتش ابراهيم را نى قلعه بود / تا بر آورد از دل نمرود دود
entries.english: Was not the fire a fortress for Abraham, so that it raised smoke from the heart of Nimrod?

entries.farsi: كوه يحيى را نه سوى خويش خواند / قاصدانش را به زخم سنگ راند
entries.english: Did not the mountain call Yahya to itself and drive off his pursuers with blows of stone?

entries.farsi: گفت اى يحيى بيا در من گريز / تا پناهت باشم از شمشير تيز
entries.english: “O Yahya,” it said, “come, take refuge in me, that I may be your shelter from the sharp sword.”

sections.title_fa: وداع كردن طوطى خواجه را و پريدن
sections.title_en: How the parrot bade farewell to the merchant and flew away.

entries.farsi: يك دو پندش داد طوطى بى‏نفاق / بعد از آن گفتش سالم الفراق
entries.english: The parrot gave him one or two counsels full of savour and after that bade him the farewell of parting.

entries.farsi: خواجه گفتش فى أمان اهلل برو / مر مرا اكنون نمودى راه نو
entries.english: The merchant said to her, “Go, God protect you! Just now you have shown to me a new Way.”

entries.farsi: خواجه با خود گفت كاين پند من است / راه او گيرم كه اين ره روشن است‏
entries.english: Said the merchant to himself, “This is the counsel for me; I will take her Way, for this Way is shining with light.

entries.farsi: جان من كمتر ز طوطى كى بود / جان چنين بايد كه نيكو پى بود
entries.english: How should my soul be meaner than the parrot? The soul ought to follow a good track like this.”

sections.title_fa: مضرت تعظيم خلق و انگشت نماى شدن
sections.title_en: The harmfulness of being honoured by the people and of becoming conspicuous

entries.farsi: تن قفس شكل است تن شد خار جان / در فريب داخالن و خارجان‏
entries.english: The body is cage-like: the body, amidst the cajoleries of those who come in and go out, became a thorn to the soul.

entries.farsi: اينش گويد من شوم هم راز تو / و آنش گويد نى منم انباز تو
entries.english: This one says to him, “I will be your confidant,” and that One says, “Nay, I am your partner.”

entries.farsi: اينش گويد نيست چون تو در وجود / در جمال و فضل و در احسان و جود
entries.english: This one says to him, “There is none in existence like you for beauty and eminence and for kindness and liberality.”

entries.farsi: آنش گويد هر دو عالم آن تست / جمله جانهامان طفيل جان تست‏
entries.english: That one says to him, “Both the worlds are yours, all our souls are your soul’s parasites.”

entries.farsi: او چو بيند خلق را سر مست خويش / از تكبر مى‏رود از دست خويش‏
entries.english: When he sees the people intoxicated with him, because of arrogance he loses self-control.

entries.farsi: او نداند كه هزاران را چو او / ديو افكنده ست اندر آب جو
entries.english: He does not know that the Devil has cast thousands like him into the water of the river.

entries.farsi: لطف و سالوس جهان خوش لقمه‏اى است / كمترش خور كان پر آتش لقمه‏اى است‏
entries.english: The world’s flattery and hypocrisy is a sweet morsel: eat less of it, for it is a morsel full of fire.

entries.farsi: آتشش پنهان و ذوقش آشكار / دود او ظاهر شود پايان كار
entries.english: Its fire is hidden and its taste is manifest: its smoke becomes visible in the end.

entries.farsi: تو مگو آن مدح را من كى خورم / از طمع مى‏گويد او پى مى‏برم‏
entries.english: Do not say, “How should I swallow that praise? He is speaking from desire: I am on his track.”

entries.farsi: مادحت گر هجو گويد بر مال / روزها سوزد دلت ز آن سوزها
entries.english: If your admirer should satirize you in public, your heart would burn for days on account of those scorches.

entries.farsi: گر چه دانى كاو ز حرمان گفت آن / كان طمع كه داشت از تو شد زيان‏
entries.english: Although you know that he said it in disappointment because the hopes he had of you brought him no gain,

entries.farsi: آن اثر مى‏ماندت در اندرون / در مديح اين حالتت هست آزمون‏
entries.english: The effect is remaining within you. The same experience happens to you in the case of praise.

entries.farsi: آن اثر هم روزها باقى بود / مايه‏ى كبر و خداع جان شود
entries.english: The effect of that too lasts for many days and becomes a source of arrogance and deception of the soul,

entries.farsi: ليك ننمايد چو شيرين است مدح / بد نمايد ز آن كه تلخ افتاد قدح‏
entries.english: But it does not show itself, because praise is sweet; the evil shows itself, because blame is bitter.

entries.farsi: همچو مطبوخ است و حب كان را خورى / تا به ديرى شورش و رنج اندرى‏
entries.english: It is like teas and pills which you swallow and for a long time you are in disturbance and pain,

entries.farsi: ور خورى حلوا بود ذوقش دمى / اين اثر چون آن نمى‏پايد همى‏
entries.english: Whereas, if you eat halwa, its taste is momentary: this effect, like the other, is not enduring for ever.

entries.farsi: چون نمى‏پايد همى‏پايد نهان / هر ضدى را تو به ضد او بدان‏
entries.english: Since it does not endure, it endures imperceptibly: recognise every opposite by means of its opposite.

entries.farsi: چون شكر پايد نهان تاثير او / بعد حينى دمل آرد نيش جو
entries.english: When the effect of sugar endures, after a while it produces boils that call for the lancet.

entries.farsi: تا توانى بنده شو سلطان مباش / زخم كش چون گوى شو چوگان مباش‏
entries.english: Pharaoh was made by abundance of praises: be lowly of spirit through meekness, do not domineer.

entries.farsi: نفس از بس مدحها فرعون شد / كن ذليل النفس هونا ال تسد
entries.english: So far as you can, become a slave, do not be a monarch. Suffer blows: become like the ball, do not be the bat.

entries.farsi: ور نه چون لطفت نماند وين جمال / از تو آيد آن حريفان را مالل‏
entries.english: Otherwise, when this elegance and beauty remains with you no more, you will be loathed by those companions.

entries.farsi: آن جماعت كت همى‏دادند ريو / چون ببينندت بگويندت كه ديو
entries.english: The set of people who used to flatter you deceitfully, when they behold you will call you a devil.

entries.farsi: جمله گويندت چو بينندت به در / مرده‏اى از گور خود بر كرد سر
entries.english: When they see you at their doors, they all will cry, “A dead man has risen from his grave.”

entries.farsi: همچو امرد كه خدا نامش كنند / تا بدين سالوس در دامش كنند
entries.english: Like the beardless youth whom they address as “Lord” that by this hypocrisy they may make him infamous.

entries.farsi: چون كه در بد نامى آمد ريش او / ديو را ننگ آيد از تفتيش او
entries.english: As soon as he has grown a beard in infamy, the Devil is ashamed to search after him.

entries.farsi: تا تو بودى آدمى ديو از پى‏ات / مى‏دويد و مى‏چشانيد او مى‏ات‏
entries.english: The Devil approaches Man for the sake of wickedness: he does not approach you because you are worse than the Devil.

entries.farsi: ديو سوى آدمى شد بهر شر / سوى تو نايد كه از ديوى بتر
entries.english: So long as you were a man the Devil was running at your heels and bidding you taste wine.

entries.farsi: چون شدى در خوى ديوى استوار / مى‏گريزد از تو ديو نابكار
entries.english: Since you have become confirmed in devilry, the Devil is fleeing from you, O good-for-nothing!

entries.farsi: آن كه اندر دامنت آويخت او / چون چنين گشتى ز تو بگريخت او
entries.english: At that time they clung to your skirt: when you became like this they all fled.

sections.title_fa: ‏تفسير ما شاء اللهَّ كان
sections.title_en: Explanation of “Whatsoever God wills comes to pass.”

entries.farsi: اين همه گفتيم ليك اندر بسيچ / بى‏عنايات خدا هيچيم هيچ‏
entries.english: We have spoken all these words, but in preparing ourselves we are nothing, nothing without the favours of God.

entries.farsi: بى‏عنايات حق و خاصان حق / گر ملك باشد سياه استش ورق‏
entries.english: Without the favours of God and God’s elect ones, angel though he be, his page is black.

entries.farsi: اى خدا اى فضل تو حاجت روا / اين قدر ارشاد تو بخشيده‏اى
entries.english: O God, O You whose bounty fulfils need, it is not allowable to mention any one beside You.

entries.farsi: تا بدين بس عيب ما پوشيده‏اى‏ / قطره‏اى دانش كه بخشيدى ز پيش
entries.english: This amount of guidance You have bestowed; till this You have covered up many a fault of ours.

entries.farsi: متصل گردان به درياهاى خويش‏ / قطره‏اى علم است اندر جان من
entries.english: Cause the drop of knowledge which You gave previously to become united with Your seas.

entries.farsi: وارهانش از هوا وز خاك تن‏ / پيش از آن كاين خاكها خسفش كنند
entries.english: In my soul there is a drop of knowledge: deliver it from sensuality and from the body’s clay,

entries.farsi: پيش از آن كاين بادها نشفش كنند / گر چه چون نشفش كند تو قادرى
entries.english: Before these clays drink it up, before these winds sweep it away,

entries.farsi: كش از ايشان واستانى واخرى‏ / قطره‏اى كاو در هوا شد يا كه ريخت
entries.english: Although, when they sweep it away, You are able to take it back from them and redeem it.

entries.farsi: از خزينه‏ى قدرت تو كى گريخت‏ / گر در آيد در عدم يا صد عدم
entries.english: The drop that vanished in the air or was spilled— when did it flee from the storehouse of Your omnipotence?

entries.farsi: چون بخوانيش او كند از سر قدم‏ / صد هزاران ضد ضد را مى‏كشد
entries.english: If it enters into non-existence or a hundred non-existences, it will make a foot of its head when You call it.

entries.farsi: بازشان حكم تو بيرون مى‏كشد / از عدمها سوى هستى هر زمان
entries.english: Hundreds of thousands of opposites are killing their opposites: Your decree is drawing them forth again.

entries.farsi: هست يا رب كاروان در كاروان‏ / خاصه هر شب جمله افكار و عقول
entries.english: There is caravan on caravan, O Lord, continually from non-existence towards existence.

entries.farsi: بر زنند از بحر سر چون ماهيان / در خزان آن صد هزاران شاخ و برگ
entries.english: In particular, every night all thoughts and understandings become nothing, plunged in the deep Sea;

entries.farsi: نيست گردد غرق در بحر نغول‏ / باز وقت صبح آن اللهيان
entries.english: Again at the time of dawn those Divine ones lift up their heads from the Sea, like fishes.

entries.farsi: از هزيمت رفته در درياى مرگ‏ / زاغ پوشيده سيه چون نوحه‏گر
entries.english: In autumn the myriads of boughs and leaves go in rout into the sea of Death,

entries.farsi: در گلستان نوحه كرده بر خضر / باز فرمان آيد از سالار ده
entries.english: In the garden the crow clothed in black like a mourner makes lament over the greenery.

entries.farsi: مر عدم را كانچه خوردى باز ده‏ / آن چه خوردى واده اى مرگ سياه
entries.english: Again from the Lord of the land comes the edict to Non-existence, Give back what you hast devoured!

entries.farsi: از نبات و دارو و برگ و گياه‏ / اى برادر عقل يك دم با خود آر
entries.english: Give up, O black Death, what you have devoured of plants and healing herbs and leaves and grass!”

entries.farsi: دم به دم در تو خزان است و بهار / باغ دل را سبز و تر و تازه بين
entries.english: O brother, collect your wits for an instant: from moment to moment there is autumn and spring within you.

entries.farsi: پر ز غنچه‏ى ورد و سرو و ياسمين‏ / ز انبهى برگ پنهان گشته شاخ
entries.english: Behold the garden of the heart, green and moist and fresh, full of rosebuds and cypresses and jasmines;

entries.farsi: ز انبهى گل نهان صحرا و كاخ‏ / اين سخنهايى كه از عقل كل است
entries.english: Boughs hidden by the multitude of leaves, vast plain and high palace hidden by the multitude of flowers

entries.farsi: بوى آن گلزار و سرو و سنبل است‏ / بوى گل ديدى كه آن جا گل نبود
entries.english: These words, which are from Universal Reason, are the scent of those flowers and cypresses and hyacinths.

entries.farsi: جوش مل ديدى كه آن جا مل نبود / بو قالووز است و رهبر مر ترا
entries.english: Did you smell the scent of a rose where no rose was? Did you see the foaming of wine where no wine was?

entries.farsi: مى‏برد تا خلد و كوثر مر ترا / بو دواى چشم باشد نور ساز
entries.english: The scent is your guide and conducts you on your way: it will bring you to Eden and Kawthar.

entries.farsi: شد ز بويى ديده‏ى يعقوب باز / بوى بد مر ديده را تارى كند
entries.english: The scent is a remedy for the eye; light-making: the eye of Jacob was opened by a scent.

entries.farsi: بوى يوسف ديده را يارى كند / تو كه يوسف نيستى يعقوب باش
entries.english: The foul scent darkens the eye; the scent of Joseph relieves the eye.

entries.farsi: همچو او با گريه و آشوب باش‏ / بشنو اين پند از حكيم غزنوى
entries.english: You who are not a Joseph, be a Jacob: be, like him, with weeping and sore distress.

entries.farsi: تا بيابى در تن كهنه نوى‏ / ناز را رويى ببايد همچو ورد
entries.english: Listen to this counsel from the Sage of Ghazna, that you may feel freshness in your old body:

entries.farsi: چون ندارى گرد بد خويى مگرد / زشت باشد روى نازيبا و ناز
entries.english: “Disdain needs a face like the rose; when you have not , do not indulge in ill-temper.

entries.farsi: سخت باشد چشم نابينا و درد / پيش يوسف نازش و خوبى مكن
entries.english: Ugly is disdain in an uncomely face, grievous is eye-ache in an unseeing eye.”

entries.farsi: جز نياز و آه يعقوبى مكن‏ / معنى مردن ز طوطى بد نياز
entries.english: In the presence of Joseph do not give yourself airs and behave like a beauty: offer nothing but the supplication and sighs of Jacob.

entries.farsi: در نياز و فقر خود را مرده ساز / تا دم عيسى ترا زنده كند
entries.english: The meaning of dying by the parrot was supplication: make yourself dead in supplication and poverty,

entries.farsi: همچو خويشت خوب و فرخنده كند / از بهاران كى شود سر سبز سنگ
entries.english: That the breath of Jesus may revive you and make you fair and blessed as itself.

entries.farsi: خاك شو تا گل برويى رنگ رنگ‏ / سالها تو سنگ بودى دل خراش
entries.english: How should a rock be covered with greenery by the spring? Become earth, that you may grow flowers of many a hue.

entries.farsi: آزمون را يك زمانى خاك باش‏ / آن شنيده ستى كه در عهد عمر
entries.english: Years have you been a heart-jagging rock: once, for the sake of experiment, be earth!

sections.title_fa: داستان پير چنگى كه در عهد عمر از بهر خدا روز بى‏نوايى چنگ زد ميان گورستان
sections.title_en: The story of the old harpist who in the time of Umar, may God be well-pleased with him, on a day when he was starving played the harp for God’s sake in the graveyard.

entries.farsi: بود چنگى مطربى با كر و فر / بلبل از آواز او بى‏خود شدى
entries.english: Have you heard that in the time of `Umar there was a harpist, a fine and glorious minstrel?

entries.farsi: يك طرب ز آواز خوبش صد شدى‏ / مجلس و مجمع دمش آراستى
entries.english: The nightingale would be made beside herself by his voice: by his beautiful voice one rapture would be turned into a hundred.

entries.farsi: وز نواى او قيامت خاستى / همچو اسرافيل كاوازش به فن
entries.english: His breath was an ornament to assembly and congregation, and at his song the dead would arise.

entries.farsi: مردگان را جان در آرد در بدن‏ / يا رسيلى بود اسرافيل را
entries.english: Like Israfil, whose voice will cunningly bring the souls of the dead into their bodies,

entries.farsi: جان دهد پوسيده‏ى صد ساله را / انبيا را در درون هم نغمه‏هاست
entries.english: Or he was an accompanist, to Israfil for his music would make the elephant grow wings.

entries.farsi: كز سماعش پر برستى فيل را / سازد اسرافيل روزى ناله را
entries.english: One day Israfil will make a shrill sound and will give life to him that has been rotten for a hundred years.

entries.farsi: طالبان را ز آن حيات بى‏بهاست‏ / نشنود آن نغمه‏ها را گوش حس
entries.english: The prophets also have notes within, whence there comes life beyond price to them that seek.

entries.farsi: كاو بود ز اسرار پريان اعجمى / گر چه هم نغمه‏ى پرى زين عالم است
entries.english: The sensual ear does not hear that melody, for the sensual ear is defiled by iniquities.

entries.farsi: كز ستمها گوش حس باشد نجس‏ / نشنود نغمه‏ى پرى را آدمى
entries.english: The note of the peri is not heard by man, for he is unable to apprehend the mysteries of the peris,

entries.farsi: نغمه‏ى دل برتر از هر دو دم است‏ / كه پرى و آدمى زندانى‏اند
entries.english: Although the note of the peri too belongs to this world. The note of the heart is higher than both breaths,

entries.farsi: هر دو در زندان اين نادانى‏اند / معشر الجن سوره‏ى رحمان بخوان
entries.english: For peri and man are prisoners: both are in the prison of this ignorance.

entries.farsi: تستطيعوا تنفذوا را باز دان‏ / نغمه‏هاى اندرون اوليا
entries.english: Recite O community of Jinn in the Suratu l’- Rahman; recognise if you are able to pass forth.

entries.farsi: اوال گويد كه اى اجزاى ال / هين ز الى نفى سرها بر زنيد
entries.english: The inward notes of the saints (Ahliya) say, at first, “O ye particles of la,

entries.farsi: اين خيال و وهم يك سو افكنيد / اى همه پوسيده در كون و فساد
entries.english: Take heed, lift up your heads from the la of negation, and put forth your heads from this fancy and vain imagining.

entries.farsi: جان باقيتان نروييد و نزاد / گر بگويم شمه‏اى ز آن نغمه‏ها
entries.english: O you who all are rotten in generation and corruption, your everlasting soul neither grew nor came to birth.”

entries.farsi: جانها سر بر زنند از دخمه‏ها / گوش را نزديك كن كان دور نيست
entries.english: If I tell a little of those notes, the souls will lift up their heads from the tombs.

entries.farsi: ليك نقل آن به تو دستور نيست‏ / هين كه اسرافيل وقت‏اند اوليا
entries.english: Put your ear close, for that (melody) is not far off, but it is not permitted to convey it to you.

entries.farsi: مرده را ز يشان حيات است و حيا / جان هر يك مرده‏اى از گور تن
entries.english: Hark! For the saints (Ahliya) are the Israfils of the time: from them to the dead comes life and freshness.

entries.farsi: بر جهد ز آوازشان اندر كفن‏ / گويد اين آواز ز آوازها جداست
entries.english: At their voice the dead souls in the body’s grave start up in their winding-sheets.

entries.farsi: زنده كردن كار آواز خداست‏ / ما بمرديم و بكلى كاستيم
entries.english: He says, “ This voice is separate from voices: to quicken is the work of the voice of God.

entries.farsi: بانگ حق آمد همه برخاستيم‏ / بانگ حق اندر حجاب و بى‏حجاب
entries.english: We died and were entirely decayed: the call of God came: we all arose.”

entries.farsi: آن دهد كو داد مريم را ز جيب‏ / اى فناتان نيست كرده زير پوست
entries.english: The call of God, veiled or unveiled, bestows that which He bestowed on Mary from His bosom.

entries.farsi: باز گرديد از عدم ز آواز دوست‏ / مطلق آن آواز خود از شه بود
entries.english: O you, who are rotten with death underneath the skin, return from non-existence at the voice of the Friend!

entries.farsi: گر چه از حلقوم عبد اهلل بود / گفته او را من زبان و چشم تو
entries.english: Absolutely, indeed, that voice is from the King, though it is from the larynx of God’s servant.

entries.farsi: من حواس و من رضا و خشم تو / رو كه بى‏يسمع و بى‏يبصر تويى
entries.english: He has said to him, “I am your tongue and eye; I am your senses and I am your good pleasure and your wrath.

entries.farsi: سر تويى چه جاى صاحب سر تويى‏ / چون شدى من كان هلل از وله
entries.english: Go, for you are, ‘By Me he hears and by Me he sees’: you are the consciousness: what is the occasion of, ‘You are the possessor of the consciousness’?

entries.farsi: من ترا باشم كه كان اللهَّ له‏ / گه تويى گويم ترا گاهى منم
entries.english: Since you have become, through bewilderment, ‘He that belongs to God,’ I am your, ‘for God shall belong to him.’

entries.farsi: هر چه گويم آفتاب روشنم‏ / هر كجا تابم ز مشكات دمى
entries.english: Sometimes I say to you, ‘It is you,’ sometimes, ‘It is I’: whatever I say, I am the Sun illuminating.

entries.farsi: حل شد آن جا مشكالت عالمى‏ / ظلمتى را كافتابش بر نداشت
entries.english: Wherever I shine forth from the lamp-niche of a breath, there the difficulties of a world are resolved.

entries.farsi: از دم ما گردد آن ظلمت چو چاشت‏ / آدمى را او به خويش اسما نمود
entries.english: The darkness which the sun did not remove, through My breath that darkness becomes like bright morning.”

entries.farsi: ديگران را ز آدم اسما مى‏گشود / خواه ز آدم گير نورش خواه از او
entries.english: To an Adam He in His own person showed the Names; to the rest He was revealing the Names by means of Adam.

entries.farsi: خواه از خم گير مى‏خواه از كدو / كاين كدو با خنب پيوسته ست سخت
entries.english: Receive His light either from Adam or from Himself: take the wine either from the jar or from the gourd,

entries.farsi: نى چو تو شاد آن كدوى نيك بخت‏ / گفت طوبى من رآني مصطفا
entries.english: For this gourd is very closely connected with the jar: the blessed gourd is not rejoiced as you are.

entries.farsi: و الذي يبصر لمن وجهي رأى‏ / چون چراغى نور شمعى را كشيد
entries.english: Mustafa said, “Happy he that has seen me and he that looks at him that saw my face.”

entries.farsi: هر كه ديد آن را يقين آن شمع ديد / همچنين تا صد چراغ ار نقل شد
entries.english: When a lamp has derived light from a candle, every one that sees it certainly sees the candle.

entries.farsi: ديدن آخر لقاى اصل شد / خواه از نور پسين بستان تو آن
entries.english: If transmission occurs in this way till a hundred lamps, the seeing of the last becomes a meeting with the original.

entries.farsi: خواه بين نورش ز شمع غابرين‏
entries.english: Either take with your soul from the hindmost light— there is no difference—or from the candelabrum.

entries.farsi: هيچ فرقى نيست خواه از شمع‏دان‏ / خواه بين نور از چراغ آخرين
entries.english: Either behold the light from the lamp of the last, or behold His light from the candle of those who have gone before.

sections.title_fa: در بيان اين حديث كه إن لربكم في أيام دهركم نفحات أال فتعرضوا لها
sections.title_en: Explanation of the Tradition, “Verily, your Lord has, during the days of your time, certain breathings: oh, address yourselves to them.”

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه نفحتهاى حق / اندر اين ايام مى‏آرد سبق‏
entries.english: The Prophet said, “ In these days the breathings of God prevail:

entries.farsi: نفحه آمد مر شما را ديد و رفت / هر كه را كه خواست جان بخشيد و رفت‏
entries.english: Keep ear and mind (attentive) to these (spiritual) influences, catch up such-like breathings.”

entries.farsi: گوش و هش داريد اين اوقات را / در رباييد اين چنين نفحات را
entries.english: The (Divine) breathing came, beheld you, and departed: it gave life to whom it would, and departed.

entries.farsi: نفحه‏ى ديگر رسيد آگاه باش / تا از اين هم وانمانى خواجه‏تاش‏
entries.english: Another breathing has arrived. Be heedful, that you may not miss this one too, O comrade.

entries.farsi: جان نارى يافت از وى انطفا / مرده پوشيد از بقاى او قبا
entries.english: The soul of fire gained there from an extinguisher of fire, the dead soul felt within itself a movement.

entries.farsi: تازگى و جنبش طوبى است اين / همچو جنبشهاى حيوان نيست اين‏
entries.english: This is the freshness and movement of the Tuba-tree; this is not like the movements of animals.

entries.farsi: گر در افتد در زمين و آسمان / زهره‏هاشان آب گردد در زمان‏
entries.english: If it fall on earth and heaven, their galls will turn to water at once.

entries.farsi: خود ز بيم اين دم بى‏منتها / باز خوان فَأَبَيْنَ أَنْ يحملنها
entries.english: Truly, from fear of this infinite breath: recite but they refused to bear it.

entries.farsi: ور نه خود أَشْ فَقْنَ مِنْها چون بدى / گرنه از بيمش دل كه خون شدى‏
entries.english: Else, how should they shrank from it have been, unless from fear of it the heart of the mountain had become blood?

entries.farsi: دوش ديگر لون اين مى‏داد دست / لقمه‏ى چندى در آمد ره ببست‏
entries.english: Last night this presented itself in a different guise some morsels came in and barred the way.

entries.farsi: بهر لقمه گشته لقمانى گرو / وقت لقمان است اى لقمه برو
entries.english: For a morsel’s sake a Luqman has become a pledge: it is the time for Luqman: begone, O morsel!

entries.farsi: از هواى لقمه‏ى اين خار خار / از كف لقمان همى‏جوييد خار
entries.english: These pricks for the sake of a morsel! Pluck ye forth the thorn from the sole of Luqman.

entries.farsi: در كف او خار و سايه‏ش نيز نيست / ليكتان از حرص آن تمييز نيست‏
entries.english: In his sole there is no thorn or even the shadow of it, but because of lust you have not that discernment.

entries.farsi: خار دان آن را كه خرما ديده‏اى / ز آن كه بس نان كور و بس ناديده‏اى‏
entries.english: Know that the thorn is that which you, because you are very greedy and very blind, have deemed to be a date.

entries.farsi: جان لقمان كه گلستان خداست / پاى جانش خسته‏ى خارى چراست‏
entries.english: Inasmuch as Luqman’s spirit is the rose-garden of God, why is the foot of his spirit wounded by a thorn?

entries.farsi: اشتر آمد اين وجود خار خوار / مصطفى زادى بر اين اشتر سوار
entries.english: This thorn-eating existence is a camel, and upon this camel one born of Mustafa. is mounted.

entries.farsi: اشترا تنگ گلى بر پشت تست / كز نسيمش در تو صد گلزار رست‏
entries.english: O camel, on your back is a bale of roses, from the perfume of which a hundred rose gardens grew within you.

entries.farsi: ميل تو سوى مغيالن است و ريگ / تا چه گل چينى ز خار مرده‏ريگ‏
entries.english: Your inclination is towards thorn-bushes and sand: I wonder what roses you will gather from worthless thorns.

entries.farsi: اى بگشته زين طلب از كو به كو / چند گويى كين گلستان كو و كو
entries.english: O you who in this search have roamed from one quarter to another, how long will you say, “Where, where is this rose-garden?”

entries.farsi: پيش از آن كين خار پا بيرون كنى / چشم تاريك است جوالن چون كنى‏
entries.english: Until you extract this thorn in your foot, your eye is dark: how will you go about?

entries.farsi: آدمى كاو مى‏نگنجد در جهان / در سر خارى همى‏گردد نهان‏
entries.english: Man, who is not contained in the world, becomes hidden in the point of a thorn!

entries.farsi: مصطفى آمد كه سازد هم دمى / كلميني يا حميراء كلمى‏
entries.english: Mustafa came to make harmony: “Speak to me, O Humayra, speak!”

entries.farsi: اى حميراء اندر آتش نه تو نعل / ناز نعل تو شود اين كوه لعل‏
entries.english: O Humayra put the horse-shoe in the fire, that by means of your horse-shoe this mountain may become rubies.

entries.farsi: اين حميراء لفظ تانيث است و جان / نام تانيث‏اش نهند اين تازيان
entries.english: This “Humayra” is a feminine word, and the Arabs call the “Jaan” feminine;

entries.farsi: ليك از تانيث جان را باك نيست / روح را با مرد و زن اشراك نيست‏
entries.english: But there is no fear to the Spirit from being feminine: the Spirit has no association with man and woman.

entries.farsi: از مونث وز مذكر برتر است / اين نه آن جان است كز خشك و تر است‏
entries.english: It is higher than feminine and masculine: this is not that spirit which is composed of dryness and moisture.

entries.farsi: اين نه آن جان است كافزايد ز نان / يا گهى باشد چنين گاهى چنان‏
entries.english: This is not that spirit which is increased by bread, or which is sometimes like this and sometimes like that.

entries.farsi: خوش كننده ست و خوش و عين خوشى / بى‏خوشى نبود خوشى اى مرتشى‏
entries.english: It is a doer of sweet, and sweet, and the essence of sweetness. Without sweetness there is no sweetness, O taker of bribes!

entries.farsi: چون تو شيرين از شكر باشى بود / كان شكر گاهى ز تو غايب شود
entries.english: When you are sweet by sugar, it may be that at some time that sugar will vanish from you;

entries.farsi: چون شكر گردى ز تاثير وفا / پس شكر كى از شكر باشد جدا
entries.english: When you become sugar from abundance of faithfulness, then how should sugar be parted from sugar?

entries.farsi: عاشق از خود چون غذا يابد رحيق / عقل آن جا گم شود گم اى رفيق‏
entries.english: When the lover is fed from himself with pure wine, there reason will remain lost and companion less.

entries.farsi: عقل جزوى عشق را منكر بود / گر چه بنمايد كه صاحب سر بود
entries.english: Partial reason is a denier of Love, though it may give out that it is a confidant.

entries.farsi: زيرك و داناست اما نيست نيست / تا فرشته ال نشد اهريمنى است‏
entries.english: It is clever and knowing, but it is not non existent: until the angel has become non existent, he is a Devil.

entries.farsi: او به قول و فعل يار ما بود / چون به حكم حال آيى ال بود
entries.english: It is our friend in word and deed, when you come to the case of inward feeling, it is nothing.

entries.farsi: ال بود چون او نشد از هست نيست / چون كه طوعا ال نشد كرها بسى است‏
entries.english: It is non existence because it did not from existence and become non- existent: since it did not become non existence willingly, there is many a one (who became nothing), unwillingly. The Spirit is perfection and its call is perfection: Mustafa used to say, “Refresh us, O Bilal!

entries.farsi: جان كمال است و نداى او كمال / مصطفى گويان ارحنا يا بالل‏
entries.english: O Bilal, lift up your mellifluous voice from that breath which I breathed into your heart,

entries.farsi: اى بالل افراز بانگ سلسلت / ز آن دمى كاندر دميدم در دلت‏
entries.english: From that breath by which Adam was dumbfounded and the wits of the people of Heaven were made witless.”

entries.farsi: ز آن دمى كادم از آن مدهوش گشت / هوش اهل آسمان بى‏هوش گشت‏
entries.english: Mustafa became beside himself at that beautiful voice: his prayer escaped him on the night of the ta‘ris,

entries.farsi: مصطفى بى‏خويش شد ز آن خوب صوت / شد نمازش از شب تعريس فوت‏
entries.english: He did not raise his head from that blessed sleep until the dawn-prayer had advanced to forenoon.

entries.farsi: سر از آن خواب مبارك بر نداشت / تا نماز صبحدم آمد به چاشت‏
entries.english: On the night of the ta‘ris, his holy spirit gained kissing hands in the presence of the Bride.

entries.farsi: در شب تعريس پيش آن عروس / يافت جان پاك ايشان دستبوس‏
entries.english: Love and the Spirit are, both of them, hidden and veiled: if I have called Him the Bride, do not find fault.

entries.farsi: عشق و جان هر دو نهانند و ستير / گر عروسش خوانده‏ام عيبى مگير
entries.english: I would have been silent from the Beloved’s displeasure, if He had granted me a respite for one moment

entries.farsi: از ملولى يار خامش كردمى / گر همو مهلت بدادى يك دمى‏
entries.english: But He keeps saying, “Say on! Come, it is no fault, it is but the requirement of the destiny in the World Unseen.”

entries.farsi: ليك مى‏گويد بگو هين عيب نيست / جز تقاضاى قضاى غيب نيست‏
entries.english: The fault is who sees nothing but fault: how should the Pure Spirit of the Invisible see fault?

entries.farsi: عيب باشد كاو نبيند جز كه عيب / عيب كى بيند روان پاك غيب‏
entries.english: Fault arises in relation to the ignorant creature, not in relation to the Lord of favour.

entries.farsi: عيب شد نسبت به مخلوق جهول / نى به نسبت با خداوند قبول‏
entries.english: Infidelity, too, is wisdom in relation to the Creator, when you impute it to us, infidelity is a noxious thing.

entries.farsi: ور يكى عيبى بود با صد حيات / بر مثال چوب باشد در نبات‏
entries.english: And if, there be one fault together with a hundred advantages, it resembles the wood in the sugarcane.

entries.farsi: در ترازو هر دو را يكسان كشند / ز آن كه آن هر دو چو جسم و جان خوشند
entries.english: Both alike are put into the scales, because they both are sweet like body and soul.

entries.farsi: پس بزرگان اين نگفتند از گزاف / جسم پاكان عين جان افتاد صاف‏
entries.english: Not idly, therefore, the great said this: “The body of the holy ones is essentially pure as spirit.”

entries.farsi: جان دشمن دارشان جسم است صرف / چون زياد از نرد او اسم است صرف‏
entries.english: Their speech and soul and form, everything is absolute spirit without trace.

entries.farsi: گفتشان و نفسشان و نقششان / جمله جان مطلق آمد بى‏نشان‏
entries.english: The spirit that regards them with enmity is a mere body; like the plus in nard, it is a mere name.

entries.farsi: آن به خاك اندر شد و كل خاك شد / وين نمك اندر شد و كل پاك شد
entries.english: That one went into the earth and became earth entirely; this went into the salt and became entirely pure

entries.farsi: آن نمك كز وى محمد املح است / ز آن حديث با نمك او افصح است‏
entries.english: The salt through which Mohammed is more refined: he is more eloquent than that salt-seasoned Hadith.

entries.farsi: اين نمك باقى است از ميراث او / با تواند آن وارثان او بجو
entries.english: This salt is surviving in his heritage: those heirs of his are with you. Seek them!

entries.farsi: پيش تو شسته ترا خود پيش كو / پيش هستت جان پيش انديش كو
entries.english: He is seated in front of you, where indeed is your “front “? He is before you, where is the soul that thinks “before”?

entries.farsi: گر تو خود را پيش و پس دارى گمان / بسته‏ى جسمى و محرومى ز جان‏
entries.english: If you fancy you have a “before” and “behind,” you are tied to body and deprived of spirit.

entries.farsi: زير و بالا پيش و پس وصف تن است / بى‏جهت آن ذات جان روشن است‏
entries.english: “Below” and “above,” “before” and “ behind” are attributes of the body: the essence of the bright spirit is without direction

entries.farsi: بر گشا از نور پاك شه نظر / تا نپندارى تو چون كوته نظر
entries.english: Open your vision with the pure light of the King. Beware of fancying, like one who is short-sighted,

entries.farsi: كه همينى در غم و شادى و بس / اى عدم كو مر عدم را پيش و پس‏
entries.english: That you are only this body in grief and joy, non-existence, where are “before” and “behind” relative to non-existence?

entries.farsi: روز باران است مى‏رو تا به شب / نى از اين باران از آن باران رب‏
entries.english: It is a day of rain: journey on till night— not by this rain but by the rain of the Lord.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى سؤال كردن عايشه از مصطفى عليه السلام كه امروز باران باريد چون تو سوى گورستان ‏رفتى جامه‏هاى تو چون تر نيست
sections.title_en: The story of A’isha, may God be well-pleased with her, how she asked Mustafa,
on whom be peace, saying, “It rained to-day: since you went to the graveyard, how is it that your clothes are not wet?”

entries.farsi: مصطفى روزى به گورستان برفت / با جنازه‏ى مردى از ياران برفت‏
entries.english: One day Mustafa went to the graveyard: he went with the bier of one of his friends.

entries.farsi: خاك را در گور او آگنده كرد / زير خاك آن دانه‏اش را زنده كرد
entries.english: He made the earth so that it filled his grave: he quickened his seed under the earth.

entries.farsi: اين درختانند همچون خاكيان / دستها بر كرده‏اند از خاكدان‏
entries.english: These trees are like the interred ones: they have lifted up their hands from the earth.

entries.farsi: سوى خلقان صد اشارت مى‏كنند / و آن كه گوش استش عبارت مى‏كنند
entries.english: They are making a hundred signs to the people and speaking plainly to him that hath ears.

entries.farsi: با زبان سبز و با دست دراز / از ضمير خاك مى‏گويند راز
entries.english: With green tongue and with long hand they are telling secrets from the earth’s conscience.

entries.farsi: همچو بطان سر فرو برده به آب / گشته طاوسان و بوده چون غراب‏
entries.english: Like ducks that have plunged their heads in water, they have become peacocks, though they were like crows.

entries.farsi: در زمستانشان اگر محبوس كرد / آن غرابان را خدا طاوس كرد
entries.english: If during the winter He imprisoned them, God made those “crows” “peacocks”.

entries.farsi: در زمستانشان اگر چه داد مرگ / زنده‏شان كرد از بهار و داد برگ‏
entries.english: Although He put them to death in winter, He revived them by means of spring and gave leaves.

entries.farsi: منكران گويند خود هست اين قديم / اين چرا بنديم بر رب كريم‏
entries.english: The skeptics say, “ This, surely, is eternal: why should we fix it on a beneficent Lord?”

entries.farsi: كورى ايشان درون دوستان / حق برويانيد باغ و بوستان‏
entries.english: God, in despite of them, caused gardens and plots of sweet flowers to grow in the hearts of His friends.

entries.farsi: هر گلى كاندر درون بويا بود / آن گل از اسرار كل گويا بود
entries.english: Every flower that is sweet-scented within, that flower is telling of the secrets of the Universal.

entries.farsi: بوى ايشان رغم انف منكران / گرد عالم مى‏رود پرده دران‏
entries.english: Their scent, to the confusion of the skeptics, is going round the world, rending the veil.

entries.farsi: منكران همچون جعل ز آن بوى گل / يا چو نازك مغز در بانگ دهل‏
entries.english: The skeptics, from the scent of the rose like a beetle, or like a delicate brain at the noise of the drum,

entries.farsi: خويشتن مشغول مى‏سازند و غرق / چشم مى‏دزدند زين لمعان برق‏
entries.english: They pretend to be occupied and absorbed, and withdraw their eyes from the flash and the lightning.

entries.farsi: چشم مى‏دزدند و آن جا چشم نى / چشم آن باشد كه بيند مأمنى‏
entries.english: They withdraw their eyes, but no eye is there: the eve is that which sees a place of safety.

entries.farsi: چون ز گورستان پيمبر باز گشت / سوى صديقه شد و هم راز گشت‏
entries.english: When the Prophet returned from the graveyard, he went to the Siddiqa and confided.

entries.farsi: چشم صديقه چو بر رويش فتاد / پيش آمد دست بر وى مى‏نهاد
entries.english: As soon as the eye of the Siddiqa fell upon his countenance, she advanced and began to lay her hand on him,

entries.farsi: بر عمامه و روى او و موى او / بر گريبان و بر و بازوى او
entries.english: On his turban, his face, his hair, on his collar, chest and arm.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر چه مى‏جويى شتاب / گفت باران آمد امروز از سحاب‏
entries.english: Said the Prophet, “What are you seeking so hastily?” She replied, “To-day rain fell from the clouds:

entries.farsi: جامه‏هايت مى‏بجويم از طلب / تر نمى‏بينم ز باران اى عجب‏
entries.english: I am searching your garments in quest, I do not feel them wet with the rain. Oh, how wonderful!”

entries.farsi: گفت چه بر سر فگندى از ازار / گفت كردم آن رداى تو خمار
entries.english: ‘The Prophet said, “What wrap have you thrown over your head?” Said she, “I made your plaid (rida) my head-covering.”

entries.farsi: فت بهر آن نمود اى پاك جيب / چشم پاكت را خدا باران غيب‏
entries.english: He said, “O pure-bosomed one that is why God revealed to your pure eye the rain of the Unseen.

entries.farsi: نيست آن باران از اين ابر شما / هست ابرى ديگر و ديگر سما
entries.english: That rain is not from your clouds: there are other clouds and another sky.”

sections.title_fa: :تفسير بيت حكيم ‏آسمانهاست در واليت جان كارفرماى آسمان جهان ‏در ره روح پست و بالاهاست كوههاى بلند و درياهاست

sections.title_en: Commentary on the verse of Hakim: “In the realm of the soul (Jaan) are skies lording over the sky of this world. In the Way of the spirit (Jahan) there are lowlands and highlands, there are lofty mountains and seas.”

entries.farsi: غيب را ابرى و آبى ديگر است / آسمان و آفتابى ديگر است‏
entries.english: The Unseen World has other clouds and water; it has another sky and sun.

entries.farsi: نايد آن الا كه بر خاصان پديد / باقيان فِي لَبْسٍ مِنْ خَلْقٍ جديد
entries.english: That is not discerned save by the elect; the rest are in doubt as to a new creation.

entries.farsi: هست باران از پى پروردگى / هست باران از پى پژمردگى‏
entries.english: There is rain for the sake of nurture; there is rain for the sake of decay.

entries.farsi: نفع باران بهاران بو العجب / باغ را باران پاييزى چو تب‏
entries.english: Marvellous is the benefit of the rain of springtime, to the garden the autumnal rain is like a fever.

entries.farsi: آن بهارى ناز پروردش كند / وين خزانى ناخوش و زردش كند
entries.english: That vernal makes it tenderly nurtured, while this autumnal makes it sickly and wan.

entries.farsi: همچنين سرما و باد و آفتاب / بر تفاوت دان و سر رشته بياب‏
entries.english: Similarly know that cold and wind and sun are at variance; and find the clue.

entries.farsi: همچنين در غيب انواع است اين / در زيان و سود و در ربح و غبين‏
entries.english: Even so in the Unseen World there are these different sorts, in loss and gain and in profit and loss.

entries.farsi: اين دم ابدال باشد ز آن بهار / در دل و جان رويد از وى سبزه‏زار
entries.english: This breath of the Abdal is from that springtide: from it there grows a green garden in heart and soul.

entries.farsi: فعل باران بهارى با درخت / آيد از انفاسشان در نيك بخت‏
entries.english: From their breaths there comes in him who is fortunate the effect of the spring rain on the tree.

entries.farsi: گر درخت خشك باشد در مكان / عيب آن از باد جان افزا مدان‏
