entries.farsi: پرتو حق است آن معشوق نيست / خالق است آن گوييا مخلوق نيست‏
entries.english: She is a ray of God, she is not that beloved: she is creative, you might say she is not created.

sections.title_fa: تسليم كردن مرد خود را به آن چه التماس زن بود از طلب معيشت و آن اعتراض زن را اشارت حق دانستن بنزد عقل هر داننده‏اى هست كه با گردنده گرداننده‏اى هست‏
sections.title_en: How the man yielded to his wife’s request that he should seek the means of livelihood, and regarded her opposition as a Divine indication: To the mind of every knowing man
it is a fact that with the revolving object there is one that causes it to revolve.

entries.farsi: مرد ز آن گفتن پشيمان شد چنان / كز عوانى ساعت مردن عوان‏
entries.english: The man became as sorry for that speech as at the hour of death a tyrannical officer for his tyranny.

entries.farsi: گفت خصم جان جان چون آمدم / بر سر جان من لگدها چون زدم‏
entries.english: He said, “How did I become the adversary of the life of my soul (jaan-i-jaan)? How did I bestow kicks on the head of my soul? “

entries.farsi: چون قضا آيد فرو پوشد بصر / تا نداند عقل ما پا را ز سر
entries.english: When the destiny comes, it muffles the sight, so that our intellect cannot distinguish foot from head.

entries.farsi: چون قضا بگذشت خود را مى‏خورد / پرده بدريده گريبان مى‏درد
entries.english: As soon as the destiny is past, it devours itself: rending the veil, it tears its bosom.

entries.farsi: مرد گفت اى زن پشيمان مى‏شوم / گر بدم كافر مسلمان مى‏شوم‏
entries.english: The man said, “O wife, I am repenting: if I have been an infidel, I will become a Moslem.

entries.farsi: من گنه‏كارم توام رحمى بكن / بر مكن يك بارگيم از بيخ و بن‏
entries.english: I am a sinner against you: have mercy, do not dig me up all at once from root and foundation.”

entries.farsi: كافر پير ار پشيمان مى‏شود / چون كه عذر آرد مسلمان مى‏شود
entries.english: If the old infidel is repenting, he becomes a Moslem when he pleads for pardon.

entries.farsi: حضرت پر رحمت است و پر كرم / عاشق او هم وجود و هم عدم‏
entries.english: He is the merciful and bountiful Lord: both existence and non-existence are in love with Him.

entries.farsi: كفر و ايمان عاشق آن كبريا / مس و نقره بنده‏ى آن كيميا
entries.english: Infidelity and faith are lovers of that Majesty, both copper and silver are slaves to that Elixir.

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه موسى و فرعون هر دو مسخر مشيت‏اند چنان كه زهر و پادزهر و ظلمات و نور و مناجات كردن فرعون به خلوت تا ناموس نشكند
sections.title_en: Explaining that both Moses and Pharaoh are subject to the Divine Will, like antidote and poison and darkness and light, and how Pharaoh conversed in solitude
with God, praying that He would not destroy his reputation.

entries.farsi: موسى و فرعون معنى را رهى / ظاهر آن ره دارد و اين بى‏رهى‏
entries.english: Moses and Pharaoh were servants of Reality; outwardly the former keeps the way, while the latter has lost the way.

entries.farsi: روز موسى پيش حق نالان شده / نيم شب فرعون گريان آمده‏
entries.english: In the daytime Moses was lamenting to God: at midnight Pharaoh would begin to weep,

entries.farsi: كاين چه غل است اى خدا بر گردنم / ور نه غل باشد كه گويد من منم‏
entries.english: Saying, “O God, what shackle is this on my neck? Were it not for the shackle, who would say ‘I am I’?

entries.farsi: ز آن كه موسى را منور كرده‏اى / مر مرا ز آن هم مكدر كرده‏اى‏
entries.english: By that whereby You have made Moses to be illumined, by that You have made me to be darkened;

entries.farsi: ز آن كه موسى را تو مه رو كرده‏اى / ماه جانم را سيه رو كرده‏اى‏
entries.english: By that whereby You have made Moses’ face like the moon You have made the moon of my soul to be black-faced.

entries.farsi: بهتر از ماهى نبود استاره‏ام / چون خسوف آمد چه باشد چاره‏ام
entries.english: My star was not better than a moon: since it has suffered eclipse, what help have I?

entries.farsi: نوبتم گر رب و سلطان مى‏زنند / مه گرفت و خلق پنگان مى‏زنند
entries.english: If they beat drums in my honour as Lord and Sultan, the moon is eclipsed and the people beat bowls.

entries.farsi: مى‏زنند آن طاس و غوغا مى‏كنند / ماه را ز آن زخمه رسوا مى‏كنند
entries.english: They beat those bowls and raise a clamour: they put the moon to shame by their blows.

entries.farsi: من كه فرعونم ز شهرت واى من / زخم طاس آن ربي الاعالى من‏
entries.english: I, who am Pharaoh, oh, woe is me because of the people: my title of My supreme Lord is the blows on the bowl.

entries.farsi: خواجه‏تاشانيم اما تيشه‏ات / مى‏شكافد شاخ را در بيشه‏ات‏
entries.english: We are fellow-servants, but Your axe is cleaving the sappy boughs in Your forest;

entries.farsi: باز شاخى را موصل مى‏كند / شاخ ديگر را معطل مى‏كند
entries.english: Then it makes one bough to be firmly planted, another bough to be left uncared for.

entries.farsi: شاخ را بر تيشه دستى هست نى / هيچ شاخ از دست تيشه جست نى‏
entries.english: The bough has no power against the axe: no bough escaped from the power of the axe.

entries.farsi: حق آن قدرت كه آن تيشه تراست / از كرم كن اين كژيها را تو راست
entries.english: By the truth of the might which belongs to Your axe, do You graciously make these crooked actions straight.”

entries.farsi: باز با خود گفته فرعون اى عجب / من نه در يا ربناام جمله شب‏
entries.english: Once more Pharaoh said to himself, “Oh, wonderful! Am not I the whole night in ‘O our Lord’ ?

entries.farsi: در نهان خاكى و موزون مى‏شوم / چون به موسى مى‏رسم چون مى‏شوم‏
entries.english: In secret I am growing humble and harmonious: when I reach Moses, how am I becoming (so different)?

entries.farsi: رنگ زر قلب ده‏تو مى‏شود / پيش آتش چون سيه رو مى‏شود
entries.english: The colour of base gold is in ten coats: how is it becoming black-faced in the presence of the fire?

entries.farsi: سبز گردم چون كه گويد كشت باش / زرد گردم چون كه گويد زشت باش‏
entries.english: Is it not that my heart and body are under His control, at one moment He makes me a kernel, at another moment a rind?

entries.farsi: نى كه قلب و قالبم در حكم اوست / لحظه‏اى مغزم كند يك لحظه پوست‏
entries.english: When He bids me be a wheat field, I become green; when He bids me be ugly, I become yellow.

entries.farsi: لحظه‏اى ماهم كند يك دم سياه / خود چه باشد غير اين كار اله‏
entries.english: At one moment He makes me a moon, at another black.” How, indeed, is the action of God other than this?

entries.farsi: پيش چوگانهاى حكم كن فكان / مى‏دويم اندر مكان و المكان‏
entries.english: Before the bat of His decree, “Be, and it was,” we are running in Space and beyond.

entries.farsi: چون كه بى‏رنگى اسير رنگ شد / موسيى با موسيى در جنگ شد
entries.english: Since colourlessness became the captive of colour, a Moses came into conflict with a Moses.

entries.farsi: چون به بى‏رنگى رسى كان داشتى / موسى و فرعون دارند آشتى‏
entries.english: When you attain unto the colourlessness which you possessed, Moses and Pharaoh are at peace

entries.farsi: گر ترا آيد بر اين نكته سؤال / رنگ كى خالى بود از قيل و قال
entries.english: If it occurs to you to ask questions about this mystery, how should colour be devoid of contradiction?

entries.farsi: اين عجب كاين رنگ از بى‏رنگ خاست / رنگ با بى‏رنگ چون در جنگ خاست‏
entries.english: The marvel is that this colour arose from that which is colourless: how did colour arise to war with the colourless?

entries.farsi: چون كه روغن را ز آب اسرشته‏اند / آب با روغن چرا ضد گشته‏اند
entries.english: Inasmuch as oil has been formed from water, why have oil and water become opposites?

entries.farsi: چون گل از خار است و خار از گل چرا / هر دو در جنگند و اندر ماجرا
entries.english: Since the rose springs from the thorn and the thorn from the rose, why are both of them at war and in recrimination?

entries.farsi: ‏ يا نه جنگ است اين براى حكمت است / همچو جنگ خر فروشان صنعت است‏
entries.english: Or is this not war? Is it for purpose, an artifice, like the bickering of those who sell asses?

entries.farsi: يا نه اين است و نه آن حيرانى است / گنج بايد جست اين ويرانى است‏
entries.english: Or is it neither this nor that? Is it bewilderment? The treasure must be sought and this is the ruin.

entries.farsi: آن چه تو گنجش توهم مى‏كنى / ز آن توهم گنج را گم مى‏كنى‏
entries.english: That which you imagine to be the treasure— through that vain imagination you are losing the treasure.

entries.farsi: چون عمارت دان تو وهم و رايها / گنج نبود در عمارت جايها
entries.english: Know that fancies and opinions are like the state of cultivation: treasure is not in cultivated spots.

entries.farsi: در عمارت هستى و جنگى بود / نيست را از هستها ننگى بود
entries.english: In the state of cultivation there is existence and strife: the non-existent is ashamed of existent things.

entries.farsi: نى كه هست از نيستى فرياد كرد / بلكه نيست آن هست را واداد كرد
entries.english: It is not the case that the existent implored help against non-existence; no, the nonexistent repelled the existent.

entries.farsi: تو مگو كه من گريزانم ز نيست / بلكه او از تو گريزان است بيست‏
entries.english: Do not say, “I am fleeing from the non-existent”; no, it is fleeing from you. Stop!

entries.farsi: ظاهرا مى‏خواندت او سوى خود / وز درون مى‏راندت با چوب رد
entries.english: Outwardly it is calling you towards itself, but inwardly it is driving you away with the cudgel of rejection.

entries.farsi: نعلهاى باژگونه ست اى سليم / نفرت فرعون مى‏دان از كليم‏
entries.english: O man of sound heart, it is walking backwards: know that the rebelliousness of Pharaoh was from Moses.

sections.title_fa: َسبب حرمان اشقيا از دو جهان كه خَسِرَ الدُّنْيا وَ الآْ خِرَة
sections.title_en: The reason why the unblessed are disappointed of both worlds, “he has lost this life and the life to come.”

entries.farsi: چون حكيمك اعتقادى كرده است / كاسمان بيضه زمين چون زرده است‏
entries.english: The dear hakim being firmly convinced that the sky is an egg and the earth like its yolk,

entries.farsi: همچو قنديلى معلق در هوا / نى به اسفل مى‏رود نى بر على‏
entries.english: Some one asked him how this earth remains, in the midst of this surrounding expanse of sky,

entries.farsi: گفت سائل چون بماند اين خاكدان / در ميان اين محيط آسمان‏
entries.english: Suspended in the air like a lamp, moving neither to the bottom nor to the top

entries.farsi: آن حكيمش گفت كز جذب سما / از جهات شش بماند اندر هوا
entries.english: The philosopher said to him, “It remains in the air because of the attraction exerted by the sky from six directions

entries.farsi: چون ز مغناطيس قبه‏ى ريخته / در ميان ماند آهنى آويخته‏
entries.english: Like a vault moulded of lodestone: a suspended piece of iron remains in the middle.”

entries.farsi: آن دگر گفت آسمان با صفا / كى كشد در خود زمين تيره را
entries.english: Said the other, “How should the pure sky draw the dark earth to itself?

entries.farsi: بلكه دفعش مى‏كند از شش جهات / ز آن بماند اندر ميان عاصفات‏
entries.english: No, it is repelling it from six directions: hence it remains amidst the violent winds.”

entries.farsi: پس ز دفع خاطر اهل كمال / جان فرعونان بماند اندر ضالل‏
entries.english: Then, because of the repulsion exerted by the hearts of the perfect, the spirits of Pharaohs remain in perdition.

entries.farsi: پس ز دفع اين جهان و آن جهان / مانده‏اند اين بى‏رهان بى‏اين و آن‏
entries.english: Therefore, through being rejected by this world and by that world, these lost ones have been left without either this or that.

entries.farsi: سركشى از بندگان ذو الجلال / دان كه دارند از وجود تو مالل‏
entries.english: If you turn away your head from the servants of the Almighty, know that they are disgusted by your existence.

entries.farsi: كهربا دارند چون پيدا كنند / كاه هستى ترا شيدا كنند
entries.english: They possess the amber: when they display it, they make the straw of your existence frenzied.

entries.farsi: كهرباى خويش چون پنهان كنند / زود تسليم ترا طغيان كنند
entries.english: When they conceal their amber, they quickly make your submission rebellion.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه مرتبه‏ى حيوانى است / كاو اسير و سغبه‏ى انسانى است‏
entries.english: That is like the stage of animality, which is captive and subject to humanity.

entries.farsi: مرتبه‏ى انسان به دست اوليا / سغبه چون حيوان شناسش اى كيا
entries.english: Know that the stage of humanity is subject to the power of the saints as the animal, O master.

entries.farsi: بنده‏ى خود خواند احمد در رشاد / جمله عالم را بخوان قُلْ يا عباد
entries.english: Ahmad in righteousness called the whole world his servants: read, “Say, O My servants.”

entries.farsi: عقل تو همچون شتربان تو شتر / مى‏كشاند هر طرف در حكم مر
entries.english: Your intellect is like the camel-driver, and you are the camel: it drives you in every direction under its bitter control.

entries.farsi: عقل عقلند اوليا و عقلها / بر مثال اشتران تا انتها
entries.english: The saints are the intellect of intellect, and intellects to the end are like camels.

entries.farsi: اندر ايشان بنگر آخر ز اعتبار / يك قالووز است جان صد هزار
entries.english: Come now, look upon them with consideration: there is one guide, and a hundred thousand souls.

entries.farsi: نك جهان در شب بمانده ميخ دوز / منتظر موقوف خورشيد است و روز
entries.english: What is the guide and what the camel-driver? Get an eye that may behold the Sun!

entries.farsi: چه قالووز و چه اشتربان بياب / ديده اى كان ديده بيند آفتاب‏
entries.english: Lo, the world has been left nailed fast in night, day is waiting expectantly, depending on the sun.

entries.farsi: اينت خورشيدى نهان در ذره‏اى / شير نر در پوستين بره‏اى‏
entries.english: Here is a sun hidden in a mote, a fierce lion in the fleece of a lamb.

entries.farsi: اينت دريايى نهان در زير كاه / پا بر اين كه هين منه با اشتباه‏
entries.english: Here is an ocean hidden beneath straw: beware, do not step on this straw with hesitancy.

entries.farsi: اشتباهى و گمانى در درون / رحمت حق است بهر رهنمون‏
entries.english: A feeling of hesitancy and doubt in the heart is a Divine mercy in regard to the guide.

entries.farsi: عالم كبرى به قدرت سحر كرد / كرد خود را در كهين نقشى نورد
entries.english: Every prophet came alone into this world: he was alone, and he had a hundred unseen worlds within him.

entries.farsi: هر پيمبر فرد آمد در جهان / فرد بود آن رهنمايش در نهان‏
entries.english: By his power he enchanted the macrocosm; he enfolded himself in a very small frame.

entries.farsi: ابلهانش فرد ديدند و ضعيف / كى ضعيف است آن كه با شه شد حريف‏
entries.english: The foolish deemed him to be lonely and weak: how is he weak who has become the King’s companion?

entries.farsi: ابلهان گفتند مردى بيش نيست / واى آن كاو عاقبت انديش نيست‏
entries.english: The foolish said, “He is a man, nothing more”: woe to him that reckons not the end!

sections.title_fa: حقير و بى‏خصم ديدن ديده‏هاى حس صالح و ناقه‏ى صالح را، چون خواهد كه حق لشكرى را هالك كند در نظر ايشان حقير نمايد خصمان را و اندك اگر چه غالب باشد آن خصم وَ يُقَلِّلُكُمْ فِي أَعْ يُنِهِمْ لِيَقْضِ يَ اللهَُّ أَمْراً كانَ مَفْعُول

sections.title_en: How the eyes of (external) sense regarded Salih and his she-camel as despicable and without a champion; when God is about to destroy an army He makes their adversaries appear despicable and few in their sight, even though the adversary be superior in strength:
“ and He was making you few in their eyes, that God might bring to pass a thing that was to be done.”

entries.farsi: ناقه‏ى صالح به صورت بد شتر / پى بريدندش ز جهل آن قوم مر
entries.english: The she-camel of Salih was in form a camel: that bitter tribe hamstrung her in their folly.

entries.farsi: از براى آب چون خصمش شدند / نان كور و آب كور ايشان بدند
entries.english: When they became her foes on account of the water, they were blind to bread and blind to water.

entries.farsi: ناقة اهلل آب خورد از جوى و ميغ / آب حق را داشتند از حق دريغ‏
entries.english: God’s she-camel drank water from brook and cloud: they withheld God’s water from God.

entries.farsi: ناقه‏ى صالح چو جسم صالحان / شد كمينى در هالك طالحان‏
entries.english: The she-camel of Salih became, like the bodies of righteous men, an ambush for the destruction of the wicked,

entries.farsi: تا بر آن امت ز حكم مرگ و درد / ناقَةَ اللهَِّ وَ سُقْياها چه كرد
entries.english: That what Let God’s she-camel have her portion of water, has brought against that people, through the ordainment of death and woe.

entries.farsi: روح همچون صالح و تن ناقه است / روح اندر وصل و تن در فاقه است‏
entries.english: The vengeance, which is God’s minister, demanded from them an entire town as the blood-price of a single camel.

entries.farsi: شحنه‏ى قهر خدا ز يشان بجست / خونبهاى اشترى شهرى درست‏
entries.english: His spirit is like Salih, and his body is the she-camel: the spirit is in union, the body in want.

entries.farsi: روح صالح قابل آفات نيست / زخم بر ناقه بود بر ذات نيست‏
entries.english: The Salih-spirit is not susceptible to afflictions: the blows fall on the camel, not on the essence.

entries.farsi: كس نيابد بر دل ايشان ظفر / بر صدف آمد ضرر نى بر گهر
entries.english: No one gains victory over their hearts: harm comes to the oyster-shell, not to the pearl.

entries.farsi: روح صالح قابل آزار نيست / نور يزدان سغبه‏ى كفار نيست‏
entries.english: The Salih-spirit is not capable of being hurt: the light of God is not subject to infidels.

entries.farsi: حق از آن پيوست با جسمى نهان / تاش آزارند و بينند امتحان‏
entries.english: The Soul attached to it the earthly body, that they might hurt and suffer tribulation,

entries.farsi: بى‏خبر كآزار اين آزار اوست / آب اين خم متصل با آب جوست
entries.english: Not knowing that to hurt this is to hurt Him: the water in this jar is joined with the water in the river.

entries.farsi: ز آن تعلق كرد با جسمى اله / تا كه گردد جمله عالم را پناه‏
entries.english: God connected with a body, in order that he might become a refuge for the whole world.

entries.farsi: ناقه‏ى جسم ولى را بنده باش / تا شوى با روح صالح خواجه‏تاش‏
entries.english: Be a slave to the camel, which is the saint’s body that you may become the fellow-servant of the Salih-spirit.

entries.farsi: گفت صالح چون كه كرديد اين حسد / بعد سه روز از خدا نقمت رسد
entries.english: Salih said, “Inasmuch as you have shown this envy, after three days the punishment will arrive from God.

entries.farsi: بعد سه روز دگر از جان ستان / آفتى آيد كه دارد سه نشان‏
entries.english: After three more days there will come from the Taker of life a calamity that has three signs.

entries.farsi: رنگ روى جمله تان گردد دگر / رنگ رنگ مختلف اندر نظر
entries.english: The colour of all your faces will be changed, colours different to look at.

entries.farsi: روز اول رويتان چون زعفران / در دوم رو سرخ همچون ارغوان‏
entries.english: On the first day your faces will be like saffron, on the second your faces will be red like arghawan.

entries.farsi: در سوم گردد همه روها سياه / بعد از آن اندر رسد قهر اله‏
entries.english: On the third, all your faces will become black: after that, the vengeance of God will arrive.

entries.farsi: گر نشان خواهيد از من زين وعيد / كره‏ى ناقه به سوى كه دويد
entries.english: If you desire from me the sign of this threatened chastisement, the she-camel’s foal has run towards the mountains:

entries.farsi: گر توانيدش گرفتن چاره هست / ور نه خود مرغ اميد از دام جست
entries.english: If you can catch him, there is help; else the bird of hope has surely escaped from the snare.”

entries.farsi: كس نتانست اندر آن كره رسيد / رفت در كهسارها شد ناپديد
entries.english: None was able to overtake the foal: he went into the mountains and vanished.

entries.farsi: گفت ديديد آن قضا مبرم شده ست / صورت اوميد را گردن زده ست
entries.english: Salih said, “You see, the destiny has been ratified and has beheaded the phantom of your hope.”

entries.farsi: كره‏ى ناقه چه باشد خاطرش / كه بجا آريد ز احسان و برش
entries.english: What is the she-camel’s foal? His heart, which you may bring back to its place by means of well-doing and piety.

entries.farsi: گر بجا آيد دلش رستيد از آن / ور نه نوميديد و ساعد را گزان‏
entries.english: If his heart comes back, you are saved from that (Divine punishment); otherwise you are despairing and biting your fore-arms (in remorse).

entries.farsi: چون شنيدند اين وعيد منكدر / چشم بنهادند و آن را منتظر
entries.english: When they heard this dark threat, they cast down their eyes and waited for it.

entries.farsi: روز اول روى خود ديدند زرد / مى‏زدند از نااميدى آه سرد
entries.english: On the first day they saw their faces yellow: from despair they were sighing heavily.

entries.farsi: سرخ شد روى همه روز دوم / نوبت اوميد و توبه گشت گم‏
entries.english: On the second day the faces of all became red: the time for hope and repentance was lost.

entries.farsi: شد سيه روز سوم روى همه / حكم صالح راست شد بى‏ملحمه
entries.english: On the third day all their faces became black: the prediction of Salih came true without dispute.

entries.farsi: چون همه در نااميدى سر زدند / همچو مرغان در دو زانو آمدند
entries.english: When they all were cast away in despair, they fell on their knees, like camels.

entries.farsi: در نبى آورد جبريل امين / شرح اين زانو زدن را جاثمين‏
entries.english: Gabriel, the trusted, brought in the Qur’an the description of this kneeling, jathimin.

entries.farsi: زانو آن دم زن كه تعليمت كنند / وز چنين زانو زدن بيمت كنند
entries.english: Kneel at the time when they are teaching you and bidding you dread such a kneeling as this.

entries.farsi: منتظر گشتند زخم قهر را / قهر آمد نيست كرد آن شهر را
entries.english: They were waiting for the stroke of vengeance: the vengeance came and annihilated this town.

entries.farsi: صالح از خلوت به سوى شهر رفت / شهر ديد اندر ميان دود و تفت‏
entries.english: Salih went from his solitude to the town: he beheld the town amidst smoke and heat.

entries.farsi: ز استخوانهاشان شنيد او ناله‏ها / اشك ريز از جانشان چون ژاله‏ها
entries.english: He heard wailing from their limbs: the lamentation was plain, those who uttered it invisible.

entries.farsi: ناله از اجزاى ايشان مى‏شنيد / نوحه پيدا نوحه گويان ناپديد
entries.english: He heard wailings from their bones: tears of blood from their spirits, like hailstones.

entries.farsi: صالح آن بشنيد و گريه ساز كرد / نوحه بر نوحه گران آغاز كرد
entries.english: Salih heard that and set to weeping: he began to lament for them that made lamentation.

entries.farsi: گفت اى قومى به باطل زيسته / وز شما من پيش حق بگريسته‏
entries.english: He said, “O people that lived in vanity, and on account of you I wept before God!

entries.farsi: حق بگفته صبر كن بر جورشان / پندشان ده بس نماند از دورشان‏
entries.english: God said, ‘Have patience with their iniquity: give them counsel, not much remains of their period.’

entries.farsi: من بگفته پند شد بند از جفا / شير پند از مهر جوشد وز صفا
entries.english: I said Counsel is barred by ill-treatment: the milk of counsel gushes forth from love and joy.

entries.farsi: بس كه كرديد از جفا بر جاى من / شير پند افسرد در رگهاى من‏
entries.english: Much ill-treatment have they bestowed on me, the milk of counsel is curdled in my veins.’

entries.farsi: حق مرا گفته ترا لطفى دهم / بر سر آن زخمها مرهم نهم‏
entries.english: God said to me, `I will give you a boon; I will lay a plaster on those wounds.’

entries.farsi: در نصيحت من شده بار دگر / گفته امثال و سخنها چون شكر
entries.english: God made my heart clear as the sky; He swept your oppression out of my mind.

entries.farsi: صاف كرده حق دلم را چون سما / روفته از خاطرم جور شما
entries.english: I went once more to admonition; I spoke parables and words as sugar,

entries.farsi: در شما چون زهر گشته آن سخن / ز آن كه زهرستان بديد از بيخ و بن‏
entries.english: I produced fresh milk from the sugar; I mingled milk and honey with my words.

entries.farsi: شير تازه از شكر انگيخته / شير و شهدى با سخن آميخته‏
entries.english: In you those words became like poison, because you were filled with poison from the root and foundation

entries.farsi: چون شوم غمگين كه غم شد سر نگون / غم شما بوديد اى قوم حرون‏
entries.english: How should I be grieved that grief is overthrown? You were grief, O obstinate people.

entries.farsi: هيچ كس بر مرگ غم نوحه كند / ريش سر چون شد كسى مو بر كند
entries.english: Does any one lament the death of grief? Does any one tear out his hair when the sore on his head is removed? “ He turned to himself and said, “ O mourner, those folk are not worth your mourning.”

entries.farsi: رو به خود كرد و بگفت اى نوحه‏گر / نوحه‏ات را مى‏نيرزد آن نفر
entries.english: Recite correctly—do not regard my misquotation— “Say, how shall I be grieved for an unjust people?”

entries.farsi: باز اندر چشم و دل او گريه يافت / رحمتى بى‏علتى در وى بتافت‏
entries.english: Again he felt a weeping in his eye and heart: an uncaused compassion shone forth in him

entries.farsi: قطره مى‏باريد و حيران گشته بود / قطره‏ى بى‏علت از درياى جود
entries.english: He was raining drops of water—and he had become distraught— an uncaused drop from the Ocean of Bounty.

entries.farsi: عقل او مى‏گفت كين گريه ز چيست / بر چنان افسوسيان شايد گريست‏
entries.english: His intellect was saying, “Why this weeping? Ought one to weep for such scoffers?

entries.farsi: بر دل تاريك پر زنگارشان / بر زبان زهر همچون مارشان‏
entries.english: Tell me, what are you weeping for? For their fraud? For the host of their ill-shod hatreds?

entries.farsi: بر چه مى‏گريى بگو بر فعلشان / بر سپاه كينه توز بدنشان‏
entries.english: For their murky hearts full of rust? For their venomous snake-like tongues?

entries.farsi: بر دم و دندان سگسارانه‏شان / بر دهان و چشم كژدم خانه‏شان‏
entries.english: For their dog-like (sagsar) breath and teeth? For their mouths and eyes teeming with scorpions?

entries.farsi: بر ستيز و تسخر و افسوسشان / شكر كن چون كرد حق محبوسشان‏
entries.english: For their wrangling and sneering and scoffing? Give thanks, since God has imprisoned them.

entries.farsi: دستشان كژ پايشان كژ چشم كژ / مهرشان كژ صلح‏شان كژ خشم كژ
entries.english: Their hands are perverse, their feet perverse, their eyes perverse, their love perverse, their peace perverse, their anger perverse.”

entries.farsi: از پى تقليد و معقوالت نقل / پا نهاده بر جمال پير عقل‏
entries.english: For the sake of imitation and the standards of tradition, they set their feet (trampled) on the camels of Reason, the venerable guide.

entries.farsi: پير خر نى جمله گشته پير خر / از رياى چشم و گوش همدگر
entries.english: They were not eager for a guide (pir-khar): they all had become an old donkey (pir khar) from paying hypocritical observance to each other’s eyes and ears.

entries.farsi: از بهشت آورد يزدان بردگان / تا نمايدشان سقر پروردگان‏
entries.english: God brought the worshippers from Paradise that He might show unto them the nurslings of Hell-fire.

sections.title_fa: ِدر معنى آن كه مَرَجَ الْبَحْ رَيْنِ يَلْتَقِيانِ بَيْنَهُما بَرْزَخٌ ال يَبْغِيان
sections.title_en: On the meaning of “He let the two seas go to meet one another: between them is a barrier which they do not seek.”

entries.farsi: اهل نار و خلد را بين هم دكان / در ميانشان بَرْزَخٌ ال يبغيان‏
entries.english: Behold the people of the Fire and those of Paradise dwelling in the same shop, between them is a barrier which they do not seek to cross.

entries.farsi: اهل نار و اهل نور آميخته / در ميانشان كوه قاف انگيخته‏
entries.english: He has mixed the people of the Fire and the people of the Light: between them He has reared the mountain of Qaf.

entries.farsi: همچو در كان خاك و زر كرد اختالط / در ميانشان صد بيابان و رباط
entries.english: He has mixed like earth and gold in the mine: between them are a hundred deserts and caravanserays.

entries.farsi: همچنان كه عقد در در و شبه / مختلط چون ميهمان يك شبه‏
entries.english: Mixed even as pearls and jet beads in the necklace, like guests of a single night.

entries.farsi: بحر را نيميش شيرين چون شكر / طعم شيرين رنگ روشن چون قمر
entries.english: One half of the sea is sweet like sugar: the taste sweet, the colour bright as the moon.

entries.farsi: نيم ديگر تلخ همچون زهر مار / طعم تلخ و رنگ مظلم فيروار
entries.english: The other half is bitter as snake’s venom: the taste bitter and the colour dark as pitch.

entries.farsi: هر دو بر هم مى‏زنند از تحت و اوج / بر مثال آب دريا موج موج‏
entries.english: Both dash against one another, from beneath and from the top, wave on wave like the water of the sea.

entries.farsi: صورت بر هم زدن از جسم تنگ / اختالط جانها در صلح و جنگ‏
entries.english: The appearance of collision, from the narrow body, is the spirits’ being intermingled in peace or war.

entries.farsi: موجهاى جنگ بر شكل دگر / مهرها را مى‏كند زير و زبر
entries.english: The waves of peace dash against each other and root up hatreds from breasts

entries.farsi: موجهاى صلح بر هم مى‏زند / كينه‏ها از سينه‏ها بر مى‏كند
entries.english: In other form do the waves of war turn loves upside down.

entries.farsi: مهر تلخان را به شيرين مى‏كشد / ز آن كه اصل مهرها باشد رشد
entries.english: Love is drawing the bitter ones to the sweet, because the foundation of loves is righteousness.

entries.farsi: قهر شيرين را به تلخى مى‏برد / تلخ با شيرين كجا اندر خورد
entries.english: Wrath is carrying away the sweet one to bitterness: how should the bitter sort with the sweet?

entries.farsi: تلخ و شيرين زين نظر نايد پديد / از دريچه‏ى عاقبت دانند ديد
entries.english: The bitter and the sweet are not visible to this sight, they can be seen through the window of the latter end.

entries.farsi: چشم آخر بين تواند ديد راست / چشم آخر بين غرور است و خطاست
entries.english: The eye that sees the end (akhir) can see truly; the eye that sees the stable (akhur) is delusion and error.

entries.farsi: آن كه زيرك‏تر به بو بشناسدش / و آن دگر چون بر لب و دندان زدش‏
entries.english: Oh, many the one that is sweet as sugar, but poison is concealed in the sugar.

entries.farsi: اى بسا شيرين كه چون شكر بود / ليك زهر اندر شكر مضمر بود
entries.english: He that is more sagacious will know it by the smell; another when it touches his lips and teeth:

entries.farsi: پس لبش ردش كند پيش از گلو / گر چه نعره مى‏زند شيطان كلوا
entries.english: Then his lips will reject it before his throat, although the Devil is shouting, “Eat!”

entries.farsi: و آن دگر را در گلو پيدا كند / و آن دگر را در بدن رسوا كند
entries.english: And to another it will declare in his throat, while to another it will unmask in his body;

entries.farsi: و آن دگر را در حدث سوزش دهد / ذوق آن زخم جگر دوزش دهد
entries.english: And to another it will give burning pain in evacuation: its outgoing will give him instruction as to its incoming:

entries.farsi: و آن دگر را بعد ايام و شهور / و آن دگر را بعد مرگ از قعر گور
entries.english: And to another after days and months; and to another after death, from the depth of the grave;

entries.farsi: ور دهندش مهلت اندر قعر گور / ال بد آن پيدا شود يوم النشور
entries.english: And if he be given a respite in the depth of the grave, it will inevitably become manifest on the Day of Resurrection.

entries.farsi: هر نبات و شكرى را در جهان / مهلتى پيداست از دور زمان‏
entries.english: Every piece of candy and sugar in the world manifestly has a period granted to it from the revolution of Time.

entries.farsi: سالها بايد كه اندر آفتاب / لعل يابد رنگ و رخشانى و تاب‏
entries.english: Years are needed in order that the ruby in the sun may obtain tint and splendour and brilliance.

entries.farsi: باز تره در دو ماه اندر رسد / باز تا سالى گل احمر رسد
entries.english: Vegetables, again, reach maturity in two months, while the red rose comes to perfection in a year.

entries.farsi: بهر اين فرمود حق عز و جل / سوره الانعام در ذكر اجل‏
entries.english: For this reason the Almighty and Glorious God in the Suratu’l-An‘am has made mention of an appointed term (ajal).

entries.farsi: اين شنيدى مو به مويت گوش باد / آب حيوان است خوردى نوش باد
entries.english: You have heard this: may the whole of you, hair by hair, be an ear! It is the Water of Life: you have drunk, may it do you good!

entries.farsi: آب حيوان خوان مخوان اين را سخن / روح نو بين در تن حرف كهن‏
entries.english: Call it the Water of Life, call it not a discourse: behold the new spirit in the body of the old letter!

entries.farsi: نكته‏ى ديگر تو بشنو اى رفيق / همچو جان او سخت پيدا و دقيق‏
entries.english: My friend, listen to another saying, like the soul, very clear and abstruse:

entries.farsi: در مقامى هست هم اين زهر مار / از تصاريف خدايى خوش گوار
entries.english: In a certain place, through Divine dispositions even this poison and snake is digestible.

entries.farsi: در مقامى زهر و در جايى دوا / در مقامى كفر و در جايى روا
entries.english: In one place poison and in one place medicine, in one place infidelity and in one place approved.

entries.farsi: گر چه آن جا او گزند جان بود / چون بدين جا در رسد درمان بود
entries.english: Although there it is injurious to the soul, when it arrives here it becomes a remedy.

entries.farsi: آب در غوره ترش باشد و ليك / چون به انگورى رسد شيرين و نيك‏
entries.english: In the young grape (ghura) the juice is sour, but it is sweet and good when the ghura comes to be a ripe grape (angur).

entries.farsi: باز در خم او شود تلخ و حرام / در مقام سركگى نعم الادام‏
entries.english: Again in the wine-jar it becomes bitter and unlawful, in the state of vinegar how excellent it is as a seasoning!

sections.title_fa: در معنى آن كه آن چه ولى كند مريد را نشايد گستاخى كردن و همان فعل كردن كه حلوا طبيب را زيان ندارد اما بيمار را زيان دارد و سرما و برف انگور َرا زيان ندارد اما غوره را زيان دارد كه در راهست كه لِيَغْفِرَ لَكَ اللهَُّ ما تَقَدَّمَ مِنْ ذَنْبِكَ وَ ما تَأَخَّر
sections.title_en: Concerning the impropriety of the murid’s presuming to do the same things as are done by the wali, inasmuch as sweetmeat does no harm to the physician,

but is harmful to the sick, and frost and snow do no harm to the ripe grape, but are injurious to the young fruit; for he is on the way, for he has not become: “That God may forgive you your former and latter sins.”

entries.farsi: گر ولى زهرى خورد نوشى شود / ور خورد طالب سيه هوشى شود
entries.english: If the saint drinks a poison it becomes an antidote, but if the seeker drinks it, his mind is darkened.

entries.farsi: رب هَبْ لِي از سليمان آمده ست / كه مده غير مرا اين ملك و دست‏
entries.english: From Solomon have come the words, “O Lord, give me a kingdom,” that is, “ do not give this kingdom and power to any but me.

entries.farsi: تو مكن با غير من اين لطف و جود / اين حسد را ماند اما آن نبود
entries.english: Do not bestow this grace and bounty on any but me.” This looks like envy, but it was not that.

entries.farsi: نكته‏ى ال يَنْبَغِي مى‏خوان به جان / سر مِنْ بَعْدِي ز بخل او مدان‏
entries.english: Read with your soul the mystery of “it behoves not,” do not deem the inward meaning of “after me” from his avarice.

entries.farsi: بلكه اندر ملك ديد او صد خطر / مو به مو ملك جهان بد بيم سر
entries.english: No, but in sovereignty he saw a hundred dangers: the kingdom of this world was, hair by hair, fear for one’s head.

entries.farsi: بيم سر با بيم سر با بيم دين / امتحانى نيست ما را مثل اين‏
entries.english: Fear for head with fear for heart with fear for religion— there is no trial for us like this.

entries.farsi: پس سليمان همتى بايد كه او / بگذرد زين صد هزاران رنگ و بو
entries.english: Therefore one must possess the high aspiration of a Solomon in order to escape from these myriads of colours and perfumes.

entries.farsi: با چنان قوت كه او را بود هم / موج آن ملكش فرومى‏بست دم‏
entries.english: Even with such strength as he had, the waves of that kingdom were stifling his breath.

entries.farsi: چون بر او بنشست زين اندوه گرد / بر همه شاهان عالم رحم كرد
entries.english: Since dust settled on him from this sorrow, he had compassion for all the kings of the world.

entries.farsi: شد شفيع و گفت اين ملك و لوا / با كمالى ده كه دادى مر مرا
entries.english: Hence he interceded and said, “ Give this kingdom with the perfection which You have given to me.

entries.farsi: هر كه را بدهى و بكنى آن كرم / او سليمان است و آن كس هم منم‏
entries.english: To whomever You will give, and You will confer that bounty, he is Solomon, and I also am he.

entries.farsi: او نباشد بعدى او باشد معى / خود معى چه بود منم بى‏مدعى‏
entries.english: He is not ‘after me,’ he is with me. What of ‘with me,’ indeed? I am without rival.”

entries.farsi: شرح اين فرض است گفتن ليك من / باز مى‏گردم به قصه‏ى مرد و زن‏
entries.english: It is my duty to explain this, but I will return to the story of the man and wife.

sections.title_fa: مخلص ماجراى عرب و جفت او
sections.title_en: The moral of the altercation of the Arab and his wife

entries.farsi: ماجراى مرد و زن را مخلصى / باز مى‏جويد درون مخلصى‏
entries.english: The heart of one who is sincere is seeking a moral for the altercation of the man and wife.

entries.farsi: ماجراى مرد و زن افتاد نقل / آن مثال نفس خود مى‏دان و عقل‏
entries.english: The altercation of the man and wife has been related: know that it is a parable of your own nafs and reason.

entries.farsi: اين زن و مردى كه نفس است و خرد / نيك بايسته ست بهر نيك و بد
entries.english: This man and wife, which are the flesh and the reason, are very necessary for good and evil;

entries.farsi: وين دو بايسته در اين خاكى سرا / روز و شب در جنگ و اندر ماجرا
entries.english: And this necessary pair in this house of earth is in strife and altercation day and night.

entries.farsi: زن همى‏خواهد هويج خانگاه / يعنى آب رو و نان و خوان و جاه‏
entries.english: The wife is craving requisites for the household, that is to say, reputation and bread and deicacies and rank.

entries.farsi: نفس همچون زن پى چاره‏گرى / گاه خاكى گاه جويد سرورى‏
entries.english: Like the wife, the flesh, in order to contrive the means, is at one time seeking humility and at another time to domination.

entries.farsi: عقل خود زين فكرها آگاه نيست / در دماغش جز غم اهلل نيست
entries.english: The reason is really unconscious of these thoughts: in its brain is nothing but love of God.

entries.farsi: گر چه سر قصه اين دانه ست و دام / صورت قصه شنو اكنون تمام‏
entries.english: Although the inner meaning of the tale is this bait and trap, listen now to the outward form of the tale in its entirety.

entries.farsi: گر بيان معنوى كافى شدى / خلق عالم عاطل و باطل بدى‏
entries.english: If the spiritual explanation were sufficient, the creation of the world would have been vain and idle.

entries.farsi: گر محبت فكرت و معنيستى / صورت روزه و نمازت نيستى‏
entries.english: If love were thought and reality, the form of your fasting and prayer would be non-existent.

entries.farsi: هديه‏هاى دوستان با همديگر / نيست اندر دوستى الا صور
entries.english: The gifts of lovers to one another are, in respect of love, naught but forms;

entries.farsi: تا گواهى داده باشد هديه‏ها / بر محبتهاى مضمر در حفا
entries.english: That the gifts may have borne testimony to feelings of love which are concealed in secrecy,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه احسانهاى ظاهر شاهدند / بر محبتهاى سر اى ارجمند
entries.english: Because outward acts of kindness bear witness to feelings of love in the heart, O dear friend

entries.farsi: شاهدت گه راست باشد گه دروغ / مست گاهى از مى و گاهى ز دوغ‏
entries.english: Your witness is sometimes true, sometimes false, sometimes drunken with wine, sometimes with sour curds.

entries.farsi: دوغ خورده مستيى پيدا كند / هاى و هوى و سر گرانيها كند
entries.english: He that has drunk sour curds makes a show of intoxication, shouts ecstatically, and behaves like one whose head is heavy:

entries.farsi: آن مرايى در صيام و در صالست / تا گمان آيد كه او مست والست‏
entries.english: That hypocrite is in fasting and praying, in order that it may be supposed that he is drunk with devotion.

entries.farsi: حاصل افعال برونى ديگر است / تا نشان باشد بر آن چه مضمر است‏
entries.english: In short, external acts are different from internal feelings, and their purpose is to indicate that which is hidden.

entries.farsi: يا رب آن تمييز ده ما را به خواست / تا شناسيم آن نشان كژ ز راست‏
entries.english: O Lord, grant us according to our desire such discernment that we may know the false indication from the true.

entries.farsi: حس را تمييز دانى چون شود / آن كه حس ينظر بنور اللهَّ بود
entries.english: Do you know how the sense-perception becomes discerning? In this way, that the sense-perception should be seeing by the light of God.

entries.farsi: ور اثر نبود سبب هم مظهر است / همچو خويشى كز محبت مخبر است‏
entries.english: And if there be no effect, the cause too makes manifest, as kinship gives information concerning love.

entries.farsi: نبود آن كه نور حقش شد امام / مر اثر را يا سببها را غالم‏
entries.english: When the light of God comes into the sensual, you will not be a slave to effect or cause

entries.farsi: يا محبت در درون شعله زند / زفت گردد وز اثر فارغ كند
entries.english: So that Love will throw a spark within, wax mighty, and make independent of effect.

entries.farsi: حاجتش نبود پى اعالم مهر / چون محبت نور خود زد بر سپهر
entries.english: He has no need for the signs of love, since Love has shot its radiance over the sky.

entries.farsi: هست تفصيالت تا گردد تمام / اين سخن ليكن بجو تو و السلام‏
entries.english: There are detailed explanations in order to complete this subject; but seek them, and farewell.

entries.farsi: گر چه شد معنى در اين صورت پديد / صورت از معنى قريب است و بعيد
entries.english: And as for him that perceived the inner meaning in this outward form, the form is near to the meaning and far.

entries.farsi: در داللت همچو آب‏اند و درخت / چون به ماهيت روى دورند سخت‏
entries.english: In regard to indication, they are like the sap and the tree; when you turn to the essence, they are very far.

entries.farsi: ترك ماهيات و خاصيات گو / شرح كن احوال آن دو ماهرو
entries.english: Take leave of quibbles and essential properties, and relate what happened to those two with faces like the moon.

sections.title_fa: ‏دل نهادن عرب بر التماس دل بر خويش و سوگند خوردن كه در اين تسليم مرا حيلتى و امتحانى نيست
sections.title_en: How the Arab set his heart on his beloved’s request and swore that in thus submitting he had no trickery and making trial.

entries.farsi: مرد گفت اكنون گذشتم از خالف / حكم دارى تيغ بر كش از غالف‏
entries.english: The man said, “Now I have ceased to oppose: you have authority: draw the sword from the sheath.

entries.farsi: هر چه گويى من ترا فرمان‏برم / در بد و نيك آمد آن ننگرم‏
entries.english: Whatever you bid me do, I will obey: I will not consider the bad or good result of it.

entries.farsi: در وجود تو شوم من منعدم / چون محبم حب يعمى و يصم‏
entries.english: I will become non-existent in your existence, because I am your lover: love makes blind and deaf.”

entries.farsi: گفت زن آهنگ برم مى‏كنى / يا به حيلت كشف سرم مى‏كنى‏
entries.english: The wife said, “Oh, I wonder if you are my friend, or whether you are discovering my secret by trickery?”

entries.farsi: دو سه گز قالب كه دادش وانمود / هر چه در الواح و در ارواح بود
entries.english: He said, by God who knows the thought most deeply hid, who out of dust created Adam pure,

entries.farsi: گفت و اهلل عالم السر الخفى / كافريد از خاك آدم را صفى‏
entries.english: Who, in the body three cubits long which He gave him, displayed everything that was contained in the tablets and the spirits.

entries.farsi: تا ابد هر چه بود او پيش پيش / درس كرد از علم الاسماء خويش‏
entries.english: Through his He taught him the Names he at the very first gave instruction concerning everything that shall come to pass unto everlasting,

entries.farsi: تا ملك بى‏خود شد از تدريس او / قدس ديگر يافت از تقديس او
entries.english: So that the angels became beside themselves at his teaching, and gained from his glorification a holiness other

entries.farsi: آن گشادى‏شان كز آدم رو نمود / در گشاد آسمانهاشان نبود
entries.english: The revelation that appeared to them from Adam was not in the amplitude of their heavens.

entries.farsi: در فراخى عرصه‏ى آن پاك جان / تنگ آمد عرصه‏ى هفت آسمان‏
entries.english: In comparison with the spaciousness of the range of that pure spirit, the expanse of the seven heavens became narrow.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه حق فرموده است / من نگنجم هيچ در بالا و پست‏
entries.english: The Prophet said that God has said, ‘I am not contained in the jar of “high” and “low”;

entries.farsi: در زمين و آسمان و عرش نيز / من نگنجم اين يقين دان اى عزيز
entries.english: I am not contained in earth or heaven or even in the empyrean— know this for certain, O noble one;

entries.farsi: در دل مومن بگنجم اى عجب / گر مرا جويى در آن دلها طلب‏
entries.english: I am contained in the true believer’s heart: oh, how wonderful! If you seek Me, search in those hearts.’

entries.farsi: گفت ادخل فى عبادي تلتقي / جنة من رؤيتي يا متقي‏
entries.english: He said, ‘Enter among My servants, you will meet with a Paradise of vision of Me, O God-fearing one.’

entries.farsi: عرش با آن نور با پهناى خويش / چون بديد آن را برفت از جاى خويش‏
entries.english: The empyrean, notwithstanding its wide light, when it beheld that, was confounded.

entries.farsi: خود بزرگى عرش باشد بس مديد / ليك صورت كيست چون معنى رسيد
entries.english: Truly, the magnitude of the empyrean is very great, but who is form when reality has arrived?

entries.farsi: هر ملك مى‏گفت ما را پيش از اين / الفتى مى‏بود بر گرد زمين‏
entries.english: Then the angels were saying, before this we had a friendship on the dust of the earth.

entries.farsi: تخم خدمت بر زمين مى‏كاشتيم / ز آن تعلق ما عجب مى‏داشتيم‏
entries.english: On the earth we were sowing the seed of service: we were marvelling at that connection,

entries.farsi: كاين تعلق چيست با اين خاكمان / چون سرشت ما بده ست از آسمان‏
entries.english: Marvelling what connection we had with that dust, inasmuch as our nature is of heaven.

entries.farsi: الف ما انوار با ظلمات چيست / چون تواند نور با ظلمات زيست‏
entries.english: Why friendship in us, who are light, with darkness? How can light live with darkness?

entries.farsi: آدما آن الف از بوى تو بود / ز آن كه جسمت را زمين بد تار و پود
entries.english: O Adam, that friendship was owing to the scent of you, because earth was the woof and warp of your body.

entries.farsi: جسم خاكت را از اينجا بافتند / نور پاكت را در اينجا يافتند
entries.english: From this place your earthly body was woven, in this place your pure light was found.

entries.farsi: اين كه جان ما ز روحت يافته ست / پيش پيش از خاك آن مى‏تافته ست‏
entries.english: This, that our souls have obtained from your spirit formerly shone from the dust.

entries.farsi: در زمين بوديم و غافل از زمين / غافل از گنجى كه در وى بد دفين‏
entries.english: We were in the earth, and heedless of the earth, heedless of the treasure that lay buried there.

entries.farsi: چون سفر فرمود ما را ز آن مقام / تلخ شد ما را از آن تحويل كام‏
entries.english: When He bade us journey from that place of abode our palates were soured by the change,

entries.farsi: تا كه حجتها همى‏گفتيم ما / كه بجاى ما كى آيد اى خدا
entries.english: So that we were arguing, ‘O God, who will come in our stead?

entries.farsi: نور اين تسبيح و اين تهليل را / مى‏فروشى بهر قال و قيل را
entries.english: Will You sell the splendour of the praise with which we glorify and magnify You for babble and chatter?’

entries.farsi: حكم حق گسترد بهر ما بساط / كه بگوييد از طريق انبساط
entries.english: The decree of God spread for us the carpet, ‘Speak ye, in the way of boldness

entries.farsi: هر چه آيد بر زبانتان بى‏حذر / همچو طفالن يگانه با پدر
entries.english: Without fear, whatever comes upon your tongues, like an only child with its father?

entries.farsi: ز آن كه اين دمها چه گر نالايق است / رحمت من بر غضب هم سابق است‏
entries.english: For what if these words are unseemly? My mercy likewise is prior to My wrath.

entries.farsi: از پى اظهار اين سبق اى ملك / در تو بنهم داعيه‏ى اشكال و شك‏
entries.english: In order to manifest this priority, O angel, I will put in you incitement to perplexity and doubt,

entries.farsi: تا بگويى و نگيرم بر تو من / منكر حلمم نيارد دم زدن‏
entries.english: That you may speak and I not take offence at you, none who denies My clemency may dare to utter a word.

entries.farsi: صد پدر صد مادر اندر حلم ما / هر نفس زايد در افتد در فنا
entries.english: Within My clemency a hundred fathers and a hundred mothers at every moment are born and vanish.

entries.farsi: حلم ايشان كف بحر حلم ماست / كف رود آيد ولى دريا به جاست‏
entries.english: Their clemency is the foam of the sea of My clemency: the foam comes and goes, but the sea is there.’

entries.farsi: حق آن كف حق آن درياى صاف / كه امتحانى نيست اين گفت و نه الف‏
entries.english: What indeed shall I say? Compared with that pearl this oyster-shell is naught but the foam of the foam of the foam of foam.

entries.farsi: خود چه گويم پيش آن در اين صدف / نيست الا كف كف كف كف‏
entries.english: By the truth of that foam, by the truth of that pure sea, that these words are not trial of you and are not vain

entries.farsi: از سر مهر و صفاء است و خضوع / حق آن كس كه بدو دارم رجوع‏
entries.english: They are from love and sincerity and humbleness, by the truth of that One to whom I turn.

entries.farsi: گر به پيشت امتحان است اين هوس / امتحان را امتحان كن يك نفس‏
entries.english: If this affection seems to you a trial, for one moment put the trial to the test.

entries.farsi: سر مپوشان تا پديد آيد سرم / امر كن تو هر چه بر وى قادرم‏
entries.english: Do not hide your secret, in order that mine may be revealed: command anything that I am able to do.

entries.farsi: دل مپوشان تا پديد آيد دلم / تا قبول آرم هر آن چه قابلم‏
entries.english: Do not hide your heart, in order that mine may be revealed and that I may accept whatever I am capable of.

entries.farsi: چون كنم در دست من چه چاره است / در نگر تا جان من چه كاره است‏
entries.english: How shall I do? What remedy is in my power? Look what a plight my soul is in.”

sections.title_fa: تعيين كردن زن طريق طلب روزى كدخداى خود را و قبول كردن او
sections.title_en: How the wife specified to her husband the way to earn daily bread

entries.farsi: گفت زن يك آفتابى تافته ست / عالمى زو روشنايى يافته ست‏
entries.english: and how he accepted.

entries.farsi: نايب رحمان خليفه‏ى كردگار / شهر بغداد است از وى چون بهار
entries.english: The wife said, “A sun has shone forth, a world has received light from him

entries.farsi: همنشينى مقبالن چون كيمياست / چون نظرشان كيميايى خود كجاست‏
entries.english: The Vicar of the Merciful, the Khalifa of the Creator: through him the city of Baghdad is as the season of spring.

entries.farsi: گر بپيوندى بدان شه شه شوى / سوى هر ادبار تا كى مى‏روى
entries.english: If you gain access to that King, you will become a king: how long will you go after every misfortune? “

entries.farsi: چشم احمد بر ابو بكرى زده / او ز يك تصديق صديق آمده‏
entries.english: Companionship with the fortunate is like the Elixir: indeed, how is an Elixir like their looks?

entries.farsi: گفت من شه را پذيرا چون شوم / بى‏بهانه سوى او من چون روم‏
entries.english: The eye of Ahmad was cast upon an Abu Bakr: he by a single act of faith became a Siddiq.

entries.farsi: نسبتى بايد مرا يا حيلتى / هيچ پيشه راست شد بى‏آلتى‏
entries.english: Said the husband, “How should I go to meet the King? How should I go to him without a pretext?
I must have some reference or device: is any handicraft right without tools?

entries.farsi: همچو آن مجنون كه بشنيد از يكى / كه مرض آمد به ليلى اندكى‏
entries.english: As the famous Majnun, when he heard from some one that Layla was a little unwell,

entries.farsi: گفت آوه بى‏بهانه چون روم / ور بمانم از عيادت چون شوم‏
entries.english: Cried, ‘Ah, how shall I go without a pretext? And if I fail to visit her when she is ill, how shall I be!

entries.farsi: شب پران را گر نظر و آلت بدى / روزشان جوالن و خوش حالت بدى‏
entries.english: Would that I were a skilled physician! I would have gone on foot to Layla first of all.’

entries.farsi: قل تعالوا گفت حق ما را بدان / تا بود شرم اشكنى ما را نشان‏
entries.english: God said to us, ‘Say, Come ye,’ in order to signify to us the vanquishing our feeling of shame.

entries.farsi: ليتني كنت طبيبا حاذقا / كنت أمشي نحو ليلى سابقا
entries.english: If bats had sight and means, they would fly about and enjoy themselves by day.”

entries.farsi: گفت چون شاه كرم ميدان رود / عين هر بى‏آلتى آلت شود
entries.english: The wife said, “When the gracious King goes into the field (maydan), the essence of every lack of means becomes a means,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه آلت دعوى است و هستى است / كار در بى‏آلتى و پستى است‏
entries.english: Because the means is pretension and self-existence: the matter lies in lack of means and non-existence.”

entries.farsi: پس گواهى بايدم بر مفلسى / تا شهم رحمى كند يا مونسى‏
entries.english: “How,” said he, “should I do business without means, unless I make it manifest that I have no means?

entries.farsi: گفت كى بى‏آلتى سودا كنم / تا نه من بى‏آلتى پيدا كنم‏
entries.english: Therefore I must have proof of my lack of means, that he may pity me.

entries.farsi: تو گواهى غير گفت‏وگو و رنگ / وانما تا رحم آرد شاه شنگ‏
entries.english: Produce some proof besides talk and show, so that the beauteous King may take pity,

entries.farsi: كاين گواهى كه ز گفت و رنگ بد / نزد آن قاضى القضاة آن جرح شد
entries.english: For the testimony that consisted of talk and show was invalidated before that Supreme Judge.

entries.farsi: صدق مى‏خواهد گواه حال او / تا بتابد نور او بى‏قال او
entries.english: He requires truth as witness to his state, so that his light shall shine forth without any words of his.”

sections.title_fa: هديه بردن عرب سبوى آب باران از ميان باديه سوى بغداد به نزد خليفه بر پنداشت آن ‏ كه آن جا هم قحط آب است
sections.title_en: How the Arab carried a jug of rain-water from the midst of the desert as a gift
to the Commander of the Faithful at Baghdad, in the belief that in that town also there was a scarcity of water.

entries.farsi: گفت زن صدق آن بود كز بود خويش / پاك برخيزى تو از مجهود خويش‏
entries.english: The wife said, “ When people with all their might rise up entirely purged of self-existence--that is veracity.

entries.farsi: آب باران است ما را در سبو / ملكت و سرمايه و اسباب تو
entries.english: We have the rain-water in the jug: it is your property and capital and means.

entries.farsi: اين سبوى آب را بردار و رو / هديه ساز و پيش شاهنشاه شو
entries.english: Take this jug of water and depart, make it a gift and go into the presence of the King of kings.

entries.farsi: گو كه ما را غير اين اسباب نيست / در مفازه هيچ به زين آب نيست‏
entries.english: Say, ‘We have no means except this: in the desert there is nothing better than this water.’

entries.farsi: گر خزينه‏ش پر متاع فاخر است / اين چنين آبش نباشد نادر است‏
entries.english: If his treasury is full of gold and jewels, he does not get water like this: it is rare.”

entries.farsi: چيست آن كوزه تن محصور ما / اندر او آب حواس شور ما
entries.english: What is that jug? Our confined body: within it is the briny water of our senses.

entries.farsi: اى خداوند اين خم و كوزه‏ى مرا / در پذير از فضل اللهَّ اشترى‏
entries.english: O Lord, accept this jar and jug of mine by the grace of “God has pur­ chased (from the believers their lives and wealth in return for Paradise).”

entries.farsi: كوزه‏اى با پنج لوله‏ى پنج حس / پاك دار اين آب را از هر نجس‏
entries.english: A jug with five spouts, the five senses: keep this water pure from every filth,

entries.farsi: تا شود زين كوزه منفذ سوى بحر / تا بگيرد كوزه‏ى من خوى بحر
entries.english: That there may be from this jug a passage to the sea, and that my jug may assume the nature of the sea,

entries.farsi: تا چو هديه پيش سلطانش برى / پاك بيند باشدش شه مشترى‏
entries.english: So that when you carry it as a gift to the King, the King may find it pure and be its purchaser;

entries.farsi: بى‏نهايت گردد آبش بعد از آن / پر شود از كوزه‏ى من صد جهان‏
entries.english: After that, its water will become without end: a hundred worlds will be filled from my jug.

entries.farsi: ريش او پر باد كاين هديه كراست / اليق چون او شهى اين است راست‏
entries.english: Stop up its spouts and keep it filled from the jar: God said, “Close your eyes to vain desire.”

entries.farsi: لوله‏ها بر بند و پر دارش ز خم / گفت غضوا عن هوا ابصاركم‏
entries.english: His beard was full of wind: “Who has such a gift as this? This, truly, is worthy of a King like him.”

entries.farsi: زن نمى‏دانست كانجا بر گذر / هست جارى دجله‏ى همچون شكر
entries.english: The wife did not know that in that place on the thoroughfare there is the great stream sweet as sugar,

entries.farsi: در ميان شهر چون دريا روان / پر ز كشتيها و شست ماهيان‏
entries.english: Flowing like a sea through the city, full of boats and fishing-nets.

entries.farsi: اين چنين حسها و ادراكات ما / قطره‏اى باشد در آن نهر صفا
entries.english: Go to the Sultan and behold this pomp and state! Behold the senses of those for whom God has prepared gardens beneath which the rivers flow!

entries.farsi: رو بر سلطان و كار و بار بين / حس تَجْ رِي تَحْ تَهَا األنهار بين‏
entries.english: Our senses and perceptions, such as they are, are a single drop in those rivers.

sections.title_fa: ‏در نمد دوختن زن عرب سبوى آب باران را و مهر نهادن بر وى از غايت اعتقاد عرب
sections.title_en: How the Arab’s wife sewed the jug of rain-water in a felt cloth and put a seal on it because of the Arab’s utter conviction.

entries.farsi: مرد گفت آرى سبو را سر ببند / هين كه اين هديه ست ما را سودمند
entries.english: “Yes,” said the husband, “stop up the mouth of the jug. Take care, for this is a gift that will bring us profit.

entries.farsi: در نمد در دوز تو اين كوزه را / تا گشايد شه به هديه روزه را
entries.english: Sew this jug in felt, that the King may break his fast with our gift,

entries.farsi: كاين چنين اندر همه آفاق نيست / جز رحيق و مايه‏ى اذواق نيست‏
entries.english: For there is no like this in the entire world: no water is as pure as this.”

entries.farsi: ز آن كه ايشان ز آبهاى تلخ و شور / دايما پر علت‏اند و نيم كور
entries.english: Because they are always full of infirmity and half-blind from bitter and briny waters.

entries.farsi: مرغ كآب شور باشد مسكنش / او چه داند جاى آب روشنش‏
entries.english: The bird whose dwelling-place is the briny water, how should it know where to find in it the clear water?

entries.farsi: اين كه اندر چشمه‏ى شور است جات / تو چه دانى شط و جيحون و فرات‏
entries.english: O you whose abode is in the briny spring, how should you know the Shatt and the Jayhun and the Euphrates?

entries.farsi: اى تو نارسته از اين فانى رباط / تو چه دانى محو و سكر و انبساط
entries.english: O you who have not escaped from this fleeting caravanseray how should you know “self-extinction”and “intoxication” and “expansion”?

entries.farsi: ور بدانى نقلت از اب وز جد است / پيش تو اين نامها چون ابجد است‏
entries.english: And if you know, it is handed down to you from father and grandfather: to you these names are like abjad.

entries.farsi: ابجد و هوز چه فاش است و پديد / بر همه طفالن و معنى بس بعيد
entries.english: How plain and evident to all children are abjad and hawwaz, and the real meaning is far away.

entries.farsi: پس سبو برداشت آن مرد عرب / در سفر شد مى‏كشيدش روز و شب‏
entries.english: Then the Arab man took up the jug and set out to journey, carrying it along day and night.

entries.farsi: بر سبو لرزان بد از آفات دهر / هم كشيدش از بيابان تا به شهر
entries.english: He was trembling for the jug, in fear of Fortune’s mischief: all the same, he conveyed it from the desert to the city.

entries.farsi: زن مصال باز كرده از نياز / رب سلم ورد كرده در نماز
entries.english: His wife unrolled the prayer-rug in supplication; she made Rabbi sallim her litany in prayer,

entries.farsi: كه نگه دار آب ما را از خسان / يا رب آن گوهر بدان دريا رسان‏
entries.english: Crying, “Keep our water safe from scoundrels! O Lord, let that pearl arrive at that sea!

entries.farsi: گر چه شويم آگه است و پر فن است / ليك گوهر را هزاران دشمن است‏
entries.english: Although my husband is shrewd and artful, yet the essence has thousands of enemies.

entries.farsi: خود چه باشد گوهر آب كوثر است / قطره‏اى زين است كاصل گوهر است‏
entries.english: Pearl indeed! “It is the water of Kawthar: it is a drop of this that is the origin of the pearl.”

entries.farsi: از دعاهاى زن و زارى او / وز غم مرد و گرانبارى او
entries.english: Through the prayers and lamentation of the wife, and through the husband’s anxiety and his patience under the heavy burden,

entries.farsi: سالم از دزدان و از آسيب سنگ / برد تا دار الخالفه بى‏درنگ‏
entries.english: He bore it without delay, safe from robbers and unhurt by stones, to the seat of the Caliphate.

entries.farsi: ديد درگاهى پر از انعامها / اهل حاجت گستريده دامها
entries.english: He saw a bountiful Court; the needy had spread their nets;

entries.farsi: دم به دم هر سوى صاحب حاجتى / يافته ز آن در عطا و خلعتى
entries.english: Everywhere, moment by moment, some petitioner gained from that Court a donation and robe of honour:

entries.farsi: بهر گبر و مومن و زيبا و زشت / همچو خورشيد و مطر نى چون بهشت‏
entries.english: It was like sun and rain, nay, like Paradise, for infidel and true believer and good folk and bad.

entries.farsi: ديد قومى در نظر آراسته / قوم ديگر منتظر برخاسته‏
entries.english: He beheld some people arrayed in the sight, and others who had risen to their feet waiting.

entries.farsi: خاص و عامه از سليمان تا به مور / زنده گشته چون جهان از نفخ صور
entries.english: High and low, from Solomon to the ant, they had become quickened with life, like the world at the blast of the trumpet.

entries.farsi: آن كه بى‏همت چه با همت شده / و آن كه با همت چه با نعمت شده‏
entries.english: The followers of Form were woven in essence; the followers of Reality had found the Sea of Reality.

entries.farsi: اهل صورت در جواهر بافته / اهل معنى بحر معنى يافته‏
entries.english: Those without aspiration—how aspiring had they become! And those of high aspiration—to what felicity had they attained!

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه چنان كه گدا عاشق كرم است و عاشق كريم، كرم كريم هم عاشق گداست اگر گدا را صبر بيش بود كريم بر در او آيد و اگر كريم را صبر ‏بيش بود گدا بر در او آيد اما صبر گدا كمال گداست و صبر كريم نقصان اوست

sections.title_en: Showing that, as the beggar is in love with bounty and in love with the bountiful giver, so the bounty of the bountiful giver is in love with the beggar: if the beggar have the greater pa­ tience, the bountiful giver will come to his door; and if the bountiful giver have the greater pa­ tience, the beggar will come to his door; but the beggar’s patience is a virtue in the beggar,
while the patience of the bountiful giver is in him a defect.

entries.farsi: بانگ مى‏آمد كه اى طالب بيا / جود محتاج گدايان چون گدا
entries.english: A loud call was coming: “Come, O seeker! Bounty is in need of beggars: like a beggar.

entries.farsi: جود مى‏جويد گدايان و ضعاف / همچو خوبان كاينه جويند صاف‏
entries.english: Bounty is seeking the beggars and the poor, just as fair ones who seek a clear mirror.

entries.farsi: روى خوبان ز آينه زيبا شود / روى احسان از گدا پيدا شود
entries.english: The face of the fair is made beautiful by the mirror; the face of Beneficence is made visible by the beggar.

entries.farsi: پس از اين فرمود حق در و الضحى / بانگ كم زن اى محمد بر گدا
entries.english: Therefore on this account God said in the Sura Wa’d-Duha “O Mohammed, do not shout at the beggar.”

entries.farsi: آن يكى جودش گدا آرد پديد / و آن دگر بخشد گدايان را مزيد
entries.english: Inasmuch as the beggar is the mirror of Bounty, take care! Breath is hurtful to the face of the mirror.

entries.farsi: چون گدا آيينه‏ى جود است هان / دم بود بر روى آيينه زيان‏
entries.english: In the one case, his bounty makes the beggar manifest, while in the other case he, bestows on the beggars more.

entries.farsi: پس گدايان آيت جود حق‏اند / و آن كه با حقند جود مطلق‏اند
entries.english: Beggars, then, are the mirror of God’s bounty, and they that are with God are the Absolute Bounty;

entries.farsi: و آن كه جز اين دوست او خود مرده‏اى است / او بر اين در نيست نقش پرده‏اى است‏
entries.english: And every one except those two is truly a dead man: he is not at this door; he is a picture on a curtain.

sections.title_fa: فرق ميان آن كه درويش است به خدا و تشنه‏ى خدا و ميان آن كه درويش است از خدا و تشنه‏ى غير است
sections.title_en: The difference between one that is poor for God and thirsting for Him and one that is poor of God and thirsting for what is other than He.

entries.farsi: نقش درويش است او نى اهل نان / نقش سگ را تو مينداز استخوان‏
entries.english: He is the picture of a dervish, he is not worthy of bread: do not throw bread to the picture of a dog!

entries.farsi: فقر لقمه دارد او نى فقر حق / پيش نقش مرده‏اى كم نه طبق‏
entries.english: He wants a morsel of food, he does not want God: do not set dishes before a lifeless picture!

entries.farsi: ماهى خاكى بود درويش نان / شكل ماهى ليك از دريا رمان‏
entries.english: The dervish that wants bread is a land-fish: the form of a fish, but fleeing from the sea.

entries.farsi: مرغ خانه ست او نه سيمرغ هوا / لوت نوشد او ننوشد از خدا
entries.english: He is a domestic fowl, not the Simurgh of the air: he swallows sweet morsels; he does not eat from God.

entries.farsi: عاشق حق است او بهر نوال / نيست جانش عاشق حسن و جمال‏
entries.english: He loves God for the sake of gain: his soul is not in love with excellence and beauty.

entries.farsi: گر توهم مى‏كند او عشق ذات / ذات نبود وهم اسما و صفات‏
entries.english: If he conceives that he is in love with the Essence, conception of the names and attributes is not the Essence.

entries.farsi: وهم مخلوق است و مولود آمده ست / حق نزاييده ست او لَمْ يولد است‏
entries.english: Conception is begotten of qualities and definition: God is not begotten, He is lam yulad.

entries.farsi: عاشق تصوير و وهم خويشتن / كى بود از عاشقان ذو المنن‏
entries.english: How should he that is in love with his own imagination and conception be one of them that love the Lord of bounties?

entries.farsi: عاشق آن وهم اگر صادق بود / آن مجاز او حقيقت كش شود
entries.english: If the lover of that illusion be sincere, that metaphor will lead him to the reality.

entries.farsi: شرح مى‏خواهد بيان اين سخن / ليك مى‏ترسم ز افهام كهن‏
entries.english: The exposition of this saying demands a commentary, but I am afraid of senile minds.

entries.farsi: فهم‏هاى كهنه‏ى كوته نظر / صد خيال بد در آرد در فكر
entries.english: Senile and short-sighted minds bring a hundred evil fancies into their thoughts.

entries.farsi: بر سماع راست هر كس چير نيست / لقمه‏ى هر مرغكى انجير نيست‏
entries.english: Not every one is able to hear rightly: the fig is not a morsel for every little bird,

entries.farsi: خاصه مرغى مرده‏اى پوسيده‏اى / پر خيالى اعميى بى‏ديده‏اى‏
entries.english: Especially a bird that is dead, putrid; a blind, eyeless filled with vain fancy.

entries.farsi: نقش ماهى را چه دريا و چه خاك / رنگ هندو را چه صابون و چه زاك‏
entries.english: To the picture of a fish what is the difference between sea and land? To the colour of a Hindu what is the difference between soap and black vitriol?

entries.farsi: نقش اگر غمگين نگارى بر ورق / او ندارد از غم و شادى سبق‏
entries.english: If you depict the portrait on the paper as sorrowful, it has no lesson of sorrow or joy.

entries.farsi: صورتش غمگين و او فارغ از آن / صورتش خندان و او ز آن بى‏نشان
entries.english: Its appearance is sorrowful, but it is free from that; its appearance is smiling, but it has no impression of that.

entries.farsi: وين غم و شادى كه اندر دل خفى است / پيش آن شادى و غم جز نقش نيست‏
entries.english: And this sorrow and joy which are delineated in the heart are naught but a picture in comparison with that joy and sorrow

entries.farsi: صورت خندان نقش از بهر تست / تا از آن صورت شود معنى درست
entries.english: The picture’s smiling appearance is for your sake, in order that by means of that picture the reality may be established.

entries.farsi: نقشهايى كاندر اين حمامهاست / از برون جامه كن چون جامهاست‏
entries.english: The pictures which are in these hot baths, from outside the undressing-room, are like clothes.

entries.farsi: تا برونى جامه‏ها بينى و بس / جامه بيرون كن در آ اى هم نفس‏
entries.english: So long as you are outside, you see only the clothes: put off your clothes and enter, O kindred spirit,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه با جامه درون سو راه نيست / تن ز جان جامه ز تن آگاه نيست‏
entries.english: Because, with your clothes, there is no way inside: the body is ignorant of the soul, the clothes of the body.

sections.title_fa: پيش آمدن نقيبان و دربانان خليفه از بهر اكرام اعرابى و پذيرفتن هديه‏ى او را
sections.title_en: How the Caliph’s officers and chamberlains came forward to pay their respects to the Bedouin and to receive his gift.

entries.farsi: آن عرابى از بيابان بعيد / بر در دار الخالفه چون رسيد
entries.english: When the Bedouin arrived from the remote desert to the gate of the Caliph’s palace,

entries.farsi: پس نقيبان پيش او باز آمدند / بس گالب لطف بر جيبش زدند
entries.english: The court officers went to meet the Bedouin: they sprinkled much rose-water of graciousness on his bosom.

entries.farsi: حاجت او فهمشان شد بى‏مقال / كار ايشان بد عطا پيش از سؤال‏
entries.english: Without speech they perceived what he wanted: it was their practice to give before being asked.

entries.farsi: پس بدو گفتند يا وجه العرب / از كجايى چونى از راه و تعب‏
entries.english: Then they said to him, “O chief of the Arabs, where do you come from? How are you after the journey and fatigue?”

entries.farsi: گفت وجهم گر مرا وجهى دهيد / بى‏وجوهم چون پس پشتم نهيد
entries.english: He said, “I am a chief, if you give me any countenance; I am without means when you put me behind your backs.

entries.farsi: اى كه در روتان نشان مهترى / فرتان خوشتر ز زر جعفرى‏
entries.english: O you in whose faces are the marks of eminence, O you whose splendour is more pleasing than the gold of Ja`far,

entries.farsi: اى كه يك ديدارتان ديدارها / اى نثار دينتان دينارها
entries.english: O you, one sight of whom is sights, O you at the sight of whom pieces of gold are scattered,

entries.farsi: اى همه ينظر بنور اللهَّ شده / از بر حق بهر بخشش آمده‏
entries.english: O you, all of whom have become seeing by the light of God, who have come from God for the sake of munificence,

entries.farsi: تا زنيد آن كيمياهاى نظر / بر سر مسهاى اشخاص بشر
entries.english: That you may cast the elixir of your looks upon the copper of human individuals,

entries.farsi: من غريبم از بيابان آمدم / بر اميد لطف سلطان آمدم‏
entries.english: I am a stranger: I have come from the desert: I have come in hope of the grace of the Sultan.

entries.farsi: بوى لطف او بيابانها گرفت / ذره‏هاى ريگ هم جانها گرفت‏
entries.english: The scent of his grace covered the deserts: even the grains of sand were ensouled.

entries.farsi: تا بدين جا بهر دينار آمدم / چون رسيدم مست ديدار آمدم‏
entries.english: I came all the way to this place for the sake of dinars: as soon as I arrived, I became drunk with sight.”

entries.farsi: بهر نان شخصى سوى نانوا دويد / داد جان چون حسن نانوا را بديد
entries.english: A person ran to the baker for bread: on seeing the beauty of the baker, he gave up the ghost.

entries.farsi: بهر فرجه شد يكى تا گلستان / فرجه‏ى او شد جمال باغبان‏
entries.english: A certain man went to the rose-garden to take his pleasure, and found it in the beauty of the gardener,

entries.farsi: همچو اعرابى كه آب از چه كشيد / آب حيوان از رخ يوسف چشيد
entries.english: Like the desert Arab who drew water from the well and tasted the Water of Life from the face of Joseph

entries.farsi: رفت موسى كاتش آرد او به دست / آتشى ديد او كه از آتش برست‏
entries.english: Moses went to fetch fire: he beheld such a Fire that he escaped from fire.

entries.farsi: جست عيسى تا رهد از دشمنان / بردش آن جستن به چارم آسمان‏
entries.english: Jesus sprang up, to escape from his enemies: that spring carried him to the Fourth Heaven.

entries.farsi: دام آدم خوشه‏ى گندم شده / تا وجودش خوشه‏ى مردم شده‏
entries.english: The ear of wheat became a trap for Adam, so that his existence became the wheat-ear of mankind.

entries.farsi: باز آيد سوى دام از بهر خور / ساعد شه يابد و اقبال و فر
entries.english: The falcon comes to the snare for food: it finds the fore-arm of the King and fortune and glory.

entries.farsi: طفل شد مكتب پى كسب هنر / بر اميد مرغ با لطف پدر
entries.english: The child went to school to acquire knowledge, in hope of its father’s pretty bird;

entries.farsi: پس ز مكتب آن يكى صدرى شده / ماهگانه داده و بدرى شده‏
entries.english: Then, by school, that child rose to the top, paid monthly fees, and became perfect.

entries.farsi: آمده عباس حرب از بهر كين / بهر قمع احمد و استيز دين‏
entries.english: Abbas had come to war for vengeance’ sake, for the purpose of subduing Ahmad and opposing the religion:

entries.farsi: من بر اين در طالب چيز آمدم / صدر گشتم چون به دهليز آمدم‏
entries.english: He and his descendants in the Caliphate became a back and front to the religion until the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: گشته دين را تا قيامت پشت و رو / در خالفت او و فرزندان او
entries.english: “I came to this court in quest of wealth: as soon as I entered the portico I became chief.

entries.farsi: آب آوردم به تحفه بهر نان / بوى نانم برد تا صدر جنان‏
entries.english: I brought water as a gift for the sake of bread: hope of bread led me to the highest place in Paradise.

entries.farsi: نان برون راند آدمى را از بهشت / نان مرا اندر بهشتى در سرشت‏
entries.english: Bread drove an Adam forth from Paradise: bread caused me to mix with those who belong to Paradise.

entries.farsi: رستم از آب و ز نان همچون ملك / بى‏غرض گردم بر اين در چون فلك‏
entries.english: I have been freed, like the angels, from water and bread: without object of desire I move round this court, like the sphere of heaven.”

entries.farsi: بى‏غرض نبود به گردش در جهان / غير جسم و غير جان عاشقان‏
entries.english: Nothing in the world is without object in its movement except the bodies and the souls of lovers.

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه عاشق دنيا بر مثال عاشق ديوارى است كه بر او تاب آفتاب زند و جهد و جهاد نكرد تا فهم كند كه آن تاب و رونق از ديوار نيست از قرص آفتاب است در آسمان چهارم الجرم كلى دل بر ديوار نهاد چون پرتو آفتاب به آفتاب پيوست او محروم ماند

sections.title_en: heart on the wall, and when the sunbeams rejoin the sun, he is left for ever in despair: “ and a bar is placed between them and that which they desire.”

entries.farsi: َابدا وَ حِيلَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَ بَيْنَ ما يَشْ تَهُون / عاشقان كل نه اين عشاق جزو
entries.english: The lovers of the Whole are not those who love the part: he that longed for the part failed to attain unto the Whole.

entries.farsi: ماند از كل آن كه شد مشتاق جزو / چون كه جزوى عاشق جزوى شود
entries.english: When a part falls in love with a part, the object of its love soon goes to its own whole.

entries.farsi: زود معشوقش به كل خود رود / ريش گاو بنده‏ى غير آمد او
entries.english: He became the laughing-stock of another’s slave: he became drowning and clung to some one weak.

entries.farsi: غرقه شد كف در ضعيفى در زد او / نيست حاكم تا كند تيمار او
entries.english: He possesses no authority, that he should care for him: shall he do his own master’s business or his ?

sections.title_fa: مثل عرب إِذا زنيت فازن بالحرة و إِذا سرقت فاسرق الدرة
sections.title_en: The Arabic proverb, “If you commit fornication; commit it with a free woman, and if you steal, steal a pearl.”

entries.farsi: كار خواجه‏ى خود كند يا كار او
entries.english: Hence, “fornicate with a free woman,” became proverbial; “steal a pearl” were transferred to this.

entries.farsi: فازن بالحرة پى اين شد مثل / فاسرق الدرة بدين شد منتقل‏
entries.english: The slave went away to his master: he was left in misery. The scent of the rose went to the rose: he was left with the thorn.

entries.farsi: بنده سوى خواجه شد او ماند زار / بوى گل شد سوى گل او ماند خار
entries.english: He was left far from the object of his desire— his labour lost, his toil useless, his foot wounded,

entries.farsi: او بمانده دور از مطلوب خويش / سعى ضايع رنج باطل پاى ريش‏
entries.english: Like the hunter who catches a shadow— how should the shadow become his property?

entries.farsi: همچو صيادى كه گيرد سايه‏اى / سايه كى گردد و را سرمايه‏اى‏
entries.english: The man has grasped tightly the shadow of a bird; the bird on the branch of the tree is fallen into amazement,

entries.farsi: سايه‏ى مرغى گرفته مرد سخت / مرغ حيران گشته بر شاخ درخت‏
entries.english: “I wonder who this crack-brained fellow is laughing at. Here’s folly for you, here’s a rotten cause!”

entries.farsi: كاين مدمغ بر كه مى‏خندد عجب / اينت باطل اينت پوسيده سبب‏
entries.english: And if you say that the part is connected with the whole, eat thorns: the thorn is connected with the rose.

entries.farsi: جز ز يك رو نيست پيوسته به كل / ور نه خود باطل بدى بعث رسل‏
entries.english: Except from one point of view, it is not connected with the whole: otherwise, indeed, the mission of the prophets would be vain,

entries.farsi: چون رسوالن از پى پيوستن‏اند / پس چه پيوندندشان چون يك تن‏اند
entries.english: Inasmuch as the prophets are in order to connect: how, then, should they connect them when they are one body?

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد اى غالم / روز بى‏گه شد حكايت كن تمام‏
entries.english: This discourse has no end. O lad, the day is late: conclude the tale.

sections.title_fa: ‏سپردن عرب هديه را يعنى سبو را به غالمان خليفه
sections.title_en: How the Arab delivered the gift, that is, the jug to theCaliph’s servants.

entries.farsi: آن سبوى آب را در پيش داشت / تخم خدمت را در آن حضرت بكاشت‏
entries.english: He presented the jug of water; he sowed the seed of homage in that court.

entries.farsi: آب شيرين و سبوى سبز و نو / ز آب بارانى كه جمع آمد به گو
entries.english: “Bear this gift,” said he, “to the Sultan; redeem the King’s suitor from indigence.

entries.farsi: گفت اين هديه بدان سلطان بريد / سايل شه را ز حاجت واخريد
entries.english: It is sweet water and a new green jug— some of the rainwater that collected in the ditch.”

entries.farsi: خنده مى‏آمد نقيبان را از آن / ليك پذرفتند آن را همچو جان‏
entries.english: The officials smiled at that, but they accepted it as life,

entries.farsi: خوى شاهان در رعيت جا كند / چرخ اخضر خاك را خضرا كند
entries.english: Because the graciousness of the good and wise King had made a mark on all the courtiers.

entries.farsi: ز آن كه لطف شاه خوب با خبر / كرده بود اندر همه اركان اثر
entries.english: The disposition of kings settles in their subjects: the green sky makes the earth verdant.

entries.farsi: شه چو حوضى دان حشم چون لوله‏ها / آب از لوله روان در كوله‏ها
entries.english: Regard the king as a reservoir with pipes in every direction, and water running from all like hoppers.

entries.farsi: چون كه آب جمله از حوضى است پاك / هر يكى آبى دهد خوش ذوقناك‏
entries.english: When the water in all is from a pure reservoir, every single one gives sweet water, pleasant to taste;

entries.farsi: ور در آن حوض آب شور است و پليد / هر يكى لوله همان آرد پديد
entries.english: But if the water in the reservoir is brackish and dirty, every pipe brings the same to view,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه پيوسته ست هر لوله به حوض / خوض كن در معنى اين حرف خوض‏
entries.english: Because every pipe is connected with the reservoir. Dive, dive into the meaning of these words.

entries.farsi: لطف شاهنشاه جان بى‏وطن / چون اثر كرده ست اندر كل تن‏
entries.english: How the imperial grace of the homeless Spirit has produced effects on the whole body;

entries.farsi: لطف عقل خوش نهاد خوش نسب / چون همه تن را در آرد در ادب‏
entries.english: How the grace of Reason, which is of goodly nature, of goodly lineage, brings the entire body into discipline;

entries.farsi: عشق شنگ بى‏قرار بى‏سكون / چون در آرد كل تن را در جنون‏
entries.english: How Love, saucy, uncontrolled, and restless, throws the whole body into madness.

entries.farsi: لطف آب بحر كاو چون كوثر است / سنگ ريزه‏ش جمله در و گوهر است‏
entries.english: The purity of the water of the Sea that is like Kawthar, its pebbles are pearls and gems.

entries.farsi: هر هنر كه استا بدان معروف شد / جان شاگردان بدان موصوف شد
entries.english: For whatever science the master is renowned, the souls of his pupils become endued with the same.

entries.farsi: پيش استاد اصولى هم اصول / خواند آن شاگرد چست با حصول‏
entries.english: With the master-theologian the quick and industrious pupil reads theology.

entries.farsi: پيش استاد فقيه آن فقه خوان / فقه خواند نى اصول اندر بيان‏
entries.english: With the master-jurist the student of jurisprudence reads jurisprudence, when he expounds it, not theology.

entries.farsi: پيش استادى كه او نحوى بود / جان شاگردش از او نحوى شود
entries.english: Then the master who is a grammarian— the soul of his pupil becomes imbued by him with grammar.

entries.farsi: باز استادى كه او محو ره است / جان شاگردش از او محو شه است‏
