entries.english: Again, the master who is absorbed in the Way— because of him the soul of his pupil is absorbed in the King.

entries.farsi: زين همه انواع دانش روز مرگ / دانش فقر است ساز راه و برگ‏
entries.english: Of all these various kinds of knowledge, on the day of death the equipment and provision for the road is the knowledge of poverty.

sections.title_fa: ‏حكايت ماجراى نحوى و كشتيبان
sections.title_en: The story of what passed between the grammarian and the boatman.

entries.farsi: آن يكى نحوى به كشتى درنشست / رو به كشتيبان نهاد آن خود پرست‏
entries.english: A certain grammarian embarked in a boat. That self-conceited person turned to the boatman

entries.farsi: گفت هيچ از نحو خواندى گفت / گفت نيم عمر تو شد در فنا
entries.english: And said, “Have you ever studied grammar?” “No,” he replied. The other said, “Half your life is gone to naught.”

entries.farsi: دل شكسته گشت كشتيبان ز تاب / ليك آن دم كرد خامش از جواب‏
entries.english: The boatman became heart-broken with grief, but at the time he refrained from answering.

entries.farsi: باد كشتى را به گردابى فگند / گفت كشتيبان به آن نحوى بلند
entries.english: The wind cast the boat into a whirlpool: the boatman spoke loud to the grammarian,

entries.farsi: هيچ دانى آشنا كردن بگو / گفت نى اى خوش جواب خوب رو
entries.english: “Tell me, do you know how to swim?” “No,” said he,” O fair-spoken good-looking man!”

entries.farsi: گفت كل عمرت اى نحوى فناست / ز آن كه كشتى غرق اين گردابهاست‏
entries.english: “O grammarian,” said he, “your whole life is nothing, because the boat is sinking in these whirlpools.”

entries.farsi: محو مى‏بايد نه نحو اينجا بدان / گر تو محوى بى‏خطر در آب ران‏
entries.english: Know that here mahw (self-effacement) is needed, not nahw (grammar): if you are mahw (dead to self), plunge into the sea without peril.

entries.farsi: آب دريا مرده را بر سر نهد / ور بود زنده ز دريا كى رهد
entries.english: The water of the sea places the dead one on its head; but if he is living, how shall he escape from the sea?

entries.farsi: چون بمردى تو ز اوصاف بشر / بحر اسرارت نهد بر فرق سر
entries.english: Inasmuch as you have died to the attributes of the flesh, the Sea of consciousness will place you on the crown of its head.

entries.farsi: اى كه خلقان را تو خر مى‏خوانده‏اى / اين زمان چون خر بر اين يخ مانده‏اى‏
entries.english: O you who halt called the people asses, at this time you art left, like an ass, upon this ice.

entries.farsi: گر تو عالمه‏ى زمانى در جهان / نك فناى اين جهان بين وين زمان‏
entries.english: If in the world you art the most learned scholar of the time, behold the passing-away of this world and this time!

entries.farsi: مرد نحوى را از آن در دوختيم / تا شما را نحو محو آموختيم
entries.english: We have stitched in the grammarian, that we might teach you the grammar (nahw) of self-effacement (mahw).

entries.farsi: فقه فقه و نحو نحو و صرف صرف / در كم آمد يابى اى يار شگرف‏
entries.english: In self-loss, O venerated friend, you will find the jurisprudence of jurisprudence, the grammar of grammar, and the accidence of accidence.

entries.farsi: آن سبوى آب دانشهاى ماست / و آن خليفه دجله‏ى علم خداست‏
entries.english: That jug of water is our different sorts of knowledge, and the Caliph is the Tigris of God’s knowledge.

entries.farsi: ما سبوها پر به دجله مى‏بريم / گر نه خر دانيم خود را ما خريم‏
entries.english: We are carrying jugs full to the Tigris: if we do not know ourselves to be asses, asses we are.

entries.farsi: بارى اعرابى بدان معذور بود / كو ز دجله بى‏خبر بود و ز رود
entries.english: After all, the Bedouin was excusable, for he was ignorant of the Tigris and of the river.

entries.farsi: گر ز دجله با خبر بودى چو ما / او نبردى آن سبو را جا به جا
entries.english: If he had been acquainted with the Tigris, as we are, he would not have carried that jug from place to place;

entries.farsi: بلكه از دجله چو واقف آمدى / آن سبو را بر سر سنگى زدى‏
entries.english: No, had he been aware of the Tigris, he would have dashed that jug against a stone.

sections.title_fa: قبول كردن خليفه هديه را و عطا فرمودن با كمال بى‏نيازى از آن هديه و از آن سبو
sections.title_en: How the Caliph accepted the gift and bestowed largesse, notwithstanding that he was entirely without need of the gift and the jug.

entries.farsi: چون خليفه ديد و احوالش شنيد / آن سبو را پر ز زر كرد و مزيد
entries.english: When the Caliph saw and heard his story, he filled the jug with gold and added.

entries.farsi: آن عرب را كرد از فاقه خالص / داد بخششها و خلعتهاى خاص‏
entries.english: He delivered the Arab from penury; he bestowed donations and special robes of honour,

entries.farsi: كاين سبو پر زر به دست او دهيد / چون كه واگردد سوى دجله‏ش بريد
entries.english: Saying, “Give into his hand this jug full of gold. When he returns, take him to the Tigris.

entries.farsi: از ره خشك آمده ست و از سفر / از ره آبش بود نزديكتر
entries.english: He has come by way of the desert and by travelling: it will be nearer for him by water.”

entries.farsi: چون به كشتى درنشست و دجله ديد / سجده مى‏كرد از حيا و مى‏خميد
entries.english: When he embarked in the boat and beheld the Tigris, he was prostrating himself in shame and bowing,

entries.farsi: كاى عجب لطف اين شه وهاب را / وين عجبتر كو ستد آن آب را
entries.english: Saying, “Oh, wonderful is the kindness of that bounteous King, and It is more wonderful that he took that water.

entries.farsi: چون پذيرفت از من آن درياى جود / آن چنان نقد دغل را زود زود
entries.english: How did that Sea of munificence so quickly accept from me such spurious coin as this?”

entries.farsi: كل عالم را سبو دان اى پسر / كاو بود از علم و خوبى تا به سر
entries.english: Know, O son that everything in the universe is a jug which is to the brim with wisdom and beauty.

entries.farsi: قطره‏اى از دجله‏ى خوبى اوست / كان نمى‏گنجد ز پرى زير پوست‏
entries.english: It is a drop of the Tigris of His beauty, which because of its fullness is not contained under the skin.

entries.farsi: گنج مخفى بد ز پرى چاك كرد / خاك را تابان تر از افلاک كرد
entries.english: It was a hidden treasure: because of its fullness it burst forth and made the earth more shining than the heavens.

entries.farsi: گنج مخفى بد ز پرى جوش كرد / خاك را سلطان اطلس پوش كرد
entries.english: It was a hidden treasure: because of its fullness it surged up and made the earth a sultan robed in satin.

entries.farsi: ور بديدى شاخى از دجله‏ى خدا / آن سبو را او فنا كردى فنا
entries.english: And if he had seen a branch of the Divine Tigris, he would have destroyed that jug, destroyed it.

entries.farsi: آن كه ديدندش هميشه بى‏خودند / بى‏خودانه بر سبو سنگى زدند
entries.english: They that saw it are always beside themselves: like one beside himself, they hurled a stone at the jug.

entries.farsi: اى ز غيرت بر سبو سنگى زده / و اين سبو ز اشكست كاملتر شده‏
entries.english: O you who from jealousy have hurled a stone at the jug, while the jug has become more perfect through being shattered,

entries.farsi: خم شكسته آب از او ناريخته / صد درستى زين شكست انگيخته‏
entries.english: The jar is shattered, the water is not spilled from it: from this shattering have arisen a hundred soundnesses.

entries.farsi: جزو جزو خم به رقص است و به حال / عقل جزوى را نموده اين محال‏
entries.english: Every piece of the jar is in dance and ecstasy, to the partial reason this seems absurd.

entries.farsi: نى سبو پيدا در اين حالت نه آب / خوش ببين و اللهَّ اعلم بالصواب‏
entries.english: In this state neither the jug nor the water is manifest. Consider well, and God knows best what is right.

entries.farsi: چون در معنى زنى بازت كنند / پر فكرت زن كه شهبازت كنند
entries.english: When you knock at the door of Reality, it will be opened to you: beat the pinion of thought, in order that you may be made a king-falcon.

entries.farsi: پر فكرت شد گل آلود و گران / ز آن كه گل خوارى ترا گل شد چو نان‏
entries.english: The pinion of your thought has become mud-stained and heavy because you are a clay-eater: clay has become to you as bread.

entries.farsi: نان گل است و گوشت كمتر خور از اين / تا نمانى همچو گل اندر زمين‏
entries.english: Bread and meat are clay: eat little of them, that you may not remain in the earth, like clay.

entries.farsi: چون گرسنه مى‏شوى سگ مى‏شوى / تند و بد پيوند و بد رگ مى‏شوى‏
entries.english: When you become hungry, you become a dog: you become fierce and ill-tempered and ill-natured.

entries.farsi: چون شدى تو سير مردارى شدى / بى‏خبر بى‏پا چو ديوارى شدى‏
entries.english: When you have eaten your fill, you have become a carcass: you have become devoid of understanding and without feet, like a wall.

entries.farsi: پس دمى مردار و ديگر دم سگى / چون كنى در راه شيران خوش تگى‏
entries.english: So at one time you are a carcass and at another time a dog: how will you run well in the road of the lions?

entries.farsi: آلت اشكار خود جز سگ مدان / كمترك انداز سگ را استخوان‏
entries.english: Know that your only means of hunting is the dog: throw bones to the dog but seldom,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه سگ چون سير شد سركش شود / كى سوى صيد و شكار خوش دود
entries.english: Because when the dog has eaten its fill, it becomes rebellious: how should it run to the goodly chase and hunt?

entries.farsi: آن عرب را بى‏نوايى مى‏كشيد / تا بدان درگاه و آن دولت رسيد
entries.english: Want of food was leading the Arab to that court, and he found his fortune.

entries.farsi: در حكايت گفته‏ايم احسان شاه / در حق آن بى‏نواى بى‏پناه‏
entries.english: We have related in the story the kindness shown by the King to that needy one who had no refuge.

entries.farsi: هر چه گويد مرد عاشق بوى عشق / از دهانش مى‏جهد در كوى عشق‏
entries.english: Whatever the man in love speaks, the scent of Love is springing from his mouth into the abode of Love.

entries.farsi: گر بگويد فقه فقر آيد همه / بوى فقر آيد از آن خوش دمدمه‏
entries.english: If he speak theology, it all turns to poverty: the scent of poverty comes from that man of sweet and beguiling discourse.

entries.farsi: ور بگويد كفر دارد بوى دين / ور به شك گويد شكش گردد يقين‏
entries.english: And if he speaks infidelity, it has the scent of religion, and if he speaks doubtfully, his doubt turns to certainty.

entries.farsi: كف كژ كز بحر صدقى خاسته است / اصل صاف آن فرع را آراسته است‏
entries.english: The perverse froth that has risen from a sea of sincerity that turbid has been set out by the pure source.

entries.farsi: آن كفش را صافى و محقوق دان / همچو دشنام لب معشوق دان‏
entries.english: Know that its froth is pure and worthy: know that it is like revilement from the lips of the beloved,

entries.farsi: گشته آن دشنام نامطلوب او / خوش ز بهر عارض محبوب او
entries.english: Whose unsought reproaches have become sweet for the sake of her cheek which he desires.

entries.farsi: از شكر گر شكل نانى مى‏پزى / طعم قند آيد نه نان چون مى‏مزى‏
entries.english: If he speaks falsehood, it seems the truth. O falsehood that would adorn the truth!

entries.farsi: گر بگويد كژ نمايد راستى / اى كژى كه راست را آراستى‏
entries.english: If you cook of sugar in the form of a loaf of bread, it will taste of candy, not of bread, while you are sucking it.

entries.farsi: ور بيابد مومنى زرين وثن / كى هلد آن را براى هر شمن‏
entries.english: If a true believer finds a golden idol, how should he leave it for the sake of a worshipper?

entries.farsi: بلكه گيرد اندر آتش افكند / صورت عاريتش را بشكند
entries.english: No, he will take it and cast it into the fire: he will break its borrowed form,

entries.farsi: تا نماند بر ذهب شكل وثن / ز آن كه صورت مانع است و راه زن‏
entries.english: In order that the idol-shape may not remain on the gold, because Form hinders and waylays.

entries.farsi: ذات زرش ذات ربانيت است / نقش بت بر نقد زر عاريت است‏
entries.english: The essence of its gold is the essence of Lordship: the idol-stamp on the sterling gold is borrowed.

entries.farsi: بهر كيكى تو گليمى را مسوز / وز صداع هر مگس مگذار روز
entries.english: Do not burn a blanket on account of a flea, and do not let the day go on account of every gnat’s headache.

entries.farsi: بت پرستى چون بمانى در صور / صورتش بگذار و در معنى نگر
entries.english: You are an idol-worshipper when you remain in forms: leave its form and look at the reality.

entries.farsi: مرد حجى همره حاجى طلب / خواه هندو خواه ترك و يا عرب‏
entries.english: If you are a man for the Pilgrimage, seek a pilgrim companion, whether he is a Hindu or a Turk or an Arab.

entries.farsi: منگر اندر نقش و اندر رنگ او / بنگر اندر عزم و در آهنگ او
entries.english: Do not look at his figure and colour, look at his purpose and intention.

entries.farsi: گر سياه است او هم آهنگ تو است / تو سپيدش خوان كه هم رنگ تو است‏
entries.english: If he is black, he is in accord with you: call him white, for his complexion is the same as yours.

entries.farsi: اين حكايت گفته شد زير و زبر / همچو فكر عاشقان بى‏پا و سر
entries.english: This story has been told up and down, like the doings of lovers, without foot or head.

entries.farsi: سر ندارد چون ز ازل بوده ست پيش / پا ندارد با ابد بوده ست خويش‏
entries.english: It has no head, inasmuch as it existed before eternity; it has no foot: it has been akin to everlastingness.

entries.farsi: بلكه چون آب است هر قطره از آن / هم سر است و پا و هم بى‏هردوان‏
entries.english: No, it is like water: every drop is both head and foot, and at the same time without both.

entries.farsi: حاش هلل اين حكايت نيست هين / نقد حال ما و تست اين خوش ببين‏
entries.english: This is not a story, mark you! God forbid! This is the ready money of my state and yours. Consider well,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه صوفى با كر و با فر بود / هر چه آن ماضى است ال يذكر بود
entries.english: Because the Sufi is grand and glorious: whatever is past is not remembered.

entries.farsi: هم عرب ما هم سبو ما هم ملك / جمله ما يُؤْفَكُ عَنْهُ مَنْ أفك‏
entries.english: We are both the Arab and the jug and the King; we are all: he that has been turned away from it shall be turned away.

entries.farsi: بشنو اكنون اصل انكار از چه خاست / ز آن كه كل را گونه گونه جزوهاست
entries.english: Know that the husband is Reason, and the wife is greed and cupidity: these two are dark and deniers; Reason is the candle.

entries.farsi: عقل را شو دان و زن را نفس و طمع / اين دو ظلمانى و منكر عقل شمع‏
entries.english: Now hear the origin of their denial, from where it arose: from the fact that the Whole has various parts.

entries.farsi: جزو كل نى جزوها نسبت به كل / نى چو بوى گل كه باشد جزو گل‏
entries.english: The parts of the Whole are not parts in relation to the Whole— not like the scent of the rose, which is a part of the rose.

entries.farsi: لطف سبزه جزو لطف گل بود / بانگ قمرى جزو آن بلبل بود
entries.english: The beauty of green herbs is a part of the Rose’s beauty, the coo of the turtle-dove is a part of that Nightingale.

entries.farsi: گر شوم مشغول اشكال و جواب / تشنگان را كى توانم داد آب‏
entries.english: If I become occupied with a difficulty and the answer, how shall I be able to give water to the thirsty?

entries.farsi: گر تو اشكالى به كلى و حرج / صبر كن الصبر مفتاح الفرج‏
entries.english: If you are wholly perplexed and in straits, have patience: patience is the key to joy.

entries.farsi: احتما كن احتما ز انديشه‏ها / فكر شير و گور و دلها بيشه‏ها
entries.english: Abstain from thoughts, abstain: thought is the lion and the wild ass, and hearts are the thickets.

entries.farsi: احتماها بر دواها سرور است / ز آن كه خاريدن فزونى گر است‏
entries.english: Acts of abstinence are superior to medicines, because scratching is an increase of the itch.

entries.farsi: احتما اصل دوا آمد يقين / احتما كن قوت جان را ببين‏
entries.english: Assuredly abstinence is the first principle of medicine: abstain, and behold the strength of the spirit (jaan).

entries.farsi: قابل اين گفته‏ها شو گوش‏وار / تا كه از زر سازمت من گوشوار
entries.english: Receive these words, like the ear, that I may make for you an earring of gold:

entries.farsi: حلقه در گوش مه زرگر شوى / تا به ماه و تا ثريا بر شوى‏
entries.english: You will become a ring in the ear of the Moon that works in gold; you will ascend to the moon and the Pleiades.

entries.farsi: اوال بشنو كه خلق مختلف / مختلف جانند از يا تا الف‏
entries.english: First, hear that the diverse created beings are spiritually different, from ya (Y) to alif (A).

entries.farsi: در حروف مختلف شور و شكى است / گر چه از يك رو ز سر تا پا يكى است‏
entries.english: Amongst the various letters there is a confusion and uncertainty, though from one point of view they are one from head to foot.

entries.farsi: از يكى رو ضد و يك رو متحد / از يكى رو هزل و از يك روى جد
entries.english: From one aspect they are opposites, and from one aspect they are unified; from one aspect they are a joke, and from one aspect they are serious.

entries.farsi: پس قيامت روز عرض اكبر است / عرض او خواهد كه با زيب و فر است‏
entries.english: Therefore the Resurrection is the day of the supreme inspection: inspection is desired by him who is glorious and splendid.

entries.farsi: هر كه چون هندوى بد سودايى است / روز عرضش نوبت رسوايى است‏
entries.english: Whoever is like a fraudulent Hindu, for him the day of inspection is the time of exposure.

entries.farsi: چون ندارد روى همچون آفتاب / او نخواهد جز شبى همچون نقاب‏
entries.english: Inasmuch as he has not a face like the sun, he desires nothing but night like a veil.

entries.farsi: برگ يك گل چون ندارد خار او / شد بهاران دشمن اسرار او
entries.english: Since his thorn has not a single rose-leaf, spring is the enemy of his conscience,

entries.farsi: و انكه سر تا پا گل است و سوسن است / پس بهار او را دو چشم روشن است‏
entries.english: While to one that is roses and lilies from head to foot spring is a pair of bright eyes.

entries.farsi: تا بپوشد حسن آن و ننگ اين / تا نبينى رنگ آن و رنگ اين‏
entries.english: The unspiritual thorn wishes for autumn, for autumn, in order that it may jostle with the rose-garden,

entries.farsi: خار بى‏معنى خزان خواهد خزان / تا زند پهلوى خود با گلستان‏
entries.english: And that it may hide the beauty of that and the shame of this, so that you may not see the colour of that and the colour of this.

entries.farsi: پس خزان او را بهار است و حيات / يك نمايد سنگ و ياقوت زكات‏
entries.english: Therefore autumn is its spring and life; the stone and the pure ruby appear one.

entries.farsi: باغبان هم داند آن را در خزان / ليك ديد يك به از ديد جهان‏
entries.english: The Gardener knows that even in autumn, but the One’s sight is better than the world’s sight.

entries.farsi: خود جهان آن يك كس است او ابله است / هر ستاره بر فلك جزو مه است‏
entries.english: Truly that One Person is the world: he is unaware of evil. The stars, every one, are all part of the Moon.

entries.farsi: پس همى‏گويند هر نقش و نگار / مژده مژده نك همى‏آيد بهار
entries.english: Therefore every fair form and shape is crying, “Good news! Good news! Lo, here comes the spring.”

entries.farsi: تا بود تابان شكوفه چون زره / كى كند آن ميوه‏ها پيدا گره
entries.english: So long as the blossom is shining like a coat of mail, how should the fruits display their knobs?

entries.farsi: چون شكوفه ريخت ميوه سر كند / چون كه تن بشكست جان سر بر زند
entries.english: When the blossom is shed, the fruit comes to a head: when the body is shattered, the spirit lifts up its head.

entries.farsi: ميوه معنى و شكوفه صورتش / آن شكوفه مژده ميوه نعمتش‏
entries.english: The fruit is the purpose; the blossom is its face: the blossom is the good news; the fruit is the bounty for it.

entries.farsi: چون شكوفه ريخت ميوه شد پديد / چون كه آن كم شد شد اين اندر مزيد
entries.english: When the blossom was shed, the fruit became visible: when that diminished this began to increase.

entries.farsi: تا كه نان نشكست قوت كى دهد / ناشكسته خوشه‏ها كى مى‏دهد
entries.english: How should bread give strength until it is broken? How should uncrushed clusters yield wine?

entries.farsi: تا هليله نشكند با ادويه / كى شود خود صحت افزا ادويه‏
entries.english: Unless flavoured oil is pounded up with medicines, how should the medicines by themselves become health-increasing?

sections.title_fa: در صفت پير و مطاوعت وى‏
sections.title_en: Concerning the qualities of the Pir and obedience to him

entries.farsi: اى ضياء الحق حسام الدين بگير / يك دو كاغذ بر فزا در وصف پير
entries.english: O Splendour of the Haqq, Husamu’ddin, take one or two sheets of paper and add in description of the Pir.

entries.farsi: گر چه جسم نازكت را زور نيست / ليك بى‏خورشيد ما را نور نيست‏
entries.english: Although your slender body has no strength, yet without the sun we have no light.

entries.farsi: گر چه مصباح و زجاجه گشته‏اى / ليك سر خيل دلى سر رشته‏اى‏
entries.english: Although you have become the lighted wick and the glass, yet you are the heart’s leader: you are the end of the thread.

entries.farsi: چون سر رشته به دست و كام تست / درهاى عقد دل ز انعام تست‏
entries.english: Inasmuch as the end of the thread is in your hand and will, the Leads on the heart’s necklace are from your bounty.

entries.farsi: بر نويس احوال پير راهدان / پير را بگزين و عين راه دان‏
entries.english: Write down what appertains to the Pir who knows the Way: Choose the Pir and regard him as the essence of the Way.

entries.farsi: پير تابستان و خلقان تير ماه / خلق مانند شب‏اند و پير ماه‏
entries.english: The Pir is summer, and people are the autumn month; people are like night, and the Pir is the moon.

entries.farsi: كرده‏ام بخت جوان را نام پير / كاو ز حق پير است نز ايام پير
entries.english: “I have bestowed on young Fortune the name of Pir, because he is old by the Truth, not old by Time.

entries.farsi: او چنان پيرى است كش آغاز نيست / با چنان در يتيم انباز نيست‏
entries.english: So old is he that he has no beginning: there is no rival to such a unique Pearl.

entries.farsi: خود قوى‏تر مى‏شود خمر كهن / خاصه آن خمرى كه باشد من لدن‏
entries.english: Verily, old wine grows more potent; verily, old gold is more highly prized.

entries.farsi: پير را بگزين كه بى‏پير اين سفر / هست بس پر آفت و خوف و خطر
entries.english: Choose a Pir, for without a Pir this journey is exceeding full of woe and affright and danger.

entries.farsi: آن رهى كه بارها تو رفته‏اى / بى‏قالووز اندر آن آشفته‏اى
entries.english: Without an escort you are bewildered on a road you have travelled many times

entries.farsi: پس رهى را كه نديده ستى تو هيچ / هين مرو تنها ز رهبر سر مپيچ‏
entries.english: Do not, then, travel alone on a Way that you have not seen at all, do not turn your head away from the Guide.

entries.farsi: گر نباشد سايه‏ى او بر تو گول / پس ترا سر گشته دارد بانگ غول‏
entries.english: Fool, if his shadow be not over you, then the cry of the ghoul will keep you with your head in a whirl.

entries.farsi: غولت از ره افكند اندر گزند / از تو داهى‏تر در اين ره بس بدند
entries.english: The ghoul will from the Way cast you into destruction: there have been in this Way many craftier than you.

entries.farsi: از نبى بشنو ضالل رهروان / كه چشان كرد آن بليس بد روان‏
entries.english: Listen to the Qur’an and the perdition of the wayfarers, what the evil-souled Iblis did unto them:

entries.farsi: صد هزاران ساله راه از جاده دور / بردشان و كردشان ادبار و عور
entries.english: He carried them far—a journey of hundreds of thousands of years— from the Highway, and made them backsliders and naked.

entries.farsi: استخوانهاشان ببين و مويشان / عبرتى گير و مران خر سويشان‏
entries.english: Behold their bones and their hair! Take warning, and drive not your ass towards them!

entries.farsi: گردن خر گير و سوى راه كش / سوى ره‏بانان و ره دانان خوش‏
entries.english: Seize the neck of your ass and lead him towards the Way, towards the good keepers and those who know the Way.

entries.farsi: هين مهل خر را و دست از وى مدار / ز آن كه عشق اوست سوى سبزه‏زار
entries.english: Beware! Do not let your ass go, and do not remove your hand from him, because his love is for the place where green herbs are plentiful.

entries.farsi: گر يكى دم تو به غفلت واهليش / او رود فرسنگ‏ها سوى حشيش‏
entries.english: If you carelessly leave him free for one moment, he will go leagues in the direction of the herbage.

entries.farsi: دشمن راه است خر مست علف / اى كه بس خر بنده را كرد او تلف‏
entries.english: The ass is an enemy to the Way, madly in love with fodder: oh, many is the attendant on him that he has brought to ruin!

entries.farsi: گر ندانى ره هر آن چه خر بخواست / عكس آن كن خود بود آن راه راست‏
entries.english: If you know not the Way, whatever the ass desires, do the opposite: that, surely, will be the right Way.

entries.farsi: با هوا و آرزو كم باش دوست / چون يضلك عن سبيل اهلل اوست‏
entries.english: “Consult them, and then oppose: he that disobeys them not will be ruined.”

entries.farsi: شاوِروهُنَّ پس آن گه خالفوا / إن من لم يعصهن تالف‏
entries.english: Be not a friend to temptation and desire, since it leads you astray from the Way of God.

entries.farsi: اين هوا را نشكند اندر جهان / هيچ چيزى همچو سايه‏ى همرهان‏
entries.english: Nothing in the world will break this passion like the shadow of fellow-travellers.

sections.title_fa: وصيت كردن رسول صلى اللهَّ عليه و اله و سلم على را عليه السلام كه چون هر كسى به نوع طاعتى تقرب جويد به حق تو تقرب جوى به نصيحت عاقل و بنده‏ى خاص تا از همه پيش قدم تر باشى
sections.title_en: How the Prophet, on whom be peace, enjoined ‘Ali—may God make his person honoured saying, “When every one seeks to draw nigh to God by means of some kind of devotional act,
do you seek the favour of God by associating with His wise and chosen servant, that you may be the first of all to arrive.”

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر على را كاى على / شير حقى پهلوانى پر دلى‏
entries.english: The Prophet said to ‘Ali, “O ‘Ali, you are the Lion of God, you are a courageous knight,

entries.farsi: ليك بر شيرى مكن هم اعتماد / اندر آ در سايه‏ى آن عاقلى
entries.english: But do not even rely upon your lion-heartedness: come into the shade of the palm-tree of hope.

entries.farsi: كش نداند برد از ره ناقلى‏ / ظل او اندر زمين چون كوه قاف
entries.english: Come into the shade of the Sage whom no conveyer can carry off from the Way.

entries.farsi: روح او سيمرغ بس عالى طواف‏ / گر بگويم تا قيامت نعت او
entries.english: His shadow on the earth is like Mount Qaf; his spirit is the Simurgh that circles exceedingly high.

entries.farsi: هيچ آن را مقطع و غايت مجو / در بشر رو پوش كرده ست آفتاب
entries.english: If I should tell of his qualities until the Resurrection, do not seek any conclusion and end to them.

entries.farsi: فهم كن و اهلل اعلم بالصواب‏ / يا على از جمله‏ى طاعات راه
entries.english: The Sun has veiled Himself in Man: apprehend, and God knows best what is right.

entries.farsi: بر گزين تو سايه‏ى خاص اله‏ / هر كسى در طاعتى بگريختند
entries.english: O ‘Ali, above all devotional acts in the Way choose the shadow of the servant of God.

entries.farsi: خويشتن را مخلصى انگيختند / تر برو در سايه‏ى عاقل گريز
entries.english: Every one took refuge in some act of devotion and discovered for themselves some means of deliverance.

entries.farsi: تا رهى ز آن دشمن پنهان ستيز / از همه طاعات اينت بهتر است
entries.english: Take refuge in the shadow of the Sage that you may escape from the Enemy that opposes in secret.

entries.farsi: سبق يابى بر هر آن سابق كه هست‏ / چون گرفتت پير هين تسليم شو
entries.english: Of all acts of devotion this is the best for you: you will gain precedence over every one that has outstripped.”

entries.farsi: همچو موسى زير حكم خضر رو / صبر كن بر كار خضرى بى‏نفاق
entries.english: When the Pir has accepted you, take heed, surrender thyself: go, like Moses, under the authority of Khizr.

entries.farsi: تا نگويد خضر رو هذا فراق‏ / گر چه كشتى بشكند تو دم مزن
entries.english: Bear patiently whatever is done by a Khizr, who is without hypocrisy, in order that Khizr may not say, “Begone, this is parting.”

entries.farsi: گر چه طفلى را كشد تو مو مكن‏ / دست او را حق چو دست خويش خواند
entries.english: Though he holed the boat, do not speak a word; though he kills a child, do not tear your hair.

entries.farsi: تا يَدُ اللهَِّ فَوْقَ أَيْدِيهِمْ براند / دست حق ميراندش زنده‏ش كند
entries.english: God has declared that his hand is as His own, since He gave out the Hand of God is above their hands.

entries.farsi: زنده چه بود جان پاينده‏ش كند / هر كه تنها نادرا اين ره بريد
entries.english: The Hand of God causes him to die and brings him to life. What of life? He makes him a spirit everlasting.

entries.farsi: هم به عون همت پيران رسيد / دست پير از غايبان كوتاه نيست
entries.english: If any one, by rare exception, traversed this Way alone, he arrived through the help of the hearts of the Pirs.

entries.farsi: دست او جز قبضه‏ى اهلل نيست‏ / غايبان را چون چنين خلعت دهند
entries.english: The hand of the Pir is not withdrawn from the absent: his hand is naught but the grasp of God.

entries.farsi: حاضران از غايبان ال شك بهند / غايبان را چون نواله مى‏دهند
entries.english: Inasmuch as they give such a robe of honour to the absent, undoubtedly the present are better than the absent.

entries.farsi: پيش مهمان تا چه نعمتها نهند / كو كسى كه پيش شه بندد كمر
entries.english: Since they are bestowing food on the absent, see what bounties they must lay before one who is present.

entries.farsi: تا كسى كه هست بيرون سوى در / چون گزيدى پير نازك دل مباش
entries.english: Where is one that girds himself before them to one that is outside the door?

entries.farsi: سست و ريزيده چو آب و گل مباش‏ / گر بهر زخمى تو پر كينه شوى
entries.english: When you have chosen your Pir, be not faint-hearted; be not weak as water and crumbly as earth.

entries.farsi: پس كجا بى‏صيقل آيينه شوى‏
entries.english: If you are enraged by every blow, then how will you become a mirror without being polished?

sections.title_fa: كبودى زدن قزوينى بر شانگاه صورت شير و پشيمان شدن او به سبب زخم سوزن
sections.title_en: How the man of Qazwin was tattooing the figure of a lion in blue on his shoulders, and repenting because of the needle-pricks.

entries.farsi: اين حكايت بشنو از صاحب بيان / در طريق و عادت قزوينيان‏
entries.english: Hear from the narrator this story about the way and custom of the people of Qazwin.

entries.farsi: بر تن و دست و كتفها بى‏گزند / از سر سوزن كبوديها زنند
entries.english: They tattoo themselves in blue with the point of a needle on body and hand and shoulders, so as to suffer no injury.

entries.farsi: سوى دالكى بشد قزوينيى / كه كبودم زن بكن شيرينيى‏
entries.english: A certain man of Qazwin went to a barber and said, “Tattoo me; do it charmingly.”

entries.farsi: گفت چه صورت زنم اى پهلوان / گفت بر زن صورت شير ژيان‏
entries.english: “O valiant sir,” said he, “what figure shall I tattoo?” He answered, “Prick in the figure of a furious lion.

entries.farsi: طالعم شير است نقش شير زن / جهد كن رنگ كبودى سير زن‏
entries.english: Leo is my ascendant: tattoo the form of a lion. Exert yourself, prick in plenty of the blue dye.”

entries.farsi: گفت بر چه موضعت صورت زنم / گفت بر شانه‏گهم زن آن رقم‏
entries.english: “On what place,” he asked, “shall I tattoo you?” Said he, “Prick the design of the beauty on my shoulder-blade.”

entries.farsi: چون كه او سوزن فرو بردن گرفت / درد آن در شانگه مسكن گرفت‏
entries.english: As soon as he began to stick in the needle, the pain of it settled in the shoulder, And the hero fell a-moaning— “O illustrious one, you have killed me: what figure are you tattooing?”

entries.farsi: پهلوان در ناله آمد كاى سنى / مر مرا كشتى چه صورت مى‏زنى‏
entries.english: “Why,” said he, “ you bade me do a lion.” “What limb,” asked the other, “did you begin with?”

entries.farsi: گفت آخر شير فرمودى مرا / گفت از چه عضو كردى ابتدا
entries.english: “I have begun at the tail,” said he. “O my dear friend,” he cried, “leave out the tail!

entries.farsi: گفت از دمگاه آغازيده‏ام / گفت دم بگذار اى دو ديده‏ام‏
entries.english: My breath is stopped by the lion’s tail and rump: his rump has tightly closed my windpipe.

entries.farsi: از دم و دمگاه شيرم دم گرفت / دمگه او دمگهم محكم گرفت‏
entries.english: Let the lion be without a tail, O lion-maker, for my heart is faint from the blows of the needle.”

entries.farsi: شير بى‏دم باش گو اى شير ساز / كه دلم سستى گرفت از زخم گاز
entries.english: That person commenced to prick in on another part without fear, without favour, without mercy.

entries.farsi: بانگ كرد او كاين چه اندام است از او / گفت اين گوش است اى مرد نكو
entries.english: He yelled—”Which of his members is this?” “This is his ear, my good man,” the barber replied.

entries.farsi: گفت تا گوشش نباشد اى حكيم / گوش را بگذار و كوته كن گليم‏
entries.english: “O Doctor,” said he, “let him have no ears: omit the ears and cut the frock short.”

entries.farsi: جانب ديگر خلش آغاز كرد / باز قزوينى فغان را ساز كرد
entries.english: The barber began to insert in another part: once more the man of Qazwin set out to wail,

entries.farsi: كاين سوم جانب چه اندام است نيز / گفت اين است اشكم شير اى عزيز
entries.english: Saying, “What is the member now on this third spot?” He replied, “This is the lion’s belly, my dear sir.”

entries.farsi: گفت تا اشكم نباشد شير را / چه شكم بايد نگار سير را
entries.english: “Let the lion have no belly,” said he: “what need of a belly for the picture that is full?”

entries.farsi: خيره شد دالك و بس حيران بماند / تا به دير انگشت در دندان بماند
entries.english: The barber became distraught and remained in great bewilderment: he stood for a long time with his fingers in his teeth;

entries.farsi: بر زمين زد سوزن از خشم اوستاد / گفت در عالم كسى را اين فتاد
entries.english: Then the master flung the needle to the ground and said, “Has this happened to any one in the world?

entries.farsi: شير بى‏دم و سر و اشكم كه ديد / اين چنين شيرى خدا خود نافريد
entries.english: Who saw a lion without tail and head and belly? God himself did not create a lion like this.”

entries.farsi: اى برادر صبر كن بر درد نيش / تا رهى از نيش نفس گبر خويش‏
entries.english: O brother, endure the pain of the lancet that you may escape from the poison of your nafs.

entries.farsi: كان گروهى كه رهيدند از وجود / چرخ و مهر و ماهشان آرد سجود
entries.english: For sky and sun and moon bow in worship to the people who have escaped from self-existence.

entries.farsi: هر كه مرد اندر تن او نفس گبر / مر و را فرمان برد خورشيد و ابر
entries.english: Any one in whose body the miscreant self has died, sun and cloud obey his command.

entries.farsi: چون دلش آموخت شمع افروختن / آفتاب او را نيارد سوختن‏
entries.english: Since his heart has learned to light the candle, the sun cannot burn him.

entries.farsi: خار جمله لطف چون گل مى‏شود / پيش جزوى كاو سوى كل مى‏رود
entries.english: God has made mention of the rising sun as turning aside— like that—from their cave.

entries.farsi: گفت حق در آفتاب منتجم / ذكر تزاور كذا عن كهفهم‏
entries.english: The thorn becomes entirely beautiful, like the rose, in the sight of the particular that is going towards the Universal.

entries.farsi: چيست تعظيم خدا افراشتن / خويشتن را خوار و خاكى داشتن‏
entries.english: What is to exalt and glorify God? To deem yourself despicable and as dust

entries.farsi: چيست توحيد خدا آموختن / خويشتن را پيش واحد سوختن‏
entries.english: What is to learn the knowledge of God’s unity? To consume yourself in the presence of the One

entries.farsi: گر همى‏خواهى كه بفروزى چو روز / هستى همچون شب خود را بسوز
entries.english: If you wish to shine like day, burn up your night-like self-existence.

entries.farsi: هستى‏ات در هست آن هستى نواز / همچو مس در كيميا اندر گداز
entries.english: Melt away your existence, as copper in the elixir, in the being of Him who fosters existence.

entries.farsi: در من و ما سخت كرده ستى دو دست / شير و گرگ و روبهى بهر شكار
entries.english: You have fastened both your hands tight on “I” and “we”: all this ruin is caused by dualism.

sections.title_fa: رفتن گرگ و روباه در خدمت شير به شكار
sections.title_en: How the wolf and fox went to hunt in attendance on the lion.

entries.farsi: رفته بودند از طلب در كوهسار / تا به پشت همدگر بر صيدها
entries.english: A lion, wolf, and fox had gone to hunt in the mountains in quest.

entries.farsi: سخت بر بندند بار قيدها / هر سه با هم اندر آن صحراى ژرف
entries.english: That by supporting each other they might tie fast the bonds and fet­ ters on the hunted animals,

entries.farsi: صيدها گيرند بسيار و شگرف‏ / گر چه ز يشان شير نر را ننگ بود
entries.english: And all three together might seize much and great quarry in that deep wilderness.

entries.farsi: ليك كرد اكرام و همراهى نمود / اين چنين شه را ز لشكر زحمت است
entries.english: Although the fierce lion was ashamed of them, yet he did them hon­ our and gave them his company on the way.

entries.farsi: ليك همره شد جماعت رحمت است‏ / اين چنين مه را ز اختر ننگهاست
entries.english: To a king like this the soldiers are an annoyance, but he accompanied them: a united party is a mercy.

entries.farsi: او ميان اختران بهر سخاست / امر شاوِرْهُمْ پيمبر را رسيد
entries.english: A moon like this is disgraced by the stars: it is amongst the stars for generosity’s sake.

entries.farsi: گر چه رايى نيست رايش را نديد / در ترازو جو رفيق زر شده ست
entries.english: The command, Consult them, came to the Prophet, though no counsel is to be compared with his own.

entries.farsi: نى از آن كه جو چو زر گوهر شده ست‏ / روح قالب را كنون همره شده ست
entries.english: In the scales barley has become the companion of gold, not because barley has become a substance like gold.

entries.farsi: مدتى سگ حارس درگه شده ست‏ / چون كه رفتند اين جماعت سوى كوه
entries.english: The spirit has now become the body’s fellow-traveller: the dog has become for a time the guardian of the palace-gate.

entries.farsi: در ركاب شير با فر و شكوه‏ / گاو كوهى و بز و خرگوش زفت
entries.english: When this party went to the mountains at the stirrup of the lion majestic and grand,

entries.farsi: يافتند و كار ايشان پيش رفت‏ / هر كه باشد در پى شير حراب
entries.english: They found a mountain-ox and goat and fat hare, and their business went forward.

entries.farsi: كم نيايد روز و شب او را كباب‏ / چون ز كه در بيشه آوردندشان
entries.english: Whoever is on the heels of him that is a lion in combat, roast-meat does not fail him by day or by night.

entries.farsi: كشته و مجروح و اندر خون كشان‏ / گرگ و روبه را طمع بود اندر آن
entries.english: When they brought them from the mountains to the jungle, killed and wounded and dragging along in blood,

entries.farsi: كه رود قسمت به عدل خسروان‏ / عكس طمع هر دوشان بر شير زد
entries.english: The wolf and fox hoped that a division would he made according to the justice of emperors.

entries.farsi: شير دانست آن طمعها را سند / هر كه باشد شير اسرار و امير
entries.english: The reflection of the hope of both of them struck the lion: the lion knew the ground for those hopes.

entries.farsi: او بداند هر چه انديشد ضمير / هين نگه دار اى دل انديشه جو
entries.english: Any one that is the lion and prince of mysteries, he will know all that the conscience thinks.

entries.farsi: دل ز انديشه‏ى بدى در پيش او / داند و خر را همى‏راند خموش
entries.english: Beware! Guard yourself, O heart disposed to thinking, from any evil thought in his presence.

entries.farsi: در رخت خندد براى روى‏پوش‏ / شير چون دانست آن وسواسشان
entries.english: He knows and keeps riding on silently: he smiles in your face in order to mask.

entries.farsi: مر شما را اى خسيسان گدا / مر شما را بس نيامد راى من
entries.english: When the lion perceived their bad ideas, he did not declare, and paid regard at the time,

entries.farsi: وانگفت و داشت آن دم پاسشان‏ / ليك با خود گفت بنمايم سزا
entries.english: But he said to himself, “I will show you what you deserve, O beggarly villains!

entries.farsi: ظنتان اين است در اعطاى من‏ / اى عقول و رايتان از راى من
entries.english: Was my judgment not enough for you? Is this your opinion of my bounty?

entries.farsi: از عطاهاى جهان آراى من‏ / نقش با نقاش چه سگالد دگر
entries.english: O you whose understanding and judgment are from my judgment and from my world-adorning gifts?

entries.farsi: چون سگالش اوش بخشيد و خبر / اين چنين ظن خسيسانه به من
entries.english: What else should the picture think of the painter, since he bestowed thought and knowledge upon it?

entries.farsi: تا بماند بر جهان اين داستان‏ / شير با اين فكر مى‏زد خنده فاش
entries.english: Had you such a vile opinion of me, O you who are a scandal to the world?

entries.farsi: مر شما را بود ننگان زمن‏ / ظانين باهلل ظن السوء را
entries.english: I will strike off the hypocritical heads of them that think ill of God.

entries.farsi: گر نبرم سر بود عين خطا / وارهانم چرخ را از ننگتان
entries.english: I will deliver the Sphere from your disgrace, so that this tale shall remain in the world.”

entries.farsi: بر تبسمهاى شير ايمن مباش‏ / مال دنيا شد تبسمهاى حق
entries.english: While thus meditating, the lion continued to smile visibly: do not trust the smiles of the lion!

entries.farsi: كرد ما را مست و مغرور و خلق‏ / فقر و رنجورى به استت اى سند
entries.english: Worldly wealth is the smiles of God: it has made us drunken and vainglorious and threadbare.

entries.farsi: كان تبسم دام خود را بر كند
entries.english: Poverty and distress are better for you, O lord, for that smile will remove its lure.

sections.title_fa: امتحان كردن شير گرگ را و گفتن كه پيش آى اى گرگ بخش كن صيدها را ميان ما
sections.title_en: How the lion made trial of the wolf and said, “Come forward, O wolf, and divide the prey amongst us.”

entries.farsi: گفت شير اى گرگ اين را بخش كن / معدلت را نو كن اى گرگ كهن‏
entries.english: The lion said, “ O wolf, divide this: O old wolf, make justice new.

entries.farsi: نايب من باش در قسمت‏گرى / تا پديد آيد كه تو چه گوهرى‏
entries.english: Be my deputy in the office of distributor, that it may be seen of what substance you are.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى شه گاو وحشى بخش تست / آن بزرگ و تو بزرگ و زفت و چست‏
entries.english: “O King,” said he, “the wild ox is your share: he is big, and you are big and strong and active.

entries.farsi: بز مرا كه بز ميانه ست و وسط / روبها خرگوش بستان بى‏غلط
entries.english: The goat is mine, for the goat is middle and intermediate; do you, O fox, receive the hare, and no mistake!”

entries.farsi: شير گفت اى گرگ چون گفتى بگو / چون كه من باشم تو گويى ما و تو
entries.english: The lion said, “O wolf, how have you spoken? Say! When I am here, do you speak of ‘I’ and ‘you’?

entries.farsi: گرگ خود چه سگ بود كاو خويش ديد / پيش چون من شير بى‏مثل و نديد
entries.english: Truly, what a cur the wolf must be, that he regarded himself in the presence of a lion like me who am peerless and unrivalled! “

entries.farsi: گفت پيش آ اى خرى كاو خود بديد / پيشش آمد پنجه زد او را دريد
entries.english: He said, “Come forward, O you self-esteeming ass!” He approached him; the lion seized him with his claws and rent him.

entries.farsi: چون نديدش مغز و تدبير رشيد / در سياست پوستش از سر كشيد
entries.english: Inasmuch as he did not see in him the kernel of right conduct, he tore the skin off his head as a punishment.

entries.farsi: گفت چون ديد منت از خود نبرد / اين چنين جان را ببايد زار مرد
entries.english: He said, “Since the sight of me did not transport you out of yourself, a spirit like this must die miserably.

entries.farsi: چون نبودى فانى اندر پيش من / فضل آمد مر ترا گردن زدن‏
entries.english: Since you were not passing away in my presence, It was an act of grace to smite your neck.”

entries.farsi: كل شى‏ء هالك جز وجه او / چون نه‏اى در وجه او هستى مجو
entries.english: Everything is perishing except His face: unless you art in His face, do not seek to exist.

entries.farsi: هر كه اندر وجه ما باشد فنا / كُلُّ شَيْ‏ءٍ هالِكٌ نبود جزا
entries.english: When any one has passed away in my face, everything is perishing are not applicable,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه در الاست او از ال گذشت / هر كه در الاست او فانى نگشت
entries.english: Because he is in except, he has transcended not: whosoever is in except has not passed away.

entries.farsi: هر كه بر در او من و ما مى‏زند / رد باب است او و بر ال مى‏تند
entries.english: Whoever is uttering ‘I’ and ‘we’ at the door, he is turned back from the door and is continuing in not.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى آن كس كه در يارى بكوفت از درون گفت كيست گفت منم، گفت چون تو تويى در نمى‏گشايم هيچ كس را از ياران نمى‏شناسم كه او من باشد
sections.title_en: The story of the person who knocked at a friend’s door: his friend from within asked who he was: he said, “It is I,” and the friend answered, “Since you art you, I will not open the door:
I know not any friend that is ‘ I.’”

entries.farsi: آن يكى آمد در يارى بزد / گفت يارش كيستى اى معتمد
entries.english: A certain man came and knocked at a friend’s door: his friend asked him, “Who are you, O trusty one?”

entries.farsi: گفت من، گفتش برو هنگام نيست / بر چنين خوانى مقام خام نيست
entries.english: He answered, “I.” The friend said, “Go, It is not the time: at a table like this there is no place for the raw.”

entries.farsi: خام را جز آتش هجر و فراق / كى پزد كى وا رهاند از نفاق‏
entries.english: Save the fire of absence and separation, who will cook the raw one? Who will deliver him from hypocrisy?

entries.farsi: رفت آن مسكين و سالى در سفر / در فراق دوست سوزيد از شرر
entries.english: The wretched man went away, and for a year in travel in separation from his friend he was burned with sparks of fire.

entries.farsi: پخته گشت آن سوخته پس باز گشت / باز گرد خانه‏ى همباز گشت‏
entries.english: That burned one was cooked: then he returned and again paced to and fro beside the house of his comrade.

entries.farsi: حلقه زد بر در به صد ترس و ادب / تا بنجهد بى‏ادب لفظى ز لب‏
entries.english: He knocked at the door with a hundred fears and respects, lest any disrespectful word might escape from his lips.

entries.farsi: بانگ زد يارش كه بر در كيست آن / گفت بر درهم تويى اى دلستان‏
entries.english: His friend called to him, “Who is at the door?” He answered, “It is you are at the door, O charmer of hearts.”

entries.farsi: گفت اكنون چون منى اى من در آ / نيست گنجايى دو من را در سرا
entries.english: “Now,” said the friend, “since you are I, come in, O myself: there is not room in the house for two I’s.

entries.farsi: نيست سوزن را سر رشته دو تا / چون كه يكتايى درين سوزن در آ
entries.english: The double end of thread is not for the needle: inasmuch as you are single, come into this needle.”

entries.farsi: رشته را با سوزن آمد ارتباط / نيست در خور با جمل سم الخياط
entries.english: It is the thread that is connected with the needle: the eye of the needle is not suitable for the camel.

entries.farsi: كى شود باريك هستى جمل / جز به مقراض رياضات و عمل‏
entries.english: How should the existence of the camel be fined down save by the shears of ascetic exercises and works?

entries.farsi: دست حق بايد مر آن را اى فالن / كاو بود بر هر محالى كن فكان‏
entries.english: For that, O reader, the hand of God is necessary, for it is the Be, and it was of every impossible thing.

entries.farsi: هر محال از دست او ممكن شود / هر حرون از بيم او ساكن شود
entries.english: By His hand every impossible thing is made possible; by fear of Him every unruly one is made quiet.

entries.farsi: اكمه و ابرص چه باشد مرده نيز / زنده گردد از فسون آن عزيز
entries.english: What of the man blind from birth and the leper? Even the dead is made living by the spell of the Almighty,

entries.farsi: و آن عدم كز مرده مرده‏تر بود / در كف ايجاد او مضطر بود
entries.english: And that non-existence which is deader than the dead— non-existence is compelled when He calls it into being.

entries.farsi: كمترين كاريش هر روز است آن / كاو سه لشكر را كند اين سو روان
entries.english: Recite, Every day He is in some affair: do not deem Him idle and inactive.

entries.farsi: ُلَّ يَوْمٍ هُوَ فِي شَأْنٍ بخوان / مر و را بى‏كار و بى‏فعلى مدان‏
entries.english: His least act, every day, is that He dispatches three armies:

entries.farsi: لشكرى ز اصالب سوى امهات / بهر آن تا در رحم رويد نبات‏
entries.english: One army from the loins towards the mothers, in order that the plant may grow in the womb;

entries.farsi: لشكرى ز ارحام سوى خاكدان / تا ز نر و ماده پر گردد جهان‏
entries.english: One army from the wombs to the Earth, that the world may be filled with male and female;

entries.farsi: لشكرى از خاك ز آن سوى اجل / تا ببيند هر كسى حسن عمل‏
entries.english: One army from the Earth beyond death, that every one may behold the beauty of works.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد هين بتاز / سوى آن دو يار پاك پاك باز
entries.english: This discourse has no end. Come, hurry back to those two sincere and devoted friends.

sections.title_fa: صفت توحيد
sections.title_en: Description of Unification

entries.farsi: گفت يارش كاندر آ اى جمله من / نى مخالف چون گل و خار چمن‏
entries.english: His friend said to him, “Come in, O you who are entirely myself, not different like the rose and thorn in the garden.”

entries.farsi: رشته يكتا شد غلط كم شد كنون / گر دو تا بينى حروف كاف و نون‏
entries.english: The thread has become single. Do not now fall into error if you see that the letters K and N are two.’

entries.farsi: كاف و نون همچون كمند آمد جذوب / تا كشاند مر عدم را در خطوب‏
entries.english: K and N are pulling like a noose, that they may draw nonexistence into great affairs.

entries.farsi: پس دو تا بايد كمند اندر صور / گر چه يكتا باشد آن دو در اثر
entries.english: Hence the noose must be double in forms, though those two are single in effect.

entries.farsi: گر دو پا گر چار پا ره را برد / همچو مقراض دو تا يكتا برد
entries.english: Whether the feet are two or four, they traverse one road, like the double shears makes one cut.

entries.farsi: آن دو همبازان گازر را ببين / هست در ظاهر خالفى ز آن و ز اين‏
entries.english: Look at those two fellow-washer men: there is apparently a difference between that one and this :

entries.farsi: آن يكى كرباس را در آب زد / و آن دگر همباز خشكش مى‏كند
entries.english: The one has thrown the cotton garments into the water, while the other partner is drying them.

entries.farsi: باز او آن خشك را تر مى‏كند / گوييا ز استيزه ضد بر مى‏تند
entries.english: Again the former makes the dry clothes wet: It is as though he were spitefully thwarting his opposite;

entries.farsi: ليك اين دو ضد استيزه نما / يكدل و يك كار باشد در رضا
entries.english: Yet these two opposites, who seem to be at strife, are of one mind and acting together in agreement.

entries.farsi: هر نبى و هر ولى را مسلكى است / ليك تا حق مى‏برد جمله يكى است‏
entries.english: Every prophet and every saint has a way, but it leads to God: all are one.

entries.farsi: رفتن اين آب فوق آسياست / رفتنش در آسيا بهر شماست‏
entries.english: When slumber overtook the concentration of the listener, the water carried the millstones away.

entries.farsi: چون كه جمع مستمع را خواب برد / سنگهاى آسيا را آب برد
entries.english: The course of this water is above the mill: its going into the mill is for your sakes.

entries.farsi: چون شما را حاجت طاحون نماند / آب را در جوى اصلى باز راند
entries.english: Since you had no further need of the mill, he made the water flow back into the original stream.

entries.farsi: ناطقه سوى دهان تعليم راست / ور نه خود آن نطق را جويى جداست‏
entries.english: The rational spirit is to the mouth for the purpose of teaching: else truly that speech has a channel apart:

entries.farsi: مى‏رود بى‏بانگ و بى‏تكرارها / تَحْ تَهَا الأَْنْهارُ تا گلزارها
entries.english: It is moving without noise and without repetitions to the rose-gardens beneath which are the rivers.

entries.farsi: اى خدا جان را تو بنما آن مقام / كاندر او بى‏حرف مى‏رويد كالم‏
entries.english: O God, reveal to the soul that place where speech is growing without letters,

entries.farsi: تا كه سازد جان پاك از سر قدم / سوى عرصه‏ى دور پهناى عدم‏
entries.english: That the pure soul may make of its head a foot towards the far-stretching expanse of non-existence—

entries.farsi: عرصه‏اى بس با گشاد و با فضا / وين خيال و هست يابد زو نوا
entries.english: An expanse very ample and spacious; and from it this phantasy and being is fed.

entries.farsi: تنگتر آمد خيالات از عدم / ز آن سبب باشد خيال اسباب غم‏
entries.english: Imagination is narrower than non-existence: on that account phantasy is the cause of pain.

entries.farsi: باز هستى تنگتر بود از خيال / ز آن شود در وى قمر همچون هالل‏
entries.english: Existence, again, was narrower than Imagination: hence in it moons become like the moon that has waned.

entries.farsi: باز هستى جهان حس و رنگ / تنگتر آمد كه زندانى است تنگ‏
entries.english: Again, the existence of the world of sense and colour is narrower, for It is a narrow prison.

entries.farsi: علت تنگى است تركيب و عدد / جانب تركيب حسها مى‏كشد
entries.english: The cause of narrowness is composition and number: the senses are moving towards composition.

entries.farsi: ز آن سوى حس عالم توحيد دان / گر يكى خواهى بدان جانب بران‏
entries.english: Know that the world of Unification lies beyond sense: if you want Unity, march in that direction.

entries.farsi: امر كن يك فعل بود و نون و كاف / در سخن افتاد و معنى بود صاف‏
entries.english: The Command KuN was a single act, and the N and K occurred in speech, while the meaning was pure.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد باز گرد / تا چه شد احوال گرگ اندر نبرد
entries.english: This discourse has no end. Return, that what happened to the wolf in combat.

sections.title_fa: ادب كردن شير گرگ را كه در قسمت بى‏ادبى كرده بود
sections.title_en: How the lion punished the wolf that had shown disrespect in dividing.

entries.farsi: گرگ را بر كند سر آن سر فراز / تا نماند دو سرى‏و امتياز
entries.english: That proud one tore off the head of the wolf, in order that two-headedness and distinction might not remain.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن رو شير با روباه كرد / گفت اين را بخش كن از بهر خورد
entries.english: It is So we took vengeance on them, O old wolf, inasmuch as you were not dead in the presence of the Amir.

entries.farsi: فَانْتَقَمْنا مِنْهُمْ است اى گرگ پير / چون نبودى مرده در پيش امير
entries.english: After that, the lion turned to the fox and said, “Divide it for breakfast.”

entries.farsi: سجده كرد و گفت كاين گاو سمين / چاشت خوردت باشد اى شاه گزين‏
entries.english: He bowed low and said, “This fat ox will be your food at breakfast, O excellent King,

entries.farsi: و آن بز از بهر ميان روز را / يخنيى باشد شه پيروز را
entries.english: And this goat will be a portion reserved for the victorious King at midday,

entries.farsi: و آن دگر خرگوش بهر شام هم / شب چره‏ى اين شاه با لطف و كرم‏
entries.english: And the hare too for supper— the meal at nightfall of the gracious and bountiful King.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى روبه تو عدل افروختى / اين چنين قسمت ز كى آموختى‏
entries.english: Said the lion, “O fox, you have made justice shine forth: from whom did you learn to divide in such a manner?

entries.farsi: از كجا آموختى اين اى بزرگ / گفت اى شاه جهان از حال گرگ‏
entries.english: When did you learn this, O eminent one?” “O King of the world,” he replied,” from the fate of the wolf.”

entries.farsi: گفت چون در عشق ما گشتى گرو / هر سه را برگير و بستان و برو
entries.english: The lion said, “Inasmuch as you have become pledged to love of me, pick up all the three, and take and depart.

entries.farsi: روبها چون جملگى ما را شدى / چونت آزاريم چون تو ما شدى‏
entries.english: O fox, since you have become entirely mine, how should I hurt you when you have become I?

entries.farsi: ما ترا و جمله اشكاران ترا / پاى بر گردون هفتم نه بر آ
entries.english: I am yours, and all the beasts of chase are yours: set your foot on the Seventh Heaven and mount!

entries.farsi: چون گرفتى عبرت از گرگ دنى / پس تو روبه نيستى شير منى‏
entries.english: Since you have taken warning from the vile wolf, you are not a fox: you are my own lion.

entries.farsi: عاقل آن باشد كه عبرت گيرد از / مرگ ياران در بالى محترز
entries.english: The wise man is he that in the shunned tribulation takes warning from the death of his friends.”

entries.farsi: روبه آن دم بر زبان صد شكر راند / كه مرا شير از پى آن گرگ خواند
entries.english: The fox said, “A hundred thanks to the lion for having called me up after that wolf.

entries.farsi: گر مرا اول بفرمودى كه تو / بخش كن اين را كه بردى جان از او
entries.english: If he had bidden me first, saying, ‘Do you divide this,’ who would have escaped from him with his life?”

entries.farsi: پس سپاس او را كه ما را در جهان / كرد پيدا از پس پيشينيان‏
entries.english: Thanks be to Him, then, that He caused us to appear in the world after those of old,

entries.farsi: تا شنيديم آن سياستهاى حق / بر قرون ماضيه اندر سبق‏
entries.english: So that we heard of the chastisements which God inflicted upon the past generations in the preceding time,

entries.farsi: تا كه ما از حال آن گرگان پيش / همچو روبه پاس خود داريم بيش‏
entries.english: That we, like the fox, may keep better watch over ourselves from the fate of those ancient wolves.

entries.farsi: امت مرحومه زين رو خواندمان / آن رسول حق و صادق در بيان‏
entries.english: On this account he that is God’s prophet and veracious in explanation called us “a people on which God has taken mercy.”

entries.farsi: استخوان و پشم آن گرگان عيان / بنگريد و پند گيريد اى مهان‏
entries.english: Behold with clear vision the bones and fur of those wolves, and take warning, O mighty ones!

entries.farsi: عاقل از سر بنهد اين هستى و باد / چون شنيد انجام فرعونان و عاد
entries.english: The wise man will put off from his head this self-existence and wind, since he heard the end of the Pharaohs and ‘Ad;

entries.farsi: ور بننهد ديگران از حال او / عبرتى گيرند از اضالل او
entries.english: And if he does not put it off, others will take warning from what befell him in consequence of his being misguided.

sections.title_fa: تهديد كردن نوح عليه السلام مر قوم را كه با من مپيچيد كه من رو پوشم در ميان پس به حقيقت با ‏خداى مى‏پيچيد اى مخذوالن
sections.title_en: How Noah, on whom be peace, threatened his people, saying,
“Do not struggle with me, for I am a veil: you are really struggling with God within this, O God-forsaken men!”

entries.farsi: گفت نوح اى سركشان من من نى‏ام / من ز جان مرده به جانان مى‏زى‏ام‏
entries.english: Noah said, “O you headstrong ones, I am not I: I am dead to the soul, I am living through the Soul of souls.

entries.farsi: چون بمردم از حواس بو البشر / حق مرا شد سمع و ادراك و بصر
entries.english: Inasmuch as I am dead to the senses of the father of mankind, God has become my hearing and perception and sight.

entries.farsi: چون كه من من نيستم اين دم ز هوست / پيش اين دم هر كه دم زد كافر اوست‏
entries.english: Since I am not I, this breath is from Him: in the presence of this breath if any one breathes he is an infidel.”

entries.farsi: هست اندر نقش اين روباه شير / سوى اين روبه نشايد شد دلير
entries.english: In the form of this fox there is the lion: it is not fitting to advance boldly towards this fox.

entries.farsi: گر ز روى صورتش مى‏نگروى / غره‏ى شيران از او مى‏نشنوى
entries.english: Unless you believe in him from his exterior aspect, you will not hear from him the lions’ roar.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى نوح را از حق يدى / پس جهانى را چرا بر هم زدى‏
entries.english: If Noah had not been the Eternal Lion, why should he have cast a whole world into confusion?

entries.farsi: صد هزاران شير بود او در تنى / او چو آتش بود و عالم خرمنى‏
entries.english: He was hundreds of thousands of lions in a single body; he was like fire, and the world a stack.

entries.farsi: چون كه خرمن پاس عشر او نداشت / او چنان شعله بر آن خرمن گماشت‏
entries.english: Forasmuch as the stack neglected the tithe due to him, he launched such a flame against that stack.

entries.farsi: هر كه او در پيش اين شير نهان / بى‏ادب چون گرگ بگشايد دهان‏
entries.english: Whoever in the presence of this hidden Lion opens his mouth disrespectfully, like the wolf,

entries.farsi: همچو گرگ آن شير بردراندش / فَانْتَقَمْنا مِنْهُمْ بر خواندش‏
entries.english: That Lion will tear him to pieces, as the wolf, and will recite to him So we took vengeance upon them.

entries.farsi: زخم يابد همچو گرگ از دست شير / پيش شير ابله بود كاو شد دلير
entries.english: He will suffer blows, like the wolf, from the Lion’s paw: foolish is he that waxed bold in the presence of the Lion.

entries.farsi: كاشكى آن زخم بر تن آمدى / تا بدى كايمان و دل سالم بدى‏
entries.english: Would that those blows fell upon the body, so that it might be that faith and heart would be safe!

entries.farsi: قوتم بگسست چون اينجا رسيد / چون توانم كرد اين سر را پديد
entries.english: My power is broken on reaching this point: how can I declare this mystery?

entries.farsi: همچو آن روبه كم اشكم كنيد / پيش او روباه بازى كم كنيد
entries.english: Make little of your bellies, like that fox: do not play fox’s tricks in His presence.

entries.farsi: جمله ما و من به پيش او نهيد / ملك ملك اوست ملك او را دهيد
entries.english: Lay the whole of your “we” and “I” before Him: the kingdom is His kingdom: give the kingdom to Him.

entries.farsi: چون فقير آييد اندر راه راست / شير و صيد شير خود آن شماست‏
entries.english: When you become poor in the right Way, truly the Lion and the Lion’s prey are yours,

entries.farsi: ز آنكه او پاك است و سبحان وصف اوست / بى‏نياز است او ز نغز و مغز و پوست‏
entries.english: Because He is holy, and Glory is His attribute: He has no need of good things and kernel or rind.

entries.farsi: نيست شه را طمع بهر خلق ساخت / اين همه دولت خنك آن كاو شناخت‏
entries.english: Every prize and every gift of grace that exists is for the sake of the servants of that King.

entries.farsi: هر شكار و هر كراماتى كه هست / از براى بندگان آن شه است‏
entries.english: The King has no desire: He has made all this empire for His creatures. Happy is he that knew!

entries.farsi: آن كه دولت آفريد و دو سرا / ملك دولتها چه كار آيد و را
entries.english: Of what use should the possession of empires be to Him who created empire and the two worlds?

entries.farsi: پيش سبحان بس نگه داريد دل / تا نگرديد از گمان بد خجل‏
entries.english: In the presence of His Glory keep close watch over your hearts, lest you be put to shame by thinking evil.

entries.farsi: كاو ببيند سر و فكر و جستجو / همچو اندر شير خالص تار مو
entries.english: For He sees conscience and thought and quest as a thread of hair in pure milk

entries.farsi: آن كه او بى‏نقش ساده سينه شد / نقشهاى غيب را آيينه شد
entries.english: He whose clear breast has become devoid of image has become a mirror for the impressions of the Invisible.

entries.farsi: سر ما را بى‏گمان موقن شود / ز آن كه مومن آينه‏ى مومن شود
entries.english: He becomes intuitively aware of our inmost thought, because the true believer is the mirror of the true believer.

entries.farsi: چون زند او نقد ما را بر محك / پس يقين را باز داند او ز شك‏
entries.english: When he rubs our poverty on the touchstone, then he knows the difference between faith and doubt.

entries.farsi: چون شود جانش محك نقدها / پس ببيند قلب را و قلب را
entries.english: When his soul becomes the touchstone of the coin, then he will see the heart and the false money.

sections.title_fa: نشاندن پادشاهان صوفيان عارف را پيش روى خويش تا چشمشان بديشان روشن شود
sections.title_en: How kings seat in front of them the Sufis who know God, in order that their eyes may be illumined by them.

entries.farsi: پادشاهان را چنان عادت بود / اين شنيده باشى ار يادت بود
entries.english: Such is the custom of kings: you will have heard of this, if you remember.

entries.farsi: دست چپشان پهلوانان ايستند / ز آنكه دل پهلوى چپ باشد ببند
entries.english: The paladins stand on their left hand, because the heart is fixed on the left side.

entries.farsi: مشرف و اهل قلم بر دست راست / ز آن كه علم و خط و ثبت آن دست راست
entries.english: On the right hand are the chancellor and the secretaries, because the science of writing and book-keeping belongs to this hand.

entries.farsi: صوفيان را پيش رو موضع دهند / كاينه‏ى جان‏اند و ز آيينه بهند
entries.english: They give the Sufis the place in front of their countenance, for they are a mirror for the soul, and better than a mirror,

entries.farsi: سينه صيقلها زده در ذكر و فكر / تا پذيرد آينه‏ى دل نقش بكر
entries.english: They have polished their breasts in commemoration and meditation, that the heart’s mirror may receive the virgin image.

entries.farsi: هر كه او از صلب فطرت خوب زاد / آينه در پيش او بايد نهاد
entries.english: Whoever is born beautiful from the loins of Creation, a mirror must he placed before him.

entries.farsi: عاشق آيينه باشد روى خوب / صيقل جان آمد و تَقْوَى القلوب‏
entries.english: The beauteous face is in love with the mirror: it is a polisher of the soul and of the fear of God in hearts.

sections.title_fa: آمدن مهمان پيش يوسف عليه السلام و تقاضا كردن يوسف از او تحفه و ارمغان‏
sections.title_en: How the guest came to Joseph, on whom be peace, and how Joseph demanded of him a gift and present on his return from abroad.

entries.farsi: آمد از آفاق يار مهربان / يوسف صديق را شد ميهمان‏
entries.english: The loving friend came from the ends of the earth and became the guest of Joseph the truthful,

entries.farsi: كآشنا بودند وقت كودكى / بر وساده‏ى آشنايى متكى‏
entries.english: For they had been well acquainted in childhood, reclining on the pillow of acquaintance,

entries.farsi: ياد دادش جور اخوان و حسد / گفت كان زنجير بود و ما اسد
entries.english: He spoke to him of the injustice and envy of his brethren: Joseph said, “That was a chain, and I was the lion.

entries.farsi: عار نبود شير را از سلسله / نيست ما را از قضاى حق گله‏
entries.english: The lion is not disgraced by the chain: I do not complain of God’s destiny.

entries.farsi: شير را بر گردن ار زنجير بود / بر همه زنجير سازان مير بود
entries.english: If the lion had a chain on his neck, he was prince over all the chain-makers.”

entries.farsi: گفت چون بودى ز زندان و ز چاه / گفت همچون در محاق و كاست ماه‏
entries.english: He asked, “How were you in regard to the prison and the well?” “Like the moon,” said Joseph, “in the interlunar period on the wane.”

entries.farsi: در محاق ار ماه نو گردد دو تا / نى در آخر بدر گردد بر سما
entries.english: If in that period the new moon is bent double, does not she at last become the full moon in the sky?

entries.farsi: گر چه دردانه به هاون كوفتند / نور چشم و دل شد و بيند بلند
entries.english: Though the seed-pearl is pounded in the mortar, it becomes the light of eye and heart and looks aloft.

entries.farsi: گندمى را زير خاك انداختند / پس ز خاكش خوشه‏ها بر ساختند
entries.english: They cast a grain of wheat under earth, then from its earth they raised up ears of corn;

entries.farsi: بار ديگر كوفتندش ز آسيا / قيمتش افزود و نان شد جان فزا
entries.english: Once more they crushed it with the mill: its value increased and it became soul-invigorating bread;

entries.farsi: باز نان را زير دندان كوفتند / گشت عقل و جان و فهم هوشمند
entries.english: Again they crushed the bread under their teeth: it became the mind and spirit and understanding of one endowed with reason;

entries.farsi: باز آن جان چون كه محو عشق گشت / يُعْجِبُ الزُّرَّاعَ آمد بعد كشت‏
entries.english: Again, when that spirit became lost in Love, it became rejoiceth the sowers after the sowing.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد باز گرد / تا كه با يوسف چه گفت آن نيك مرد
entries.english: This discourse has no end. Come back, that we may see what that good man said to Joseph.

entries.farsi: بعد قصه گفتنش گفت اى فالن / هين چه آوردى تو ما را ارمغان
entries.english: After he had told him his story, he said, “Now, O so-and-so, what traveller’s gift have you brought for me?”

entries.farsi: بر در ياران تهى دست اى فتى / هست چون بى‏گندمى در آسيا
entries.english: To come empty-handed to the door of friends is like going without wheat to the mill.

entries.farsi: حق تعالى خلق را گويد به حشر / ارمغان كو از براى روز نشر
entries.english: God, exalted is He, will say to the people at the gathering, “Where is your present for the Day of Resurrection?

entries.farsi: جئتمونا و فرادى بى‏نوا / هم بدان سان كه خلقناكم كذا
entries.english: You have come to Us and alone without provision, just in the same guise as We created you.

entries.farsi: هين چه آورديد دست آويز را / ارمغانى روز رستاخيز را
entries.english: Hark, what have you brought as an offering— a gift on homecoming for the Day when you rise from the dead?

entries.farsi: يا اميد باز گشتنتان نبود / وعده‏ى امروز باطلتان نمود
entries.english: Or had you no hope of returning? Did the promise of to-day seem vain to you?”

entries.farsi: وعده‏ى مهمانى‏اش را منكرى / پس ز مطبخ خاك و خاكستر برى‏
entries.english: Do you disbelieve in the promise of being His guest? Then from the kitchen you will get dust and ashes.

entries.farsi: ور نه‏اى منكر چنين دست تهى / در در آن دوست چون پا مى‏نهى‏
entries.english: And if you are not disbelieving, how with such empty hands are you setting foot in the Court of that Friend?

entries.farsi: اندكى صرفه بكن از خواب و خور / ارمغان بهر مالقاتش ببر
entries.english: Cut back a little on sleep and food: bring the gift for your meeting with Him.

entries.farsi: شو قليل النوم مما يهجعون / باش در اسحار از يستغفرون‏
entries.english: Become scant of sleep were slumbering; in the hours of dawn be of were asking pardon of God.

entries.farsi: اندكى جنبش بكن همچون جنين / تا ببخشندت حواس نور بين‏
entries.english: Stir a little, like the embryo, in order that you may be given the senses which behold the Light,

entries.farsi: وز جهان چون رحم بيرون روى / از زمين در عرصه‏ى واسع شوى‏
entries.english: And you are outside of this womb-like world: you goes from the earth into a wide expanse.

entries.farsi: آن كه ارض اللهَّ واسع گفته‏اند / عرصه‏اى دان كانبيا در رفته‏اند
entries.english: Know that the saying, “God’s earth is wide,” refers to that ample region into which the saints have entered.

entries.farsi: دل نگردد تنگ ز آن عرصه‏ى فراخ / نخل تر آن جا نگردد خشك شاخ‏
entries.english: The heart is not oppressed by that spacious expanse: there the fresh boughs of the palm-tree do not become dry.

entries.farsi: حاملى تو مر حواست را كنون / كند و مانده مى‏شوى و سر نگون‏
entries.english: At present you art bearing your senses: you are becoming weary and exhausted and headlong.

entries.farsi: چون كه محمولى نه حامل وقت خواب / ماندگى رفت و شدى بى‏رنج و تاب‏
entries.english: Since, at the time of sleep, you are borne, and are not bearing, your fatigue is gone and you are free from pain and anguish.

entries.farsi: چاشنيى دان تو حال خواب را / پيش محمولى حال اوليا
entries.english: Regard the time of sleep as a taste in comparison with the state in which the saints are borne.

entries.farsi: اوليا اصحاب كهفند اى عنود / در قيام و در تقلب هُمْ رقود
entries.english: The saints are the Men of the Cave, O obstinate one: they are asleep in rising up and turning to and fro.

entries.farsi: مى‏كشدشان بى‏تكلف در فعال / بى‏خبر ذات اليمين ذات الشمال‏
entries.english: He is drawing them, without their taking trouble to act, without consciousness, to the right hand and to the left.

entries.farsi: چيست آن ذات اليمين فعل حسن / چيست آن ذات الشمال اشغال تن‏
entries.english: What is that right hand? Good deeds. What is that left hand? The affairs of the body

entries.farsi: مى‏رود اين هر دو كار از انبيا / بى‏خبر زين هر دو ايشان چون صدا
entries.english: These two actions proceed from the saints, they are unconscious of them both, like the echo:

entries.farsi: گر صدايت بشنواند خير و شر / ذات كوه از هر دو باشد بى‏خبر
entries.english: If the echo causes you to hear good and evil, the mountain itself is unconscious of either.

sections.title_fa: گفتن مهمان يوسف عليه السلام را كه آينه آوردمت ارمغان تا هر بارى كه در وى نگرى روى خوب خود بينى مرا ياد كنى
sections.title_en: How the guest said to Joseph, “I have brought you the gift of a mirror, so that whenever you look in it you will see Your own fair face and remember me.”

entries.farsi: گفت يوسف هين بياور ارمغان / او ز شرم اين تقاضا زد فغان‏
entries.english: Joseph said, “Come, produce the gift.” He, on account of shame at this demand, sobbed aloud.

entries.farsi: حبه‏اى را جانب كان چون برم / قطره‏اى را سوى عمان چون برم‏
entries.english: “How many a gift,” said he, “did I seek for you! No gift came into my sight.

entries.farsi: گفت من چند ارمغان جستم ترا / ارمغانى در نظر نامد مرا
entries.english: How should I bring a grain to the mine? How should I bring a drop to the `Uman?

entries.farsi: زيره را من سوى كرمان آورم / گر به پيش تو دل و جان آورم‏
entries.english: I shall bring cumin to Kirman, if I bring my heart and soul to you.

entries.farsi: نيست تخمى كاندر اين انبار نيست / غير حسن تو كه آن را يار نيست‏
entries.english: There is no seed that is not in this barn, except your beauty which has no equal.

entries.farsi: اليق آن ديدم كه من آيينه‏اى / پيش تو آرم چو نور سينه‏اى‏
entries.english: I deemed it fitting that I should bring to you a mirror like the light of a breast,

entries.farsi: تا ببينى روى خوب خود در آن / اى تو چون خورشيد شمع آسمان‏
entries.english: ‘That you may behold your beauteous face therein, O you who, like the sun, are the candle of heaven.

entries.farsi: آينه آوردمت اى روشنى / تا چو بينى روى خود يادم كنى‏
entries.english: I have brought you a mirror, O light, so that when you see your face you may think of me.”

entries.farsi: آينه بيرون كشيد او از بغل / خوب را آيينه باشد مشتغل‏
entries.english: He drew forth the mirror from beneath his arm: the fair one’s business is with a mirror.

entries.farsi: آينه‏ى هستى چه باشد نيستى / نيستى بر گر تو ابله نيستى‏
entries.english: What is the mirror of Being? Not-being. Bring not-being, if you are not a fool.

entries.farsi: هستى اندر نيستى بتوان نمود / مال داران بر فقير آرند جود
entries.english: Being can be seen in not-being: the rich bestow generosity on the poor.

entries.farsi: آينه‏ى صافى نان خود گرسنه ست / سوخته هم آينه‏ى آتش زنه ست‏
entries.english: The clear mirror of bread is truly the hungry man; tinder, likewise, is the mirror of that from which fire is struck.

entries.farsi: نيستى و نقص هر جايى كه خاست / آينه‏ى خوبى جمله‏ى پيشه‏هاست‏
entries.english: Not-being and defect, wherever they arise, are the mirror which displays the excellence of all crafts.

entries.farsi: چون كه جامه چست و دوزيده بود / مظهر فرهنگ درزى چون شود
entries.english: When a garment is neat and well-stitched, how should it enable the tailor to exhibit his skill?

entries.farsi: ناتراشيده همى‏بايد جذوع / تا دروگر اصل سازد يا فروع‏
entries.english: Trunks of trees must be uncut in order that the woodcutter may fashion the stem or the branches.

entries.farsi: خواجه‏ى اشكسته بند آن جا رود / كه در آن جا پاى اشكسته بود
entries.english: The doctor who sets broken bones goes to the place where the person with the fractured leg is.

entries.farsi: كى شود چون نيست رنجور نزار / آن جمال صنعت طب آشكار
entries.english: How shall the excellence of the art of medicine be made manifest when there is no emaciated invalid?

entries.farsi: خوارى و دونى مسها بر مال / گر نباشد كى نمايد كيميا
entries.english: How shall the Elixir be shown if the vileness and baseness of coppers is not notorious?

entries.farsi: ز آن كه ضد را ضد كند پيدا يقين / ز آن كه با سركه پديد است انگبين‏
entries.english: Defects are the mirror of the quality of perfection, and that vileness is the mirror of power and glory,

entries.farsi: نقصها آيينه‏ى وصف كمال / و آن حقارت آينه‏ى عز و جلال‏
entries.english: Because contrary is certainly made evident by its contrary; because honey is perceived with vinegar

entries.farsi: هر كه نقص خويش را ديد و شناخت / اندر استكمال خود ده اسبه تاخت‏
entries.english: Whoever has seen and recognised his own deficiency has ridden post-haste in perfecting himself.

entries.farsi: ز آن نمى‏پرد به سوى ذو الجلال / كاو گمانى مى‏برد خود را كمال‏
entries.english: The reason why he is not flying towards the Lord of glory is that he supposes himself to be perfect.

entries.farsi: علتى بدتر ز پندار كمال / نيست اندر جان تو اى ذو دالل‏
entries.english: There is no worse malady in your soul, O haughty one, than the conceit of perfection.

entries.farsi: از دل و از ديده‏ات بس خون رود / تا ز تو اين معجبى بيرون رود
entries.english: Much blood must flow from your heart and eye that self-complacency may leave you.

entries.farsi: علت ابليس انا خيرى بده ست / وين مرض در نفس هر مخلوق هست‏
entries.english: The fault of Iblis lay in thinking “I am better,” and this disease is in the soul of every creature.

entries.farsi: گر چه خود را بس شكسته بيند او / آب صافى دان و سرگين زير جو
entries.english: Though he regards himself as very broken, know that it is clear water and dung under the stream.

entries.farsi: چون بشوراند ترا در امتحان / آب سرگين رنگ گردد در زمان‏
entries.english: When he stirs you in trial, immediately the water becomes dung-coloured.

entries.farsi: در تگ جو هست سرگين اى فتى / گر چه جو صافى نمايد مر ترا
entries.english: There is dung in the bed of the stream, my man, though to you the stream appears pure.

entries.farsi: هست پير راه دان پر فطن / باغهاى نفس كل را جوى كن‏
entries.english: It is the Pir full of wisdom, well-acquainted with the Way, that digs a channel for the streams of the flesh and the body.

entries.farsi: جوى خود را كى تواند پاك كرد / نافع از علم خدا شد علم مرد
entries.english: Can the water of the stream clear out the dung? Can man’s knowledge sweep away the ignorance of his sensual self?

entries.farsi: كى تراشد تيغ دسته‏ى خويش را / رو به جراحى سپار اين ريش را
entries.english: How shall the sword fashion its own hilt? Go, entrust this wound to a surgeon.

entries.farsi: بر سر هر ريش جمع آمد مگس / تا نبيند قبح ريش خويش كس‏
entries.english: Flies gather on every wound, so that no one sees the foulness of his wound.

entries.farsi: آن مگس انديشه‏ها و آن مال تو / ريش تو آن ظلمت احوال تو
entries.english: Those flies are your thoughts and your possessions: your wound is the darkness of your states;

entries.farsi: ور نهد مرهم بر آن ريش تو پير / آن زمان ساكن شود درد و نفير
entries.english: And if the Pir lays a plaster on your wound, at once the pain and lamentation are stilled,

entries.farsi: تا كه پندارد كه صحت يافته ست / پرتو مرهم بر آن جا تافته ست‏
entries.english: So that you fancy it is healed, the ray of the plaster has shone upon the spot.

entries.farsi: هين ز مرهم سر مكش اى پشت ريش / و آن ز پرتو دان مدان از اصل خويش‏
entries.english: Beware! Do not turn your head away from the plaster, O you who are wounded in the back, but recognise that that proceeds from the ray: do not regard it as from your own constitution.

sections.title_fa: مرتد شدن كاتب وحى به سبب آن كه پرتو وحى بر او زد آن آيت را پيش از پيغامبر صلى اللهَّ عليه و ‏اله بخواند گفت پس من هم محل وحيم
sections.title_en: How the writer of the Revelation fell into apostasy because the ray of the Revelation shot upon him, he recited the verse before the Prophet, on whom be peace,; then he said,
“So I too am one upon whom Revelation has descended.”

entries.farsi: پيش از عثمان يكى نساخ بود / كاو به نسخ وحى جدى مى‏نمود
entries.english: Before `Uthman there was a scribe who used to be diligent in writing down the Revelation.

entries.farsi: چون نبى از وحى فرمودى سبق / او همان را وانبشتى بر ورق‏
entries.english: Whenever the Prophet dictated the Revelation, he would write out the same on the leaf.

entries.farsi: عين آن حكمت بفرمودى رسول / زين قدر گمراه شد آن بو الفضول
entries.english: The beams of that Revelation would shine upon him, and he would find Wisdom within him.

entries.farsi: پرتو آن وحى بر وى تافتى / او درون خويش حكمت يافتى‏
entries.english: The substance of that Wisdom was dictated by the Prophet: by this amount that meddling fool was led astray,

entries.farsi: كانچه مى‏گويد رسول مستنير / مر مرا هست آن حقيقت در ضمير
entries.english: Thinking, “I have in my conscience the Truth of that which the illumined Prophet is saying.”

entries.farsi: پرتو انديشه‏اش زد بر رسول / قهر حق آورد بر جانش نزول‏
entries.english: The ray of his thought struck the Prophet: the wrath of God descended on his soul.

entries.farsi: هم ز نساخى بر آمد هم ز دين / شد عدوى مصطفى و دين به كين‏
entries.english: He abandoned both his work as a scribe and the Religion: he became the malignant foe of Mustafa and the Religion.

entries.farsi: مصطفى فرمود كاى گبر عنود / چون سيه گشتى اگر نور از تو بود
entries.english: Mustafa said, “O obstinate miscreant, if the Light was from you, how you should have become black?

entries.farsi: گر تو ينبوع الهى بوديى / اين چنين آب سيه نگشوديى‏
entries.english: If you had been the Divine fountain, you wouldst not have let out such black water as this.”

entries.farsi: تا كه ناموسش به پيش اين و آن / نشكند بر بست اين او را دهان
entries.english: Lest his reputation should be ruined in the sight of all and sundry, this kept his mouth shut.

entries.farsi: اندرون مى‏سوختش هم زين سبب / توبه كردن مى‏نيارست اين عجب‏
entries.english: His heart is being darkened; hence he is unable to repent: this is wonderful.
