entries.farsi: آه مى‏كرد و نبودش آه سود / چون در آمد تيغ و سر را در ربود
entries.english: He was crying “Alas,” but “Alas” was of no use to him when the sword came on and took off his head.

entries.farsi: كرده حق ناموس را صد من حديد / اى بسا بسته به بند ناپديد
entries.english: God has made reputation a ton of iron: oh, many a one is bound in the unseen chain!

entries.farsi: كبر و كفر آن سان ببست آن راه را / كه نيارد كرد ظاهر آه را
entries.english: Pride and infidelity have barred that Way in such wise that he cannot utter a sigh.

entries.farsi: گفت اغالال فهم به مقمحون / نيست آن اغالل بر ما از برون‏
entries.english: He said, “We have put shackles on their necks, and therefore they are forced to lift up their heads”: those shackles are not on us from outside.

entries.farsi: خلفهم سدا فأغشيناهم / مى‏نبيند بند را پيش و پس او
entries.english: “And We have put behind them a barrier, and We have made a covering of darkness over them”: the uncle is not seeing the barrier in front and behind.

entries.farsi: رنگ صحرا دارد آن سدى كه خاست / او نمى‏داند كه آن سد قضاست‏
entries.english: The barrier that arose has the appearance of open country: he does not know that it is the barrier of the Divine destiny.

entries.farsi: شاهد تو سد روى شاهد است / مرشد تو سد گفت مرشد است‏
entries.english: Your beloved is a barrier to the face of the Beloved: your guide is a barrier to the words of the guide.

entries.farsi: اى بسا كفار را سوداى دين / بندشان ناموس و كبر آن و اين‏
entries.english: Oh, many are the infidels that have a passionate longing for the Religion: his chain is reputation and pride and that and this.

entries.farsi: بند پنهان ليك از آهن بتر / بند آهن را كند پاره تبر
entries.english: The chain is hidden, but it is worse than iron: the iron chain is cloven by the axe.

entries.farsi: بند آهن را توان كردن جدا / بند غيبى را نداند كس دوا
entries.english: The iron chain can be removed: none knows how to cure the invisible chain.

entries.farsi: مرد را زنبور اگر نيشى زند / طبع او آن لحظه بر دفعى تند
entries.english: If a man is stung by a wasp, he extracts the wasp’s sting from his body,

entries.farsi: زخم نيش اما چو از هستى تست / غم قوى باشد نگردد درد سست‏
entries.english: But since the stinging wound is from your self-existence, the pain continues with violence and the anguish is not relieved.

entries.farsi: شرح اين از سينه بيرون مى‏جهد / ليك مى‏ترسم كه نوميدى دهد
entries.english: The explanation of this is springing forth from my breast, but I am afraid it may give despair.

entries.farsi: نى مشو نوميد و خود را شاد كن / پيش آن فريادرس فرياد كن‏
entries.english: No, do not despair: make yourself cheerful, call for help to Him who comes at the call,

entries.farsi: كاى محب عفو از ما عفو كن / اى طبيب رنج ناسور كهن‏
entries.english: Saying, “Forgive us, O You who loves to forgive, O You who have a medicine for the old gangrenous disease!”

entries.farsi: عكس حكمت آن شقى را ياوه كرد / خود مبين تا بر نيارد از تو گرد
entries.english: The reflection of Wisdom led astray that miserable one: be not self-conceited, lest it rise up the dust from you.

entries.farsi: اى برادر بر تو حكمت جاريه ست / آن ز ابدال است و بر تو عاريه ست‏
entries.english: O brother, Wisdom is flowing in upon you: it comes from the Abdal, and in you it is a borrowed thing.

entries.farsi: گر چه در خود خانه نورى يافته ست / آن ز همسايه‏ى منور تافته ست
entries.english: Although the house has found a light within it, that has shone forth from a light-giving neighbour.

entries.farsi: شكر كن غره مشو بينى مكن / گوش دار و هيچ خود بينى مكن
entries.english: Render thanks, be not beguiled by vanity, do not turn up your nose, hearken attentively, and do not show any self-conceit.

entries.farsi: صد دريغ و درد كاين عاريتى / امتان را دور كرد از امتى‏
entries.english: “If is a hundred pities and griefs that this borrowed state has put the religious communities far from religious communion.

entries.farsi: من غالم آن كه او در هر رباط / خويش را واصل نداند بر سماط
entries.english: I am the slave of him who does not regard himself in every caravanseray as having attained to the table.

entries.farsi: بس رباطى كه ببايد ترك كرد / تا به مسكن در رسد يك روز مرد
entries.english: Many is the caravanseray that we must leave, in order that one day the man may reach home.

entries.farsi: گر شود پر نور روزن يا سرا / تو مدان روشن مگر خورشيد را
entries.english: Though the iron has become red, it is not red: it is a ray borrowed from something that strikes fire.

entries.farsi: گر چه آهن سرخ شد او سرخ نيست / پرتو عاريت آتش زنى است‏
entries.english: If the window or the house is full of light, do not deem anything luminous except the sun.

entries.farsi: هر در و ديوار گويد روشنم / پرتو غيرى ندارم اين منم‏
entries.english: Every door and wall says, “ I am luminous: I do not hold the rays of another, I am this.”

entries.farsi: پس بگويد آفتاب اى نارشيد / چون كه من غارب شوم آيد پديد
entries.english: Then the sun says, “ O you who art not right, when I set it will become evident.”

entries.farsi: سبزه‏ها گويند ما سبز از خوديم / شاد و خندانيم و بس زيبا خديم‏
entries.english: The plants say, “We are green of ourselves, we are gay and smiling and we are tall.”

entries.farsi: فصل تابستان بگويد اى امم / خويش را بينيد چون من بگذرم‏
entries.english: The season of summer says, “O peoples behold yourselves when I depart!”

entries.farsi: تن همى‏نازد به خوبى و جمال / روح پنهان كرده فر و پر و بال‏
entries.english: The body is boasting of its beauty and attractiveiness, the spirit, having concealed its glory and pinions and plumes,

entries.farsi: گويدش اى مزبله تو كيستى / يك دو روز از پرتو من زيستى‏
entries.english: Says to it, “O dunghill, who are you? Through my beams you have come to life for a day or two.

entries.farsi: غنج و نازت مى‏نگنجد در جهان / باش تا كه من شوم از تو جهان‏
entries.english: Your flirtatious and prideful airs are not contained in the world; wait till I spring up from you!

entries.farsi: گرم‏دارانت ترا گورى كنند / طعمه‏ى موران و مارانت كنند
entries.english: They whose love warmed you will dig a grave for you; they will make you a morsel for ants and reptiles.

entries.farsi: بينى از گند تو گيرد آن كسى / كاو به پيش تو همى‏مردى بسى‏
entries.english: That one who many a time in your presence was dying will hold his nose at your stench.”

entries.farsi: پرتو روح است نطق و چشم و گوش / پرتو آتش بود در آب جوش‏
entries.english: The glare of the spirit is speech and eye and ear: the glare of fire is the bubbling in the water.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه پرتو جان بر تن است / پرتو ابدال بر جان من است‏
entries.english: As the glare of the spirit falls on the body, so fall the glare of the Abdal on my soul.

entries.farsi: جان جان چون واكشد پا را ز جان / جان چنان گردد كه بى‏جان تن بدان‏
entries.english: When the Soul of the soul withdraws from the soul, the soul becomes even as the soulless body. Know!

entries.farsi: سر از آن رو مى‏نهم من بر زمين / تا گواه من بود در روز دين‏
entries.english: For that reason I am laying my head on the earth, so that she may be my witness on the Day of Judgment.

entries.farsi: يوم دين كه زلزلت زلزالها / اين زمين باشد گواه حالها
entries.english: On the Day of Judgment, when she shall be made to quake mightily, this earth will bear witness to all that passed;

entries.farsi: كاو تحدث جهرة أخبارها / در سخن آيد زمين و خاره‏ها
entries.english: For she will plainly declare what she knows: earth and rocks will begin to speak

entries.farsi: فلسفى منكر شود در فكر و ظن / گو برو سر را بر آن ديوار زن‏
entries.english: The philosopher, in his thought and opinion, becomes disbelieving: bid him go and dash his head against this wall!

entries.farsi: نطق آب و نطق خاك و نطق گل / هست محسوس حواس اهل دل‏
entries.english: The speech of water, the speech of earth, and the speech of mud are apprehended by the senses of them that have hearts.

entries.farsi: فلسفى كاو منكر حنانه است / از حواس اوليا بيگانه است‏
entries.english: The philosopher who disbelieves in the moaning pillar is a stranger to the senses of the saints.

entries.farsi: گويد او كه پرتو سوداى خلق / بس خيالات آورد در راى خلق‏
entries.english: He says that the beam of melancholia brings many images into people’s minds.

entries.farsi: بلكه عكس آن فساد و كفر او / اين خيال منكرى را زد بر او
entries.english: No, but the reflection of his wickedness and infidelity cast this idle fancy of skepticism upon him.

entries.farsi: فلسفى مر ديو را منكر شود / در همان دم سخره‏ى ديوى بود
entries.english: The philosopher comes to deny the existence of the Devil, and at the same time he is possessed by a devil.

entries.farsi: گر نديدى ديو را خود را ببين / بى‏جنون نبود كبودى بر جبين‏
entries.english: If you have not seen the Devil, behold yourself: without diabolic possession there is no blueness in the forehead.

entries.farsi: هر كه را در دل شك و پيچانى است / در جهان او فلسفى پنهانى است‏
entries.english: Whoever has doubt and perplexity in his heart, he in this world is a secret philosopher.

entries.farsi: مى‏نمايد اعتقاد و گاه گاه / آن رگ فلسف كند رويش سياه
entries.english: He is professing firm belief, but some time or other that philosophical vein will blacken his face.

entries.farsi: الحذر اى مومنان كان در شماست / در شما بس عالم بى‏منتهاست
entries.english: Take care, O you Faithful! For that is in you: in you is many an infinite world.

entries.farsi: جمله هفتاد و دو ملت در تو است / وه كه روزى آن بر آرد از تو دست‏
entries.english: In you are all the two-and-seventy sects: woe if one day they gain the upper hand over you.

entries.farsi: هر كه او را برگ آن ايمان بود / همچو برگ از بيم اين لرزان بود
entries.english: From fear of this, every one who has the fortune (barg) of this Faith is trembling like a leaf (barg).

entries.farsi: بر بليس و ديو از آن خنديده‏اى / كه تو خود را نيك مردم ديده‏اى‏
entries.english: You have laughed at Iblis and the devils because you have regarded yourself as a good man.

entries.farsi: بر دكان هر زرنما خندان شده ست / ز آنكه سنگ امتحان پنهان شده ست‏
entries.english: When the soul shall turn its coat inside out, how many a “Woe is me” will it extort from the followers of the Religion!

entries.farsi: چون كند جان باژگونه پوستين / چند وا ويال بر آيد ز اهل دين‏
entries.english: On the counter everything that looks like gold is smiling, because the touchstone is out of sight.

entries.farsi: پرده اى ستار از ما بر مگير / باش اندر امتحان ما مجير
entries.english: O Coverer, do not lift up the veil from us, be a protector to us in our test.

entries.farsi: قلب پهلو مى‏زند با زر به شب / انتظار روز مى‏دارد ذهب‏
entries.english: At night the false coin jostles with the gold: the gold is waiting for day.

entries.farsi: با زبان حال زر گويد كه باش / اى مزور تا بر آيد روز فاش‏
entries.english: With the tongue of its (inward) state the gold says, “Wait, O tinselled one, till day rises clear.”

entries.farsi: صد هزاران سال ابليس لعين / بود ز ابدال و امير المؤمنين‏
entries.english: Hundreds of thousands of years the accursed Iblis was a saint and the prince of true believers;

entries.farsi: پنجه زد با آدم از نازى كه داشت / گشت رسوا همچو سرگين وقت چاشت‏
entries.english: On account of the pride which he had, he grappled with Adam and was put to shame, like dung in the morning.

sections.title_fa: دعا كردن بلعم باعور كه موسى و قومش را از اين شهر كه حصار داده‏اند بى‏مراد باز گردان
sections.title_en: How Bal’ am son of Ba’ur prayed, saying, “Cause Moses and his people to turn back, without having gained their desire, from this city which they have besieged.”

entries.farsi: بلعم باعور را خلق جهان / سغبه شد مانند عيساى زمان‏
entries.english: To Bal’am son of Ba’ur the people of the world became subject, like unto the Jesus of the time.

entries.farsi: سجده ناوردند كس را دون او / صحت رنجور بود افسون او
entries.english: They bowed to none but him: his spell was health to the sick.

entries.farsi: پنجه زد با موسى از كبر و كمال / آن چنان شد كه شنيده ستى تو حال‏
entries.english: From pride and perfection he grappled with Moses: his plight became such as you have heard.

entries.farsi: صد هزار ابليس و بلعم در جهان / همچنين بوده ست پيدا و نهان‏
entries.english: Even so there have been in the world, manifest or hidden, a hundred thousand like Iblis and Bal’am.

entries.farsi: اين دو را مشهور گردانيد اله / تا كه باشد اين دو بر باقى گواه‏
entries.english: God caused these two to be notorious, that these two might bear witness against the rest.

entries.farsi: اين دو دزد آويخت از دار بلند / ور نه اندر قهر بس دزدان بدند
entries.english: These two thieves He hanged on a high gallows; else there were many thieves in His vengeance.

entries.farsi: اين دو را پرچم به سوى شهر برد / كشتگان قهر را نتوان شمرد
entries.english: These two He dragged by their forelocks to the city; it is impossible to number the victims of His wrath.

entries.farsi: نازنينى تو ولى در حد خويش / اللهَّ اهلل پا منه از حد خويش‏
entries.english: You are a favourite, but within your bounds. God, God, do not set foot beyond bounds.

entries.farsi: گر زنى بر نازنين تر از خودت / در تگ هفتم زمين زير آردت‏
entries.english: If you combat with one who is a greater favourite than yourself, it will bring you down to the lowest depth of the seventh earth.

entries.farsi: قصه‏ى عاد و ثمود از بهر چيست / تا بدانى كانبيا را نازكى است‏
entries.english: For what purpose is the tale of Ad and Thamud? That you may know that the prophets have disdain.

entries.farsi: اين نشان خسف و قذف و صاعقه / شد بيان عز نفس ناطقه‏
entries.english: These signs—the swallowing up, the hurling of stones, and the thunderbolts— were evidence of the dearness of the Rational Soul.

entries.farsi: جمله حيوان را پى انسان بكش / جمله انسان را بكش از بهر هش‏
entries.english: Kill all animals for the sake of man; kill all mankind for the sake of Reason.

entries.farsi: هش چه باشد عقل كل هوشمند / هوش جزوى هش بود اما نژند
entries.english: What is Reason? The Essence of Knowledge of the man endowed with reason. Partial reason is reason, but it is infirm.

entries.farsi: جمله حيوانات وحشى ز آدمى / باشد از حيوان انسى در كمى‏
entries.english: All the animals that are wild to man are inferior to the human animal.

entries.farsi: خون آنها خلق را باشد سبيل / ز انكه وحشى‏اند از عقل جليل‏
entries.english: Their blood is free to mankind, since they have not become capable of human actions.

entries.farsi: عزت وحشى بدين افتاد پست / كه مر انسان را مخالف آمده ست‏
entries.english: The honour of the wild animals is fallen because they have grown hostile to man.

entries.farsi: خر نشايد كشت از بهر صالح / چون شود وحشى شود خونش مباح‏
entries.english: What honour, then, will be yours, O marvel, since you have become timorous wild asses?

entries.farsi: پس چه عزت باشدت اى نادره / چون شدى تو حُمُرٌ مستنفرة
entries.english: Because of his usefulness, the ass ought not to be killed; when he turns wild, his blood becomes lawful.

entries.farsi: گر چه خر را دانش زاجر نبود / هيچ معذورش نمى‏دارد ودود
entries.english: Although the ass had no knowledge to restrain him, the Loving One is not excusing him at all.

entries.farsi: الجرم كفار را شد خون مباح / همچو وحشى پيش نشاب و رماح‏
entries.english: How, then, shall man be excused, O noble friend, when he has become wild to that Word?

entries.farsi: پس چو وحشى شد از آن دم آدمى / كى بود معذور اى يار سمى‏
entries.english: Of necessity permission was given to shed the blood of the infidels, like a wild beast before the arrows and lances.

entries.farsi: جفت و فرزندانشان جمله سبيل / ز آنكه بى‏عقلند و مردود و ذليل‏
entries.english: All their wives and children are free spoil, because they are wildly averse to the august Reason.

entries.farsi: باز عقلى كاو رمد از عقل عقل / كرد از عقلى به حيوانات نقل‏
entries.english: Once more, a reason that flees from the Reason of reason is transported from rationality to the animals.

sections.title_fa: اعتماد كردن هاروت و ماروت بر عصمت خويش و آميزى اهل دنيا خواستن و در فتنه افتادن
sections.title_en: How Harut and Marut relied upon their immaculateness and desired to mix with the people of this world and fell into temptation.

entries.farsi: همچو هاروت و چو ماروت شهير / از بطر خوردند زهر آلود تير
entries.english: How, because of their arrogance, the celebrated Harut and Marut were struck by the poisoned arrow.

entries.farsi: اعتمادى بودشان بر قدس خويش / چيست بر شير اعتماد گاوميش‏
entries.english: They had confidence in their holiness, what is it for the buffalo to have confidence in the lion?

entries.farsi: گر چه او با شاخ صد چاره كند / شاخ شاخش شير نر پاره كند
entries.english: Though he makes a hundred shifts with his horn, the fierce lion will tear him to pieces limb by limb.

entries.farsi: گر شود پر شاخ همچون خار پشت / شير خواهد گاو را ناچار كشت‏
entries.english: If he becomes as full of horns as a hedgehog, the buffalo will inevitably be killed by the lion.

entries.farsi: گر چه صرصر بس درختان مى‏كند / با گياه تر وى احسان مى‏كند
entries.english: Though the Sarsar wind uproots many trees, it makes every blade of grass glisten with beauty.

entries.farsi: بر ضعيفى گياه آن باد تند / رحم كرد اى دل تو از قوت ملند
entries.english: That violent wind had pity on the weakness of the grass: do not you, O heart, brag vainly of your strength.

entries.farsi: تيشه را ز انبوهى شاخ درخت / كى هراس آيد ببرد لخت لخت‏
entries.english: How should the axe be afraid of the thickness of the branches? It cuts them to pieces.

entries.farsi: ليك بر برگى نكوبد خويش را / جز كه بر نيشى نكوبد نيش را
entries.english: But it does not beat itself against a leaf, it does not beat its edge except against an edge.

entries.farsi: شعله را ز انبوهى هيزم چه غم / كى رمد قصاب از خيل غنم‏
entries.english: What does the flame care for the great quantity of firewood? How should the butcher flee in terror from the multitude of sheep?

entries.farsi: پيش معنى چيست صورت بس زبون / چرخ را معنيش مى‏دارد نگون‏
entries.english: What is form in the presence of reality? Very feeble It is the reality of the sky that keeps it upside down.

entries.farsi: تو قياس از چرخ دوالبى بگير / گردشش از كيست از عقل مشير
entries.english: Judge by the analogy of the celestial wheel: from whom does its motion proceed? From directive Reason

entries.farsi: گردش اين باد از معنى اوست / همچو چرخى كان اسير آب جوست‏
entries.english: The motion of this shield-like body is from the veiled spirit, O son.

entries.farsi: گردش اين قالب همچون سپر / هست از روح مستر اى پسر
entries.english: The motion of this wind is from its reality, like the wheel that is captive to the water of the stream.

entries.farsi: جر و مد و دخل و خرج اين نفس / از كه باشد جز ز جان پر هوس‏
entries.english: The ebb and flow and incoming and outgoing of this breath — from whom does it proceed but from the spirit that is filled with desire?

entries.farsi: گاه جيمش مى‏كند گه حا و دال / گاه صلحش مى‏كند گاهى جدال‏
entries.english: Now it makes the breath jim, now ha and dal; now it makes it peace, now strife.

entries.farsi: همچنين اين باد را يزدان ما / كرده بد بر عاد همچون اژدها
entries.english: Even so our God had made this wind like a dragon against Ad.

entries.farsi: باز هم آن باد را بر مومنان / كرده بد صلح و مراعات و امان‏
entries.english: Again, He had also made that wind peace and regard-fulness and safety for the true believers.

entries.farsi: گفت المعنى هو اللهَّ شيخ دين / بحر معنيهاى رب العالمين‏
entries.english: “The Reality is Allah,” said the Shaykh of the Religion, the sea of the spiritual realities of the Lord of created beings.

entries.farsi: جمله اطباق زمين و آسمان / همچو خاشاكى در آن بحر روان‏
entries.english: All the tiers of earth and heaven are as straws in that flowing sea.

entries.farsi: حمله‏ها و رقص خاشاك اندر آب / هم ز آب آمد به وقت اضطراب‏
entries.english: The rushing and tossing of the straws in the water is produced by the water when it is agitated.

entries.farsi: چون كه ساكن خواهدش كرد از مرا / سوى ساحل افكند خاشاك را
entries.english: When it wishes to make them cease from struggling, it casts the straws toward the shore.

entries.farsi: چون كشد از ساحلش در موج گاه / آن كند با او كه آتش با گياه‏
entries.english: When it draws them from the shore into the surge it does with them that which the Sarsar does with the grass.

entries.farsi: اين حديث آخر ندارد باز ران / جانب هاروت و ماروت اى جوان‏
entries.english: This topic is endless. Speed back, O youth, to Harut and Marut.

sections.title_fa: ‏باقى قصه‏ى هاروت و ماروت و نكال و عقوبت ايشان هم در دنيا به چاه بابل
sections.title_en: The rest of the story of Harut and Marut, and how an exemplary punishment was inflicted on them, even in this world, in the pit of Babylon

entries.farsi: چون گناه و فسق خلقان جهان / مى‏شدى بر هر دو روشن آن زمان‏
entries.english: Inasmuch as the sin and wickedness of the people of the world was becoming clearly visible to them both from the latticed window,

entries.farsi: دست‏خاييدن گرفتندى ز خشم / ليك عيب خود نديدندى به چشم‏
entries.english: They began to gnaw their hands in wrath, but had no eyes for their own fault.

entries.farsi: خويش در آيينه ديد آن زشت مرد / رو بگردانيد از آن و خشم كرد
entries.english: The ugly man saw himself in the mirror: he turned his face away from that and was enraged.

entries.farsi: خويش بين چون از كسى جرمى بديد / آتشى در وى ز دوزخ شد پديد
entries.english: When the self-conceited person has seen any one commit a sin, there appears in him a fire from Hell.

entries.farsi: حميت دين خواند او آن كبر را / ننگرد در خويش نفس گبر را
entries.english: He calls that pride defence of the Religion: he regards not the soul of arrogance in himself.

entries.farsi: حميت دين را نشانى ديگر است / كه از آن آتش جهانى اخضر است‏
entries.english: Defence of the Religion has a different character, for from that fire a world is green.

entries.farsi: گفت حقشان گر شما روشان‏گريد / در سيه كاران مغفل منگريد
entries.english: God said to them, “If you are enlightened, look not carelessly upon the doers of black deeds who have been made forgetful.

entries.farsi: شكر گوييد اى سپاه و چاكران / رسته‏ايد از شهوت و از چاك ران‏
entries.english: Render thanks, O Host and Servants! You are freed from lust and sexual intercourse.

entries.farsi: گر از آن معنى نهم من بر شما / مر شما را بيش نپذيرد سما
entries.english: If I impose that kind of nature on you, Heaven will accept you no more.

entries.farsi: عصمتى كه مر شما را در تن است / آن ز عكس عصمت و حفظ من است‏
entries.english: The preservation which you have in your bodies is from the reflection of My preservation and care.

entries.farsi: آن ز من بينيد نز خود هين و هين / تا نچربد بر شما ديو لعين‏
entries.english: Oh, beware! Regard that as from Me, not from yourselves, lest the accursed Devil prevail against you.”

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه كاتب وحى رسول / ديد حكمت در خود و نور اصول‏
entries.english: As the writer of the Revelation given to the Prophet deemed the Wisdom and the Original Light in himself.

entries.farsi: خويش را هم صوت مرغان خدا / مى‏شمرد آن بد صفيرى چون صدا
entries.english: He was reckoning himself a fellow-songster of the Birds of God, that was a whistle resembling an echo.

entries.farsi: لحن مرغان را اگر واصف شوى / بر مراد مرغ كى واقف شوى‏
entries.english: If you become an exponent of the song of birds, how will you become acquainted with the meaning of the bird?

entries.farsi: گر بياموزى صفير بلبلى / تو چه دانى كاو چه دارد با گلى‏
entries.english: If you learn the note of a nightingale, how will you know what it has towards a rose?

entries.farsi: ور بدانى باشد آن هم از گمان / چون ز لب جنبان گمانهاى كران‏
entries.english: Or if you do know, it will be from analogy and surmise, like the conjectures formed by deaf people from those who move their lips.

sections.title_fa: ‏به عيادت رفتن كر بر همسايه‏ى رنجور خويش
sections.title_en: How the deaf man went to visit his sick neighbour.

entries.farsi: آن كرى را گفت افزون مايه‏اى / كه ترا رنجور شد همسايه‏اى‏
entries.english: One possessed of much wealth said to a deaf man, “A neighbour of yours is fallen ill.”

entries.farsi: گفت با خود كر كه با گوش گران / من چه دريابم ز گفت آن جوان‏
entries.english: The deaf man said to himself, “Being hard of hearing, what shall I understand of the words spoken by that youth?

entries.farsi: خاصه رنجور و ضعيف آواز شد / ليك بايد رفت آن جا نيست بد
entries.english: Especially he is ill and his voice is weak; but I must go there, there’s no escape.

entries.farsi: چون ببينم كان لبش جنبان شود / من قياسى گيرم آن را هم ز خود
entries.english: When I see his lips moving, I will form a conjecture as to that from myself.

entries.farsi: چون بگويم چونى اى محنت كشم / او بخواهد گفت نيكم يا خوشم‏
entries.english: When I say, ‘ How are you, O my suffering (friend)?’ he will reply, ‘ I am fine’ or I am pretty well.’

entries.farsi: من بگويم شكر چه خوردى ابا / او بگويد شربتى يا ماشبا
entries.english: I will say, Thanks! What potion have you had to drink?’ He will reply, some sherbet or a decoction of kidney-beans.’

entries.farsi: من بگويم صحه نوشت كيست آن / از طبيبان پيش تو گويد فالن‏
entries.english: I will say, May you enjoy health! Who is the doctor attending you?’ He will answer, So-and-so.’

entries.farsi: من بگويم بس مبارك پاست او / چون كه او آمد شود كارت نكو
entries.english: He is one who brings great luck with him,’ I will remark; `since he has come, things will go well for you.

entries.farsi: پاى او را آزمودستيم ما / هر كجا شد مى‏شود حاجت روا
entries.english: I have experienced his foot: wherever he goes, the desired object is attained.’”

entries.farsi: اين جوابات قياسى راست كرد / پيش آن رنجور شد آن نيك مرد
entries.english: The good man made ready these conjectural answers, and went to see the invalid.

entries.farsi: گفت چونى گفت مردم گفت شكر / شد از اين رنجور پر آزار و نكر
entries.english: “How are you?” he asked. “I am at the point of death,” said he. ”Thanks!” cried the deaf man. At this, the patient became resentful and indignant,

entries.farsi: كين چه شكر است او مگر با ما بد است / كر قياسى كرد و آن كژ آمده ست‏
entries.english: Saying, “What thanksgiving is this? He has been my enemy.” The deaf man made a conjecture, and it has turned out to be wrong.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفتش چه خوردى گفت زهر / گفت نوشت باد افزون گشت قهر
entries.english: After that, he asked him what he had drunk. “Poison,” said he. “May it do you good and give you health!” said the deaf man. His wrath increased.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفت از طبيبان كيست او / كاو همى‏آيد به چاره پيش تو
entries.english: Then he inquired, “Which of the doctors is it that is coming to attend you?”

entries.farsi: كر برون آمد بگفت او شادمان / شكر كش كردم مراعات اين زمان‏
entries.english: He replied, “Azrael is coming. Go!” “His foot,” said the deaf man, “is very blessed: be glad!”

entries.farsi: گفت عزراييل مى‏آيد برو / گفت پايش بس مبارك شاد شو
entries.english: The deaf man went forth. He said gaily, “Thanks for that! Now I will take leave.”

entries.farsi: گفت رنجور اين عدوى جان ماست / ما ندانستيم كاو كان جفاست‏
entries.english: The invalid said, “This is my mortal foe: I did not know he was a mine of iniquity.”

entries.farsi: خاطر رنجور جويان صد سقط / تا كه پيغامش كند از هر نمط
entries.english: The mind of the invalid was seeking a hundred abusive terms, that he might send him a message of every description,

entries.farsi: چون كسى كاو خورده باشد آش بد / مى‏بشوراند دلش تا قى كند
entries.english: As, when any one has eaten bad food, it is turning his heart until he vomits.

entries.farsi: تا بريزم بر وى آن چه گفته بود / كان زمان شير ضميرم خفته بود
entries.english: Suppression of anger is this: do not vomit it, so that you may gain sweet words in recompense.

entries.farsi: كظم غيظ اين است آن را قى مكن / تا بيابى در جزا شيرين سخن‏
entries.english: Since he had no patience, he was tormented. “Where,” he cried, “ is this cur, this infamous cuckold,

entries.farsi: چون نبودش صبر مى‏پيچيد او / كاين سگ زن روسپى حيز كو
entries.english: That I may pour upon him what he said, for at that time the lion of my Thought was asleep.

entries.farsi: چون عيادت بهر دل آرامى است / اين عيادت نيست دشمن كامى است‏
entries.english: Inasmuch as visiting the sick is for the purpose of tranquility, this is not a visit to the sick: it is the satisfaction of an enemy’s wish.

entries.farsi: تا ببيند دشمن خود را نزار / تا بگيرد خاطر زشتش قرار
entries.english: That he should see his enemy enfeebled and that his wicked heart should be at peace.”

entries.farsi: بس كسان كايشان ز طاعت گمره‏اند / دل به رضوان و ثواب آن دهند
entries.english: Many are they that do works of devotion and set their hearts on being approved and rewarded for the same

entries.farsi: خود حقيقت معصيت باشد خفى / بس كدر كان را تو پندارى صفى‏
entries.english: It is in truth a lurking sin: that, which he thinks pure is foul,

entries.farsi: همچو آن كر كه همى‏پنداشته ست / كو نكويى كرد و آن بر عكس جست‏
entries.english: As the deaf man, who fancied that he did a kindness, but it had the opposite result.

entries.farsi: او نشسته خوش كه خدمت كرده‏ام / حق همسايه به جا آورده‏ام‏
entries.english: He sits down well-pleased, saying, “I have paid my respects, I have performed what was due to my neighbour”;

entries.farsi: بهر خود او آتشى افروخته ست / در دل رنجور و خود را سوخته ست‏
entries.english: he has kindled a fire against himself in the invalid’s heart and burned himself.

entries.farsi: فاتقوا النار التي أوقدتم / إنكم في المعصية ازددتم‏
entries.english: Beware, then, of the fire that you have kindled: truly you have increased in sin.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر به يك صاحب ريا / صل إنك لم تصل يا فتى‏
entries.english: The Prophet said to our hypocrite, “Pray, for indeed you have not prayed, my man.”

entries.farsi: از براى چاره‏ى اين خوفها / آمد اندر هر نمازى اهدنا
entries.english: As a means of preventing these dangers, “Guide us” comes in every prayer,

entries.farsi: كاين نمازم را مياميز اى خدا / با نماز ضالين و اهل ريا
entries.english: That is to say, “O God, do not mingle my prayer with the prayer of the erring and the hypocrites.”

entries.farsi: از قياسى كه بكرد آن كر گزين / صحبت ده ساله باطل شد بدين‏
entries.english: By the analogical reasoning which the deaf man adopted a ten years’ friendship was made vain.

entries.farsi: خاصه اى خواجه قياس حس دون / اندر آن وحيى كه هست از حد فزون‏
entries.english: Especially, O master, the analogy drawn by the low senses in regard to the Revelation which is illimitable.

entries.farsi: گوش حس تو به حرف ار در خور است / دان كه گوش غيب گير تو كر است
entries.english: If your sensuous ear is fit for the letter, know that your ear that receives the occult is deaf.

sections.title_fa: اول كسى كه در مقابله‏ى نص قياس آورد ابليس بود
sections.title_en: The first to bring analogical reasoning to bear against the Revealed Text was Iblis.

entries.farsi: اول آن كس كاين قياسكها نمود / پيش انوار خدا ابليس بود
entries.english: The first person who produced these paltry analogies in the presence of the Lights of God was Iblis.

entries.farsi: گفت نار از خاك بى‏شك بهتر است / من ز نار و او ز خاك اكدر است‏
entries.english: He said, “ Beyond doubt fire is superior to earth: I am of fire, and he is of dingy earth.

entries.farsi: پس قياس فرع بر اصلش كنيم / او ز ظلمت ما ز نور روشنيم‏
entries.english: Let us, then, judge by comparing the secondary with its principal: he is of darkness, I of radiant light.”

entries.farsi: گفت حق نى بل كه ال انساب شد / زهد و تقوى فضل را محراب شد
entries.english: God said, “No, but on the contrary there shall be no lineage: asceticism and piety shall be the avenue to pre-eminence.”

entries.farsi: اين نه ميراث جهان فانى است / كه به انسابش بيابى جانى است‏
entries.english: This is not the heritage of the fleeting world, so that you should gain it by ties of relationship: it is a spiritual.

entries.farsi: بلكه اين ميراثهاى انبياست / وارث اين جانهاى اتقياست‏
entries.english: No, these things are the heritage of the prophets; the inheritors of these are the spirits of the devout.

entries.farsi: پور آن بو جهل شد مومن عيان / پور آن نوح نبى از گمرهان‏
entries.english: The son of Bu Jahl became a true believer for all to see; the son of Noah became one of those who lost the way.

entries.farsi: اين قياسات و تحرى روز ابر / يا به شب مر قبله را كرده ست حبر
entries.english: “The child of earth became illumined like the moon; you are the child of fire: leave with your black face!”

entries.farsi: زاده‏ى خاكى منور شد چو ماه / زاده‏ى آتش تويى رو رو سياه‏
entries.english: The wise man has made such reasoning and investigation on a cloudy day or at night for the sake of the qibla;

entries.farsi: ليك با خورشيد و كعبه پيش رو / اين قياس و اين تحرى را مجو
entries.english: But with the sun and with the Ka`ba before your face, do not seek to reason and investigate in this manner.

entries.farsi: كعبه ناديده مكن رو زو متاب / از قياس اللهَّ أعلم بالصواب‏
entries.english: Do not pretend that you cannot see the Ka`ba, do not avert Your face from it because you have reasoned. God knows best what is right.

entries.farsi: چون صفيرى بشنوى از مرغ حق / ظاهرش را ياد گيرى چون سبق‏
entries.english: When you hear a pipe from the Bird of God, you commit its outward to memory, like a lesson,

entries.farsi: وانگهى از خود قياساتى كنى / مر خيال محض را ذاتى كنى‏
entries.english: And then from yourself you make some analogies: you make mere fancy into a substance.

entries.farsi: اصطالحاتى است مر ابدال را / كه نباشد ز آن خبر اقوال را
entries.english: The Abdal have certain mystical expressions of which the doctrines are ignorant.

entries.farsi: منطق الطيرى به صوت آموختى / صد قياس و صد هوس افروختى‏
entries.english: You have learned the birds’ language by the sound; you have kindled a hundred analogies and a hundred caprices.

entries.farsi: همچو آن رنجور دلها از تو خست / كر به پندار اصابت گشته مست‏
entries.english: The hearts are wounded by you, as the invalid, the deaf man became intoxicated with the vain notion of success.

entries.farsi: كاتب آن وحى ز آن آواز مرغ / برده ظنى كاو بود همباز مرغ‏
entries.english: The writer of the Revelation, from the Bird’s voice, supposed that he was the Bird’s equal:

entries.farsi: مرغ پرى زد مر او را كور كرد / نك فرو بردش به قعر مرگ و درد
entries.english: The Bird flapped a wing and blinded him: lo, it plunged him in the abyss of death and bale.

entries.farsi: هين به عكسى يا به ظنى هم شما / در ميفتيد از مقامات سما
entries.english: “Beware! Do not also be beguiled by a reflection or an opinion, fall from the dignities of Heaven!

entries.farsi: گر چه هاروتيد و ماروت و فزون / از همه بر بام نحن الصافون
entries.english: Although you are Harut and Marut and superior to all on the terrace of We are they that stand in ranks,

entries.farsi: بر بديهاى بدان رحمت كنيد / بر منى و خويش بينى كم تنيد
entries.english: Take mercy on the wickednesses of the wicked: do not cling to egoism and self-conceit.

entries.farsi: هين مبادا غيرت آيد از كمين / سر نگون افتيد در قعر زمين‏
entries.english: Beware, lest jealousy comes from ambush and you fall headlong to the bottom of the earth.”

entries.farsi: هر دو گفتند اى خدا فرمان تراست / بى‏امان تو امانى خود كجاست‏
entries.english: They both said, “ O God, Yours is the command: without Your security where indeed is any security?”

entries.farsi: اين همى‏گفتند و دلشان مى‏طپيد / بد كجا آيد ز ما نعم العبيد
entries.english: They were saying that, but their hearts were throbbing. How should evil come from us? Good servants are we!”

entries.farsi: خار خار دو فرشته هم نهشت / تا كه تخم خويش بينى را نكشت‏
entries.english: The prick of desire in the two angels did not leave until it sowed the seed of self-conceit.

entries.farsi: ما بر اين گردون تتقها مى‏تنيم / بر زمين آييم و شادُروان زنيم‏
entries.english: Then they were saying, “O you that are composed of the elements unacquainted with the purity of the spiritual beings,

entries.farsi: پس همى‏گفتند كاى اركانيان / بى‏خبر از پاكى روحانيان‏
entries.english: We will draw curtains over this sky, we will come to earth and set up the canopy,

entries.farsi: عدل توزيم و عبادت آوريم / باز هر شب سوى گردون بر پريم‏
entries.english: We will deal justice and perform worship and every night we will fly up again to Heaven,

entries.farsi: تا شويم اعجوبه‏ى دور زمان / تا نهيم اندر زمين امن و امان‏
entries.english: That we may become the wonder of the world, that we may establish safety and security on the earth.”

entries.farsi: آن قياس حال گردون بر زمين / راست نايد فرق دارد در كمين‏
entries.english: The analogy between the state of Heaven and the earth is inexact: it has a concealed difference.

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه حال خود و مستى خود پنهان بايد داشت از جاهالن
sections.title_en: Explaining that one must keep one’s own state and intoxication hidden from the ignorant

entries.farsi: بشنو الفاظ حكيم پرده‏اى / سر همانجا نه كه باده خورده‏اى‏
entries.english: Hearken to the words of the Sage who lived in seclusion, “Lay your head in the same place where you have drunk the wine.”

entries.farsi: چون كه از ميخانه مستى ضال شد / تسخر و بازيچه‏ى اطفال شد
entries.english: When the drunken man has gone astray from a tavern, he becomes the children’s laughing-stock and plaything.

entries.farsi: او چنين و كودكان اندر پى‏اش / بى‏خبر از مستى و ذوق مى‏اش‏
entries.english: Whatever way he goes, he is falling in the mud, on this side and on that side, and every fool is laughing at him.

entries.farsi: مى‏فتد او سو به سو بر هر رهى / در گل و مى‏خنددش هر ابلهى‏
entries.english: He like this, while the children at his heels are without knowledge of his intoxication and the taste of his wine.

entries.farsi: خلق اطفال‏اند جز مست خدا / نيست بالغ جز رهيده از هوا
entries.english: All mankind are children except him that is intoxicated with God; none is grown-up except him that freed from sensual desire.

entries.farsi: گفت دنيا لعب و لهو است و شما / كودكيد و راست فرمايد خدا
entries.english: He said, “This world is a play and pastime, and you are children”; and God speaks truth.

entries.farsi: از لعب بيرون نرفتى كودكى / بى‏ذكات روح كى باشد ذكى‏
entries.english: If you have not gone forth from play, you are a child: without purity of spirit how will you be fully intelligent?

entries.farsi: چون جماع طفل دان اين شهوتى / كه همى‏رانند اينجا اى فتى‏
entries.english: Know, O youth, that the lust in which men are indulging here is like the sexual intercourse of children.

entries.farsi: آن جماع طفل چه بود بازيى / با جماع رستمى و غازيى‏
entries.english: What is the child’s sexual intercourse? An idle play, compared with the sexual intercourse of a Rustam and a brave champion of Islam.

entries.farsi: جنگ خلقان همچو جنگ كودكان / جمله بى‏معنى و بى‏مغز و مهان‏
entries.english: The wars of mankind are like children’s fights— all meaningless, pithless, and contemptible.

entries.farsi: جمله با شمشير چوبين جنگشان / جمله در الينفعى آهنگشان‏
entries.english: All their fights are with wooden swords, all their purposes are in futility;

entries.farsi: جمله‏شان گشته سواره بر نيى / كاين براق ماست يا دلدل پيى‏
entries.english: They all are riding on a reed-cane, saying, “This is our Buraq or mule that goes like Duldul”

entries.farsi: حامل‏اند و خود ز جهل افراشته / راكب و محمول ره پنداشته‏
entries.english: They are carrying, but in their folly they have raised themselves on high: they have fancied themselves to be riders carried along the road.

entries.farsi: باش تا روزى كه محموالن حق / اسب تازان بگذرند از نه طبق‏
entries.english: “Wait till the day when those who are borne aloft by God shall pass, galloping, beyond the nine tiers!

entries.farsi: تعرج الروح إليه و الملك / من عروج الروح يهتز الفلك‏
entries.english: “The spirit and the angels shall ascend to Him”: at the ascension of the spirit Heaven shall tremble.

entries.farsi: از حق إِنَّ الظَّ نَّ ال يُغْنِي رسيد / مركب ظن بر فلك‏ها كى دويد
entries.english: Like children, you all are riding on your skirts: you have taken hold of the corner of your skirt as a horse.

entries.farsi: همچو طفالن جمله‏تان دامن سوار / گوشه‏ى دامن‏گرفته اسب‏وار
entries.english: From God came, “Truly, opinion doth not enable to dispense”: when did the steed of opinion run to the Heavens?

entries.farsi: اغلب الظنين فى ترجيح ذا / ال تمارى الشمس فى توضيحها
entries.english: While preferring the stronger of the two opinions, do not doubt whether you see the sun when it is shining!

entries.farsi: آن گهى بينيد مركبهاى خويش / مركبى سازيده‏ايد از پاى خويش‏
entries.english: At that time behold your steeds! You have made a steed of your own foot.

entries.farsi: وهم و فكر و حس و ادراك شما / همچو نى دان مركب كودك هال
entries.english: Come, recognise that your imagination and reflection and sense-perception and apprehension are like the reed-cane on which children ride.

entries.farsi: علمهاى اهل دل حمالشان / علمهاى اهل تن احمالشان‏
entries.english: The sciences of the mystics bear them; the sciences of sensual men are burdens to them.

entries.farsi: علم چون بر دل زند يارى شود / علم چون بر تن زند بارى شود
entries.english: When knowledge strikes on the heart, it becomes a helper (yari); when knowledge strikes on the body, it becomes a burden (bari).

entries.farsi: گفت ايزد يحمل اسفاره / بار باشد علم كان نبود ز هو
entries.english: God has said, “Like an ass laden with his books”: burdensome is the knowledge that is not from Himself.

entries.farsi: علم كان نبود ز هو بى‏واسطه / آن نپايد همچو رنگ ماشطه‏
entries.english: The knowledge that is not immediately from Himself does not endure, like makeup.

entries.farsi: ليك چون اين بار را نيكو كشى / بار بر گيرند و بخشندت خوشى‏
entries.english: But when you carry this burden well, the burden will be removed and you will be given joy.

entries.farsi: هين مكش بهر هوا آن بار علم / تا ببينى در درون انبار علم‏
entries.english: Beware! Do not carry this burden of knowledge for the sake of selfish desire, so that you may ride on the smooth-paced steed of knowledge,

entries.farsi: تا كه بر رهوار علم آيى سوار / بعد از آن افتد ترا از دوش بار
entries.english: So that you may mount the smooth-paced steed of knowledge, afterwards the burden may fall from your shoulder.

entries.farsi: از هواها كى رهى بى‏جام هو / اى ز هو قانع شده با نام هو
entries.english: Flow will you be freed from selfish desires without the cup of Hu, O you who have become content with no more of Hu than the name of Hu?

entries.farsi: از صفت و ز نام چه زايد خيال / و آن خيالش هست دالل وصال‏
entries.english: From attribute and name what comes to birth? Imagination; and that imagination shows the way to union with Him.

entries.farsi: ديده‏اى دالل بى‏مدلول هيچ / تا نباشد جاده نبود غول هيچ‏
entries.english: Have you ever seen a subject that shows without an object that is shown: unless there is the road, there can never be the ghoul?

entries.farsi: هيچ نامى بى‏حقيقت ديده‏اى / يا ز گاف و الم گل گل چيده‏اى‏
entries.english: Have you ever seen a name without the reality? Or have you plucked a rose (gul ) from gaf and lam of gul?

entries.farsi: اسم خواندى رو مسمى را بجو / مه به بالا دان نه اندر آب جو
entries.english: You have pronounced the name: go, seek the thing named. Know that the moon is on high, not in the water of the stream

entries.farsi: گر ز نام و حرف خواهى بگذرى / پاك كن خود را ز خود هين يك سرى‏
entries.english: If you would pass beyond name and letter, oh, make yourself wholly purged of self.

entries.farsi: همچو آهن ز آهنى بى‏رنگ شو / در رياضت آينه‏ى بى‏زنگ شو
entries.english: Like iron, lose the ferruginous colour; become in your ascetic discipline a mirror without rust.

entries.farsi: خويش را صافى كن از اوصاف خود / تا ببينى ذات پاك صاف خود
entries.english: Make yourself pure from the attributes of self, that you may behold yours own pure untarnished essence,

entries.farsi: بينى اندر دل علوم انبيا / بى‏كتاب و بى‏معيد و اوستا
entries.english: And you will see within your heart the sciences of the prophets, without book and without preceptor and master.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه هست از امتم / كاو بود هم گوهر و هم همتم‏
entries.english: The Prophet said, “Among my people are some who are one with me in nature and aspiration:

entries.farsi: مر مرا ز آن نور بيند جانشان / كه من ايشان را همى‏بينم بدان‏
entries.english: Their spirits behold me by the same light by which I am beholding them.”

entries.farsi: بى‏صحيحين و احاديث و رواه / بلكه اندر مشرب آب حيات‏
entries.english: Without the two Sahihs and Traditions and Traditionists; no, in the place where they drink the Water of Life

entries.farsi: سر امسينا لكرديا بدان / راز اصبحنا عرابيا بخوان‏
entries.english: Know the secret of “In the evening I was a Kurd”; read the mystery of “In the morning I was an Arab.”

entries.farsi: ور مثالى خواهى از علم نهان / قصه گو از روميان و چينيان‏
entries.english: And if you desire a parable of the hidden knowledge, relate the story of the Greeks and the Chinese.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى مرى كردن روميان و چينيان در علم نقاشى و صورتگرى
sections.title_en: The story of the contention between the Rumis and the Chinese in the art of painting and picturing

entries.farsi: چينيان گفتند ما نقاش‏تر / روميان گفتند ما را كر و فر
entries.english: The Chinese said, “We are the better artists;” the Greeks said,” The power and excellence belongs to us.”

entries.farsi: گفت سلطان امتحان خواهم در اين / كز شماها كيست در دعوى گزين‏
entries.english: “I will put you to the test in this matter,” said the Sultan, “which of you are approved in your claim.”

entries.farsi: اهل چين و روم چون حاضر شدند / روميان از بحث در مكث آمدند
entries.english: The Chinese and the Greeks began to debate: the Greeks retired from the debate.

entries.farsi: چينيان گفتند يك خانه به ما / خاص بسپاريد و يك آن شما
entries.english: The Chinese said, “Hand over to us a particular room, and one for you.”

entries.farsi: بود دو خانه مقابل دربدر / ز آن يكى چينى ستد رومى دگر
entries.english: There were two rooms with door facing door: the Chinese took one, the Greeks the other.

entries.farsi: چينيان صد رنگ از شه خواستند / پس خزينه باز كرد آن ارجمند
entries.english: The Chinese requested the King to give them a hundred colours: the King opened his treasury that they might receive that.

entries.farsi: روميان گفتند نى نقش و نه رنگ / در خور آيد كار را جز دفع زنگ‏
entries.english: Every morning, by bounty, the colours were dispensed from the treasury to the Chinese.

entries.farsi: هر صباحى از خزينه رنگها / چينيان را راتبه بود از عطا
entries.english: The Greeks said, “No tints and colours are proper for our work, except to remove the rust.”

entries.farsi: از دو صد رنگى به بى‏رنگى رهى است / رنگ چون ابر است و بى‏رنگى مهى است‏
entries.english: They shut the door and went on burnishing: they became clear and pure like the sky.

entries.farsi: در فرو بستند و صيقل مى‏زدند / همچو گردون ساده و صافى شدند
entries.english: There is a way from many-colouredness to colourlessness: colour is like the clouds, and colourlessness is a moon.

entries.farsi: هر چه اندر ابر ضو بينى و تاب / آن ز اختر دان و ماه و آفتاب‏
entries.english: Whatever light and splendour you see in the clouds, know that it comes from the stars and the moon and the sun.

entries.farsi: شه در آمد ديد آن جا نقشها / مى‏ربود آن عقل را و فهم را
entries.english: When the Chinese had finished their work, they were beating drums for joy.

entries.farsi: چينيان چون از عمل فارغ شدند / از پى شادى دهلها مى‏زدند
entries.english: The King entered and saw the pictures there: that, as he encountered it, was robbing him of his wits.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن آمد به سوى روميان / پرده را بالا كشيدند از ميان‏
entries.english: After that, he came towards the Greeks: they removed the intervening curtain.

entries.farsi: عكس آن تصوير و آن كردارها / زد بر اين صافى شده ديوارها
entries.english: The reflection of those pictures and works struck upon these walls which had been made pure.

entries.farsi: هر چه آن جا ديد اينجا به نمود / ديده را از ديده خانه مى‏ربود
entries.english: All that he had seen there seemed more beautiful here: it was snatching the eye from the socket.

entries.farsi: روميان آن صوفيانند اى پدر / بى‏ز تكرار و كتاب و بى‏هنر
entries.english: The Greeks, O father, are the Sufis: without study and books and erudition,

entries.farsi: ليك صيقل كرده‏اند آن سينه‏ها / پاك از آز و حرص و بخل و كينه‏ها
entries.english: But they have burnished their breasts pure from greed and cupidity and avarice and hatreds.

entries.farsi: آن صفاى آينه وصف دل است / كاو نقوش بى‏عدد را قابل است‏
entries.english: That purity of the mirror is, beyond doubt, the heart which receives images innumerable.

entries.farsi: صورت بى‏صورت بى‏حد غيب / ز آينه‏ى دل تافت بر موسى ز جيب‏
entries.english: That Moses holds in his bosom the formless infinite form of the Unseen from the mirror of his heart.

entries.farsi: گر چه آن صورت نگنجد در فلك / نه به عرش و فرش و دريا و سمك‏
entries.english: Although that form is not contained in Heaven, nor in the empyrean nor in the sphere of the stars, nor on the Fish,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه محدود است و معدود است آن / آينه‏ى دل را نباشد حد بدان‏
entries.english: Because those are bounded and numbered: know that the mirror of the heart has no bound.

entries.farsi: عقل اينجا ساكت آمد يا مضل / ز آنكه دل با اوست يا خود اوست دل‏
entries.english: Here the understanding becomes silent or it leads into error, because the heart is with Him, or indeed the heart is He.

entries.farsi: عكس هر نقشى نتابد تا ابد / جز ز دل هم با عدد هم بى‏عدد
entries.english: The reflection of every image shines unto everlasting from the heart alone, both with plurality and without.

entries.farsi: تا ابد هر نقش نو كايد بر او / مى‏نمايد بى‏حجابى اندر او
entries.english: Unto everlasting every new image that falls on it is appearing therein without any imperfection.

entries.farsi: اهل صيقل رسته‏اند از بوى و رنگ / هر دمى بينند خوبى بى‏درنگ‏
entries.english: They that burnish have escaped from scent and colour: they behold Beauty at every moment without tarrying.

entries.farsi: نقش و قشر علم را بگذاشتند / رايت عين اليقين افراشتند
entries.english: They have relinquished the form and husk of knowledge; they have raised the banner of the eye of certainty.

entries.farsi: رفت فكر و روشنايى يافتند / نحر و بحر آشنايى يافتند
entries.english: Thought is gone, and they have gained light: they have gained the throat and the sea of gnosis.

entries.farsi: مرگ كاين جمله از او در وحشت‏اند / مى‏كنند اين قوم بر وى ريشخند
entries.english: Death, of which all these are sore afraid, this people are holding in derision.

entries.farsi: كس نيابد بر دل ايشان ظفر / بر صدف آيد ضرر نى بر گهر
entries.english: None gains the victory over their hearts: the hurt falls on the oyster-shell, not on the pearl.

entries.farsi: گر چه نحو و فقه را بگذاشتند / ليك محو و فقر را برداشتند
entries.english: Though they have let go grammar (nahw) and jurisprudence (fiqh), yet they have taken up mystical self-effacement (mahw) and spiritual poverty (faqr).

entries.farsi: تا نقوش هشت جنت تافته ست / لوح دلشان را پذيرا يافته ست
entries.english: Ever since the forms of the Eight Paradises have appeared, they have found the tablets of their hearts receptive.

entries.farsi: برترند از عرش و كرسى و خال / ساكنان مقعد صدق خدا
entries.english: A hundred impressions from the empyrean and the starry sphere and the void: what impressions? No, it is the very sight of God.

sections.title_fa: پرسيدن پيغامبر عليه السلام مر زيد را امروز چونى و چون برخاستى و جواب گفتن او كه اصبحت مومنا يا رسول الل
sections.title_en: How the Prophet, on whom be peace, asked Zayd, “How are you to-day and in what state have you risen?” and how Zayd answered him, saying,
“This morning I am a true believer, OMessenger of Allah.”

entries.farsi: گفت عبدا مومنا باز اوش گفت / كو نشان از باغ ايمان گر شگفت‏
entries.english: One morning the Prophet said to Zayd, “How are you this morning, O sincere Companion?”

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر صباحى زيد را / كيف اصبحت اى رفيق با صفا
entries.english: He replied, “a faithful servant of God.” Again he said to him, “Where is your token from the garden of Faith, if it has bloomed?”

entries.farsi: گفت تشنه بوده‏ام من روزها / شب نخفته ستم ز عشق و سوزها
entries.english: He said, “I have thirsted in the daytime, at night I have not slept because of love and burning griefs,

entries.farsi: تا ز روز و شب گذر كردم چنان / كه از اسپر بگذرد نوك سنان‏
entries.english: So that I passed through day and night, as the point of the spear passes through the shield;

entries.farsi: كه از آن سو جمله‏ى ملت يكى ست / صد هزاران سال و يك ساعت يكى ست‏
entries.english: For beyond, nativity and continued growth are one: hundreds of thousands of years are the same as a single hour.

entries.farsi: هست ازل را و ابد را اتحاد / عقل را ره نيست آن سو ز افتقاد
entries.english: Everlastingness and eternity are unified: the understanding has no way beyond reason by means of intellect.”

entries.farsi: گفت از اين ره كو رهاوردى بيار / در خور فهم و عقول اين ديار
entries.english: The Prophet said, “Where is the traveller’s gift from this journey? Produce it. Where is the token of sincerity from that fair land?”

entries.farsi: گفت خلقان چون ببينند آسمان / من ببينم عرش را با عرشيان‏
entries.english: Zayd said, “When people see the sky, I behold the Empyrean with those who dwell there.

entries.farsi: هشت جنت هفت دوزخ پيش من / هست پيدا همچو بت پيش شمن‏
entries.english: The Eight Paradises and the Seven Hells are as visible to me as the idol to the idolater.

entries.farsi: يك به يك وامى‏شناسم خلق را / همچو گندم من ز جو در آسيا
entries.english: I am distinguishing the people, one by one, like wheat from barley in the mill,

entries.farsi: كه بهشتى كيست و بيگانه كى است / پيش من پيدا چو مار و ماهى است‏
entries.english: So that who is for Paradise and who shall be a stranger is as clear to me as snake and fish.”

entries.farsi: اين زمان پيدا شده بر اين گروه / يوم تبيض و تسود وجوه‏
entries.english: The day of birth for Anatolians and Ethiopians and every race is the Day when faces shall become white or black.

entries.farsi: پيش از اين هر چند جان پر عيب بود / در رحم بود و ز خلقان غيب بود
entries.english: Before this, however sinful the spirit was, it was in the womb and was hidden from the people.

entries.farsi: الشقى من شقى فى بطن الام / من سمات الجسم يعرف حالهم‏
entries.english: The damned are they that are damned in the mother’s womb: all of them are known by the marks of God.

entries.farsi: تن چو مادر طفل جان را حامله / مرگ درد زادن است و زلزله‏
entries.english: The body, like a mother, is big with the spirit-child: death is the pangs and throes of birth.

entries.farsi: جمله جانهاى گذشته منتظر / تا چگونه زايد آن جان بطر
entries.english: All the spirits that have passed over are waiting to see in what state that proud spirit shall be horn.

entries.farsi: زنگيان گويند خود از ماست او / روميان گويند بس زيباست او
entries.english: The Ethiopians say, “It belongs to us”; the Anatolians say, “No, it is comely.”

entries.farsi: چون بزايد در جهان جان و جود / پس نماند اختالف بيض و سود
entries.english: As soon as it is born into the world of spirit and grace, there is no further difference between the whites and the blacks.

entries.farsi: گر بود زنگى برندش زنگيان / روم را رومى برد هم از ميان‏
entries.english: If it is an Ethiopian, the Ethiopians carry it off; and if it is an Anatolian, the Anatolians lead it away.

entries.farsi: تا نزاد او مشكالت عالم است / آن كه نازاده شناسد او كم است‏
entries.english: Until it is born, it is a riddle for the world: few are they that know the unborn.

entries.farsi: او مگر ينظر بنور اهلل بود / كاندرون پوست او را ره بود
entries.english: Such a one surely is seeing by the light of God, for he has the way within the skin.

entries.farsi: اصل آب نطفه اسپيد است و خوش / ليك عكس جان رومى و حبش‏
entries.english: The fundamental of embryo’s water is white and enchanting, but the reflection of the spirit, Anatolian or Ethiopian,

entries.farsi: مى‏دهد رنگ احسن التقويم را / تا به اسفل مى‏برد اين نيم را
entries.english: Is giving colour to those who are most excellent in their constitution, it is bearing this half down to the lowest depth.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد باز ران / تا نمانيم از قطار كاروان‏
entries.english: This discourse has no end. Hurry back, that we may not be left behind by the caravan’s file of camels.

entries.farsi: يوم تبيض و تسود وجوه / ترك و هندو شهره گردد ز آن گروه‏
entries.english: On the Day when faces shall become white or black, by whom shall reverence still be paid to Turk and Hindu?

entries.farsi: در رحم پيدا نباشد هند و ترك / چون كه زايد بيندش زار و سترگ‏
entries.english: In the womb Hindu and Turk are not distinguishable, when each is born he sees that each is miserable or glorious.

entries.farsi: جمله را چون روز رستاخيز من / فاش مى‏بينم عيان از مرد و زن‏
entries.english: “I am seeing them all plainly, as on the Day of Resurrection, like people, men and women.

entries.farsi: هين بگويم يا فرو بندم نفس / لب گزيدش مصطفى يعنى كه بس‏
entries.english: Listen, shall I tell or shall I stop my breath?” Mustafa bit his lip, as though to say, “Enough!”

entries.farsi: يا رسول اللهَّ بگويم سر حشر / در جهان پيدا كنم امروز نشر
entries.english: “O Messenger of Allah, shall I tell the mystery of the Gathering, shall I make the Resurrection manifest in the world to-day?

entries.farsi: هل مرا تا پرده‏ها را بر درم / تا چو خورشيدى بتابد گوهرم‏
entries.english: Let me be, that I may rend the curtains asunder, that my substance may shine forth like a sun;

entries.farsi: تا كسوف آيد ز من خورشيد را / تا نمايم نخل را و بيد را
entries.english: That the sun may be eclipsed by me, that I may show the date-palm and the willow.

entries.farsi: وا نمايم راز رستاخيز را / نقد را و نقد قلب آميز را
entries.english: I will show forth the mystery of Resurrection, the sterling coin and the coin mixed with alloy,

entries.farsi: دستها ببريده اصحاب شمال / وانمايم رنگ كفر و رنگ آل‏
entries.english: The people of the left with their hands cut off; I will show forth the colour of infidelity and the colour of the folk.

entries.farsi: واگشايم هفت سوراخ نفاق / در ضياى ماه بى‏خسف و محاق‏
entries.english: I will lay bare the seven rifts of hypocrisy in the light of the moon that suffers no eclipse or waning.

entries.farsi: وانمايم من پالس اشقيا / بشنوانم طبل و كوس انبيا
entries.english: I will display the woollen frocks of the damned, I will cause the drums and kettle-drums of the prophets to be heard.

entries.farsi: دوزخ و جنات و برزخ در ميان / پيش چشم كافران آرم عيان‏
entries.english: Hell and the Gardens and the intermediate state I will bring clearly before the eyes of the infidels.

entries.farsi: وانمايم حوض كوثر را به جوش / كآب بر روشان زند بانگش به گوش‏
entries.english: I will display the pond of Kawthar heaving, which dashes water on their faces, its sound in their ears;

entries.farsi: و آن كسان كه تشنه بر گردش دوان / گشته‏اند اين دم نمايم من عيان‏
entries.english: And those who are running athirst round Kawthar I will name one by one who they are:

entries.farsi: مى‏بسايد دوششان بر دوش من / نعره‏هاشان مى‏رسد در گوش من‏
entries.english: Their shoulders are rubbing against my shoulder, their cries are piercing my ears.

entries.farsi: اهل جنت پيش چشمم ز اختيار / در كشيده يكدگر را در كنار
entries.english: Before my eyes the people of Paradise, from free choice, clasp each other to their bosoms,

entries.farsi: دست همديگر زيارت مى‏كنند / از لبان هم بوسه غارت مى‏كنند
entries.english: Visiting one another’s high places of honour and snatching kisses from the lips.

entries.farsi: كر شد اين گوشم ز بانگ آه آه / از خسان و نعره‏ى وا حسرتاه‏
entries.english: This ear of mine has become deafened by the cries of ‘Alas, Alas!’ by the vile wretches and by the screams of ‘O sorrow!’

entries.farsi: اين اشارتهاست گويم از نغول / ليك مى‏ترسم ز آزار رسول‏
entries.english: These are hints. I would speak from the depth, but I fear to offend the Messenger.”

entries.farsi: همچنين مى‏گفت سر مست و خراب / داد پيغمبر گريبانش به تاب‏
entries.english: He was speaking in this wise, intoxicated and distraught: the Prophet twitched his collar

entries.farsi: گفت هين در كش كه اسبت گرم شد / عكس حق ال يَسْ تَحْ يِي زد شرم شد
entries.english: And said,” Beware ! Draw, for your horse has become hot. The reflection of God is not ashamed strikes, shame is gone.

entries.farsi: آينه‏ى تو جست بيرون از غالف / آينه و ميزان كجا گويد خالف‏
entries.english: Your mirror has shot out of the case: how shall mirror and balance speak falsehood?

entries.farsi: آينه و ميزان كجا بندد نفس / بهر آزار و حياى هيچ كس‏
entries.english: How shall mirror and balance stop their breath for fear of hurting and shaming any one?

entries.farsi: آينه و ميزان محكهاى سنى / گر دو صد سالش تو خدمتها كنى‏
entries.english: Mirror and balance are noble touchstones: if you do service to them for two hundred years,

entries.farsi: كز براى من بپوشان راستى / بر فزون بنما و منما كاستى‏
entries.english: Saving, ‘Conceal the truth for my sake, display the surplus and do not display the deficiency,’

entries.farsi: اوت گويد ريش و سبلت بر مخند / آينه و ميزان و آن گه ريو و پند
entries.english: They will say to you, do not laugh at your beard and moustache: mirror and balance, and then deceit and trickery!’

entries.farsi: چون خدا ما را براى آن فراخت / كه به ما بتوان حقيقت را شناخت‏
entries.english: Since God has raised us up in order that by means of us it may be possible to know the truth,

entries.farsi: اين نباشد ما چه ارزيم اى جوان / كى شويم آيين روى نيكوان‏
entries.english: If this does not happen, what worth have we, O young man? How shall we become a standard for the face of the fair?’

entries.farsi: ليك در كش در نمد آيينه را / گر تجلى كرد سينا سينه را
entries.english: But slip the mirror into the cloth, if illumination has made your breast a Sinai.”

entries.farsi: هم دغل را هم بغل را بر درد / نه جنون ماند به پيشش نه خرد
entries.english: He said, “Why, shall the Sun of the Truth and the Sun of Eternity be contained any wise under the armpit?

entries.farsi: گفت آخر هيچ گنجد در بغل / آفتاب حق و خورشيد ازل‏
entries.english: It bursts asunder both armpit (baghal) and imposture (daghal); in its presence neither madness nor understanding remains.”

entries.farsi: گفت يك اصبع چو بر چشمى نهى / بيند از خورشيد عالم را تهى‏
entries.english: He (the Prophet) said, “When you lay one finger on an eye, you see the world empty of the sun.

entries.farsi: يك سر انگشت پرده‏ى ماه شد / وين نشان ساترى اهلل شد
entries.english: One finger-tip becomes a veil over the moon— and this is a symbol of God’s covering—

entries.farsi: تا بپوشاند جهان را نقطه‏اى / مهر گردد منكسف از سقطه‏اى‏
entries.english: So that the world may be covered by a single point, and the sun be eclipsed by a splinter.”

entries.farsi: لب ببند و غور دريايى نگر / بحر را حق كرد محكوم بشر
entries.english: Close your lips and gaze on the depth of the sea: God made the sea subject to man,

entries.farsi: همچو چشمه‏ى سلسبيل و زنجبيل / هست در حكم بهشتى جليل‏
entries.english: Even as the fountains of Salsabil and Zanjabil are under the control of the exalted ones of Paradise.

entries.farsi: چار جوى جنت اندر حكم ماست / اين نه زور ما ز فرمان خداست‏
entries.english: The four rivers of Paradise are under our control; this is not our might, it is the command of God:

entries.farsi: هر كجا خواهيم داريمش روان / همچو سحر اندر مراد ساحران‏
entries.english: We keep them flowing wherever we will, like magic according to the desire of the magicians,

entries.farsi: همچو اين دو چشمه‏ى چشم روان / هست در حكم دل و فرمان جان‏
entries.english: Just as these two flowing eye-fountains are under the control of the heart and subject to the command of the spirit.

entries.farsi: گر بخواهد رفت سوى زهر و مار / ور بخواهد رفت سوى اعتبار
entries.english: If it will, they turn towards poison and the snake, and if it will, they turn to consideration.

entries.farsi: گر بخواهد سوى محسوسات رفت / ور بخواهد سوى ملبوسات رفت‏
entries.english: If it will, they turn to sensuous things, and if it will, they turn to things clothed.

entries.farsi: گر بخواهد سوى كليات راند / ور بخواهد حبس جزويات ماند
entries.english: If it will, they advance towards universals, and if it will, they remain turned towards particulars.

entries.farsi: همچنين هر پنج حس چون نايزه / بر مراد و امر دل شد جايزه‏
entries.english: Similarly all the five senses are passing according to the will and command of the heart, like the drainage pipe.

entries.farsi: هر طرف كه دل اشارت كردشان / مى‏رود هر پنج حس دامن كشان‏
entries.english: All the five senses are moving and trailing their skirts in whatever direction the heart indicates to them.

entries.farsi: دست و پا در امر دل اندر مال / همچو اندر دست موسى آن عصا
entries.english: Hand and foot are plainly under command of the heart, like the staff in the hand of Moses.

entries.farsi: دل بخواهد پا در آيد زو به رقص / يا گريزد سوى افزونى ز نقص‏
entries.english: If the heart will, at once the foot begins to dance, or flees from defect towards increase.

entries.farsi: دل بخواهد دست آيد در حساب / با اصابع تا نويسد او كتاب‏
entries.english: If the heart will, the hand comes to terms with the fingers to write a book.

entries.farsi: دست در دست نهانى مانده است / او درون تن را برون بنشانده است‏
entries.english: The hand remains in a hidden hand: it within has set the body outside.

entries.farsi: گر بخواهد بر عدو مارى شود / ور بخواهد بر ولى يارى شود
entries.english: If it will, it becomes a snake to the enemy; and if it will, it becomes a helper to the friend;

entries.farsi: ور بخواهد كفچه‏اى در خوردنى / ور بخواهد همچو گرز ده منى‏
entries.english: And if it wills, a spoon in food; and if it will, a mace weighing a ton.

entries.farsi: دل چه مى‏گويد بديشان اى عجب / طرفه وصلت طرفه پنهانى سبب‏
entries.english: I wonder what the heart is saying to them. It is a marvellous connection, a marvellous hidden link.

entries.farsi: دل مگر مهر سليمان يافته ست / كه مهار پنج حس بر تافته ست‏
entries.english: Surely the heart has gotten the seal of Solomon, so that it has pulled the reins of the five senses.

entries.farsi: پنج حسى از برون ميسور او / پنج حسى از درون مأمور او
entries.english: Five external senses are easy for it to manage; five internal senses are under its command.

entries.farsi: ده حس است و هفت اندام و دگر / آن چه اندر گفت نايد مى‏شمر
entries.english: There are ten senses and seven limbs et cetera: count over what is not mentioned.

entries.farsi: چون سليمانى دال در مهترى / بر پرى و ديو زن انگشترى‏
entries.english: O heart, since you are a Solomon in empire, cast your seal-ring upon peri and demon.

entries.farsi: گر در اين ملكت برى باشى ز ريو / خاتم از دست تو نستاند سه ديو
entries.english: If in this kingdom you art free from deceit, the three demons will not take the seal out of your hand;

entries.farsi: بعد از آن عالم بگيرد اسم تو / دو جهان محكوم تو چون جسم تو
entries.english: After that, your name will conquer the world: the two worlds ruled by you like your body.

entries.farsi: ور ز دستت ديو خاتم را ببرد / پادشاهى فوت شد بختت بمرد
entries.english: And if the demon takes the seal off your hand, your kingdom is past, your fortune is dead;

entries.farsi: بعد از آن يا حسرتا شد يا عباد / بر شما محتوم تا يوم التناد
entries.english: After that, O servants, “O sorrow!” is your inevitable doom till the day when ye are gathered together.

entries.farsi: مكر خود را گر تو انكار آورى / از ترازو و آينه كى جان برى‏
entries.english: And if you are denying your deceit, how will you save your soul from the scales and the mirror?

sections.title_fa: متهم كردن غالمان و خواجه‏تاشان مر لقمان را كه آن ميوه‏هاى ترونده كه مى‏آورديم او خورده است‏
sections.title_en: How suspicion was thrown upon Luqman by the slaves and fellow-servants who said that he had eaten the fresh fruit which they were bringing.

entries.farsi: بود لقمان پيش خواجه‏ى خويشتن / در ميان بندگانش خوار تن
entries.english: In the eyes of his master, amongst the slaves, Luqman was despicable on account of his body.

entries.farsi: مى‏فرستاد او غالمان را به باغ / تا كه ميوه آيدش بهر فراغ‏
entries.english: He used to send the slaves to the garden, that fruit might come for his pleasure.

entries.farsi: بود لقمان در غالمان چون طفيل / پر معانى تيره صورت همچو ليل‏
entries.english: Amongst the slaves Luqman was like a parasite; full of ideas, dark-complexioned as night.

entries.farsi: آن غالمان ميوه‏هاى جمع را / خوش بخوردند از نهيب طمع را
entries.english: Those slaves, being impelled by greed, ate the whole of the fruit with enjoyment,

entries.farsi: خواجه را گفتند لقمان خورد آن / خواجه بر لقمان ترش گشت و گران‏
entries.english: And told their master that Luqman had eaten it, the master became bitter and sorely displeased with Luqman.

entries.farsi: چون تفحص كرد لقمان از سبب / در عتاب خواجه‏اش بگشاد لب‏
entries.english: When Luqman inquired the cause, he opened his lips to reproach his master.

entries.farsi: گفت لقمان سيدا پيش خدا / بنده‏ى خاين نباشد مرتضا
entries.english: “O sir,” said Luqman, “an unfaithful servant is not approved in the sight of God.

entries.farsi: امتحان كن جمله‏مان را اى كريم / سيرمان در ده تو از آب حميم‏
entries.english: Put us all to the test, O noble sir: give us our fill of hot water

entries.farsi: بعد از آن ما را به صحرايى كالن / تو سواره ما پياده مى‏دوان‏
entries.english: And afterwards make us run into a great plain, you being mounted and we on foot.

entries.farsi: آن گهان بنگر تو بد كردار را / صنعهاى كاشف الاسرار را
entries.english: Then behold the evil-doer, the things that are done by Him who revealeth mysteries!”

entries.farsi: گشت ساقى خواجه از آب حميم / مر غالمان را و خوردند آن ز بيم‏
entries.english: The master gave the servants hot water to drink, and they drank it in fear.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن مى‏راندشان در دشتها / مى‏دويدندى ميان كشتها
entries.english: Afterwards he was driving them into the plains, and they were running amidst the wheat fields.

entries.farsi: قى در افتادند ايشان از عنا / آب مى‏آورد ز يشان ميوه‏ها
entries.english: From distress they began to vomit: the water was bringing up the fruit from them.

entries.farsi: چون كه لقمان را در آمد قى ز ناف / مى‏برآمد از درونش آب صاف‏
entries.english: When Luqman began to vomit from his navel, there was coming up from within him the pure water.

entries.farsi: حكمت لقمان چو داند اين نمود / پس چه باشد حكمت رب الوجود
entries.english: Inasmuch as Luqman’s wisdom can show forth this, then what must be the wisdom of the Lord of existence !

entries.farsi: يَوْمَ تُبْلَى، السَّرائِرُ كلها / بان منكم كامن ال يشتهى
entries.english: On the day when all the inmost thoughts shall be searched out, there will appear from you something latent, which is not desired.

entries.farsi: چون سُقُوا ماءً حَمِيماً قطعت / جملة األستار مما أفظعت‏
entries.english: When they shall be given hot water to drink, all the veils will be cut asunder from that which is abhorred.

entries.farsi: نار از آن آمد عذاب كافران / كه حجر را نار باشد امتحان‏
entries.english: The fire is made the torment of the infidels because fire is the test for stones.

entries.farsi: آن دل چون سنگ را ما چند چند / نرم گفتيم و نمى‏پذرفت پند
entries.english: How often, how often, have we spoken gently to our stony hearts, and they would not accept the counsel!

entries.farsi: ريش بد را داروى بد يافت رگ / مر سر خر را سزد دندان سگ‏
entries.english: For a bad wound the vein gets a bad remedy: the teeth of the dog are suitable for the donkey’s head.

entries.farsi: الخبيثات الخبيثين حكمت است / زشت را هم زشت جفت و بابت است‏
entries.english: “The wicked women to the wicked men” is wisdom: the ugly is the mate and fitting for the ugly.

entries.farsi: پس تو هر جفتى كه مى‏خواهى برو / محو و هم شكل و صفات او بشو
entries.english: Whatever, then, you wish to mate with, go, become absorbed in the loved, and assume its shape and qualities.

entries.farsi: نور خواهى مستعد نور شو / دور خواهى خويش بين و دور شو
entries.english: If you wish for the light, make yourself ready to receive the light; if you wish to be far, become self-conceited and far;

entries.farsi: ور رهى خواهى ازين سجن خرب / سر مكش از دوست وَ اسْ جُدْ وَ اقترب‏
entries.english: And if you wish a way out of this ruined prison, do not turn your head away from the Beloved, but bow in worship and draw nigh.

sections.title_fa: بقيه‏ى قصه‏ى زيد در جواب رسول عليه السلام‏
sections.title_en: The remainder of the story of Zayd in answer to the Prophet, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد خيز زيد / بر براق ناطقه بر بند قيد
entries.english: This discourse has no end. “Arise, O Zayd, and tie a shackle on the Buraq of your rational spirit.

entries.farsi: غيب مطلوب حق آمد چند گاه / اين دهل‏زن را بران بر بند راه‏
entries.english: Since the rational spirit exposes faults, it is rending the curtains of concealment.

entries.farsi: ناطقه چون فاضح آمد عيب را / مى‏دراند پرده‏هاى غيب را
entries.english: Concealment is desired by God for awhile. Drive away this drummer, bar the road!

entries.farsi: تك مران در كش عنان مستور به / هر كس از پندار خود مسرور به‏
entries.english: Do not gallop, draw rein, it is better your spirit should be veiled; it is better that every one should be gladdened by his own fancy.

entries.farsi: حق همى‏خواهد كه نوميدان او / زين عبادت هم نگردانند رو
entries.english: God is wishing that even His despairing ones should not avert their faces from this worship.

entries.farsi: هم به اوميدى مشرف مى‏شوند / چند روزى در ركابش مى‏دوند
entries.english: Even on the ground of a hope they become ennobled: for a few days they are running at its stirrup.

entries.farsi: خواهد آن رحمت بتابد بر همه / بر بد و نيك از عموم مرحمه‏
entries.english: He wishes that that mercy should shine upon all, on the evil and the good, because of the universality of His mercy

entries.farsi: حق همى‏خواهد كه هر مير و اسير / با رجا و خوف باشند و حذير
entries.english: God is wishing that every prince and captive should be hopeful and fearful.

entries.farsi: اين رجا و خوف در پرده بود / تا پس اين پرده پرورده شود
entries.english: This hope and fear are in the veil, that they may be fostered behind this veil.

entries.farsi: چون دريدى پرده كو خوف و رجا / غيب را شد كر و فرى بر مال
entries.english: When you have rent the veil, where are fear and hope? Might and majesty and testing belong to the unseen.”

entries.farsi: بر لب جو برد ظنى يك فتا / كه سليمان است ماهى‏گير ما
entries.english: A young man on the bank of a river thought, “Our fisherman is Solomon.

entries.farsi: گر وى است اين از چه فرد است و خفى است / ور نه سيماى سليمانيش چيست‏
entries.english: If this is he, why is he alone and disguised? And if not, why has he the aspect of Solomon?”

entries.farsi: اندر اين انديشه مى‏بود او دو دل / تا سليمان گشت شاه و مستقل‏
entries.english: Thus thinking, he remained in two minds until Solomon became king and absolute ruler.

entries.farsi: ديو رفت از ملك و تخت او گريخت / تيغ بختش خون آن شيطان بريخت‏
entries.english: The demon departed and fled from his kingdom and throne: the sword of his fortune shed that devil’s blood.

entries.farsi: كرد در انگشت خود انگشترى / جمع آمد لشكر ديو و پرى‏
entries.english: He put the ring upon his finger, the hosts of demons and spirits assembled.

entries.farsi: آمدند از بهر نظاره رجال / در ميانشان آن كه بد صاحب خيال‏
entries.english: The men came to look, amongst them he who had the fancy.

entries.farsi: چون در انگشتش بديد انگشترى / رفت انديشه و تحرى يك سرى‏
entries.english: When he opened his hand and saw the ring, his pondering and seeking vanished all at once.

entries.farsi: وهم آن گاه است كان پوشيده است / اين تحرى از پى ناديده است‏
entries.english: Anxiety occurs at the time when that is hidden: this searching is after the unseen.

entries.farsi: شد خيال غايب اندر سينه زفت / چون كه حاضر شد خيال او برفت‏
entries.english: While he was absent, worry waxed strong in his breast: as soon as he was present, his imaginings departed.

entries.farsi: گر سماى نور بى‏باريده نيست / هم زمين تار بى‏باليده نيست‏
entries.english: If the radiant sky is not without rain, neither is the dark earth without vegetation.

entries.farsi: يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْغَيْبِ مى‏بايد مرا / ز آن ببستم روزن فانى سرا
entries.english: “I want they believe in the unseen: on that account I have shut the window of the fleeting world.

entries.farsi: چون شكافم آسمان را در ظهور / چون بگويم هل ترى فيها فطور
entries.english: If I open its window as on the day of the trump, how should I say, ‘do you see any clefts therein?’”

entries.farsi: تا در اين ظلمت تحرى گسترند / هر كسى رو جانبى مى‏آورند
entries.english: In order that in this darkness they may make endeavours, they are turning, every one, and their faces in some direction.

entries.farsi: مدتى معكوس باشد كارها / شحنه را دزد آورد بر دارها
entries.english: For awhile things are reversed: the thief brings the magistrate to the gallows,

entries.farsi: تا كه بس سلطان و عالى همتى / بنده‏ى بنده‏ى خود آيد مدتى‏
entries.english: So that many a sultan and man of lofty spirit becomes the slave of his own slave for awhile.

entries.farsi: بندگى در غيب آيد خوب و گش / حفظ غيب آيد در استعباد خوش‏
entries.english: Service in absence is fair and comely; when service is demanded, it is pleasing that the absent should be remembered.

entries.farsi: كو كه مدح شاه گويد پيش او / تا كه در غيبت بود او شرم رو
entries.english: Where is one that praises the king in his presence, compared with one that is shamefaced in absence?

entries.farsi: قلعه دارى كز كنار مملكت / دور از سلطان و سايه‏ى سلطنت‏
entries.english: The governor of a fortress who, on the border of the kingdom, far from the sultan and the shadow of the sultanate,

entries.farsi: پاس دارد قلعه را از دشمنان / قلعه نفروشد به مال بى‏كران‏
entries.english: Guards the fortress from enemies and will not sell it for boundless riches,

entries.farsi: غايب از شه در كنار ثغرها / همچو حاضر او نگه دارد وفا
entries.english: Who, Though absent from the king on the outskirt of the frontiers, keeps faith like one who is present—

entries.farsi: پيش شه او به بود از ديگران / كه به خدمت حاضرند و جان فشان‏
entries.english: He in the king’s sight is better than the rest who are serving in his presence and ready to devote their lives.

entries.farsi: پس به غيبت نيم ذره‏ى حفظ كار / به كه اندر حاضرى ز آن صد هزار
entries.english: Therefore half an atom of regard to one’s duty in absence is better than a hundred-thousand fold observance thereof in presence.

entries.farsi: طاعت و ايمان كنون محمود شد / بعد مرگ اندر عيان مردود شد
entries.english: Obedience and faith are praiseworthy now; after death, when all is plainly shown, they will be spurned.

entries.farsi: چون كه غيب و غايب و رو پوش به / پس لبان بر بند لب خاموش به‏
entries.english: “Inasmuch as the unseen and the absent and the veil is better, close your mouth: it is better for us to be silent.

entries.farsi: اى برادر دست وا دار از سخن / خود خدا پيدا كند علم لدن‏
entries.english: O brother, refrain from speech: God himself will make manifest the knowledge that is with Him.

entries.farsi: بس بود خورشيد را رويش گواه / أَي شي‏ء أعظم الشاهد إله‏
entries.english: Witness enough for the sun is its face: what thing is the greatest witness? God.”

entries.farsi: نه بگويم چون قرين شد در بيان / هم خدا و هم ملك هم عالمان‏
entries.english: “No, speak I will, since both God and the angels and the men of learning are allied in setting forth.

entries.farsi: يشهد اللهَّ و الملك و اهل العلوم / إنه ال رب إال من يدوم‏
entries.english: God and the angels and those learned in the sciences bear witness that there is no Lord except Him who endureth for ever.”

entries.farsi: چون گواهى داد حق كه بود ملك / تا شود اندر گواهى مشترك‏
entries.english: Since God has given testimony, who are the angels, that they should be associated in the testimony?

entries.farsi: ز آن كه شعشاع حضور آفتاب / بر نتابد چشم و دلهاى خراب‏
entries.english: Because unsound eyes and hearts cannot support the radiance and testimony of the Sun,

entries.farsi: چون خفاشى كاو تف خورشيد را / بر نتابد بگسلد اوميد را
entries.english: Like a bat, which cannot bear the glow of the sun and abandons hope.

entries.farsi: پس ماليك را چو ما هم يار دان / جلوه گر خورشيد را بر آسمان‏
entries.english: Know, then, that the angels, as we also, are helpers— displayers of the sun in heaven—

entries.farsi: كاين ضيا ما ز آفتابى يافتيم / چون خليفه بر ضعيفان تافتيم‏
entries.english: Who say, “We have derived light from a Sun, we have shone upon the weak, like vicegerents.”

entries.farsi: چون مه نو يا سه روزه يا كه بدر / مرتبه‏ى هر يك ملك در نور و قدر
