entries.english: The new moon or the moon seven days old or the full moon, every angel has his rank in respect of light and worth.

entries.farsi: ز اجنحه‏ى نور ثالث او رباع / بر مراتب هر ملك را آن شعاع‏
entries.english: Every angel, according to their degrees, has that radiance, consisting of three or four luminous wings,

entries.farsi: همچو پرهاى عقول انسيان / كه بسى فرق است شان اندر ميان‏
entries.english: Just as the wings of human intellects, amongst which there is great difference.

entries.farsi: پس قرين هر بشر در نيك و بد / آن ملك باشد كه مانندش بود
entries.english: Hence the associate of every human being in good and evil is that angel whose dignity is corresponding to his or hers.

entries.farsi: چشم اعمش چون كه خور را بر نتافت / اختر او را شمع شد تا ره بيافت‏
entries.english: The stars shine, for the sake of guidance, upon the dim-sighted man who cannot bear even the moon.

sections.title_fa: گفتن پيغامبر عليه السلام مر زيد را كه اين سر را فاش تر از اين مگو و متابعت نگاه دار
sections.title_en: How the Prophet, on whom be peace, said to Zayd, “Do not tell this mystery more plainly than this, and take care to comply.”

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه اصحابى نجوم / رهروان را شمع و شيطان را رجوم‏
entries.english: The Prophet said, “My Companions are the stars, a candle to travellers, and meteors to be cast at the devils.”

entries.farsi: كى ستاره حاجت استى اى ذليل / كه بدى بر نور خورشيد او دليل‏
entries.english: If every one had the eye and the strength to receive light from the sun of heaven,

entries.farsi: هر كسى را گر بدى آن چشم و زور / كاو گرفتى ز آفتاب چرخ نور
entries.english: No moon and stars would be needed to serve as witnesses to a sun.

entries.farsi: ماه مى‏گويد به خاك و ابر و فى / من بشر بودم ولى يوحى الى‏
entries.english: The Moon is saying to earth and cloud and shadow, “I am a man, I am one like you, it is revealed to me.

entries.farsi: چون شما تاريك بودم در نهاد / وحى خورشيدم چنين نورى بداد
entries.english: Like you, I was dark in my nature: the Sun’s revelation gave me such a light as this.

entries.farsi: ظلمتى دارم به نسبت با شموس / نور دارم بهر ظلمات نفوس‏
entries.english: I have limited light in comparison with the suns; I have light for the darkness of the population.

entries.farsi: ز آن ضعيفم تا تو تابى آورى / كه نه مرد آفتاب انورى‏
entries.english: I am faint in order that you may be able to bear, for you are not man enough to bear the most radiant Sun.

entries.farsi: همچو شهد و سركه در هم بافتم / تا سوى رنج جگر ره يافتم‏
entries.english: I was woven together, like honey and vinegar, that I might find the way to sickness of heart.

entries.farsi: چون ز علت وارهيدى اى رهين / سركه را بگذار و مى‏خور انگبين‏
entries.english: Since you have recovered from yours illness, O you, who are in thrall, leave the vinegar and continue to eat the honey.”

entries.farsi: تخت دل معمور شد پاك از هوا / بين كه الرَّحْ منُ عَلَى الْعَرْشِ استوى‏
entries.english: The throne of the heart has become restored to soundness and purged of sensuality, thereon the Merciful God is seated on His Throne.

entries.farsi: حكم بر دل بعد از اين بى‏واسطه / حق كند چون يافت دل اين رابطه‏
entries.english: After this, God controls the heart without intermediary, since the heart has attained to this relation.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد زيد كو / تا دهم پندش كه رسوايى مجو
entries.english: This discourse has no end. Where is Zayd, that I may counsel him not to seek notoriety?

sections.title_fa: رجوع به حكايت زيد
sections.title_en: The return to the story of Zayd

entries.farsi: زيد را اكنون نيابى كاو گريخت / جست از صف نعال و نعل ريخت‏
entries.english: You will not find Zayd now, for he has fled: he has darted away from the shoe-row and dropped his shoes.

entries.farsi: تو كه باشى زيد هم خود را نيافت / همچو اختر كه بر او خورشيد تافت‏
entries.english: Who are you? Zayd cannot even find himself, like the star on which the sun shone.

entries.farsi: نى از او نقشى بيابى نى نشان / نى كهى يابى نه راه كهكشان‏
entries.english: You will find neither mark nor trace of him; you will not find a straw in the straw-strewn Way.

entries.farsi: حسها و عقلهاشان در درون / موج در موج لَدَيْنا محضرون‏
entries.english: Our senses and finite speech are obliterated in the knowledge and wisdom of our King.

entries.farsi: شد حواس و نطق با پايان ما / محو نور دانش سلطان ما
entries.english: Their senses and understandings within are, wave on wave, in they are assembled before Us.

entries.farsi: چون شب آمد باز وقت بار شد / انجم پنهان شده بر كار شد
entries.english: When night comes, it is again the time of the burden: the stars, which had become hidden, go to work.

entries.farsi: بى‏هشان را وادهد حق هوشها / حلقه حلقه حلقه‏ها در گوشها
entries.english: God gives back to the senseless ones their senses: troop after troop, with rings in their ears,

entries.farsi: آن جلود و آن عظام ريخته / فارسان گشته غبار انگيخته‏
entries.english: Dancing, waving their hands in praise, triumphing, “O Lord, You have brought us to life.”

entries.farsi: پاى كوبان دست افشان در ثنا / ناز نازان ربنا أحييتنا
entries.english: Those crumbled skins and bones have become horsemen and have raised the dust:

entries.farsi: ‏ حمله آرند از عدم سوى وجود / در قيامت هم شكور و هم كنود
entries.english: At Resurrection both the thankful and the ungrateful rush along from non-existence towards existence.

entries.farsi: سر چه مى‏پيچى كنى ناديده‏اى / در عدم ز اول نه سرپيچيده‏اى‏
entries.english: Why do you turn away your head and pretend not to see? Did you not turn away your head at first, in non-existence?

entries.farsi: در عدم افشرده بودى پاى خويش / كه مرا كه بر كند از جاى خويش‏
entries.english: You had planted your foot in non-existence, saying, “ Who will uproot me from my place?”

entries.farsi: مى‏نبينى صنع ربانيت را / كه كشيد او موى پيشانيت را‏
entries.english: Are not you beholding the action of your Lord, who dragged you by the forelock,

entries.farsi: تا كشيدت اندر اين انواع حال / كه نبودت در گمان و در خيال‏
entries.english: Until He drew you into these various states, which were not in your thought or fancy?

entries.farsi: آن عدم او را هماره بنده است / كار كن ديوا سليمان زنده است‏
entries.english: That non-existence is always His slave: work, O demon! Solomon is living.

entries.farsi: ديو مى‏سازد جِفانٍ كالجواب / زهره نى تا دفع گويد يا جواب‏
entries.english: The demon is making large bowls like watering-troughs: he dare not say a word in refusal or in retort.

entries.farsi: خويش را بين چون همى‏لرزى ز بيم / مر عدم را نيز لرزان دان مقيم‏
entries.english: Look at yourself, how you are trembling with fear: know that non-existence also is constantly trembling.

entries.farsi: ور تو دست اندر مناصب مى‏زنى / هم ز ترس است آن كه جانى مى‏كنى‏
entries.english: And if you are grasping at dignities, it is from fear too that you are suffering agony of spirit.

entries.farsi: هر چه جز عشق خداى احسن است / گر شكر خوارى است آن جان كندن است‏
entries.english: Except love of the most beauteous God everything, though it is eating sugar, is agony of spirit.

entries.farsi: چيست جان كندن سوى مرگ آمدن / دست در آب حياتى نازدن‏
entries.english: What is agony of spirit? To advance towards death and not grasp the Water of Life.

entries.farsi: جهد كن تا صد گمان گردد نود / شب برو ور تو بخسبى شب رود
entries.english: People fix both their eyes on earth and death: they have a hundred doubts concerning the Water of Life.

entries.farsi: خلق را دو ديده در خاك و ممات / صد گمان دارند در آب حيات‏
entries.english: Strive that the hundred doubts may become ninety: go in the night, for if you slumber, the night will go.

entries.farsi: در شب تاريك جوى آن روز را / پيش كن آن عقل ظلمت سوز را
entries.english: In the dark night seek that Day: put in front the darkness-consuming Reason.

entries.farsi: در شب بد رنگ بس نيكى بود / آب حيوان جفت تاريكى بود
entries.english: In the evil-coloured night there is much good: the Water of Life is the mate of darkness.

entries.farsi: سر ز خفتن كى توان برداشتن / با چنين صد تخم غفلت كاشتن‏
entries.english: How is it possible to lift up the head from slumber, while you are sowing a hundred such poppy seeds?

entries.farsi: خواب مرده لقمه‏ى مرده يار شد / خواجه خفت و دزد شب بر كار شد
entries.english: Dead slumber and dead food became friends: the merchant fell asleep and the night-thief got to work.

entries.farsi: تو نمى‏دانى كه خصمانت كى‏اند / ناريان خصم وجود خاكى‏اند
entries.english: Do you not know who your enemies are? Those made of fire are enemies to the existence of those made of earth.

entries.farsi: نار خصم آب و فرزندان اوست / همچنان كه آب خصم جان اوست‏
entries.english: Fire is the enemy of water and its children, even as water is an enemy to the life of fire.

entries.farsi: آب آتش را كشد زيرا كه او / خصم فرزندان آب است و عدو
entries.english: Water kills fire because it is the enemy and foe of the children of water.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن اين نار نار شهوت است / كاندر او اصل گناه و زلت است‏
entries.english: To proceed, this fire is the fire of appetites, wherein is the source of sin and error.

entries.farsi: نار بيرونى به آبى بفسرد / نار شهوت تا به دوزخ مى‏برد
entries.english: The external fire may be quenched by some water; the fire of appetites is bringing to Hell.

entries.farsi: نار شهوت مى‏نيارامد به آب / ز انكه دارد طبع دوزخ در عذاب‏
entries.english: The fire of appetites is not allayed by water, because it has the nature of Hell in respect of torment.

entries.farsi: نار شهوت را چه چاره نور دين / نوركم اطفاء نار الكافرين‏
entries.english: What is the remedy for the fire of appetites? The light of the God: your light is the extinguishing the fire of the infidels.

entries.farsi: چه كشد اين نار را نور خدا / نور ابراهيم را ساز اوستا
entries.english: What kills this fire? The Light of God. Make the light of Abraham your teacher,

entries.farsi: تا ز نار نفس چون نمرود تو / وارهد اين جسم همچون عود تو
entries.english: That this body of yours, which resembles wood, may be delivered from the fire of the Nimrod-like flesh (nafs)

entries.farsi: شهوت نارى به راندن كم نشد / او به ماندن كم شود بى‏هيچ بد
entries.english: Fiery appetite is not diminished by indulging it: it is diminished, without any escape, by leaving it.

entries.farsi: تا كه هيزم مى‏نهى بر آتشى / كى بميرد آتش از هيزم كشى‏
entries.english: So long as you are laying logs on a fire, how will the fire be extinguished by a wood carrier?

entries.farsi: چون كه هيزم باز گيرى نار مرد / ز انكه تقوى آب سوى نار برد
entries.english: When you withhold the sticks, the fire dies out, because fear of God carries water to the fire.

entries.farsi: كى سيه گردد ز آتش روى خوب / كاو نهد گلگونه از تَقْوَى القلوب‏
entries.english: How should the fire blacken the beauteous face which lays rose-colour from the God that is in hearts?

sections.title_fa: آتش افتادن در شهر به ايام عمر
sections.title_en: How a fire occurred in the city in the days of ‘Umar, may God be well-pleased with him.

entries.farsi: آتشى افتاد در عهد عمر / همچو چوب خشك مى‏خورد او حجر
entries.english: A conflagration occurred in the time of `Umar: it was devouring stones as Though they were dry wood.

entries.farsi: در فتاد اندر بنا و خانه‏ها / تا زد اندر پر مرغ و النه‏ها
entries.english: It fell upon buildings and houses, until it darted at the wings and nests of birds.

entries.farsi: نيم شهر از شعله‏ها آتش گرفت / آب مى‏ترسيد از آن و مى‏شگفت‏
entries.english: Half the city caught fire from the flames: water was afraid of it and amazed.

entries.farsi: مشكهاى آب و سركه مى‏زدند / بر سر آتش كسان هوشمند
entries.english: Some intelligent persons were throwing skins of water and vinegar on the fire,

entries.farsi: آتش از استيزه افزون مى‏شدى / مى‏رسيد او را مدد از بى‏حدى‏
entries.english: Out of spite the fire was increasing: aid was coming to it from One who is infinite.

entries.farsi: خلق آمد جانب عمر شتاب / كاتش ما مى‏نميرد هيچ از آب‏
entries.english: The people came in haste to `Umar, saying, “Our fire will not be quenched at all by water.”

entries.farsi: گفت آن آتش ز آيات خداست / شعله‏اى از آتش بخل شماست‏
entries.english: He said, “ That fire is one of God’s signs: it is a flame from the fire of your wickedness.

entries.farsi: آب بگذاريد و نان قسمت كنيد / خلق گفتندش كه در بگشوده‏ايم
entries.english: Discard water and deal out bread, discard avarice if you are my people.”

entries.farsi: ما سخى و اهل فتوت بوده‏ايم‏ / گفت نان در رسم و عادت داده‏ايد
entries.english: The folk said to him, “We have opened our doors; we have been bountiful and devoted to generosity.”

entries.farsi: دست از بهر خدا نگشاده‏ايد / بهر فخر و بهر بوش و بهر ناز
entries.english: He replied, “You have given bread by rule and habit, you have not opened your hands for the sake of God

entries.farsi: نه از براى ترس و تقوى و نياز / مال تخم است و به هر شوره منه
entries.english: For glory and for ostentation and for pride, not because of fear and piety and supplication”

entries.farsi: تيغ را در دست هر ره زن مده‏ / اهل دين را باز دان از اهل كين
entries.english: Wealth is seed, and do not lay it in every salty ground: do not put a sword in the hand of every highwayman.

entries.farsi: كاغه پندارد كه او خود كار كرد
entries.english: Distinguish the friends of the Way (ahl-i Din) from the enemies of God (ahl-i kin): seek the man that sits with God, and sit with him.

entries.farsi: همنشين حق بجو با او نشين‏ / هر كسى بر قوم خود ايثار كرد
entries.english: Every one shows favour to his own folk: the fool thinks he has really done work.

sections.title_fa: ‏خدو انداختن خصم در روى امير المؤمنين على عليه السلام و انداختن على شمشير را از دست
sections.title_en: How an enemy spat in the face of the Prince of the Faithful, ‘ Ali, may God honour his person, and how `Ali dropped the sword from his hand.

entries.farsi: از على آموز اخالص عمل / شير حق را دان مطهر از دغل‏
entries.english: Learn how to act with focused intention from ‘Ali: know that the Lion of God was purged of deceit.

entries.farsi: در غزا بر پهلوانى دست يافت / زود شمشيرى بر آورد و شتافت‏
entries.english: In fighting against the infidels he got the upper hand of (vanquished) a certain knight, and quickly drew a sword and made haste.

entries.farsi: او خدو انداخت در روى على / افتخار هر نبى و هر ولى‏
entries.english: He spat on the face of Ali, the pride of every prophet and every saint;

entries.farsi: آن خدو زد بر رخى كه روى ماه / سجده آرد پيش او در سجده‏گاه‏
entries.english: He spat on the countenance before which the face of the moon bows low in the place of worship.

entries.farsi: در زمان انداخت شمشير آن على / كرد او اندر غزايش كاهلى‏
entries.english: Ali at once threw his sword away and relaxed in fighting him.

entries.farsi: گشت حيران آن مبارز زين عمل / وز نمودن عفو و رحمت بى‏محل‏
entries.english: That champion was astounded by this act and by his showing forgiveness and mercy without occasion.

entries.farsi: گفت بر من تيغ تيز افراشتى / از چه افكندى مرا بگذاشتى‏
entries.english: He said, “You lifted your keen sword against me: why have you flung it aside and spared me?

entries.farsi: آن چه ديدى بهتر از پيكار من / تا شدى تو سست در اشكار من‏
entries.english: What did you see that was better than combat with me, so that you have become slack in hunting me down?

entries.farsi: آن چه ديدى كه چنين خشمت نشست / تا چنان برقى نمود و باز جست‏
entries.english: What did you see, so that such anger as yours abated, and so that such a lightning flashed and recoiled?

entries.farsi: آن چه ديدى كه مرا ز آن عكس ديد / در دل و جان شعله اى آمد پديد
entries.english: What did you see that from seeing the reflection thereof a flame appeared in my heart and soul?

entries.farsi: آن چه ديدى برتر از كون و مكان / كه به از جان بود و بخشيديم جان‏
entries.english: What did you see, beyond existence and space, that was better than life?—and you gave me life.

entries.farsi: در شجاعت شير ربانى ستى / در مروت خود كه داند كيستى‏
entries.english: In bravery you are the Lion of the Lord: in generosity who indeed knows who you are?

entries.farsi: در مروت ابر موسايى به تيه / كآمد از وى خوان و نان بى‏شبيه‏
entries.english: In generosity you are Moses’ cloud in the desert, from which came the dishes of food and bread incomparable.”

entries.farsi: ابرها گندم دهد كان را به جهد / پخته و شيرين كند مردم چو شهد
entries.english: The clouds give wheat which man with toil makes cooked and sweet as honey.

entries.farsi: ابر موسى پر رحمت بر گشاد / پخته و شيرين بى‏زحمت بداد
entries.english: Moses’ cloud spread the wings of mercy and gave cooked and sweet food that was without trouble.

entries.farsi: از براى پخته خواران كرم / رحمتش افراشت در عالم علم‏
entries.english: For the sake of those who partook of its bounty, its mercy raised a banner in the world.

entries.farsi: تا چهل سال آن وظيفه و آن عطا / كم نشد يك روز از آن اهل رجا
entries.english: During forty years that ration and largesse did not fail the hopeful people for a single day,

entries.farsi: امت احمد كه هستند از كرام / تا قيامت هست باقى آن طعام‏
entries.english: Until they too, because of their vileness, arose and demanded leeks and green herbs and lettuce.

entries.farsi: تا هم ايشان از خسيسى خاستند / گندنا و تره و خس خواستند
entries.english: O people of Ahmad, who are of the noble, that food is continuing, till the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: چون ابيت عند ربى فاش شد / يطعم و يسقى كنايت زاش شد
entries.english: When, “I pass the night with my Lord,” was uttered, “He gives food” and “He gives drink” referred metaphorically to food.

entries.farsi: هيچ بى‏تاويل اين را در پذير / تا در آيد در گلو چون شهد و شير
entries.english: Accept this without any interpretation that it may come into your throat as honey and milk.

entries.farsi: آن خطا ديدن ز ضعف عقل اوست / عقل كل مغز است و عقل جزو پوست‏
entries.english: Because interpretation is a rejection of the gift, since he regards that real meaning as faulty.

entries.farsi: ز آن كه تاويل است وا داد عطا / چون كه بيند آن حقيقت را خطا
entries.english: The view that it is faulty arises from the weakness of his understanding: Universal Reason is the kernel, and our reason is like the rind.

entries.farsi: خويش را تاويل كن نه اخبار را / مغز را بد گوى نى گلزار را
entries.english: Interpret yourself, not the Traditions: abuse your brain, not the rose-garden.

entries.farsi: اى على كه جمله عقل و ديده‏اى / شمه اى واگو از آن چه ديده‏اى‏
entries.english: “O ‘Ali, you who art all mind and eye, relate a little of that which you have seen!

entries.farsi: تيغ حلمت جان ما را چاك كرد / آب علمت خاك ما را پاك كرد
entries.english: The sword of your forbearance has rent my soul; the water of your knowledge has purified my earth.

entries.farsi: باز گو دانم كه اين اسرار هوست / ز آن كه بى‏شمشير كشتن كار اوست‏
entries.english: Tell it forth ! I know that these are His mysteries, because it is His work to kill without sword.

entries.farsi: صانع بى‏آلت و بى‏جارحه / واهب اين هديه‏هاى رابحه‏
entries.english: He that works without tools and without limbs, He that bestows these profitable gifts,

entries.farsi: صد هزاران مى‏چشاند هوش را / كه خبر نبود دو چشم و گوش را
entries.english: Causes the intelligence to taste myriads of wines in such wise that eyes and ears are unaware.

entries.farsi: باز گو اى باز عرش خوش شكار / تا چه ديدى اين زمان از كردگار
entries.english: Tell it forth, O falcon of the empyrean that finds goodly prey, that what you Have seen at this time from the Maker.

entries.farsi: چشم تو ادراك غيب آموخته / چشمهاى حاضران بر دوخته‏
entries.english: Yours eye has learned to perceive the Unseen, the eyes of bystanders are sealed.”

entries.farsi: آن يكى ماهى همى‏بيند عيان / و آن يكى تاريك مى‏بيند جهان‏
entries.english: One man is beholding a moon plainly, while another sees the world dark,

entries.farsi: و آن يكى سه ماه مى‏بيند به هم / اين سه كس بنشسته يك موضع نعم‏
entries.english: And another beholds three moons together. These three persons are seated in one place, yes.

entries.farsi: چشم هر سه باز و گوش هر سه تيز / در تو آويزان و از من در گريز
entries.english: The eyes of all three are open, and the ears of all three are sharp; fastened on you and in flight from me.

entries.farsi: سحر عين است اين عجب لطف خفى است / بر تو نقش گرگ و بر من يوسفى است‏
entries.english: Is this an enchantment of the eye? Is it a marvellous hidden grace? The form of the wolf is on you, and on me is the quality of Joseph.

entries.farsi: عالم ار هجده هزار است و فزون / هر نظر را نيست اين هجده زبون‏
entries.english: If the worlds are eighteen thousand and more, these eighteen are not subject (accessible) to every eye.

entries.farsi: راز بگشا اى على مرتضى / اى پس سوء القضاء حسن القضاء
entries.english: “Reveal the mystery, O ‘Ali, you who are approved, O you who are good fate after evil fate.

entries.farsi: يا تو واگو آن چه عقلت يافته ست / يا بگويم آن چه بر من تافته ست‏
entries.english: Either do you declare that which your reason has found, or I will tell that which has shone forth on me.

entries.farsi: از تو بر من تافت چون دارى نهان / مى‏فشانى نور چون مه بى‏زبان‏
entries.english: From you it shone forth on me: how should you hide it? Without tongue you art darting rays of light, like the moon.

entries.farsi: ليك اگر در گفت آيد قرص ماه / شب روان را زودتر آرد به راه‏
entries.english: But if the moon’s orb comes to speech, it more quickly leads the night-travellers into the way.

entries.farsi: از غلط ايمن شوند و از ذهول / بانگ مه غالب شود بر بانگ غول‏
entries.english: They become safe from error and heedlessness: the voice of the moon prevails over the voice of the ghoul.

entries.farsi: ماه بى‏گفتن چو باشد رهنما / چون بگويد شد ضيا اندر ضيا
entries.english: Inasmuch as the moon without speech is showing the way, when it speaks it becomes light upon light.

entries.farsi: چون تو بابى آن مدينه‏ى علم را / چون شعاعى آفتاب حلم را
entries.english: Since you are the gate of the city of Knowledge, since you are the beams of the sun of Clemency,

entries.farsi: باز باش اى باب بر جوياى باب / تا رسد از تو قشور اندر لباب‏
entries.english: Be open, O Gate, to him that seeks the gate, so that by means of you the husks may reach the core.

entries.farsi: باز باش اى باب رحمت تا ابد / بارگاه ما لَهُ كُفُواً أحد
entries.english: Be open unto everlasting, O Gate of Mercy, O Entrance-hall to None is like unto Him.”

entries.farsi: هر هوا و ذره‏اى خود منظرى است / ناگشاده كى گود كانجا درى است‏
entries.english: Every air and mote is indeed a place for vision, unopened, who says “There is a door”?

entries.farsi: تا بنگشايد دزى را ديدبان / در درون هرگز نجنبد اين گمان‏
entries.english: Unless the Watcher open a door, this idea never stirs within.

entries.farsi: غافلى ناگه به ويران گنج يافت / سوى هر ويران از آن پس مى‏شتافت‏
entries.english: When a door is opened, he becomes amazed, grows wings, and begins to fly on the idea.

entries.farsi: چون گشاده شد دزى حيران شود / مرغ اوميد و طمع پران شود
entries.english: A careless man suddenly found the treasure in the ruin: after that, he was hastening to every ruin.

entries.farsi: تا ز درويشى نيابى تو گهر / كى گهر جويى ز درويشى دگر
entries.english: Till you gain the pearl from one dervish, how should you seek the pearl from another dervish?

entries.farsi: سالها گر ظن دود با پاى خويش / نگذرد ز اشكاف بينيهاى خويش‏
entries.english: Though opinion run with its own feet for years, it will not pass beyond the cleft of its own nostrils.

entries.farsi: تا به بينى نايدت از غيب بو / غير بينى هيچ مى‏بينى بگو
entries.english: Say, are you seeing aught except your nose? Say, how will you see if you turn up your nose?

sections.title_fa: سؤال كردن آن كافر از امير المؤمنين على عليه السلام كه بر چون منى مظفر شدى شمشير را از دست چون انداختى ‏پس بگفت آن نو مسلمان ولى از سر مستى و لذت با على

sections.title_en: How that infidel asked ‘Ali, may God honour his person, saying, “Since you went victorious over such a man as I am, how did you drop the sword from your hand?”

entries.farsi: كه بفرما يا امير المؤمنين / تا بجنبد جان بتن در چون جنين‏
entries.english: He said, “ Speak, O Prince of the Faithful, that my soul may stir within my body, like the embryo.”

entries.farsi: هفت اختر هر جنين را مدتى / مى‏كنند اى جان به نوبت خدمتى‏
entries.english: How has the embryo the means during the period when it is ruled? It comes from the stars towards the sun.

entries.farsi: چون كه وقت آيد كه جان گيرد جنين / آفتابش آن زمان گردد معين‏
entries.english: When the time comes for the embryo to receive the spirit, at that time the sun becomes its helper.

entries.farsi: اين جنين در جنبش آيد ز آفتاب / كافتابش جان همى‏بخشد شتاب‏
entries.english: This embryo is brought into movement by the sun, for the sun is quickly endowing it with spirit.

entries.farsi: از دگر انجم بجز نقشى نيافت / اين جنين تا آفتابش بر نتافت‏
entries.english: From the other stars this embryo received only an impression, until the sun shone upon it.

entries.farsi: از كدامين ره تعلق يافت او / در رحم با آفتاب خوب رو
entries.english: By which way did it become connected in the womb with the beauteous sun?

entries.farsi: از ره پنهان كه دور از حس ماست / آفتاب چرخ را بس راههاست‏
entries.english: By the hidden way that is remote from our sense-perception. The sun in heaven has many ways:

entries.farsi: آن رهى كه زر بيابد قوت از او / و آن رهى كه سنگ شد ياقوت از او
entries.english: The way whereby gold receives nourishment, and the way whereby the stone is made a jacinth,

entries.farsi: آن رهى كه سرخ سازد لعل را / و آن رهى كه برق بخشد نعل را
entries.english: And the way whereby it makes the ruby red, and the way whereby it gives the lightning-flash to the horse-shoe,

entries.farsi: آن رهى كه پخته سازد ميوه را / و آن رهى كه دل دهد كاليوه را
entries.english: And the way whereby it ripens the fruit, and the way whereby it gives heart to one distraught.

entries.farsi: باز گو اى باز عنقا گير شاه / اى سپاه اشكن به خود نى با سپاه‏
entries.english: “Say it forth, O falcon with shining wings who has learned with the King and with his fore-arm.

entries.farsi: باز گو اى باز پر افروخته / با شه و با ساعدش آموخته‏
entries.english: Say it forth, O royal falcon that catches the Anqa, O you that vanquishes an army by yourself, not with an army.

entries.farsi: امت وحدى يكى و صد هزار / باز گو اى بنده بازت را شكار
entries.english: You alone are the community; you are one and a hundred thousand. Say it forth, O you to whose falcon your slave has fallen a prey.

entries.farsi: در محل قهر اين رحمت ز چيست / اژدها را دست‏دادن راه كيست‏
entries.english: Wherefore this mercy in the place of vengeance? Whose way is it to give the hand to a dragon?”

sections.title_fa: ‏جواب گفتن امير المؤمنين كه سبب افكندن شمشير از دست چه بود در آن حالت
sections.title_en: How the Prince of the Faithful made answer what was the reason of his dropping the sword from his hand on that occasion.

entries.farsi: شير حقم نيستم شير هوا / فعل من بر دين من باشد گوا
entries.english: He said, “I am wielding the sword for God’s sake, I am the servant of God, I am not under the command of the body.

entries.farsi: ما رميت إذ رميتم در حراب / من چو تيغم و آن زننده آفتاب‏
entries.english: I am the Lion of God, I am not the lion of passion: my deed bears witness to my religion.

entries.farsi: گفت من تيغ از پى حق مى‏زنم / بنده‏ى حقم نه مأمور تنم‏
entries.english: In war I am you did not throw when you threw: I am as the sword, and the wielder is the Sun.

entries.farsi: رخت خود را من ز ره برداشتم / غير حق را من عدم انگاشتم‏
entries.english: I have removed the baggage of self out of the way; I have deemed other than God to be non-existence.

entries.farsi: سايه‏ام من كدخدايم آفتاب / حاجبم من نيستم او را حجاب‏
entries.english: I am a shadow, the Sun is my lord; I am the chamberlain, I am not the curtain to Him.

entries.farsi: من چو تيغم پر گهرهاى وصال / زنده گردانم نه كشته در قتال‏
entries.english: I am filled with the pearls of union, like a sword: in battle I make living, not slain.

entries.farsi: خون نپوشد گوهر تيغ مرا / باد از جا كى برد ميغ مرا
entries.english: Blood does not cover the sheen of my sword: how should the wind sweep away my clouds?

entries.farsi: كه نيم كوهم ز حلم و صبر و داد / كوه را كى در ربايد تند باد
entries.english: I am not a straw, I am a mountain of forbearance and patience and justice: how should the fierce wind carry off the mountain?”

entries.farsi: آن كه از بادى رود از جا خسى است / ز آن كه باد ناموافق خود بسى است‏
entries.english: That which is removed from its place by a wind is rubbish, for indeed the contrary winds are many.

entries.farsi: باد خشم و باد شهوت باد آز / برد او را كه نبود اهل نماز
entries.english: The wind of anger and the wind of lust and the wind of greed swept away him that performed not the prayers.

entries.farsi: كوهم و هستى من بنياد اوست / ور شوم چون كاه با دم ياد اوست‏
entries.english: “I am a mountain, and my being is His building; and if I become like a straw, my wind is His wind.

entries.farsi: جز به باد او نجنبد ميل من / نيست جز عشق احد سر خيل من‏
entries.english: My longing is not stirred save by His wind; my captain is nothing but love of the One.

entries.farsi: تيغ حلمم گردن خشمم زده ست / خشم حق بر من چو رحمت آمده ست‏
entries.english: Anger is king over kings, and to me it is a slave: even anger I have bound under the bridle.

entries.farsi: خشم بر شاهان شه و ما را غالم / خشم را هم بسته‏ام زير لگام‏
entries.english: The sword of my forbearance has smitten the neck of my anger; the anger of God has come on me like mercy.

entries.farsi: غرق نورم گر چه سقفم شد خراب / روضه گشتم گر چه هستم بو تراب‏
entries.english: I am plunged in light although my life is ruined; I have become a garden although I am Bu Turab.

entries.farsi: چون در آمد علتى اندر غزا / تيغ را ديدم نهان كردن سزا
entries.english: Since other than God has intervened, it benefits to sheathe my sword,

entries.farsi: تا احب هلل آيد نام من / تا كه ابغض هلل آيد كام من‏
entries.english: That my name may be he loves for God’s sake, that my desire may be he hates for God’s sake,

entries.farsi: تا كه اعطا هلل آيد جود من / تا كه امسك هلل آيد بود من‏
entries.english: That my generosity may be he gives for God’s sake, that my being may be he withholds for God’s sake.

entries.farsi: بخل من هلل عطا هلل و بس / جمله هلل‏ام نيم من آن كس‏
entries.english: My stinginess is for God’s sake, my bounty is for God’s sake alone: I belong entirely to God, I do not belong to any one;

entries.farsi: و آن چه هلل مى‏كنم تقليد نيست / نيست تخييل و گمان جز ديد نيست‏
entries.english: And that which I am doing for God’s sake is not conformity, it is not fancy and opinion, it is naught but intuition

entries.farsi: ز اجتهاد و از تحرى رسته‏ام / آستين بر دامن حق بسته‏ام‏
entries.english: I have been freed from effort and search; I have tied my sleeve to the skirt of God.

entries.farsi: گر همى‏پرم همى‏بينم مطار / ور همى‏گردم همى‏بينم مدار
entries.english: If I am flying, I behold the place to which I soar; and if I am circling, I behold the axis on which I revolve;

entries.farsi: ور كشم بارى بدانم تا كجا / ماهم و خورشيد پيشم پيشوا
entries.english: And if I am dragging a burden, I know whither: I am the moon, and the Sun is in front of me as the guide.”

entries.farsi: بيش از اين با خلق گفتن روى نيست / بحر را گنجايى اندر جوى نيست‏
entries.english: There is no means of communicating more than this to the people: in the river there is no room for the Sea.

entries.farsi: پست مى‏گويم به اندازه‏ى عقول / عيب نبود اين بود كار رسول‏
entries.english: I speak low according to the measure of understandings: it is no fault; this is the practice of the Prophet.

entries.farsi: از غرض حرم گواهى حر شنو / كه گواهى بندگان نه ارزد دو جو
entries.english: “I am free from self-interest : hear the testimony of a freeman, for the testimony of slaves is not worth two barleycorns.”

entries.farsi: در شريعت مر گواهى بنده را / نيست قدرى وقت دعوى و قضا
entries.english: In the religious law the testimony of a slave has no value at litigation and judgment.

entries.farsi: گر هزاران بنده باشندت گواه / بر نسنجد شرع ايشان را به كاه‏
entries.english: If thousands of slaves bear witness on your behalf, the law does not accept their testimony as worth a straw.

entries.farsi: بنده‏ى شهوت بتر نزديك حق / از غالم و بندگان مسترق‏
entries.english: In God’s sight the slave of lust is worse than menials and slaves brought into servitude,

entries.farsi: كاين به يك لفظى شود از خواجه حر / و آن زيد شيرين و ميرد سخت مر
entries.english: For the latter becomes free at a single word from his master, while the former lives sweet but dies sour and bitter.

entries.farsi: بنده‏ى شهوت ندارد خود خالص / جز به فضل ايزد و انعام خاص‏
entries.english: The slave of lust has no release at all except through the grace of God and His special favour.

entries.farsi: در چهى افتاد كان را غور نيست / و آن گناه اوست جبر و جور نيست‏
entries.english: He has fallen into a pit that has no bottom, and that is his sin: it is not compulsion and injustice.

entries.farsi: در چهى انداخت او خود را كه من / در خور قعرش نمى‏يابم رسن‏
entries.english: He has cast himself into such a pit that I find no rope capable of its bottom.

entries.farsi: بس كنم گر اين سخن افزون شود / خود جگر چه بود كه خارا خون شود
entries.english: I will make an end. If this discourse go further, not only hearts but rocks will bleed.

entries.farsi: اين جگرها خون نشد نز سختى است / غفلت و مشغولى و بد بختى است‏
entries.english: These hearts have not bled, it is not because of hardness, it is perplexity and preoccupation and ill-fatedness.

entries.farsi: خون شود روزى كه خونش سود نيست / خون شو آن وقتى كه خون مردود نيست‏
entries.english: They will bleed one day when blood is no use to them: bleed at a time when blood is not rejected.

entries.farsi: چون گواهى بندگان مقبول نيست / عدل او باشد كه بنده‏ى غول نيست‏
entries.english: Inasmuch as the testimony of slaves is not accepted, the approved witness is he that is not the slave of the ghoul.

entries.farsi: گشت ارسلناك شاهد در نذر / ز آن كه بود از كون او حر ابن حر
entries.english: We have sent you as a witness came in the Warning, because he was entirely free from existence.

entries.farsi: چون كه حرم خشم كى بندد مرا / نيست اينجا جز صفات حق در آ
entries.english: “Since I am free, how should anger bind me? Nothing is here but Divine qualities. Come in!

entries.farsi: اندر آ كازاد كردت فضل حق / ز آن كه رحمت داشت بر خشمش سبق‏
entries.english: Come in, for the grace of God has made you free, because His mercy had the precedence over His wrath.

entries.farsi: اندر آ اكنون كه رستى از خطر / سنگ بودى كيميا كردت گهر
entries.english: Come in now, for you have escaped from the peril: you were a stone; the Elixir has made you a jewel.

entries.farsi: رسته‏اى از كفر و خارستان او / چون گلى بشكفته در بستان هو
entries.english: You have been delivered from unbelief and its thorn-thicket: blossom like a rose in the cypress-garden of Hu.

entries.farsi: تو منى و من توام اى محتشم / تو على بودى على را چون كشم‏
entries.english: You are I and I am you, I am happy with you: you were Ali—how should I kill Ali?

entries.farsi: معصيت كردى به از هر طاعتى / آسمان پيموده‏اى در ساعتى‏
entries.english: You have committed a sin better than any act of piety; you have traversed Heaven in a single moment.”

entries.farsi: بس خجسته معصيت كان كرد مرد / نى ز خارى بر دمد اوراق ورد
entries.english: Very fortunate the sin which the man committed: do not rose-leaves spring from a thorn?

entries.farsi: نى گناه عمر و قصد رسول / مى‏كشيدش تا به درگاه قبول‏
entries.english: Was not the sin of `Umar and his attempt on the Prophet leading him to the gate of acceptance?

entries.farsi: نى به سحر ساحران فرعونشان / مى‏كشيد و گشت دولت عونشان‏
entries.english: Was not Pharaoh because of the magic of the magicians drawing them, and did not fortune come to their aid?

entries.farsi: گر نبودى سحرشان و آن جحود / كى كشيديشان به فرعون عنود
entries.english: Had it not been for their magic and denial, which would have brought them to rebellious Pharaoh?

entries.farsi: كى بديدندى عصا و معجزات / معصيت طاعت شد اى قوم عصات‏
entries.english: How would they have seen the rod and the miracles? Disobedience became obedience, O disobedient people

entries.farsi: نااميدى را خدا گردن زده است / چون گنه مانند طاعت آمده ست‏
entries.english: God has smitten the neck of despair, inasmuch as sin and disobedience have become obedience.

entries.farsi: چون مبدل مى‏كند او سيئات / طاعتى‏اش مى‏كند رغم وشات‏
entries.english: Since He changes evil acts, He makes it an act of obedience in despite of slanderer.

entries.farsi: زين شود مرجوم شيطان رجيم / و ز حسد او بطرقد گردد دو نيم‏
entries.english: By this the stoned Devil is driven away and bursts with envy and is cloven asunder.

entries.farsi: او بكوشد تا گناهى پرورد / ز آن گنه ما را به چاهى آورد
entries.english: He strives to foster a sin (in us) and by means of that sin bring us into a pit;

entries.farsi: چون ببيند كان گنه شد طاعتى / گردد او را نامبارك ساعتى‏
entries.english: When he sees that the sin has become an act of obedience, there comes round for him an unblest hour.

entries.farsi: اندر آ من در گشادم مر ترا / تف زدى و تحفه دادم مر ترا
entries.english: “Come in! I open the door to you. You spat and I give you a present.

entries.farsi: مر جفاگر را چنينها مى‏دهم / پيش پاى چپ چه سان سر مى‏نهم‏
entries.english: Such things I am giving to the doer of iniquity: in what fashion I lay my head before the left foot.

entries.farsi: پس وفاگر را چه بخشم تو بدان / گنجها و ملكهاى جاودان‏
entries.english: What then do I bestow on the doer of righteousness? I bestow treasures and kingdoms everlasting.

sections.title_fa: گفتن پيغامبر عليه السلام به گوش ركابدار امير المؤمنين على عليه السلام كه كشتن على بر دست ‏تو خواهد بودن خبرت كردم
sections.title_en: How the Prophet, on whom he peace, said in the ear of the stirrup-holder
of the Prince of the Faithful, may God honour his person, “I tell you, ‘ Ali will be slain by your hand.”

entries.farsi: من چنان مردم كه بر خونى خويش / نوش لطف من نشد در قهر نيش‏
entries.english: I am such a man that the honey of my kindness did not become poison in wrath against my murderer.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر به گوش چاكرم / كاو برد روزى ز گردن اين سرم‏
entries.english: The Prophet said in the ear of my servant that one day he would sever this head of mine from my neck.

entries.farsi: كرد آگه آن رسول از وحى دوست / كه هالكم عاقبت بر دست اوست‏
entries.english: The Prophet by inspiration informed my friend that in the end my destruction would be by his hand.

entries.farsi: او همى‏گويد بكش پيشين مرا / تا نيايد از من اين منكر خطا
entries.english: He says; Kill me first, in order that this hateful crime may not proceed from me.

entries.farsi: من همى‏گويم چو مرگ من ز تست / با قضا من چون توانم حيله جست‏
entries.english: I say, since my death is from you, how can I seek to evade the destiny?’

entries.farsi: او همى‏افتد به پيشم كاى كريم / مر مرا كن از براى حق دو نيم‏
entries.english: He falls before me, saying, ‘O generous man, for God’s sake cut me in two,

entries.farsi: تا نيايد بر من اين انجام بد / تا نسوزد جان من بر جان خود
entries.english: That this evil end may not come upon me; and that my soul may not burn for its life.’

entries.farsi: من همى‏گويم برو جف القلم / ز آن قلم بس سر نگون گردد علم‏
entries.english: I say, go: the Pen is dry; by that Pen many a landmark is overthrown.

entries.farsi: هيچ بغضى نيست در جانم ز تو / ز آن كه اين را من نمى‏دانم ز تو
entries.english: There is no hatred of you in my soul, because I do not regard this as from you.

entries.farsi: آلت حقى تو فاعل دست حق / چون زنم بر آلت حق طعن و دق‏
entries.english: You are God’s instrument, God’s hand is the agent: how should I assail and oppose God’s instrument? ‘”

entries.farsi: گفت او پس آن قصاص از بهر چيست / گفت هم از حق و آن سر خفى است‏
entries.english: He said, “For what reason, then, is retaliation?” “It is from God, too,” said ‘Ali, “and that is a hidden mystery.

entries.farsi: اعتراض او را رسد بر فعل خود / ز آن كه در قهر است و در لطف او احد
entries.english: If He takes offence at His own act, He causes gardens to grow from that taking offence.

entries.farsi: گر كند بر فعل خود او اعتراض / ز اعتراض خود بروياند رياض‏
entries.english: He seems to take offence at His own act, inasmuch as in vengeance and mercy He is One.

entries.farsi: اندر اين شهر حوادث مير اوست / در ممالك مالك تدبير اوست‏
entries.english: In this city of phenomena He is the Prince; in the realms He is the Ruler.

entries.farsi: آلت خود را اگر او بشكند / آن شكسته گشته را نيكو كند
entries.english: If He breaks His own instrument, He mends that which has become broken.”

entries.farsi: رمز ننسخ آيه او ننسها / نأت خيرا در عقب مى‏دان مها
entries.english: Recognise, O noble sir, the indication of, verse We shall cancel or cause to be forgotten, followed by We shall bring a better.

entries.farsi: هر شريعت را كه حق منسوخ كرد / او گيا برد و عوض آورد ورد
entries.english: Every law that He has cancelled— He has taken away grass and brought roses in exchange

entries.farsi: شب كند منسوخ شغل روز را / بين جمادى خرد افروز را
entries.english: Night cancels the business of day: behold an inanimateness that enlightens the intellect !

entries.farsi: باز شب منسوخ شد از نور روز / تا جمادى سوخت ز آن آتش فروز
entries.english: Again, night is cancelled by the light of day, so that the inanimateness is consumed by that fire-kindling one.

entries.farsi: گر چه ظلمت آمد آن نوم و سبات / نى درون ظلمت است آب حيات‏
entries.english: Although that sleep and rest are darkness, is not the Water of Life within the darkness?

entries.farsi: نى در آن ظلمت خردها تازه شد / سكته‏اى سرمايه‏ى آوازه شد
entries.english: Did not minds become refreshed in that darkness? Did not a pause become the source of the voice?

entries.farsi: كه ز ضدها ضدها آمد پديد / در سويدا روشنايى آفريد
entries.english: For contraries are manifested by means of contraries: in the black core He created the everlasting light.

entries.farsi: جنگ پيغمبر مدار صلح شد / صلح اين آخر زمان ز آن جنگ بد
entries.english: The wars of the Prophet became the pivot of peace: the peace of this latter age was from those wars.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران سر بريد آن دلستان / تا امان يابد سر اهل جهان‏
entries.english: That heart-ravisher cut off hundreds of thousands of heads, in order that the heads of the world’s people might win security.

entries.farsi: باغبان ز آن مى‏برد شاخ مضر / تا بيابد نخل قامتها و بر
entries.english: The gardener lops the green bough, in order that the date-palm may gain stature and goodness.

entries.farsi: مى‏كند از باغ دانا آن حشيش / تا نمايد باغ و ميوه خرميش‏
entries.english: The expert digs up the weeds from the garden, in order that his garden and fruit may look flourishing.

entries.farsi: مى‏كند دندان بد را آن طبيب / تا رهد از درد و بيمارى حبيب‏
entries.english: The physician extracts bad teeth, in order that the beloved may be saved from pain and sickness.

entries.farsi: بس زيادتها درون نقصهاست / مر شهيدان را حيات اندر فناست‏
entries.english: Many advantages are within defects: for martyrs there is life in death.

entries.farsi: حلق حيوان چون بريده شد به عدل / حلق انسان رست و افزون گشت فضل‏
entries.english: When the throat has been cut that swallowed the daily bread, receiving the bounty, rejoicing, shall be delicious.

entries.farsi: چون بريده گشت حلق رزق خوار / يرزقون فرحين شد گوار
entries.english: When the throat of an animal is cut duly, there grows the throat of man, and its excellence is increased.

entries.farsi: حلق انسان چون ببرد هين ببين / تا چه زايد كن قياس آن بر اين‏
entries.english: When a man’s throat is cut, come; consider what the result will be! Judge of this by the analogy of that

entries.farsi: حلق ثالث زايد و تيمار او / شربت حق باشد و انوار او
entries.english: A third throat will be born, and care of it will be the sherbet of God and His lights.

entries.farsi: حلق ببريده خورد شربت ولى / حلق از ال رسته مرده در بلى‏
entries.english: The throat that has been cut drinks sherbet, but the throat that has been delivered from No and has died in Yes.

entries.farsi: بس كن اى دون همت كوته بنان / تا كى‏ات باشد حيات جان به نان‏
entries.english: Make an end, O cowardly short-fingered one! How long will the life of your spirit be by bread?

entries.farsi: ز آن ندارى ميوه‏اى مانند بيد / كآبرو بردى پى نان سپيد
entries.english: Like the willow, you have no fruit, because you have lost yours honour for the sake of white bread.

entries.farsi: گر ندارد صبر زين نان جان حس / كيميا را گير و زر گردان تو مس‏
entries.english: If the sensual soul cannot refrain from this bread, take the elixir and turn your copper into gold.

entries.farsi: جامه شويى كرد خواهى اى فالن / رو مگردان از محله‏ى گازران‏
entries.english: Wouldst you wash your garment, O so-and-so, do not avert your face from the bleachers’ quarter.

entries.farsi: گر چه نان بشكست مر روزه‏ى ترا / در شكسته بند پيچ و برتر آ
entries.english: Although the bread has broken your fast, cling to Him that binds what is broken, and ascend!

entries.farsi: چون شكسته بند آمد دست او / پس رفو باشد يقين اشكست او
entries.english: Inasmuch as His hand binds what is broken, it follows that His breaking is assuredly mending.

entries.farsi: گر تو آن را بشكنى گويد بيا / تو درستش كن ندارى دست و پا
entries.english: If you break it, He will say to you, “Come, make it whole”; and you are clumsy.

entries.farsi: پس شكستن حق او باشد كه او / مر شكسته گشته را داند رفو
entries.english: Therefore He has the right to break, for He can mend what has been broken.

entries.farsi: آن كه داند دوخت او داند دريد / هر چه را بفروخت نيكوتر خريد
entries.english: He that knows how to sew knows how to tear; whatsoever He sells, He buys better.

entries.farsi: خانه را ويران كند زير و زبر / پس به يك ساعت كند معمورتر
entries.english: He lays the house in ruins, upside down; then in one moment He makes it more habitable.

entries.farsi: گر يكى سر را ببرد از بدن / صد هزاران سر بر آرد در زمن‏
entries.english: If He severs one head from the body, He at once raises up hundreds of thousands of heads.

entries.farsi: گر نفرمودى قصاصى بر جناة / يا نگفتى فى القصاص آمد حيات
entries.english: If He had not ordained retaliation upon the guilty, or if He had riot said, “In retaliation there is a life,”

entries.farsi: خود كه را زهره بدى تا او ز خود / بر اسير حكم حق تيغى زند
entries.english: Who would have the stomach of himself to wield a sword against him that is a thrall to the decree of God?—

entries.farsi: ز آن كه داند هر كه چشمش را گشود / كآن كشنده سخره‏ى تقدير بود
entries.english: Because every one whose eyes He has opened would know that the slayer was constrained by predestination.

entries.farsi: هر كه را آن حكم بر سر آمدى / بر سر فرزند هم تيغى زدى‏
entries.english: Any one on whom that predestination became a collar would strike a sword-blow even at the head of his child.

entries.farsi: رو بترس و طعنه كم زن بر بدان / پيش دام حكم عجز خود بدان‏
entries.english: Go, fear and do not rail at the wicked: know yours own impotence before the snare of the decree.

sections.title_fa: تعجب كردن آدم عليه السلام از ضاللت ابليس لعين و عجب آوردن
sections.title_en: How Adam, on whom be peace, marvelled at the perdition of Iblis and showed vanity.

entries.farsi: چشم آدم بر بليسى كو شقى ست / از حقارت و از زيافت بنگريست‏
entries.english: One day Adam looked with contempt and scorn on Iblis who is damned.

entries.farsi: خويش بينى كرد و آمد خود گزين / خنده زد بر كار ابليس لعين‏
entries.english: He behaved with self-conceit and became self-approving: he laughed at the plight of accursed Iblis.

entries.farsi: بانگ بر زد غيرت حق كاى صفى / تو نمى‏دانى ز اسرار خفى‏
entries.english: The jealousy of God cried out— “O chosen one, you are ignorant of the hidden mysteries.

entries.farsi: پوستين را باژگونه گر كند / كوه را از بيخ و از بن بر كند
entries.english: If He should turn the fur inside out, He would tear up from root and bottom the mountain;

entries.farsi: پرده‏ى صد آدم آن دم بر درد / صد بليس نو مسلمان آورد
entries.english: At that instant He would rend the veil of a hundred Adams and bring a hundred Devils newly converted to Islam.”

entries.farsi: گفت آدم توبه كردم زين نظر / اين چنين گستاخ ننديشم دگر
entries.english: Adam said, “I repent of this look; I will not think so disrespectfully again.”

entries.farsi: يا غياث المستغيثين اهدنا / ال افتخار بالعلوم و الغنى‏
entries.english: O Help of them that call for help, lead us! There is no pride in knowledge or riches.

entries.farsi: ال تزغ قلبا هديت بالكرم / و اصرف السوء الذى خط القلم‏
entries.english: Do not let a heart stray that You Have guided by Your grace, and avert the evil which the Pen has written.

entries.farsi: بگذران از جان ما سوء القضا / وا مبر ما را ز اخوان صفا
entries.english: Let the evil of Your ordainment pass from our souls: do not cut us off from those who are well-pleased.

entries.farsi: تلخ‏تر از فرقت تو هيچ نيست / بى‏پناهت غير پيچا پيچ نيست‏
entries.english: There is naught more bitter than separation from You: without Your protection there is naught but perplexity.

entries.farsi: رخت ما هم رخت ما را راه زن / جسم ما مر جان ما را جامه كن‏
entries.english: Our goods waylay our goods: our bodies tear the garment from our souls.

entries.farsi: دست ما چون پاى ما را مى‏خورد / بى‏امان تو كسى جان چون برد
entries.english: Inasmuch as our hand devours our foot, how shall any one save his soul without Your security?

entries.farsi: ور برد جان زين خطرهاى عظيم / برده باشد مايه‏ى ادبار و بيم‏
entries.english: And if he save his soul from these awful dangers, he will have saved a stock of misfortune and fear,

entries.farsi: ز آن كه جان چون واصل جانان نبود / تا ابد با خويش كور است و كبود
entries.english: Because the soul, when it is not united with the Beloved, is blind and blue with itself for ever.

entries.farsi: چون تو ندهى راه جان خود برده گير / جان كه بى‏تو زنده باشد مرده گير
entries.english: When You will not give him admission—even suppose he has saved his soul, regard as dead the soul that would live without You.

entries.farsi: گر تو طعنه مى‏زنى بر بندگان / مر ترا آن مى‏رسد اى كامران‏
entries.english: If You are upbraiding Your slaves, that is suitable to You, O You whose every wish is fulfilled.

entries.farsi: ور تو ماه و مهر را گويى جفا / ور تو قد سرو را گويى دوتا
entries.english: And if You say that the sun and moon are scum, and if You say that the stature of the cypress is double,

entries.farsi: ور تو چرخ و عرش را خوانى حقير / ور تو كان و بحر را گويى فقير
entries.english: And if You call the empyrean and the sky contemptible, and if You say that the mine and the sea are poor—

entries.farsi: آن به نسبت با كمال تو رواست / ملك اكمال فناها مر تراست‏
entries.english: That is proper in reference to Your perfection: Yours is the power of perfecting mortalities,

entries.farsi: كه تو پاكى از خطر و ز نيستى / نيستان را موجد و معنيستى‏
entries.english: For You are holy from danger and from nonexistence: You are He that brings the non-existent ones into being and endows.

entries.farsi: آن كه رويانيد داند سوختن / ز آن كه چون بدريد داند دوختن‏
entries.english: He that made to grow can burn, because when He has torn, He can sew.

entries.farsi: مى‏بسوزد هر خزان مر باغ را / باز روياند گل صباغ را
entries.english: Every autumn He burns the garden; He makes to grow again the rose that dyes,

entries.farsi: كاى بسوزيده برون آ تازه شو / بار ديگر خوب و خوب آوازه شو
entries.english: Saying, “ O you who were withered, come forth, be fresh, once more be fair and of fair renown!”

entries.farsi: چشم نرگس كور شد بازش بساخت / حلق نى ببريد و بازش خود نواخت‏
entries.english: The eye of the narcissus became blind: He restored it; the throat of the reed was cut: He himself fostered it again.

entries.farsi: ما چو مصنوعيم و صانع نيستيم / جز زبون و جز كه قانع نيستيم‏
entries.english: Since we are made and are not makers, we are not but humble and content.

entries.farsi: ز آن ز اهرمن رهيدستيم ما / كه خريدى جان ما را از عمى‏
entries.english: We all are of the Nafs and busy with me-mine: if You call us not, we all are Devils.

entries.farsi: ما همه نفسى و نفسى مى‏زنيم / گر نخوانى ما همه اهرمنيم‏
entries.english: We have been delivered from Satan, because You Have redeemed our souls from blindness.

entries.farsi: تو عصا كش هر كه را كه زندگى است / بى‏عصا و بى‏عصا كش كور چيست‏
entries.english: You are the Guide of every one that has life: who is the blind man without staff and guide?

entries.farsi: غير تو هر چه خوش است و ناخوش است / آدمى سوز است و عين آتش است‏
entries.english: Excepting You, whatever is sweet or unsweet is man-destroying and the essence of fire.

entries.farsi: هر كه را آتش پناه و پشت شد / هم مجوسى گشت و هم زردشت شد
entries.english: Any one to whom fire is a refuge and support becomes both a Magian and a Zoroaster.

entries.farsi: كل شي‏ء ما خال اللهَّ باطل / إن فضل اللهَّ غيم هاطل‏
entries.english: Everything except Allah is vain: truly the grace of Allah is a cloud pouring abundantly and continually.

sections.title_fa: باز گشتن به حكايت امير المؤمنين على عليه السلام و مسامحت كردن او با خونى خويش‏
sections.title_en: Returning to the story of the Prince of the Faithful, ‘Ali—may God honour his person!— and how generously he behaved to his murderer.

entries.farsi: باز رو سوى على و خونى‏اش / و آن كرم با خونى و افزونى‏اش‏
entries.english: Go back to ‘Ali and his murderer, and the kindness he showed to the murderer, and his superiority.

entries.farsi: گفت دشمن را همى‏مى‏بينم به چشم / روز و شب بر وى ندارم هيچ خشم
entries.english: He said, “Day and night I see the murderer with my eyes; I have no anger against him,

entries.farsi: ز آنكه مرگم همچو من خوش آمده ست / مرگ من در بعث چنگ اندر زده ست‏
entries.english: Because death has become sweet as manna to me: my death has laid fast hold of resurrection.”

entries.farsi: مرگ بى‏مرگى بود ما را حالل / برگ بى‏برگى بود ما را نوال‏
entries.english: The death of deathlessness is lawful to us, the provision of unprovidedness is a bounty to us.

entries.farsi: ظاهرش مرگ و به باطن زندگى / ظاهرش ابتر نهان پايندگى‏
entries.english: It is death outwardly but life inwardly: apparently it is a cutting-off, in secret it is permanence.

entries.farsi: در رحم زادن جنين را رفتن است / در جهان او را ز نو بشكفتن است‏
entries.english: To the embryo in the womb birth is a going: in the world it blossoms anew.

entries.farsi: چون مرا سوى اجل عشق و هواست / نهى ال تُلْقُوا بِأَيْدِيكُمْ مراست‏
entries.english: “Since I have intense love and longing for death, the prohibition do not cast yourselves is for me,

entries.farsi: ز آنكه نهى از دانه‏ى شيرين بود / تلخ را خود نهى حاجت كى شود
entries.english: Because the sweet berry is prohibited; how should it become necessary to prohibit the sour one?

entries.farsi: دانه‏اى كه تلخ باشد مغز و پوست / تلخى و مكروهى‏اش خود نهى اوست‏
entries.english: The berry that has a sour kernel and rind— its very sourness and disagreeableness are a prohibition of it.

entries.farsi: دانه‏ى مردن مرا شيرين شده ست / بل هم احياء پى من آمده ست‏
entries.english: To me the berry of dying has become sweet: no, they are living has come on my account.

entries.farsi: إن في موتي حياتي يا فتى / كم أفارق موطني حتى متى‏
entries.english: Slay me, my trusty friends, slay me, vile as I am: truly, in my being slain is my life for evermore.

entries.farsi: اقتلوني يا ثقاتي الئما / إن في قتلي حياتي دايما
entries.english: Truly, in my death is my life, O youth— how long shall I be parted from my home? Until when?

entries.farsi: فرقتي لو لم تكن في ذا السكون / لم يقل إِنَّا إِلَيْهِ راجعون‏
entries.english: If there were not in my staving my separation, He would not have said, ‘Truly, we are returning to Him.’”

entries.farsi: راجع آن باشد كه باز آيد به شهر / سوى وحدت آيد از تفريق دهر
entries.english: The returning one is he that comes back to his city, and from the revolution of Time approaches the Unity.

sections.title_fa: افتادن ركابدار هر بارى پيش على عليه السلام كه اى امير المؤمنين از بهر خدا مرا بكش و از اين قضا برهان‏
sections.title_en: How the stirrup-holder of ‘Ali, may God honour his person, came, saying,
“For God’s sake, kill me and deliver me from this doom.”

entries.farsi: باز آمد كاى على زودم بكش / تا نبينم آن دم و وقت ترش‏
entries.english: “He came back, saving, ‘O ‘Ali, kill me quickly, that I may not see that bitter moment and hour.

entries.farsi: من حاللت مى‏كنم خونم بريز / تا نبيند چشم من آن رستخيز
entries.english: Shed my blood, I make it lawful to you, so that my eye may not behold that resurrection ‘.

entries.farsi: گفتم ار هر ذره‏اى خونى شود / خنجر اندر كف به قصد تو رود
entries.english: I said, if every atom should become a murderer and, dagger in hand, go to attack you,

entries.farsi: يك سر مو از تو نتواند بريد / چون قلم بر تو چنان خطى كشيد
entries.english: None could cut from you the tip of a single hair, since the Pen has written against you such a line.

entries.farsi: ليك بى‏غم شو شفيع تو منم / خواجه‏ى روحم نه مملوك تنم‏
entries.english: But do not grieve: I am intercessor for you: I am the spirit’s master, I am not the body’s slave.

entries.farsi: پيش من اين تن ندارد قيمتى / بى‏تن خويشم فتى ابن الفتى‏
entries.english: This body bath no value in my sight: without my body I am the noble, the son of the noble.

entries.farsi: خنجر و شمشير شد ريحان من / مرگ من شد بزم و نرگسدان من‏
entries.english: Dagger and sword have become my sweet basil: my death has become my banquet and narcissus-plot.’”

entries.farsi: آن كه او تن را بدين سان پى كند / حرص ميرى و خالفت كى كند
entries.english: He that hamstrings his body in this fashion, how should he covet the Princedom and the Caliphate?

entries.farsi: ز آن به ظاهر كوشد اندر جاه و حكم / تا اميران را نمايد راه و حكم‏
entries.english: Outwardly he strives after power and authority, that he may show to princes the way and judgment

entries.farsi: تا اميرى را دهد جانى دگر / تا دهد نخل خالفت را ثمر
entries.english: That he may give another spirit to the Princedom; that he may give fruit to the palm-tree of the Caliphate.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه فتح طلبيدن پيغامبر عليه السلام مكه را و غير مكه را بجهت دوستى ملك دنيا نبود چون فرموده است كه الدنيا جيفه بلكه به امر بود
sections.title_en: Explaining that the motive of the Prophet, on whom be peace, in seeking to conquer Mecca and other than Mecca was not love of worldly dominion, inasmuch as he has said
“This world is a carcase,” but that on the contrary it was by the command.

entries.farsi: جهد پيغمبر به فتح مكه هم / كى بود در حب دنيا متهم‏
entries.english: Likewise the Prophet’s struggle to conquer Mecca— how can he be suspected of love of this world?

entries.farsi: آن كه او از مخزن هفت آسمان / چشم و دل بر بست روز امتحان‏
entries.english: He who on the day of trial shut his eyes and heart to the treasury of the Seven Heavens,

entries.farsi: از پى نظاره‏ى او حور و جان / پر شده آفاق هر هفت آسمان‏
entries.english: The horizons of all the Seven Heavens were full of houris and genies to gaze upon him,

entries.farsi: خويشتن آراسته از بهر او / خود و را پرواى غير دوست كو
entries.english: Having arrayed themselves for his sake— how indeed should he care for anything except the Beloved?

entries.farsi: آن چنان پر گشته از اجلال حق / كه در او هم ره نيابد آل حق‏
entries.english: He had become so filled with magnification of God that even those nearest to God would find no way there.

entries.farsi: ال يسع فينا نبي مرسل / و الملك و الروح ايضا فاعقلوا
entries.english: “In Us is no room for a prophet sent as an apostle, nor yet for the Angels or the Spirit. Therefore, understand!”

entries.farsi: گفت ما زاغيم همچون زاغ نه / مست صباغيم مست باغ نه‏
entries.english: He said, “We are ma zagh (Our eye did not rove), we are not like crows (zagh); We are intoxicated with the Dyer, we are not intoxicated with the garden.”

entries.farsi: چون كه مخزنهاى افلاک و عقول / چون خسى آمد بر چشم رسول‏
entries.english: Inasmuch as to the eye of the Prophet the treasuries of the celestial spheres and intelligences seemed as a straw,

entries.farsi: آن گمان بر وى ضمير بد كند / كه قياس از جهل و حرص خود كند
entries.english: What, then, would Mecca and Syria and Iraq be, that he should show fight and longing?

entries.farsi: پس چه باشد مكه و شام و عراق / كه نمايد او نبرد و اشتياق‏
entries.english: That thought and opinion is the hypocrite’s, who judges from the analogy of his own wicked soul.

entries.farsi: آبگينه‏ى زرد چون سازى نقاب / زرد بينى جمله نور آفتاب‏
entries.english: When you make yellow glass a veil, you see all the sunlight yellow.

entries.farsi: بشكن آن شيشه‏ى كبود و زرد را / تا شناسى گرد را و مرد را
entries.english: Break those blue and yellow glasses, in order that you may know the dust and the man.

entries.farsi: گرد فارس گرد سر افراشته / گرد را تو مرد حق پنداشته‏
entries.english: The dust has lifted up its head around the horseman: you have fancied the dust to be the man of God.

entries.farsi: گرد ديد ابليس و گفت اين فرع طين / چون فزايد بر من آتش جبين‏
entries.english: Iblis saw the dust, and said, “How should this offspring of clay be superior to me of the fiery brow?”

entries.farsi: تا تو مى‏بينى عزيزان را بشر / دان كه ميراث بليس است آن نظر
entries.english: So long as you are regarding the holy as men, know that that view is an inheritance from Iblis.

entries.farsi: گر نه فرزند بليسى اى عنيد / پس به تو ميراث آن سگ چون رسيد
entries.english: If you are not the child of Iblis, O rebellious one, then how has the inheritance of that cur come to you?

entries.farsi: من نيم سگ شير حقم حق پرست / شير حق آن است كز صورت برست‏
entries.english: “I am not a cur, I am the Lion of God, a worshipper of God: the lion of God is he that has escaped from form.

entries.farsi: شير دنيا جويد اشكارى و برگ / شير مولى جويد آزادى و مرگ‏
entries.english: The lion of this world seeks a prey and provision; the lion of the Lord seeks freedom and death.

entries.farsi: چون كه اندر مرگ بيند صد وجود / همچو پروانه بسوزاند وجود
entries.english: Inasmuch as in death he sees a hundred existences, like the moth he burns away existence.”

entries.farsi: شد هواى مرگ طوق صادقان / كه جهودان را بد اين دم امتحان‏
entries.english: Desire for death became the badge of the sincere, for this word was a test for the Jews.

entries.farsi: در نبى فرمود كاى قوم يهود / صادقان را مرگ باشد گنج و سود
entries.english: He said in the Qur’an, “O people of the Jews (self-reliant ones?), death is a treasure and gain to the sincere.

entries.farsi: همچنان كه آرزوى سود هست / آرزوى مرگ بردن ز آن به است‏
entries.english: Even as there is desire for profit, the desire to win death is better than that.

entries.farsi: اى جهودان بهر ناموس كسان / بگذرانيد اين تمنا بر زبان‏
entries.english: O Jews, for the sake of honour by men of worth, let this wish be uttered on your tongues.”

entries.farsi: يك جهودى اين قدر زهره نداشت / چون محمد اين علم را بر فراشت‏
entries.english: Not a single Jew had so much courage, when Mohammed raised this banner .

entries.farsi: گفت اگر رانيد اين را بر زبان / يك يهودى خود نماند در جهان‏
entries.english: He said, “If they should utter this on their tongues, truly not one Jew would be left in the world.”

entries.farsi: پس يهودان مال بردند و خراج / كه مكن رسوا تو ما را اى سراج‏
entries.english: Then the Jews brought the property and land-tax, saying, “Do not put us to shame, O Lamp.”

entries.farsi: اين سخن را نيست پايانى پديد / دست با من ده چو چشمت دوست ديد
entries.english: “There is no end in sight to this discourse: give me your hand, since yours eye has seen the Friend.”

sections.title_fa: گفتن امير المؤمنين على عليه السلام با قرين خود كه چون خدو انداختى در روى من نفس من جنبيد و اخالص عمل نماند، مانع كشتن تو آن شد
sections.title_en: How the Prince of the Faithful, ‘Ali—may God honour his person! —said to his antagonist, “When you did spit in my face, my fleshly self was aroused and I could no longer act with entire sincerity: that hindered me from slaying you.”

entries.farsi: گفت امير المؤمنين با آن جوان / كه به هنگام نبرد اى پهلوان‏
entries.english: The Prince of the Faithful said to that youth, “In the hour of battle, O knight,

entries.farsi: چون خدو انداختى در روى من / نفس جنبيد و تبه شد خوى من‏
entries.english: When you didst spit in my face, my fleshly self was aroused and my disposition was corrupted.

entries.farsi: نيم بهر حق شد و نيمى هوا / شركت اندر كار حق نبود روا
entries.english: Half came to be for God’s sake, and half idle passion: in God’s affair partnership’ is not allowable.

entries.farsi: تو نگاريده‏ى كف موالستى / آن حقى كرده‏ى من نيستى‏
entries.english: You are limned by the hand of the Lord: you are God’s, you are not made by me.

entries.farsi: نقش حق را هم به امر حق شكن / بر زجاجه‏ى دوست سنگ دوست زن‏
entries.english: Break God’s image, by God’s command; cast at the Beloved’s glass, the Beloved’s stone.”

entries.farsi: گبر اين بشنيد و نورى شد پديد / در دل او تا كه زنارى بريد
entries.english: The fire-worshipper heard this, and a light appeared in his heart, so that he cut a girdle.

entries.farsi: گفت من تخم جفا مى‏كاشتم / من ترا نوعى دگر پنداشتم‏
entries.english: He said, “I was sowing the seed of wrong: I fancied you otherwise.

entries.farsi: تو ترازوى احد خو بوده‏اى / بل زبانه‏ى هر ترازو بوده‏اى‏
entries.english: You have been the balance with the nature of the One; no, you have been the tongue of every balance.

entries.farsi: تو تبار و اصل و خويشم بوده‏اى / تو فروغ شمع كيشم بوده‏اى‏
entries.english: You have been my race and stock and kin, you have been the radiance of the candle of my religion.

entries.farsi: من غالم آن چراغ چشم جو / كه چراغت روشنى پذرفت از او
entries.english: I am the slave of that eye-seeking Lamp from which your lamp received splendour.

entries.farsi: من غالم موج آن درياى نور / كه چنين گوهر بر آرد در ظهور
entries.english: I am the surrendered to the surge of that Sea of Light which brings a pearl like this into view.

entries.farsi: عرضه كن بر من شهادت را كه من / مر ترا ديدم سرافراز زمن‏
entries.english: Offer me the profession of the Faith, for I regard you as the exalted one of the time.”

entries.farsi: قرب پنجه كس ز خويش و قوم او / عاشقانه سوى دين كردند رو
entries.english: Near fifty persons of his kindred and tribe lovingly turned their faces towards the Religion.

entries.farsi: او به تيغ حلم چندين حلق را / وا خريد از تيغ و چندين خلق را
entries.english: By the sword of clemency he redeemed so many throats of such a multitude from the sword.

entries.farsi: تيغ حلم از تيغ آهن تيزتر / بل ز صد لشكر ظفر انگيزتر
entries.english: The sword of clemency is sharper than the sword of iron; no, it is more productive of victory than a hundred armies.

sections.title_fa: ‏خاتمه‏ى دفتر اول

entries.farsi: اى دريغا لقمه‏اى دو خورده شد / جوشش فكرت از آن افسرده شد
entries.english: Oh, alas, two mouthfuls were eaten, and thereby the ferment of thought was frozen up.

entries.farsi: گندمى خورشيد آدم را كسوف / چون ذنب شعشاع بدرى را خسوف‏
entries.english: A grain of wheat eclipsed the sun of Adam, as the descending node is eclipse to the brilliance of the full-moon.

entries.farsi: اينت لطف دل كه از يك مشت گل / ماه او چون مى‏شود پروين گسل‏
entries.english: Behold the beauty of the heart, how its moon scatters the Pleiades by a single handful of clay.

entries.farsi: نان چو معنى بود خوردش سود بود / چون كه صورت گشت انگيزد جحود
entries.english: When the bread was spirit, it was beneficial; since it became form, it rouses disbelief.

entries.farsi: همچو خار سبز كاشتر مى‏خورد / ز ان خورش صد نفع و لذت مى‏برد
entries.english: As the green thistles which a camel eats, and gains from eating them a hundred benefits and pleasures:

entries.farsi: چون كه آن سبزيش رفت و خشك گشت / چون همان را مى‏خورد اشتر ز دشت‏
entries.english: When the camel from the desert eats those same thistles, after their greenness is gone and they have become dry,

entries.farsi: مى‏دراند كام و لنجش اى دريغ / كان چنان ورد مربى گشت تيغ‏
entries.english: They rend his palate and cheek— oh, alas that such a nourishing rose became a sword!

entries.farsi: نان چو معنى بود بود آن خار سبز / چون كه صورت شد كنون خشك است و گبز
entries.english: When the bread was spirit, it was the green thistles; since it became form, it is now dry and gross.

entries.farsi: تو بد آن عادت كه او را پيش از اين / خورده بودى اى وجود نازنين‏
entries.english: According as you hadst formerly been in the habit of eating it, O gracious being,

entries.farsi: بر همان بو مى‏خورى اين خشك را / بعد از آن كاميخت معنى با ثرى‏
entries.english: In the same hope you are eating this dry stuff, after the spirit has become mingled with clay.

entries.farsi: گشت خاك آميز و خشك و گوشت بر / ز آن گياه اكنون بپرهيز اى شتر
entries.english: It has become mixed with earth and dry and flesh-cutting: abstain now from that herbage, O camel!

entries.farsi: سخت خاك آلود مى‏آيد سخن / آب تيره شد سر چه بند كن‏
entries.english: The words are coming very earth-soiled; the water has become turbid: stop up the mouth of the well,

entries.farsi: تا خدايش باز صاف و خوش كند / او كه تيره كرد هم صافش كند
entries.english: That God may again make it pure and sweet, that He who made it turbid may likewise make it pure.

entries.farsi: صبر آرد آرزو را نه شتاب / صبر كن و اهلل اعلم بالصواب‏
entries.english: Patience brings the object of desire, not Haste. Have patience—and God know best what is right.

sections.title_fa: پایان دفتر اول
